artist.name: Lackluster
artist.profile:
His first major label release, Container ([l=deFocus], UK) was well received by critics and the electronic enthusiasts alike. He has after that had releases thru labels like [l=Merck] (Miami,US), [l=Rikos Records] (Finland), [l=U-Cover] (Belgium), [l=Inc.us] (Belgium), [l=Zealrecords] (Belgium), [l=Psychonavigation Records] (Ireland), [l=New Speak] (Sweden).
Born October 26, 1978 in Helsinki, Esa first became interested in computer music when he was 6. Some of his early favorites were the songs created for C64 games, later modules by such luminaries as Aleksi Eeben, Niko Nyman, Sami Järvinen, and others, on the Commodore Amiga. By 1994, Esa had shifted his musical interests to ambient/IDM and has later on flourished as a member of the Helsinki scene, providing music for various netlabels like [l=Monotonik] and [l=Kahvi Collective].
Currently, Esa continues to release thru several international labels and explores new tracks & techniques at his flat in eastern Helsinki.
Contact: esaruoho@gmail.com

artist.name: Meek
artist.profile: Mike Baugh, based in Montreal Canada, has been making out of the ordinary electronic music for 7 years. He has played live in various places around the country with the likes of Apparat, Deadbeat, Pan Sonic, Unit and Stewart Walker. Amid 4 independent releases (CDR1, CDR2, CDR3, and self-titled) are appearances on compilations by Fat Cat, Worm Interface, Six Records, Cocosolidciti and Mutek_Rec.

artist.name: Gamers In Exile
artist.profile: [b]Gamers In Exile[/b] was a UK-based experimental electronics duo, formed by two Italian producers in the early 2000s. The collective operated the [l=Unbearable Recordings] label in London to release the bulk of their recorded output.

artist.name: Solenoid
artist.profile: Solenoid is a Portland (Oregon-USA) based electronic music composer who has explored a variety of genres. He co-runs the label [b][l=Community Library][/b] As part of a free-improv duo, [b][a=Office Products][/b], and solo as [b][a=Mr. Pharmacist][/b], he makes more purely-experimental music. Solenoid sometimes uses a drum pad or other live instruments when performing or dj'ing.

artist.name: Galerie Stratique
artist.profile: Sound artist from Quebec, Canada.

artist.name: The Sons Of Silence
artist.profile: The group formed when Daniel Mudford, Pete Woodhead, and Joe Gardiner were invited by Andrew Hulme to play live with [a63379]. Performing and recording gave them a moment of confidence long enough to go out with their own ideas. Their first inspiration was the idea of [a581] going to a nightclub; their main interest currently is making music in the Kosmische idiom and guitar-powered drone.

artist.name: Ultramarine
artist.profile:
London and Essex-based UK duo Ian Cooper and Paul Hammond; formed in 1989. Cooper & Hammond were both former members of the band [a=A Primary Industry].
Ultramarine's early releases were issued on the Brussels-based label [l=Les Disques du Crépuscule] and by [l=Brainiak Records], a label affiliated to [l2481919], an early purveyor of regular House music nights in Soho, London. Their debut album [r=32318] was issued by Crépuscule in 1990. Their classic second album [r=201342] was initially released on Brainiak in 1991 and given a wider audience with the release of an expanded edition on [l=Rough Trade (UK)] in 1992. The album was neatly described by [a715566] in his book [i]Energy Flash[/i] as:
[i]Perhaps the first and best stab at that seeming contradiction-in-terms, pastoral techno... all sun-ripened, meandering lassitude and undulant dub-sway tempos... like acid-house suffused with the folky-jazzy ambience of the Canterbury scene.[/i]

artist.name: Mike Paradinas
artist.profile:
Born: 1971 in Wimbledon, United Kingdom.
Artist working across a variety of electronic dance music styles. Runs the [l=Planet Mu] label, which reflects a wide range of influences and interests.

artist.name: T.Power
artist.profile:
This prolific artist has been producing breakbeat, jungle, and drum and bass since the early 90s. The "Mutant Jazz" single and the critically acclaimed album "Self Evident Truth Of An Intuitive Mind" (both released in 1995) are touted as staple Drum and Bass classics.
In 1998, T Power, along with Cris Stevens, formed "Chocolate Weasel" and released the single "Music for Body Lockers", followed by a funk and hip-hop album called "Spaghettification".
T Power also collaborated with Shy FX (Andre Williams), both as Ebony Dubsters and Shy FX and T Power. The duo's 2001 breakthrough track "Shake Ur Body" with vocalist Di reached Number 7 hit in the UK Singles Chart. Released on EMI subsidiary Positiva, it gained widespread support from both club and commercial radio DJs and helped to further popularize drum & bass as a mainstream music genre. The success of "Shake Ur Body" was followed by the duo's album "Set It Off", released in 2002 on Pete Tong's label F.F.R.R.. Several EPs and single releases followed; by 2005, Shy FX had made the decision to consolidate his releases onto his own label and founded Digital Soundboy Recordings. In collaboration with T Power, they subsequently released the album Diary of a Digital Sound Boy on 17 October 2005, featuring the songs "Feelings", "On The Run" and "Plastic Soul". Shy FX and T Power also released "Don't Wanna Know" featuring Di and MC Skibadee. The song was notably used on the UK television programme Soccer AM as part of the 'Skills Skool' feature.
In 2009, T Power reincarnated himself as Mr. Sofalumpkins, an electro/breakbeat/techno DJ, performing live Ableton sets, which consisted of a new sound and original new tracks.
In 2013, T Power collaborated with John Rolodex on the single, "That Jungle Vibe - Featuring Khadija", which was released on V Records.
In 2014, T Power released "The Lazy Nine", his first album under his own name Marc Royal, on Adastra Recordings (his own record label).

artist.name: Stereolab
artist.profile:
Stereolab formed in London in 1990 by record collector [a=Tim Gane] (°1964) and French chanteuse [a=Laetitia Sadier] (°1968). They were active between 1990 and 2009, after which the band went into hiatus for ten years. In 2019 they reunited for live performances, to promote a year-long seven album reissue campaign. 2025 sees the release of their first newly recorded material since 2009/10. [a=Sean O'Hagan] was a member from 1993 to 1994, and continued appearing on later records for occasional guest appearances.
Guitar, [a=Tim Gane] (1990-2009, 2019-) Vocals, [a=Laetitia Sadier] (1990-2009, 2019-) Vocals, [a=Gina Morris] (1991) Vocals, [a=Mary Hansen] (1992-2002) (died 2002) Bass, [a=Martin Kean] (1990-1993) Bass, [a=Duncan Brown] (1993-1996) Bass, [a=Richard Harrison] (1996-1998) Bass, [a=Simon Johns] (1998-2009) Bass, [a=Xavi Muñoz (2)] (2019-) Drums, [a=Joe Dilworth] (1990-1992) Drums, [a=Andy Ramsay] (1992-2009, 2019-) Keyboards, [a=Sean O'Hagan] (1993-1994) Keyboards, [a=Katharine Gifford] (1993-1994) Keyboards, [a=Morgane Lhote] (1996-2001) Keyboards, [a=Dominic Jeffery] (2001-2006) Keyboards, [a=Joe Watson] (2006-2009, 2019-) Horn, [a=Joe Walters] (2006-2009)

artist.name: Bola
artist.profile:
Abstract, downtempo and IDM producer from Manchester, England Active since 1994.
[l=The Bolamachine] studio.

artist.name: David Toop
artist.profile:
David Toop (born 1949) has been developing a practice that crosses boundaries of sound, listening, music and materials since 1970. This encompasses improvised music performance, writing, electronic sound, field recording, exhibition curating, sound art installations and opera. It includes eight acclaimed books, including Rap Attack (1984), [r=322126] (1995), Sinister Resonance (2010), Into the Maelstrom (2016), Flutter Echo, a memoir first published in Japan in 2017 (May 2019) and Inflamed Invisible: Writing On Art and Sound 1976-2018 (November 2019).
Briefly a member of David Cunningham’s pop project The Flying Lizards in 1979, he has released fifteen solo albums, from New and Rediscovered Musical Instruments on Brian Eno’s Obscure label (1975) and Sound Body on David Sylvian’s Samadhisound label (2006) to Entities Inertias Faint Beings (2016), Apparition Paintings and Field recording and Fox Spirits (2020) on Lawrence English’s ROOM40. His 1978 Amazonas recordings of Yanomami shamanism and ritual were released on Sub Rosa as Lost Shadows (2016). In recent years his collaborations include Rie Nakajima, Akio Suzuki, Tania Caroline Chen, John Butcher, Ken Ikeda, Elaine Mitchener, Henry Grimes, Sharon Gal, Camille Norment, Sidsel Endresen, Alasdair Roberts, Fred Frith, Thurston Moore, Ryuichi Sakamoto and a revived Alterations, the iconoclastic improvising quartet with Steve Beresford, Peter Cusack and Terry Day first formed in 1977. Curator of sound art exhibitions including Sonic Boom at the Hayward Gallery (2000), his opera – Star-shaped Biscuit – was performed as an Aldeburgh Faster Than Sound project in 2012. He is currently Professor of Audio Culture and Improvisation at London College of Communication.

namevariations.name: D. Toop
D.T.
Dave
Dave Toop
Toop
デヴィッド・トゥープ

artist.name: Wiggle
artist.profile: Italian project.

artist.name: Kit Clayton
artist.profile: Born: 1974, Evanston, Illinois. Currently living in Portland, Oregon, USA.

artist.name: Antye Greie-Fuchs
artist.profile:
German vocalist, songwriter, producer, performer, poet and calligrapher.
Born 1969 in East Germany. Currently living in Hailuoto, Finland.

artist.name: Wang Inc.
artist.profile: Wang Inc is electronic musician Bartolomeo Sailer.

artist.name: Benge
artist.profile: Benge is Ben Edwards. He has been exploring the sonic possibilities of electronic instruments since he was a small boy when he would spend his evenings in the music room of the school his parents ran. Being the 1970s it was full of synths and organs as well as percussion, recording and other classroom instruments. After graduating from art school in 1990 he began to set up a music studio and started recording his own blend of electronica, culminating in his debut album "[r=20743]" and the formation of [l=Expanding Records] in 1995. Since then he has released fourteen albums as Benge.

artist.name: Opiate
artist.profile: Danish electronic music artist and producer (b. 7 february 1973, Glostrup, Copenhagen). Founder of [l183] / [l931556].

artist.name: Carl Stone
artist.profile: Carl Stone (born February 10, 1953) is an American composer, primarily working in the field of live electronic music. He studied composition at the California Institute of the Arts with [a=Morton Subotnick] and [a=James Tenney] and has composed electro-acoustic music since 1972. He is a retired faculty member in the Department of Information Media at Chukyo University in Tokyo, Japan where he lives most of the year.

artist.name: Kaffe Matthews
artist.profile: Kaffe Matthews is one of the most active artists on the new electronic music scene, whose surprisingly rare approach exploiting digital gadgets and live performance has resulted in some unique works which transgress any boundary between sound and image. since 1996 she has been regularly performing all over the world in clubs, galleries, concert halls, tea rooms, boats, warehouses, the outback, releasing the best of these events on the six cd's, 'cd Ann', 'cd Bea' , 'cd cecile' ,'cd dd', 'cd eb and flo' presenting an annual document of her ever developing sound worlds. Recently she has collaborated and performed with agf, Ryoko Kuwajima, MIMEO, [a=Sachiko M], [a=Ikue Mori], [a=Marina Rosenfeld], [a=Oren Ambarchi], [a=Christian Fennesz], and is currently working on a new formation of the lappetites with [a=Eliane Radigue], pan-European electronics orchestra MIMEO, weightless animals (a trio with visual artist Mandy McIntosh and Zeena Parkins) making interactive sonic space cartoons from research at NASA, as well as numerous concert collaborations worldwide.

artist.name: Kreidler
artist.profile: German band founded 1994 in Düsseldorf

artist.name: Vladislav Delay
artist.profile: Finnish electronic musician and producer.

artist.name: Apache 61
artist.profile: Starting out in 1996, the Japanese artist known as Apache 61 now resides in Brixton. Her first single and album came out in 1997 on the label Daisyworld. Since then Mieko Shimizu has appeared on a variety of compilations, and last appered on the [l=No Immortal] label.

artist.name: Osymyso
artist.profile: Osymyso is a musician, producer and a DJ that employs cutting edge digital technology to create a sound with elements of electro, hip-hop, synth pop and plunderphonics. His best known works are intricate collages of existing music with clips from the television and radio although more than half of his releases contain fully original compositions. As a DJ he's renowned for his 'mash-up' approach, fusing meaty underground electro, breaks and dirty instrumental hip-hop with contemporary pop, adverts, film clips and daytime tv samples.

artist.name: Funkturm
artist.profile:
Funkturm began recording in 1996, and is processed, programmed and produced by Richard Gallon. Gallon's previous projects have included ambient experimental percussion duo Chemical Plant, Headbutt, and live sample improvisors Fork.
Funkturm transmuted from Operation Mind Control, who released one album - Funkturm - on D.O.R. in 1994, as well as three cassette-only releases on the cult Chainsaw label from 1990 to 1993.
The debut Funkturm album, Urban Mantras, is 9 tracks of minimalist breakbeats and hypnotic soundscapes. Funkturm takes its name from Berlin's radio tower. The name was chosen because of a fascination with the sounds and textures of international short-wave radio broadcasts. Radio sounds have been used throughout previous releases. A new album - provisionally titled Bon Tempi - is currently in progress, to be released later in 2001. The album is likely to feature more organic textures and instrumentation, heralding a return to the more improvisational nature of previous projects, melded with the hypnotic electronic rhythms and structures of Funkturm / Operation Mind Control work to date. The new material will be previewed on this site throughout the duration of recording. Stay tuned.

artist.name: Doppler 20:20
artist.profile: Doppler 20:20 is an experimetal audio/visual producer working in various genres under different alias's

artist.name: Noon
artist.profile: Producer and studio engineer from Warsaw, Poland. Noon used to be a member of one of the most popular polish hip-hop bands [a=Grammatik] and one half of the [a=Pezet-Noon] duo. He was born in 1979.

artist.name: Funkstörung
artist.profile: German IDM duo from Rosenheim.

artist.name: Chris De Luca
artist.profile: Music Producer

artist.name: Richard Devine
artist.profile:
American electronic musician and sound designer.
Devine's music is characterized by complex rhythms and heavy use of DSP. Gained popularity among IDM fans after remixing the [a=Aphex Twin] track "[url=https://www.discogs.com/Various-Warp103-Remixes/master/2775]Come To Daddy[/url]" (1999).
As sound designer he's done patches for several manufacturers including by Korg, Native Instruments, Clavia, Eventide, and more.

artist.name: Photek
artist.profile:
UK producer and musician. Born 6 September 1971 in St Albans, England
A fixture on the UK jungle scene since the early nineties, Photek a.k.a. Rupert Parkes (associated with Ipswich in the nineties) is best known for his role in developing the "intelligent" drum & bass genre.

artist.name: Max Tundra
artist.profile: Ben Jacobs (born 7 June 1974), more commonly known by the stage name Max Tundra, is an English multi-instrumental musician, singer and music producer.

artist.name: Fila Brazillia
artist.profile:
In 1990 David 'Man' McSherry and Steve Cobby began to record as Fila Brazillia. Hitherto their collaborations had been infrequent and ultra-spontaneous. Both had maintained positions in conventional bands but needed to trade the constraints of the "write - rehearse - gig - record" treadmill for a more fluent, and ultimately enjoyable approach. A nursery period for a music culled from sonic experiments and ideas quickly put to tape emerged. A treatment of all sounds as "objet trouvé" and a hobbyist attitude prevailed.
When Dave Brennand a.k.a. [a=Porky (2)] heard a piece composed on a wet Wednesday, he asked to release it. They happily agreed. As previous unreleased work hadn't warranted anything but comedy monickers - "Dada Black Sheep" being one - a name was needed. At that time the media's "killer dog frenzy" was in full swing and a breed known as the fila brasiliero was the subject of a debate in the Commons. This was misheard on the radio by Steve, proposed and accepted for the single and eventually the name stuck. The track "Mermaids", was Fila Brazillia's first single, released in Spring 1991, and marked the beginning of a decade which saw Fila yield seven highly acclaimed albums and a skip-full of singles on Pork Recordings and over 60 remixes."

artist.name: John Moffett
artist.profile: American engineer and remix artist who founded and owned DJ remix service [l=Eurotracks Remix Service].

artist.name: Tied & Tickled Trio
artist.profile:
German electronica/dub/jazz musical collective, formed in 1994 in Weilheim, Bavaria.
Members: Ulrich Wangenheim (tenor saxophone, bass clarinet, flute) Johannes Enders (tenor saxophone, flute, keyboards) Micha Acher (bass, trumpet, trombone, keyboards, organ) Markus Acher (drums, keyboards, percussion) Andreas Gerth (electronic, delays) Christoph Brandner (electronic drums) Robert Klinger (bass)

artist.name: Christof Kurzmann
artist.profile:
Saxophonist, born in Vienna, Austria. A musician of mainly the clarinet and computer, he wanders between electropop, improvisation and "new music". He is occasionally a soloist though prefers to be part of a collective or working group. Kurzmann has performed on every continent but Australia.
Owns and runs the label [l=charhizma] (right spelling charhizma with "c" instead of "C"), which currently functions as a platform to present musicians from around the world in various live settings. Under that program, he organizes various events in the music- and media-genres. Kurzmann also works as a sound engineer, mixes and masters audio: mainly for releases on charihizma.
A conscientious objector of military and civil service, he currently lives in Vienna, Austria and Buenos Aires, Argentina.

artist.name: Console
artist.profile: Electronic music band started between 1994 and 1995 and led by [a=Martin Gretschmann] who remains the main composer and producer.

artist.name: Kandis
artist.profile: German electronica producer.

artist.name: The Rip-Off Artist
artist.profile: Electronic artist based in California. Ran the short-lived [l=Inflatabl Labl] from 2003 to 2005.

artist.name: Bisk
artist.profile: Japanese producer of electronic music, born in Tokyo.

artist.name: Ensemble
artist.profile:
Electronic music project founded by Olivier Alary. Ensemble initially consisted solely of Alary and various guests, but officially became a trio when Johannes Malfatti and Darcy Conroy joined for their album [m=348784].
[b]Do not use this for a generic Ensemble[/b] If no specific Ensemble can be found, see [g=2.9.1] for information on how to properly create an Ensemble.

artist.name: Richard Thomas
artist.profile: Richard Thomas was born and raised in South Wales and moved to London in 1997. Gaining a reputation as a contending improviser and abstract composer, he has collaborated with many artist since the beginning, such as the Wormholes and Squarepusher amongst others. Utilising almost everything he can lay his hands on, drumkit, melodica, toys, clocks, transducers, self-made instruments, etc. beside the listed groups he is also a member of The Bohman Brothers Extended Family and the conductor of the Hairy Butter Acoustic Orchestra.

artist.name: Tone Rec
artist.profile: Between 1997 and 1999, Tone Rec produced 3 full-length albums for Sub Rosa + an extented remix project in 2000. They combined electronics, field recordings and drum loops.

artist.name: To Rococo Rot
artist.profile:
German post rock and electronic music band founded in 1995 in Berlin.
Members: Stefan Schneider: bass guitar (ex-member of [a=Kreidler]) Robert Lippok: guitar, electronics Ronald Lippok: drums, effects

artist.name: Scanner
artist.profile: Electronic musician and multimedia artist (born 1964 in London, UK).

artist.name: Calla
artist.profile:
American indie rock / post rock band formed in New York City in 1997.
Aurelio Valle and Wayne Magruder were previously playing together in [a=The Factory Press] who broke up the same year when Calla was started.

artist.name: Bret Easton Ellis
artist.profile: American novelist and short story writer, born 7 March 1964 in Los Angeles, California, USA.

artist.name: Rick Preston
artist.profile: DJ and producer from California

artist.name: Kevin McKay
artist.profile: Owns [b][l=Glasgow Underground][/b] home to Romanthony, Mateo & Matos, DJ Q, Milton Jackson, Powder Productions and others... Owns the [b][l=Breastfed Recordings][/b] label. Mixed all the Mylo tracks released on Breastfed and co-produced half of the UK platinum selling album [b][r=305827][/b] Owns the [b][l=Heartbeats][/b] label, home to [b][a=Grum][/b] From 2003-2005 was the writer/producer half of [b][a=Linus Loves][/b], wrote "Night Music" and produced "The Terrace" and the album "Stage Invader". One half of [b][a=Muzique Tropique][/b] (releases on [b][l=Jus' Trax][/b] and remixes for the likes of Larry Heard, Two Lone Swordsmen, House of 909 and 16B.)

artist.name: DJ Q
artist.profile: DJ & producer from Glasgow.

artist.name: Minion
artist.profile: Tom Sitterding started the Minion project in 1998 with the release of the limited split 7" with Acid Enema on [l30337] as his first output. He has been an active member of the Los Angeles underground scene since the second half of the nineties and helped starting the [l16919] collective.

artist.name: Rude 66
artist.profile:
Dutch producer Rude 66 started his first band in the 1980’s and released his first EP in 1994 on [l1908] as the 2nd ever artist on the label. He can be considered one of the earliest influences for what is now known as "West Coast sound of Holland". With his wife [a688446] often on vocals and vocoder, Rude combines Electro and Acid with Italo, Wave, and Industrial, while their songs are populated by shady characters such as dictators and serial killers. The duo have played live (and done the occasional DJ set) from illegal squats to large festivals all over the world since the mid of the 90s.
As a mastering engineer, Rude specializes in vinyl mastering and restoring old tape recordings for reissues. Masters are both credited to Rude 66 or [a688445].
Rude ran and curated [l9874] in the early 2000’s and occasionally uses his own [l23252] for releases.

artist.name: Mark Broom
artist.profile: DJ and producer from London, UK. Born 6 May 1971. He was the resident DJ at Skism.

artist.name: Unit Moebius
artist.profile:
Starting as Europe's only true answer to Underground Resistance, the godfathers of the Hague's industrial techno/planet rock scene, evolved gradually from deep alienating lo-fi acid house and crazy rigid jack trax (mixing the best of the detroit and chicago styles) to hard pounding dark industrial techno, freaky techno funk pop and mean minimalistic machine music. Raw primitive production and sound (the first 7 albums were recorded on a nearly-broken cassette recorder - it was all they had!), very diverse in style, but always with that typical twisted, paranoid and fucked-up drive, a bad sweaty groove that feels like a mindfuck.
Members: Menno van Os, Guy Tavares, Jan Duivenvoorden, Ferenc E. van der Sluijs

artist.name: Starfish Pool
artist.profile: The electronic project of [a326156] - that evolved out of [a83997] - that was active between 1993 and 2003. Starfish Pool was working in the experimental area of minimal, rhythmic, ambient and noise in all its aspects. Since the very start it is the manipulation of loops - the basic idea of the way tracks were created as Starfish Pool. This concept that has nothing to do with the sound but more with the way that tracks where practically made. In 10 years' time Starfish Pool had more than 25 releases.

artist.name: Andrew Hulme
artist.profile: UK musician, movie editor and movie director.

artist.name: Michael Prime
artist.profile: Electronic avant-garde performer. Ran [l=Mycophile Records].

artist.name: Dallas Simpson
artist.profile: Dallas Simpson has performed at the London College of Music in 1998 with composer Chris Thorpe, in York (UK) with Chris Thorpe and Linda Merrick (2000); in Glasgow, Scotland in 1999 as part of the Drift environmental sound art / acoustic ecology project, with Max Eastley and Helmut Lemke at Creswell Craggs, Derbyshire UK on two occasions 1997/8. All his work is recorded in binaural stereo giving full periphonic surround sound on good quality headphones. Tracks of his have been released through the acclaimed Time Recording's em:t series (Abha - em:t 2296, Waterpump - em:t 1197). He was included as a composer in the "Music for Spaces" series on BBC Radio 3 (UK) (1997) some four years ago. He is also a professional CD Mastering Engineer trading under the name [l282702].

artist.name: Plug
artist.profile: British electronic music composer and producer, born 26 January 1973 in Cornwall, UK.

artist.name: Wheel
artist.profile: British electronic music composer and producer.

artist.name: Pushy!
artist.profile: French producer. He is the founder of [l=Pushy].

artist.name: PCM
artist.profile: Formed in 1989 by Nik Wells and Neil Harvey, PCM have carved their own niche in the Bham underground scene with their uncompromisingly "dark" take on drum and bass. Combining nitrous-fuelled beats with dirty, subsonic baselines, they have recorded for labels such as Law and Auder, Possible and Manifold (USA) as well as remixing fellow Brummies Scorn and Pram.

artist.name: Finitribe
artist.profile: Production team from Edinburgh, Scotland. The original line up is Chris Connelly, Simon McGlynn, Andrew McGregor, John Vick, Philip Pinsky and David Miller

artist.name: The Black Dog
artist.profile: The Black Dog was formerly the trio of [a=Ken Downie], [a=Ed Handley] and [a=Andy Turner], also known at the time as [a=Black Dog Productions]. Ed and Andy left to pursue their [a=Plaid] project, Ken continued with several different line-ups of Black Dog before choosing to work with [a=Martin Dust] and [a=Richard Dust] from [l=Dust Science Recordings] after a long break from the music scene. Ken Downie died December 20th 2025.

namevariations.name: Black Dog
Black Dog Project
TBD
The Blackdog
tBd
ザ・ブラック・ドッグ

artist.name: Ajax Disco Spanner
artist.profile: Techno project from Dublin, Ireland.

artist.name: Koru
artist.profile: Koru is a UK based Maori global, tribal, progressive producer/live artist, who has released many tracks and played extensively worldwide, including Brazil, Thailand, India, UK, Australia, New Zealand, France, Slovenia, Ireland and more. Koru has pioneered psychedelic drum n bass as well as very able in Tribal House, and various forms of Trance. He has developed a new sound/style called Trip funk. Koru plays live of a midi controlled laptop and has a great live show which includes beat connected tribal/global visuals.

artist.name: Muslimgauze
artist.profile: The main musical project of Bryn Jones (17 June 1961 - 14 January 1999), a British ethnic electronica and experimental musician who was influenced by conflicts and history in the Muslim world, often with an emphasis on the conflict between Israel and Palestine. The name Muslimgauze is a play on the word muslin (a type of gauze) combined with Muslim, referring to Bryn Jones' preoccupation with conflicts throughout the Muslim world.

artist.name: Spooncurve
artist.profile: Spooncurve is fronted by singer Faye Rochelle, and produced by Martin Lee-Stephenson, Fayes co-writer credits include Kate Bush.

artist.name: Buckfunk 3000
artist.profile: Apparently "Buckfunk" is the sort of music that Buck Rogers might listen to in a 25th century bar scene.

artist.name: Freq Nasty
artist.profile: Breakbeat DJ based in Los Angeles, United States.

artist.name: Sons Of Mecha
artist.profile: Alex Orton-Green (Yoof) and Jody Shires (Bionic) are two brothers behind the Sunz Of Mecha, Uncouth Youth and Mechanoise Sound System guises. Along with their many aliases they are the label owners/managers of the underground UK breaks label [l=Mechanoise Records] and sublabels [l=BreaksFM], [l=Defbox], [l=Last Meal Recordings] & [l=Revolution Music].

namevariations.name: Sons Of Mecha [VR Boy & DJ Erb]
Sonz Of Mecha
Sunz Of Mecha
Sunz Of Mecha Underground
Sunz of Mecha
The Sunz Of Mecha Underground

artist.name: Thomas Krome
artist.profile: Swedish techno producer, no longer active.

artist.name: Nudge
artist.profile: American IDM/leftfield/experimental group. For the [a349] side-project, please see [a223138].

artist.name: Synapse
artist.profile: US techno duo John Selway & Szostek

artist.name: Bolz Bolz
artist.profile: Since 1991 Bolz Bolz is an electro/techno influenzed analouge working producer. He is the owner of the [l=World Electric] Label, located in Cologne, which started in 1997. From 1998 to 2006 he released 2 albums and 12 EPs, mostly on his own Label. His last album [m63776] was released on the German [l=Feis] Label.

artist.name: Phthalocyanine
artist.profile: US-based electronic musician, founder and owner of the [l2233] label.

artist.name: Brothomstates
artist.profile: Currently only Lassi Nikko is operating under the Brothomstates moniker.

artist.name: V/Vm
artist.profile: Earliest known project of James Kirby, under which he formed V/Vm Test Records and the netlabel Vukzid as a means to release his own works, as well as those of like-minded artists and collaborators. As V/Vm, he specialized in noise and plunderphonics, although his catalogue under this name is eclectic, with a wide range of approaches. Works tended to be harsh, highly experimental or otherwise confrontational.

namevariations.name: MC V/Vm
V Vm
V/VM
V/Vm Live At Stockport Town Hall
VVM
VVM as MRS VVMILLS
VVM/VVM
V_vm
v/v/m

artist.name: Proem
artist.profile: Proem formerly a resident of Austin, Texas, he now resides in Philadelphia, PA. He has been releasing music since 1999 on labels like Merck, n5MD, and Hydrant. Bailey started experimenting with electronic devices at an early age, using thrift store turntables, some tape decks, a few cheap Casio SK-5s, and some circuit bent bargain bin keyboards. He currently sequences in FL Studio, using a number of VST plugins, such as Kontakt and Reaktor. He is regarded as an early member of the US IDM scene.

artist.name: Chaircrusher
artist.profile: Runs the [l=Cornwarning] label

artist.name: Cex
artist.profile: Rjyan Kidwell (born c. 1980), also known by his stage name Cex, is an electronic producer who was raised in Baltimore, but later (2003) moved to Oakland, California. He is most known for his confrontational vocal style, especially prevailing in 'Tall, Dark & Handcuffed', released in 2002. Rjyan is also known for his various works with Miguel Depedro (Kid606) and Aaron Funk (Venetian Snares).

artist.name: Isabel Waidner
artist.profile:
Isabel Waidner is an experimental novelist, academic and journal editor.
In addition to [a272646], they played in The Pictures with [a635455] and [a1309513].

artist.name: Team Doyobi
artist.profile: British electronic music duo, comprised of Christopher Gladwin (born 1976) and Alexander Peverett (born 1976).

artist.name: Wevie Stonder
artist.profile: Wevie Stonder are an electronic music collective originally from Brighton, with current and former members now residing in the UK, Germany, Portugal and Spain. Current members are Alan Boorman, Chris Umney and Henry Sargeant, with former members Richard Sothcott and Nadir Al-Badri.

artist.name: Arovane
artist.profile: Experimental IDM/ambient artist born in Hameln, Germany in 1965.

artist.name: Total Eclipse
artist.profile: Psy-Trance project formed in 1992 by 3 French producers. They became known for their first two goa trance albums from 1995 and 1996. In 1999 the group unexpectedly released a drum n bass / breaks album. Serge Souque left the group in 2000 and continued his career as [a11430], an alias that was used for some Total Eclipse tracks in the '90s. Around the same time Total Eclipse became primarily a full on psy-trance project. Loïc Van Poucke left the project in 2004 leaving Stephane Holweck as the only remaining member for a while, until Loïc rejoined for a time. Currently the group consists of Stéphane Holweck & Marios Makar (aka Plasmatix).

artist.name: Manitoba
artist.profile:
A native of Dundas, Ontario, Dan Snaith has been signed to the Leaf label since 2000. Despite using a vast variety of sounds, his work - particularly 2003's Up In Flames - has attracted the media-friendly tag 'folktronica', alongside that of his friend and occasional collaborator Kieran Hebden (Four Tet).
In 2004, a lawsuit forced him to change his pseudonym 'Manitoba'. He has since used the pseudonym 'Caribou'.

artist.name: B. Fleischmann
artist.profile:
Born 1975 in Vienna, Austria.
Austrian producer who has successfully established himself as a crucial element to the European electronic experimental scene with his two 1999 releases, [r=3599] CD on the [l=Charhizma] label (Austria) and the [r=88249] on [l=Morr Music] (Germany) and Charhizma. He has also worked remixing the US rap group [a=Subtle]. Some of his songs are overtly political statements, like those on [r=201992], which are statements about Austrian politics and modern democracy.

artist.name: Rob Ellis
artist.profile:
[b]British musician - producer - composer - arranger[/b]
For photographer / creative director use [a2235686]
Born in 1962. Perhaps his most widely acknowledged work is that with [a=PJ Harvey], with whom he has worked since 1991. He's also highly regarded in the world of contemporary classical music having released records, "Music For The Home" Vols. 1 and 2, on the [l=Leaf] label. His own band [a=Spleen (4)], recorded two well-regarded 'soundtrack' albums for the [l=Swarffinger Records], and he also recorded as [a=Christmas (5)] with [a=Tim Farthing] and [a=Roo Farthing]. He has worked with [a=Laika], [a=Placebo], [a=Pooka] and [a=Swell].
In recent years, between extensive world tours with PJ Harvey, Rob has been involved with a wide range of creatively rewarding projects.

310 are two old friends from New York: Joseph Dierker (currently resident in Seattle) and Tim Donovan (engineer at some of NYC's top studios; has worked with A Tribe Called Quest, Wu Tang, etc). The duo work by relaying tapes of new work across the continent, each building on the other's previous work. Andrew Sigler collaborated (as Fire/fly) on several 310 tracks. Just before the Recessional album he joined officially.

artist.name: Rothko
artist.profile:
Based in London, Rothko were founded by Mark Beazley in the spring of 1997, playing their first show at [l369677] in August the same year. The original lineup consisted of Mark Beazley, Crawford Blair, and Jon Meade.
In 2000 Mark signed a publishing deal with [l114113]. The original lineup disbanded in 2001, and Mark carried on the Rothko name. Late in 2001, Mark signed a deal with Too Pure Records. In 2001, he started his own label, [l62852].
In 2004, the lineup consisted of Mark Beazley, with Michael Donnellly on bass guitar, Ben Page on keyboards, and Tom Page on drums and percussion. The group toured extensively in France. In October of 2010, they amicably parted ways to concentrate on new musical endeavours.
Since August 2015, the band consists of Mark Beazley and Michael Donnelly.

artist.name: Op:l Bastards
artist.profile: Finnish electronic project based in Helsinki formed by Timo Kaukolampi, Vilunki 3000 (real name: Mikko Viljakainen) and Tuomo Puranen.

artist.name: Bass Communion
artist.profile:
Bass Communion is a project of [a227943]. The records released under this name are in an ambient, drone, and/or electronic vein. They come about as experiments in texture made from processing instrumental performances and field recordings - with the exception of the first three albums, which include some electronic sources.
Bass Communion has collaborated with many leading experimental musicians, including [a10355] of [a70828], saxophonist [a236892], [a484], and [a605817] (on the ongoing [a294502] project).

artist.name: Ashley Beedle
artist.profile: British DJ and producer, born in Hemel Hempstead in 1962 and raised in Harrow, northwest London. A DJ since the late 1980s, a producer since 1990 and owner of numerous labels including [l=Black Sunshine Records] (1993-), [l=On Delancey Street] (1993-), [l=United Sound Audio] (1994), [l=Afro Art] (1996-), [l=Soundboy Entertainment] (1996-), [l=Heavy Disco (2)] (2006-), [l=Out Hear Audio] (2006-) and [l=Modern Artifacts] (2010-). Married to [a=Jo Wallace (2)] who runs the [l=Ramrock] and [l=F*CLR Music] labels.

artist.name: Sofa Surfers
artist.profile: Austrian band that plays a mixture of rock and electronic music floating between trip-hop, dub and acid jazz chillout music. They have also composed film scores.

artist.name: Monolake
artist.profile: Electronic music act based in Berlin, founded in 1995 by [a=Robert Henke] and [a=Gerhard Behles]. Monolake is now perpetuated by Henke alone while Behles focuses on running music software company Ableton, which they founded in 1999 together with Bernd Roggendorf.

artist.name: Eardrum
artist.profile: Eardrum is percussionists Lou Ciccotelli and Richard Olatunde Baker from London UK.

artist.name: Roel Meelkop
artist.profile: Roel Meelkop (b. 1963) studied visual arts and art theory at the Willem de Kooning Academy in Rotterdam, The Netherlands during the early 80's. During a post-graduate course at the same academy he decided to dedicate his work to sound and music. His musical activities date back to the early eighties when he started THU20, together with Jac van Bussel, Peter Duimelinks, Jos Smolders and Guido Doesborg. THU20 have released several tapes and CD’s and performed regularly in Europe. This period was crucial in forming his ideas and concepts about sound and how to organise it, but it was not until the mid nineties that he was able to fully realise these ideas. The purchase of a sampler and later a computer radically changed his possibilities of working with sound, offering infinitely more control and freedom. Since then he has worked steadily on a body of work, most of which was received enthusiastically in the small but dedicated world of sound art. His other activities include working with Kapotte Muziek and GOEM, together with Frans de Waard and Peter Duimelinks, and organising sound events, mostly in Rotterdam. Aside from releases, Meelkop also creates site-specific sound installations and performance pieces in collaboration with other artists. In the last couple of years, Meelkop has focused on solo works in composition and installation work. A new project with Frans de Waard called Zèbra has also taken up some well spent time.

artist.name: Artificial Memory Trace
artist.profile: Artificial Memory Trace [a.k.a. Slavek Kwi) is a cultural and musical refugee. Intensity and the power of his will is present in all that he does, be it the organic sound realm he creates, or the graphics and handwriting he invented. His musical universe is a precisely structured and perfectly balanced mix of diverse "musique concrete" materials. Frederick Rzewski, his musical mentor, has assisted him over the past 5 years and has been a major influence, as has been [a=John Cage].

artist.name: Bernhard Günter
artist.profile:
Born 1957, in Neuwied, Germany, Bernhard Günter began playing drums at 12, switching mostly to the electric guitar at 17. In 1980, he moved to Paris to study contemporary compositional techniques at IRCAM and College de France and work in the libraries of IRCAM and Centre Pompidou. While he lived in Paris, he would work with the Ensemble Choreographique de Vitry several times, providing music for performances. In 1986 he returned to germany where, in 1987, he began to work on his first compositions for computer music, releasing his first cd, "un peu de neige salie," in 1992, after 4 years of preparation and one year of work, releasing a second cd, "détails agrandis"shortly after. In 1995 he founded the label [l10118] to release his own work and that of similar artists.
Since the mid 90s, he has played concerts all over the world. His music is regularly played (in absentia of the composer) at the Futura Festival de musique acousmatique in Crest, France, and "the ant moves / the black and yellow carcass / a little closer" has received one of twelve Honorable Mentions at the 1999 Prix Ars Electronica.
Bernhard remains active as a performer and recording artist, largely switching from composing to improvising. After briefly working with the trio [b]+minus[/b] and the duo [b]Klangstaub[/b], he now performs with the duo [b]brachklang[/b] and as a solo improviser, mainly on soprano and alto saxophone, alto clarinet and electric guitar, all combined with live electronics.
An updated biography and a full discography can be found on his website.

artist.name: Richard Chartier
artist.profile:
Richard Chartier (b.1971) is a Los Angeles based artist / composer considered one of the key figures in minimalist sound art. Chartier’s works explore the inter-relationships between the spatial nature of sound, silence, focus, perception, and the act of listening itself.
Chartier’s critically acclaimed sound works have been published since 1998 on a variety of labels internationally including Room40 (Australia), Editions Mego (Austria), Important Records (US), Ash International / Touch (UK), mAtter (Japan), Raster-Noton (Germany), Spekk (Japan), Trente Oiseaux (Germany), NVO (Austria), Farmacia901 (Italy), 12k (US), and his own imprint LINE (US).
He has collaborated with composer William Basinski, sound artists ELEH, France Jobin, Robert Curgenven, Taylor Deupree, AGF, CoH, and Yann Novak, and German electronic music pioneer Asmus Tietchens. In installation form, he has created works with multimedia artists Evelina Domnitch and Dmitry Gelfand, visual artist Linn Meyers, and projected light artist Anthony McCall. Under his guise Pinkcourtesyphone, an ambient project restarted in 2012, he has collaborated with Cosey Fanni Tutti, Kid Congo Powers, harpist Gwyneth Wentink, AGF, and thereminist Evelina Domnitch.
Chartier’s sound works/installations have been presented in museums and galleries internationally including the Whitney Biennial and the influential sound art exhibit Sounding Spaces at NTT/ICC in Tokyo. His performances have occurred live across Europe, Asia, Australia, and North America.
Since 2000, Chartier has curated his influential recording label [l=LINE], publishing over 120 editions documenting the compositional and installation work of international sound and video artists who explore the aesthetics of contemporary and digital minimalism.

artist.name: Jason Lescalleet
artist.profile: Composer and sound artist based in Berwick, ME. Runs the label [l=Glistening Examples].

artist.name: Howard Stelzer
artist.profile: Howard Stelzer (b. 1974, New York) is a composer of electro-acoustic music. Beginning in 1991, almost all of his sounds are generated by, processed by, recorded onto and played back out of cassettes and consumer-grade tape players. Stelzer ran the [l=Intransitive Recordings] label from 1997 until 2012.

artist.name: Pimmon
artist.profile: pimmon (official capitalization) is an experimental/glitch/EAI/electronic project from Sydney, Australia.

artist.name: Brekekekexkoaxkoax
artist.profile: Josh Ronsen has been producing juxtapositions of sound, light and movement since 1995 under the guise of Brekekekexkoaxkoax. Focusing on no one style or approach, Breke...'s performances have ranged from presenting Fluxus works to instrumental free-improvisation, to DJ-ing, to avant-classical composition, to action performances. He lives and works in Austin, Texas.

artist.name: Michael Northam
artist.profile: Michael Northam's (a.k.a. mnortham) artistic trajectory has been a gradual process extending from early actionistic performances to current sound actions, field recordings and compositions. Northam explores the avenue of direct electroacoustic improvisation, solo and with a variety of collaborators. His many 'sound actions' have taken place at venues throughout Europe and North America. Since his first CD was released in 1993, he has consistently produced works that give an opportunity for the listeners to challenge and examine themselves-exploring ideas of place on "Stomach of the Sky", qualities of weather on "Coyot", and perceptions of microscopic worlds on "From Within the Solar Cave". Similar such ideas have formed the basis of his installations such as "Entering Molecular Streams' (Observatori festival, Valencia, Spain 2000) and "Circulating Phases" (Klangturm, St. Pölten, Austria 2001).

artist.name: Taylor Deupree
artist.profile: Sound artist, graphic designer, and photographer residing in Brooklyn, New York. Born 1971.

artist.name: Oren Ambarchi
artist.profile: Australian experimental electronic guitarist and percussionist. Born in Sydney in 1969, performing live since 1986. Owner of [l=Black Truffle].

artist.name: Kid606
artist.profile: Kid606, aka Miguel Depedro, born July 26th, 1979 in Caracas, Venezuela and raised in California, dropped out of school to make music. Inspired by the best of electronica, punk, jungle, hiphop, bass, dancehall and techno, Kid606 fuses it all together with a love of pop culture. Depedro has critically acclaimed releases on Ipecac, Mille Plateaux, Fatcat, 555, and his own Tigerbeat6 label, as well as remixes for artists.

artist.name: Adrian Lee
artist.profile:
English keyboardist, synthesizer player (born 9 September 1957, London, England). In 1980 he replaced [a=Pete Bush] in [a=Toyah]'s band.
Ex-husband of [a=Lorna Wright]

artist.name: Lieutenant Caramel
artist.profile: Lieutenant Caramel was born May 24, 1961 somewhere in France. Philippe Blanchard composes his music in his personal Studio Acteon, now Studio Forum in Annecy, in the french alps. He's also the founding member the Noise of Snow Festival in Annecy. He defines himself as a hunter of sounds, and collects the resonant matters from his different journeys (Syria, Iran, Roma, Venice, Samarkand or Madrid). He has won awards from several international competition: GMEB in Bourges, France, the Luigi Russolo prize in Italy, Radio France, La Muse in Circuit (Mauricio Kagel prize), CIMES-FICS in Prague. He received the Radio France International award "Hunters of sounds" in 2000 for "Captain Cook".

artist.name: Ecclesiastical Scaffolding
artist.profile: Email: blair@mindlesseye.com

artist.name: Dale Anderson
artist.profile: DJ And Producer. Born and raised in Vancouver, Canada.

artist.name: Rod Stasick
artist.profile: A composer and sound artist who works with various media and techniques, such as field recordings, EMF sounds, graphic signs, symbols, and random numbers. His interest is in creating event-systems for different situations and exploring the connections between sound, image, text, and information.

artist.name: Matthew Thomas
artist.profile: Sound artist from Adelaide, Australia Commonly credited as 'Matt Thomas' on releases.

artist.name: Kuljit Bhamra
artist.profile: British composer, record producer and percussionist, born in 1959. One of the UK’s most influential British Asian musicians, a pioneer of Bhangra in Britain. His MBE was awarded in 2009 with the citation “For services to Bhangra and British Asian Music”. He received an honorary Doctorate by the University of Exeter in 2010.

artist.name: James Hardway
artist.profile: When he played live he was joined by [a=Simon Smith (2)] on drums. See also [a989597].

artist.name: TJ Rehmi
artist.profile: TJ Rehmi has been a professional musician, composer and producer for many years now. As well as his own music Rehmi has been a keen remixer and some of his remix work includes remixing Natacha Atlas, Alibi, Tony de Bart and DJ Cheb I Sabbah. After leaving Nation Records he built his own studio, signed a publishing deal with Real World Works and released his US debut 'Invisible Rain'.

artist.name: Makyo
artist.profile: Electronica project formed in 1993

artist.name: Decal
artist.profile: Electronic music project from Dublin, Ireland. Started as a duo by Dennis McNulty and Alan O'Boyle. Since January 2003 only Alan O'Boyle is recording under the name of Decal.

artist.name: Ansuman Biswas
artist.profile: London based artist and musician, born in Calcutta, brother of [a168233] ([a385]).

artist.name: Monophone
artist.profile: dan@chikbudo.com

artist.name: Joi
artist.profile: British music group founded in the 1980's by brothers [a771292] and [a892916]. After the death of [a892916] in 1999, [a771292] continued the group on his own.

artist.name: Rick Wilson
artist.profile: Multi-instrumentalist (but mainly known as a drummer) from London, UK.

namevariations.name: Kumo (Featuring Drums Off Chaos And Ludwig Van)

artist.name: Zoviet France
artist.profile:
Music group from Newcastle upon Tyne in north east England. Their music, often experimental, at times dissonant and made of industrial textures, falls into the atmospheric category.
Members: Lisa Hale (1980-1981) Robin Storey (1980-1992) Peter Jensen (1980-1984) Ben Ponton (1980-present) Paolo di Paolo (1984-1986) Mark Spybey (1987-1989) Andy Eardley (1990-1995) Mark Warren (1995-present)

artist.name: Jimpster
artist.profile:
British DJ, producer, artist and label owner.
Co-founder, owner and manager of labels [l=Freerange Records] and [l=Delusions Of Grandeur].
Son of [a=Roger Odell] ([a=Shakatak]'s drummer) with other family being singers [a=Emma Odell] and [a=Larraine Odell].

artist.name: Rhys Chatham
artist.profile: Born: September 19, 1952, Manhattan, New York Composer & guitarist.

groups.name: Love Of Life Orchestra
Rhys Chatham & His Guitar Trio All-Stars
The Flying Hearts

artist.name: DJ Cheb I Sabbah
artist.profile: Cheb i Sabbah (August 7, 1947 – November 6, 2013) was a DJ and composer/producer. With a DJ career spanning three decades, it would be difficult to find a more fascinating DJ than Cheb i Sabbah. Born in Algeria, a great source of his inspiration were his two uncles who were masters of the Malouf style, also known as Andalusian music. Cheb I described himself as a 'hajji', a musical pilgrim of the spirit. His name is another reference to his pilgrimage: Cheb is a nickname used by Algerian rai singers; Sabbah means 'morning', but the link is actually to Hassan i Sabbah, the Persian mystic whose legendary library is rumoured to be the largest in the world. Cheb i Sabbah passed away on November 6, 2013 in his home in San Francisco due to a stomach cancer.

groups.name: DJ Cheb I Sabbah Wa Mektoub

artist.name: Adi Lukovac
artist.profile:
Bosnian electro-pop musician, born September 06, 1970 in Sarajevo, Bosnia-Herzegovina. He died in a car accident on June 18, 2006 near Blažuj, Bosnia-H.
He was consider as one of the pioneer of modern electronic music movement in Bosnia. He was the most prominent artist on Electro-Ambient-Etno sound. Since 2001 founder and owner of label [l=Post War Sound].

artist.name: Radar
artist.profile: Electronic music group

artist.name: Oojami
artist.profile: Oojami is the brainchild of Necmi Cavli, originally a native of Bodrum - a popular Turkish holiday resort. He moved to London in the nineties and started DJ'ing his own music in a small basement club in the Turkish quarter of North London. At the time nobody, including himself, predicted the exciting journey that he has was about to undertake. Over the last 6 years Oojami has produced 3 albums (internationally released on Miles Copland's label), played hundreds of gigs in over 15 countries, including a tour of the US/Canada, and featured on more than 30 compilations around the world. Recently he's begun writing soundtracks for major Hollywood movies, and his music has been licensed to EMI Arabia. Oojami's music is groove orientated, and band is described as 'global band with Turkish influences'.

artist.name: Koan
artist.profile: Electronic artist from UK.

artist.name: James Asher
artist.profile: Composer of world music and relaxation music.

artist.name: Josh Abrahams
artist.profile: Melbourne-based producer and bassist.

artist.name: Faultline
artist.profile: Breakbeat DJ from the United Kingdom.

artist.name: Power-Pill
artist.profile: Born: August 18, 1971, Limerick, Ireland

artist.name: Micky Finn
artist.profile:
British DJ and producer.
Born: 3 December 1966 in South East London, raised in Woolwich, and currently lives in Kent.
Best known as Micky Finn (though often equally spelt "Mickey"), Drum 'n' Bass DJ and producer Michael Hearn is one of the original rave scene Hardcore and Jungle pioneers.
He got the DJ nickname from his older sister (who used to manage the Tunnel club in Greenwich) because she thought he was so skinny. His first big break was given to him by [a=Andrew Weatherall] after meeting him at a party in a cafe in Chapel Street Market.
Along with running his own labels [l=Finn People Productions] and its sister label [l=Finn S], he also co-founded label [l=Urban Takeover] together with Gavin King. Also co-owns the Urban Takeover DJ agency.
He co-promotes Sunbeatz with Darren Jay and used to co-promote A.W.O.L. & Innovation. Also used to own the Bitin' Back record shop in Gravesend.
Father of [a=Logan D].

artist.name: Third Eye Foundation
artist.profile: [b]DO NOT USE.[/b]

artist.name: Solvent
artist.profile: Electronic music producer born in Zimbabwe and now based in Toronto, Canada. He is co-founder of [l=Suction Records].

artist.name: Frederik Schikowski
artist.profile: Electronic musician from Berlin/Germany.

artist.name: The Remote Viewer
artist.profile: This UK outfit comprises [a=Craig Tattersall] and [a=Andrew Johnson (3)] (both ex-[a=Hood] and [a=Famous Boyfriend]). They make diligently processed melancholic pop which combines the aesthetics of early 90s miserablism and the technical astuteness of contemporary electronica.

artist.name: Lexaunculpt
artist.profile: Lexaunculpt was part of the American revolution in IDM in the late 90s and early 00s, along with others like Phoenecia & Richard Devine. Before the age of 20, he released two EPs and one LP and then disappeared, making appearances every so often on compilations and remixing other people’s work. He later formed The Internal Tulips with Brad Laner of Medicine.

artist.name: Phonem
artist.profile: Musician from southwest England. Currently resides in Torquay, Devon. UK.

artist.name: Dynamo
artist.profile: Torsten Pröfrock runs the german label [url=http://www.discogs.com/label/DIN]DIN[/url], member of the Hardwax-clan, famous record store in Berlin.

artist.name: ISAN
artist.profile: ISAN (Integrated Services Analogue Network) was formed in 1996.

artist.name: Geiom
artist.profile: Runs the [l=Berkane Sol] label.

artist.name: Pole
artist.profile: Dubtronic/glitch artist, producer and mastering engineer based in Berlin. He is the founder of [l=~scape] (now defunct) and [l=Pole] labels and [l=Scape Mastering] studio.

artist.name: Goodiepal
artist.profile: Vester was born and raised in a small town in the Faro Islands. He is a horologist, art collector, author, and musician prolific in computer music circuits. His music is difficult to categorize.

artist.name: Roger van Lunteren
artist.profile: Dutch born german bred european and producer of various electronic music styles, based in CGN, AMS & ZRH

artist.name: The Notwist
artist.profile: German indie rock band from Weilheim in Upper Bavaria, formed in 1989.

artist.name: Christian Kleine
artist.profile: German producer. Also programs Max for Live plugins for Ableton under the name Max4Cats.

artist.name: Herrmann & Kleine
artist.profile: German duo, split in 2005.

artist.name: E*Vax
artist.profile: Solo project from American musician and electronic/hip-hop producer Evan Mast.

artist.name: Lali Puna
artist.profile: German electronic-pop band based in Munich. Valerie Trebeljahr started Lali Puna after her first band – the all-girl-band L.B.Page – dissolved. The line-up was soon completed by Markus Acher, Christoph Brandner and Florian Zimmer. After some changes, the band was reduced to Trebeljahr (vocals, keyboard, computer), Heiss (keyboard, computer) and Brandner (drums).

artist.name: Flowchart
artist.profile: Long-standing project centered around Philadelphia DJ and producer Sean O'Neal.

artist.name: Schneider TM
artist.profile: Schneider TM is a multidimensional music project of Dirk Dresselhaus, named after his nickname Schneider, which oscillates extensively between adventurous electronic pop-music and experimental, sometimes improvised freeform music, while occasionally bringing these and other opposing elements together.

artist.name: Ms. John Soda
artist.profile: Indietronica pop band founded in 1999 in Weilheim, Germany.

artist.name: Accelera Deck
artist.profile: Accelera Deck is Chris Jeely from Birmingham, Alabama, USA. Chris played and recorded in numerous rock bands, influenced heavily by the textures of My Bloody Valentine and the early 90s ‘shoegaze’ scene. It was the release of Aphex Twin’s ‘Selected Ambient Works Volume II’ and Seefeel’s ‘Quique’ that inspired Chris to begin experimenting with guitar and sampler. He began recording as Accelera Deck sometime after October 1995, when a fire claimed all of his music equipment. With a little insurance money, a four track and guitar were purchased. From then on Chris’ music touched every colour of the musical spectrum: from beat-oriented electronics to acoustic guitarstrumming. Throughout his time recording Chris has dabbled in releasing music under a variety of names like September Plateau, Exhalera Deck, Audio Wishe and Your Favorite Horse. Many limited edition singles and ep’s have been recorded for a variety of esteemed labels such as Morr Music, Neo Ouija, Blackbean & Placenta and 555 Recordings.

artist.name: Snd
artist.profile: Electronic music duo from Sheffield.

artist.name: Brian Eno
artist.realname: Brian Peter George St John le Baptiste de la Salle Eno
artist.profile:
Brian Eno is an electronic musician and producer. Also known for his multifaceted interests outside of music, especially technological. He was born 15 May 1948 in Woodbridge, Suffolk, UK.
He attended Ipswich and Winchester Arts Schools where he studied with [a=Tom Phillips (3)], Roy Ascott, [a=Christian Wolff], Anthony Benjamin, [a=Noel Forster] and [a=George Brecht].
Starting his musical career early, first thing of note being with the band [a=Roxy Music], Eno has gone on to produce a number of highly eclectic and increasingly ambient electronic and acoustic albums, and also works frequently as producer for many popular artists.
He is widely cited as coining the term "ambient music", with his series of albums using the term recorded between 1978 and 1982.
He collaborated with [a=David Byrne], formerly of [a=Talking Heads], on "[url=http://www.discogs.com/Brian-Eno-David-Byrne-My-Life-In-The-Bush-Of-Ghosts/master/6392]My Life In The Bush Of Ghosts[/url]" (1981) which was one of the first albums not in the rap or hip-hop genres to extensively feature sampling. Eno collaborated with [a=David Bowie] as a writer and musician on Bowie's influential "Berlin Trilogy" of albums, "[url=http://www.discogs.com/David-Bowie-Low/master/22382]Low[/url]" (1977), "[url=http://www.discogs.com/David-Bowie-Heroes/master/22294]Heroes[/url]" (1977), and "[url=http://www.discogs.com/David-Bowie-Lodger/master/22318]Lodger[/url]" (1979), on Bowie's later album, "[url=http://www.discogs.com/David-Bowie-1-Outside-The-Nathan-Adler-Diaries-A-Hyper-Cycle/master/22350]1. Outside - The Nathan Adler Diaries: A Hyper Cycle[/url]" (1995), and on the track "[url=http://www.discogs.com/David-Bowie-Im-Afraid-Of-Americans/master/53966]I'm Afraid Of Americans[/url]" (1997). He has also collaborated with [a=Robert Fripp] of [a=King Crimson], [a=Robert Wyatt] on his "[url=http://www.discogs.com/Robert-Wyatt-Shleep/master/23627]Shleep[/url]" (1997) album, with [a=Jon Hassell] and with the German duo [a=Cluster].
Eno is also well known for acting as a producer for a number of bands, these include [a=U2], [a=James], [a=Coldplay], et al. He won the Best Producer award at the 1994 and 1996 BRIT awards. He is an innovator across many fields of music and collaborated on the development of the Koan algorithmic music generator. He has also been involved in the field of visual arts.
In 1996 Brian Eno, and others, started the Long Now Foundation to educate the public into thinking about the very long term future of society. Brian Eno is also a columnist for the British newspaper, The Observer.
Inducted into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame in 2019 as a member of Roxy Music.

artist.name: Herbert
artist.profile:
Vocalist on the 2005 Spanish house release [r846390].
Please note this artist page is not related to [a=Matthew Herbert]'s output. Herbert's discography and credits should be listed under [a=Matthew Herbert] using the artist name variation feature when needed.

artist.name: Aqua Bassino
artist.profile: Producer from Edinburgh, Scotland.

artist.name: Modaji
artist.profile:
British electronic DJ, producer, and remixer.
Born: 17 December 1972. Died: 19 April 2019 in Gerrards Cross, Buckinghamshire, England, UK (aged 46).
Modaji was the main moniker of Dominic Jacobson, a major player on the west London broken beat scene and beyond, producing several seminal albums, 12"s, and remixes.
While studying at Exeter University in the mid 1990s, he was also DJ-ing and working in influential record shop (and label) [l=Mighty Force] Records in Exeter (sometimes seen behind the counter there with [a=Tom Middleton]), which ceased trading in 1995 when the label moved to London.
Later, he was a member of [a=Phil Asher]'s [a=Restless Soul] crew, producing broken beat tracks, later moving into more deep jazz-inflected house, using both the Modaji name and another alias, [a=Harvey Lindo].

artist.name: A Reminiscent Drive
artist.profile: Project of ambient music created by Jay Alanski.

artist.name: Bent Halos
artist.profile: Birthed by a partnership between San Francisco artists Andrew Johnson (Astral Matrix) & DJ Galen

artist.name: Kings Of Tomorrow
artist.profile: Kings Of Tomorrow was formerly a production team comprising Sandy Rivera and [a=Jay "Sinister" Sealée] who also collaborated as [a=D'Menace], [a=Mysterious People] and [a=Sanjay]. Currently run by Sandy Rivera only.

artist.name: Miracle
artist.profile: Drum 'n' Bass and Dubstep producer from London, UK. He ran [l=Miraculous Recordings] and its sister label [l=Miraculous Recordings Digital] before introducing Dan Miracle Music in 2012.

artist.name: Julien Jabre
artist.profile: French composer, sound engineer and producer based in Paris.

artist.name: Hi-Lo
artist.profile: They run the Central Records label, originally in Vienna, Austria, now in Berlin.

artist.name: Sueño Latino
artist.profile: Italian House project.

artist.name: Derrick May
artist.profile: Producer from Belleville, Michigan, United States, (born April 6, 1963). Known for his work in techno, Mr. May has worked with musical artists such as [a=ABC], [a=Depeche Mode], [a=Fine Young Cannibals] and [a=System 7]. Founder of Transmat, one of the most important techno labels created in Detroit before 1990, alongside Metroplex and KMS.

artist.name: Digital
artist.profile: Ipswich-based drum and bass artist.

artist.name: Special Forces
artist.profile: Born : 1971 United Kingdom, moved from St. Albans to Ipswich with his family.

artist.name: Voodoo Child
artist.profile:
Born : September 11, 1965 // New York, NY, United States
Voodoo Child is one of [a=Moby]'s many aliases, and the only one he has kept using since the early 1990s. The name Voodoo Child has mostly been used as an outlet for underground techno and hard house releases, with the exception of one ambient album (titled "The End Of Everything") in 1996.

artist.name: Cujo
artist.realname: Amon Adonai Santos de Araújo Tobin
artist.profile:
Cujo was the first project of Amon Tobin, before he started going by his own name.
Do not confuse this alias with the drum and bass artist [a=Cujo (2)].

artist.name: Lamb
artist.profile: Electronic music duo formed in Manchester in 1995. Their musical style is a distinctive mix of trip-hop, jungle, dub, and jazz. The duo split from 2004 to 2009. In 2024, Lou Rhodes made an official statement on Instagram that Lamb will not be releasing anything in the future.

artist.name: A Guy Called Gerald
artist.profile: Musician and record producer from Manchester, UK.

artist.name: Mike & Rich
artist.profile: [a=Richard D. James] ([a=Aphex Twin]) and [a=Mike Paradinas] (leader of the [l=Planet Mu] label who is better known as [a=µ-ziq]) joined forces in 1996 to form a project called Mike & Rich. The duo released one album titled "Expert Knob Twiddlers".

artist.name: Scorn
artist.profile:
Electronic project from Birmingham, England, UK. Formed in 1991 as a duo project of former [a=Napalm Death] members Mick Harris and Nik Bullen. In their early years Scorn was often associated with industrial and experimental music. After the departure of Nik Bullen in April 1995, Scorn became a solo project of Mick Harris, and the music mostly offers minimalist beats with deep bass lines, often resembling dub and trip-hop in structure.
Project was stopped in May 1997, but started again in March 2000. Then it was stopped for the second time in November 2011, but officially started again in January 2019. In June 2022 Mick Harris teased a new upcoming Scorn album with guitars by Justin Broadrick. But in August 2023 Mick Harris posted an update that "Scorn is dead" again since late 2022 and "it was a bad mistake bringing it back after I stopped it in 2012". So the last Scorn performance was on October 1 2022 at the venue "Golden Cabinet" in Shipley, UK. In his Instagram post from December 19 2023 Mick confirmed that "Scorn is dead long dead".
Final lineup: [a=Mick Harris] - electronics (1991-1997, 2000-2011, 2019-2022), drums (1991-1994)
Former members: [a=Nicholas Bullen] - vocals, bass, electronics (1991-1995)
Session/live musicians: [a=Justin K Broadrick] - guitars (1991-1992, 2022) (on "[m=13003]" album (1992), unreleased 2022 album) [a=Pat McCahan] - guitars (1992) (live) [a=Paul Neville] - guitars (1992) (on "Peel Session 1992") [a=James Plotkin] - guitars, guitar synth (1994) (on "[m=13043]" (1994) album, "[m=2158957]" archive live EP (2021, recorded 1994), "Peel Session 1994" and live) [a=Umar Bin Hassan] - rap (1997) (on unreleased 1997 EP) [a=Nick Garnett] - didgeridoo (1994) (on "[m=13043]" (1994) album (track "Exodus")) [a=David Knight] - guitars (2000) (on track "Stinger" from "[r=341714]" (2000) compilation) [a=Ian Treacy] - drums (2009-2011) (on "[m=270539]" album (2010) (almost all tracks) and "[m=314032]" (2011) EP (track "Piper")) [a=Jason Williamson (3)] - rap (2019) (on "Cafe Mor" (2019) album (track "Talk Whiff")) [a=Kool Keith] - rap (2021) (on "[m=2095303]" (2021) single and "[m=2180884]" (2021) album (track "Distortion")) [a=Kurt Gluck] aka [a=Submerged] - bass (2021) (on "[m=2095303]" (2021) single and "[m=2180884]" (2021) album (track "Distortion")) [a=Shane Embury] - bass (2022) (on unreleased 2022 album)

artist.name: Meat Beat Manifesto
artist.profile:
This industrial, electronic, experimental music act was started by John Stephen Corrigan (aka [a=Jack Dangers]) and [a=Jonny Stephens], in 1987 in Swindon, United Kingdom. Jack Dangers has led the group and been the only permanent member, collaborating with other members along the way.
Meat Beat Manifesto's earlier work is widely credited with influencing trip-hop, drum & bass, and big beat. Their tracks, Radio Babylon in particular, have been widely sampled, including by artists such as [a=The Prodigy], [a=Goldie], [a=Aphex Twin], and [a=The Chemical Brothers].
Meat Beat Manifesto worked with [a=Consolidated] and [a68336] to pioneer the early '90s "industrial hip-hop" sound.

artist.name: Coil
artist.profile: Formed in London in 1983 by [a=John Balance] as a solo side project to Psychic TV, Coil developed into a full-scale musical group in 1984, when Balance cemented a partnership with Peter 'Sleazy' Christopherson. Christopherson had been a founder of Psychic TV and member of Throbbing Gristle. John Balance (1962–2004) died tragically in an accident at his home on November 13th, 2004. Peter Christopherson decided that, with the passing of John Balance, Coil would not continue. Peter Martin Christopherson died 25 November 2010 (aged 55).

artist.name: Bill Laswell
artist.profile:
American bassist, producer and record label owner. Also owner of the [l=Orange Music (5)] studio, in West Orange, New Jersey, since 1997.
Born February 12, 1955 in Salem, Illinois and raised in Albion, Michigan. He was married to Ethiopian singer [a=Ejigayehu Shibabaw] alias [a=Gigi].
Laswell ranks among the most prolific of musicians, being involved in hundreds of recordings with many musicians from all over the world. Laswell's music draws upon many different genres, most notably funk, various world music, jazz, dub and ambient styles. He has also played or produced music from the noisier, more aggressive end of the rock spectrum, like hardcore punk and metal.
