artist.name: Astral Engineering
artist.profile: Ambient / ambient dub project by [a=Simon Rees] from Brighton, active off and on since 1993.

artist.name: Lisa Shaw
artist.profile: R&B and house music singer-songwriter from Toronto. Formerly married to [a=David Winsett] aka [a=DJ Swingsett].

artist.name: Vincenzo
artist.profile: [b]Christian Vincenzo Kruse aka Vincenzo Ragone[/b] Brother of [a=Florian Kruse] and [a=Benjamin Kruse]. Being influenced by Hip-Hop and Black Music in his teenager days, later by the vibe of early Acid-House. He started to DJ in Hamburg in the early 90's and had his first regular gigs at the Club Front. Soon he got into the production of his own tracks, started working with artists like Duffer Swift, Rasoul, Elmar Schubert and more.

artist.name: Peter Benisch
artist.profile:
Classically trained composer, hailing from Stockholm, Sweden. Started early developing an interest for synthesizers and computers, and has built some of the synthesizers used in his musical productions. He is also running the company Frost Network, which builds and sells music-optimised computers.
Started Globe Studios in Stockholm along with old school mate Joel Mull and friends Adam Beyer and Jesper Dahlbäck, where all of them have their own studio.
Releases ambient music under his own name, electro under the name FPU, and is also involved in a lot of collaborations with artists in the heavier techno genre - always aiming at doing something different with every new album.

artist.name: Bjørn Torske
artist.profile: Norwegian producer and DJ originally from Tromsdalen, Norway. Torske's debut release was the "T.O.S. EP" compilation where he appeared as Alegria and with Ole Johan Mjøs as Radikal Buzz. Torske and Mjøs then formed Ismistik and released 3 EPs on Djax, as well as collaborating with Rune Lindbæk in Open Skies and Volcano. Torske then moved to Bergen and continued Ismistik as a solo project without Mjøs, recording the classic "Remain" album which was released in 1995. Ismistik seems to have been abandoned since then, Torske preferring to record under his own name, occasionally using the Krisp moniker for his more techno-ish ventures.

artist.name: DJ Deep
artist.profile: French DJ and producer based in Paris. Runs [l=Deeply Rooted (3)] formerly [l=Deeply Rooted House].

aliases.name: Yogi Haughton & The Horns Of East Harlem
Yogi Haughton

artist.name: Callisto
artist.profile: Prolific House Music producer hailing from Boston, MA. He died January 28th, 2013.

artist.name: Abacus
artist.profile: Electronic music producer / DJ from Toronto, Canada.

artist.name: Storm Bryant
artist.profile: House vocalist from New York City. AKA Storm Orion.

artist.name: DJ D
artist.profile:
He started his music career as an hardcore Dj producer when he was only 16 years old. He made (now too!) his music only with an simple PC, not anymore! The story of Hardcore Blasters label is deeply attached to Dj D's, since the label was born after he gave (1999) a tape demo to Simone (chairman!) and he like it. Of course the first demo was not released on vinyl (however insert on Jump vol.1 compilation), but with this one , DJ D career could have to start! During 2000 Dj D try to improve the sound quality of his products, so in November 2000 , with his friend Luca Morlini (Dj Lux) performed his first record DJ D vs DJ LUX "Inside myself E.P." and Hardcore Blasters label started to moved! Through the years he improved his sound quality level, performing some remixes for some djs! Hits like "Original Style" DJ D remix was been included into several hardcore compilations and played by some famous Djs like Promo,Endymion,Nosferatu. During the 2002, he started to develop his own unique style of production which resulted in his first 10" record called "X-ploration" which was released in June 2002. "Mankind warning" was the hit-track played by several djs like Endymion & Nosferatu in some parties! It was included in several important hardcore compilation like MOH, Always Hardcore,Hardcore Ressuretion and many other... So after this project, Dj D performed a remix of "Getting High" for Ferox released on Bionic records, for The Blaster, B 747 & Angels of Darkness. Together with Valentino Oliviero (The Blaster), he became PSYCHORUDES and he is 50% of Angels of Darkness & Noize Creator too! Later he created two albums; DB Area and The Melody Man. His greatest hits came in 2007 and 2008 with "Gloria" and "Imagine (When I Found You)". Several other hits followed, as well as collaborations with new producers of Hardcore Blasters like Nitrogenetics and Rayden.
In 2013 Diego Buffoni announced his retirement from the hardcore scene and from Hardcore Blasters. He stated that he no longer has the time to keep quality of his production high. At that time he had a 16 months old daughter, choosing family above music.

artist.name: Austin Bascom
artist.profile: Electronic music producer / DJ from Toronto, Canada.

artist.name: Nigel Hayes
artist.profile: Producer from London. Deep house, Nu Jazz, Afro Beat, Chill Out. Founder of label [l=Twilight].

artist.name: Ron Trent
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Chicago. Co-owned [l=Prescription] with [a=Chez Damier]. Runs [l=MusicandPower Group] and its associated labels.

artist.name: Anthony Nicholson
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Chicago. Anthony Nicholson has been producing music since the early 1990s, first coming to fame when he met [a693] and started releasing records under various pseudonyms such as [a1713] and [a1215] on the hugely sought after and influential [l292] and [l291] labels. Anthony's solo career really began to take off with his "Dance Anthology" releases on [l253] in 1998-99. Since then he has gone on to record under his own name for [l88] and [l1914], as well recording under the [a5333] moniker for Clairaudience. Father of [a=Quincy Nicholson].

artist.name: Glenn Underground
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Chicago. Founder member of [a=Strictly Jaz Unit]. Nephew of David Collins aka [a=Dae-Dae].

artist.name: Boo Williams
artist.profile: Born July 20, 1967 in Chicago, Willie Griffin has been a part of the city's house music entourage since 1981. This was when he first stepped up to the turntables and started DJing. Later on in the 1990's, Willie began production and then later remixing in '93. He started out parting on the city's south side soon introduced to Glenn Underground who got him started in music production.

artist.name: Chris Brann
artist.profile:
Growing up in Atlanta, Chris discovered house and electronic music by the somewhat long-winded way of getting sent tapes from Europe. He began assembling a home studio but it wasn't until 1994 that he began his music career in earnest and formed Wamdue Kids with DJs Deep C, aka Chris Clarke, and Udoh, aka Chris Udoh.
In 1995 they released the classic 'Higher' on the Acacia label, which led to the seminal debut album 'These Branching Moments'. In 1996 Chris' prolific attitude to work came into its own solo album 'Deep Fall,' reflecting a different direction to that of his productions with Wamdue.

artist.name: Mark Grant
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Chicago. Runs [l=Blackstone Recordings].

artist.name: Terry Hunter
artist.profile: DJ, producer and record label executive from Chicago, Illinois. Founded [l=Vibe Music] (1993-1995) with [a=Georgie Porgie] and [a=Maurice Joshua]. Established [l=Vinyl Soul] (1998-2004), [l=T's Box] (2004) and [l=Mirror Ball Recordings] (2019). Related production company: [l=T's Box Productions].

artist.name: Alton Miller
artist.profile: Growing up in the 1970s, Miller soaked up the musical environment surrounding him in the Motor City, taking a particular interest in the sounds of Motown, Philadelphia, Parliament-Funkadelic, and Santana. It was during the early '80s once the "dance music crazed" Alton became friends with a young [a=Derrick May] that he decided to start spinning records, citing Chicago DJs such as [a=Ron Hardy] and [a=Frankie Knuckles] as prime influences. By the latter part of the same decade, Miller joined forces with George Baker and [a=Chez Damier] to start the Music Institute, a short-lived but legendary Detroit club that has since become near-mythical, thanks to the pioneering techno efforts of figures such as May. Following the demise of The Music Institute, Miller took an interest in Conga drumming in addition to DJing, which led to a period between 1989 and 1991 where he toured the world with his music. He then joined forces once again with May, first as an employee of the artist's [l=Transmat] Records label (he also played bongos at [a=Silent Phase]'s early shows), then as Aphrodisiac, the title under which he would begin releasing his music. Besides his EP on Transmat's sublabel [l=Fragile], he also released his music on Kevin Saunderson's [l=KMS] and a series of EPs on [l=Serious Grooves]. By the mid to late '90s, he had increased his presence in the Detroit area through a number of DJ performances and a stream of stunning twelves. His debut album for Distance has been highly acclaimed as all things house. His current [l=Peacefrog] album 'Stories Of Bohemia' is a powerful, moving collection of mature jazz tinged, soulful house grooves with flavors of African and roots music. Alton Miller is picking up from where he left off in 2015 and rolling in 2016 on mission to make this year one of his most prolific ever. 2015 saw him re-introduce his label InnerMuse Recordings with his own signature sound on Whereabouts Known. During Movement 2016 in Detroit, Alton Miller played with Chez Damier for the first time since the Music Institute closed in 1989, during a party organized by fellow DJ [a=Kai Alce]. Alton Miller also inaugurated the new Adeen Record label with a first EP, called “More Positive Things”. He released another EP “The Vault” on Italian DJ Volcov’ record label, [l=Neroli] early October 2016.

artist.name: Solaris Heights
artist.profile: Original lineup: Bryan Robson, Mark Husskisson & Anton Fielding.

artist.name: Seafoam
artist.profile: Brother of Doug Cavender ([a=ILK]), Matt Cavender ([a=The Missing Link]) and Brad Cavender ([a=Space Is The Place]).

artist.name: Mutabaruka
artist.profile: Jamaican Dub Poet. Born 26th December, 1952 in Rae Town, Kingston, Jamaica. Mutabaruka means "He who is always victorious" in Rwandan.

artist.name: Dominic Dawson
artist.profile: DJ, producer and musician born in 1968. He started DJing in his home town of Brighton from the mid 1980's Moved to London in 1987 and started to produce and DJ house music. He has had national top 40 success with his Hydraulic Dogs moniker with the track 'Shake It For Me'. He has DJ'd at clubs from Tokyo to NY, from South Africa to most major cities in Europe for 25+ years. He has released music on labels including Defected (Glitterbox, 4 To The Floor), Strictly Rhythm, King Street, Sony (Directions), Other & Guidance, and currently runs his own two labels Disco Sonitus and Momotaro Music. He has been an in demand remixer since the early 1990's for the likes of A Man Called Adam, The Chemical Brothers, Jacob Collier, Chaz Jankel (of Ian Dury & The Blockheads) Arthur Baker, First Choice, Al Green, Odessey, Imagination, Will Dawson, Kenny Hawkes, Mutabaruka, & C. J. Glover to name a few.

artist.name: Joe Claussell
artist.profile: New York-based DJ, producer and musician, born in Brooklyn in 1971. Runs [l=Spiritual Life Music], [l=Sacred Rhythm Music] and its sublabel [l=Circuit T Promo]. One third of the [l=Body & Soul NYC] team with [a=François Kevorkian] and [a=Danny Krivit]. Brother of percussionist [a=José "Cochise" Claussell].

artist.name: Nuspirit Helsinki
artist.profile:
Nuspirit Helsinki is a multi-talented Finnish collective of DJs, producers, and musicians that has ascended to the forefront of the burgeoning European eclectic electronic music scene on the strength of their productions for [l=Guidance Recordings], [l=Jazzanova Compost Records (JCR)], and [l=Ubiquity Records].
The group was conceived in 1998 by founding members Tuomas Kallio and Hannu Nieminen (aka DJ Ender) who enlisted a core group of Helsinki's finest musicians to develop a dynamic 21st century big band sound informed by a wealth of jazz, funk, soul, afro and latin influences. Equally inspired by cutting edge hip-hop, nu soul, broken beat, two step, house, and drum'n'bass, Nuspirit's musical mission is to fuse live organic instrumentation with electronic textures to produce soulful and sophisticated music that can be enjoyed on or off the dance floor.
Nuspirit Helsinki are [a=Tuomas Kallio], [a=Hannu Nieminen] (aka [a=DJ Ender]), [a=Kim Rantala] (aka [a=Kasio]), [a=Toni Rantanen] (aka DJ [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/Toni+Rantanen?anv=Lil+Tony]Lil' Tony[/url]), and [a=Eppu Helle] (aka DJ Eppu).

artist.name: Metamatics
artist.profile: British electronic music producer currently based in Devon. Born 1970.

artist.name: Merrick Brown
artist.profile: Runs Austin, Texas house label [l=Chalant Music].

artist.name: Silverlining
artist.profile: DJ and producer from UK.

artist.name: Neo
artist.profile: UK jungle / drum n bass artist. Started as Keen, released tracks originally on Formation Records under the name Keen before changing to Neo and signing to Flex Records

artist.name: Female
artist.profile: Producer from UK. He is one of the founders of [l=Downwards].

artist.name: Richard Salter
artist.profile: Richard Salter is a London based record producer/ composer whose career has spanned over 25 years, both in the UK & New York City. With a deep love & knowledge of music and technology he has been working professionally in the music industry since 1993. He started his career in London as a tape operator then quickly progressed to producer/ engineer. He has worked in house and freelance at studios and live venues in London & New York City including the legendary Bass Hit NYC & Mark Angelo London. He had his first commercial release in 1993, his first record deal in 1994, and over the years has worked with some of the biggest names in the industry including: Aretha Franklin, James Brown, Prince and Chaka Khan. He has worked on thousands of pieces of music, including tracks & remixes for the likes of: EMI, BMG, Motown, Warner and Universal, to name but a few.

artist.name: Hawke
artist.profile: South African DJ and producer. Owner and founder of [l3434]. He is also a published author. Based in San Francisco, California. Hardkiss helped pioneer the US electronic dance scene in the '90s.

artist.name: Morgan Page
artist.profile: American DJ & Music Producer. Born May 31, 1981. Based in Los Angeles, United States.

artist.name: Kage
artist.profile: https://controlteam.bandcamp.com/music

artist.name: Wyndell Long
artist.profile: A true Chicago techno and house artist with some of the best labels in the world like Peace Frog, ProJEx, Primate, UKR, EUR, Mona, Djax, Isophlux, Reel Musiq, Subspace, Majesty, Elypsia, R&S and many more. His tracks have been played by some of the biggest names in the business as well, and appear regularly in DJ sets all over the world.

artist.name: Circulation
artist.profile:
UK based tech house artist and label by same name. Originally a duo but as of 2022 "Paul Davis returns as Circulation with his multi-volume 'Recirculate' series" as declared on bandcamp.
Do not confuse with the US based Dj / producer [a44042]

artist.profile:
Duo from The Netherlands.
info@acidjunkies.com

artist.name: Jori Hulkkonen
artist.profile: Finnish DJ and producer of house music, born 28 September 1973, Kemi, Finland.

artist.profile: For the German Happy Hardcore/Dance act please use [a=Dune (3)]

artist.name: Aurora Borealis
artist.profile: Aurora Borealis was the trance alias of Shazz.

artist.name: Scan X
artist.profile:
Stéphane Dri aka Scan X is one of the original pioneers of the French Techno scene. His career began in 1993 when he released his first two EPs on the now legendary FNAC MUSIC imprint.
Also has recorded soundtrack material for the video game Ghost In The Shell (1997) for PlayStation and the films Papa (1997) And Step By Step (2001).

artist.name: Iberian
artist.profile: One of Alex Martin's aliases. Not the same as Ricardo D. Collado Jiménez.

artist.name: Laurent Garnier
artist.profile:
French DJ and producer, born February 1, 1966.
He started mixing House music in the late of 1980's in Manchester scene. Co-founder of the label [l=f communications], alongside [a=Eric Morand]. Also co-founder of the label [l=cod3 qr] with [a=Scan X] and [a=Oliver Way]
Previously worked as a waiter at the French Embassy in London until 1986.

artist.name: LFO
artist.profile:
LFO (Low Frequency Oscillation) was formed in 1988 in Leeds, West Yorkshire, United Kingdom and was active until 2014.
Gez & Mark originally met whilst part of rival breakdancing crews in the Merrion shopping centre in Leeds in 1984. They then met again whilst at college studying photography and graphic design in 1988 and formed LFO.
Members: [a167] (1988-2014), [a6981] (1988-96) & [a45786] (1988-90)
LFO (Low Frequency Oscillation), named after the Low Frequency Oscillator component of synthesizers, were pioneers of the "Bleep Techno" sound in the UK at the end of the Eighties and are best known for the early self-titled techno anthem "LFO".
Their last release as a duo was the album "Advance" in 1996. In 2003 Mark Bell resumed use of the name as a solo pseudonym.
Mark Bell died in October 2014 from complications after an operation.

artist.name: Lady B
artist.profile: French DJ and producer.

artist.name: Lenny Dee
artist.profile: Hardcore DJ from New York, United States.

artist.name: Taho
artist.profile: French DJ and producer from Aix en Provence, France.

artist.name: Nightmares On Wax
artist.profile: The longest serving artist on the Warp Records roster. Originally founded in 1988 by George "DJ E.A.S.E." Evelyn and Kevin "Boywonder" Harper. Harper left before the release of Smokers Delight. When playing live they were also joined by MC Toz 180 and guitarist Chris Dawkins.

artist.name: Kid Loops
artist.profile: Combining his love for science fiction, old-school hip-hop and the lush side of Detroit techno and jazz fusion, Jamie Lexton's productions as Kid Loops skirt the edge where raw breakbeat chemistry becomes bland coffee-table fare (a place once inhabited by several fusion legends as well). A native of North London, Lexton listened to Public Enemy, Stetsasonic and Mantronix as a child, and began working in the studio with help from his brother Charlie, who co-owns the Filter label and records as Cool Breeze. His studio prowess and wide-eyed youth gained him the name Kid Loops, and he recorded his first single "Alien Resident" for Filter in 1995. Lexton again worked with his brother on Kid Loops Vs. Cool Breeze, an LP released as Special Projects in late 1996. Singles followed for All Good Vinyl and Filter once again, before Lexton's proper debut album, Time Quake, was released in mid-1997. - John Bush

artist.name: Plaid
artist.profile: London duo [a=Ed Handley] and [a=Andy Turner] started recording as Plaid while still members of [a=Black Dog Productions]. The first Plaid album, [r=22422], was released in 1991. In 1995, Ed and Andy ended their five year collaboration with [a=Ken Downie] and left The Black Dog. Plaid released the [r=5002] EP that same year, and the pair began rechanneling their efforts full-time.

artist.name: Incognito

London group. The line up of Incognito is constantly changing. Here's a list of artists who have collaborated with the group:

Gangadeen, Gavin Harrison, Matthew Stuart, Richard Bailey, Ski Oakenfull, Ian Thomas [b]Percussion[/b]: Chris Joris, Daniel Sadownick, Karl Vandenbossche, Louis Jardin, Maxton 'Gig' Beesley Jr., artist.name: Vanessa Daou
artist.profile: American singer, songwriter, poet, visual artist and dancer, born 4 October 1967 in St. Thomas, U.S. Virgin Islands. Former wife of [a=Peter Daou].

artist.name: Solar Quest
artist.profile: Ambient/acid/trance artist currently based in London, UK.

artist.name: Minus 8
artist.profile:
Downtempo/Electronica DJ from Zürich, Switzerland.
Brother of Swiss "techno pope" [a=Arnold Meyer].

artist.name: The Gentle People
artist.profile: Formed in London in 1994, with members from the UK, France, and the USA.

artist.name: Tim Koch
artist.profile: Electronic producer from Adelaide, Australia.

artist.name: Alaska
artist.profile: Techno act consisting of DJ/producers L. Garnier and N. Tritton

artist.name: DJ Cam
artist.profile: French DJ/producer, born on the 29th of March 1973 in Paris. He has been one of the most influential artists in the "French Touch" era, thanks to his participation in [a=La Yellow 357] together with [a=Dimitri From Paris] and his masterpiece [m=57350], released in 1995 on his label [l=Street Jazz Productions], later renamed [l=Inflamable]. With deep jazz and hip-hop roots, he released 5 studio albums until [r=98210] in 2002. Since then, he produced several compilations but also [a=Tassel & Naturel]'s [r=212733] and the first album of In Love. In 2007 he produced an album under the name of [a=Bouncer Crew]. Labels: [l3225], [l1729728]

artist.name: Hyphen
artist.profile: 90s Downtempo, Ambient act.

artist.name: Norma Jean Bell
artist.profile: Saxophone player, producer and singer in the house and dance music scene. In the early 90s she started the Detroit based Pandamonium record label. She was a part of Frank Zappa's band from November 1, 1975, until December of the same year. She has also performed with Tommy Bolin, Dave Mason, Raydio, Narada Michael Walden and Milt Jackson.

artist.name: Square
artist.profile: Band founded in Dijon France in 1993 originally with 3 members : [A=Olivier Saint Denis] , [A=Olivier Kaiser] and Olivier Regis. The name "Square" comes from the initials of each last name : SKR.

artist.name: Dune (4)
artist.profile: The duo, known individually as Tarrantella (Bourne) and Redanka (Holt) also operate the label RIP Records.

artist.name: Wax Doctor
artist.profile: British drum n' bass producer, best known for his releases on Metalheadz and R&S Records. One of the early pioneers of the more melodic style of dnb alongside artists like Hidden Agenda, Alex Reece, and LTJ Bukem.

artist.name: Aux 88
artist.profile: Electro/Techno band from Detroit, Michigan, USA. Aux 88 is composed of Tommy (Tom Tom) Hamilton and BJ Smith (Posatronix). The group was originally formed as 'RX-7' in 1985, including [a=Anthony Horton]. The group also formed another project, [a=Sight Beyond Sight], before forming Aux 88 in 1993. In 1995, Tucker went solo and Hamilton continued as Aux 88 together with former dancer William '[a=BJ (11)]' Smith (aka [a=Posatronix]). After Smith left in 1998, Aux 88 became Hamilton's solo project. Tucker later rejoined the band. Keith Tucker left in 2020. Contact: info@aux88.com

artist.name: Ready Made
artist.profile: French multi-instrumentist electronic music producer, composer, arranger, songwriter and illustrator based in Paris. He has composed and produced soundtracks & scores for movies like "LoL", "LoL USA" among many others.

artist.name: Lil' Louis
artist.profile:
DJ and producer from Chicago, Illinois.
One of the most popular Chicago house producers during the late 1980s thanks to his massive club hit "French Kiss", Lil' Louis was also the only Chicago producer to successfully deal with the major labels; he released two albums for Epic, and only left the label at his own instigation. Born in Chicago, Louis was the son of guitarist Bobby Sims, who recorded for Chess and appeared with the psychedelic-soul unit [a=Rotary Connection]. He grew up with nine siblings and played both drums and bass as a child, then began DJing in the mid-1970s (he earned his nickname after appearances at the club River's Edge while still in middle school). By the end of the decade he had his own club, the Future, where he began working on his editing techniques, thanks to a cassette deck and later a reel-to-reel recorder.
By the 1980s, Lil' Louis was hosting the biggest house parties in Chicago, and he began recording his productions around that time as well. His first single "How I Feel" appeared on his own label, and he began collaborating with [a=Marshall Jefferson] on several tracks, including "Seven Ways To Jack" by [a=Hercules], [a=Byron Stingily]'s "I Can't Stay Away". In 1989, his new single "French Kiss", released on [l=Diamond Records], a label he founded with [a=Byron Stingily], became a local hit, then a platinum-selling international classic after being licensed to [l=CBS] and [l=FFRR]. The success triggered a major label contract through [l=Epic], and the release of his debut album "From The Mind Of Lil' Louis" (1989).
Charting a course across jazz-fusion and R&B as well as house, the album was one of the best produced by any of the Chicago figures, and included session contributions from [a=Larry Heard], [a=Die Warzau], and his own father on drums. From the album, the moody single "I Called U" became another club hit. His follow-up album, the more stylistically unified "Journey With The Lonely", didn't fare as well and Lil' Louis retired from recording for over four years, preferring instead to set up his own studio in New York and work on production with [a=Babyface] and [a=Me'Shell Ndegeocello]. He returned by collaborating with "Little" [a=Louie Vega] of [a=Masters at Work] and also worked on production for [a=Black Magic].
Brother of [a=Curtis Harman].

aliases.name: Lil Louis And The Diamond Corp.
Lil' Louis & The World
Lil' Louis & The Party
Marvin Burns

artist.name: Mr. Oizo
artist.profile:
French electronic artist.
Best known pseudonym of musician and movie director [a=Quentin Dupieux], Mr. Oizo became famous after his instrumental track "[m=64910]" (1999) was used in two different commercials for the [l=Levi's] jeans brand, which also featured the puppet Flat Eric, created by Dupieux.
The term "Oizo" is a homophonic intentional misspelling of the word "oiseau" (pronounced wa-zo), meaning "bird" in the French language.

artist.name: Alexkid
artist.profile:
French DJ and producer, b. 15/04/1975.
Not to be confused with British artist [a=Alex Kidd].

artist.name: ManMachineMan
artist.profile: Jost Gerischer, Levent Faki

artist.name: De-Phazz
artist.profile: Downtempo jazz ensemble from Germany integrating modern turntablism and elements of soul, Latin, trip hop and drum and bass into a lounge music sound. Years active: 1997 - present

artist.name: Barbara Lahr
artist.profile: Guitar/ Bass, vocals, Germany

artist.name: The Mulder
artist.profile:
[a=Jon Silva] alias The Mulder
Len Faki has left the group.

artist.name: Jon Silva
artist.profile: German Multi-style artist with a focus on House music. Jon Silva performs as singer, producer (also for other performers) and remixer. He runs the labels [url=http://www.discogs.com/label/Pesto+Music]Pesto Music[/url] and [url=http://www.discogs.com/label/Traxx+Recordings]Traxx Recordings[/url].

artist.name: Terry Lee Brown Jr.
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Darmstadt, Germany. Born Oct 7, 1963. Active since 1989.

artist.name: Laidback Luke
artist.profile:
Lucas Cornelis van Scheppingen (born 22 October 1976 in Manila, Philippines), better known under his stage name Laidback Luke, is a Filipino-born Dutch DJ and music producer. He notably remixed the Robin S. song, "Show Me Love" which charted at No. 11 on the UK charts in 2009. He is also the founder of Mixmash Records.
He was born to a Dutch father, Kees van Scheppingen and Filipina mother, Lucy Baruelo in Manila and grew up in Hoofddorp, Netherlands together with his younger brother, Asley.
Before becoming a DJ was a graffiti artist when he was a teenager & a black belt in kung fu.

artist.name: Lexicon
artist.profile: Jost Gerischer, Levent Faki

artist.name: Mark Ambrose
artist.profile: DJ and producer from UK. He is the founder of [l=Crayon].

artist.name: Appleheadz
artist.profile: Electronic music producer duo.

artist.name: U.S.O.S.
artist.profile: Abbreviation for Underground Sound Of Swansea

artist.name: Chi-Saw Gang
artist.profile: Individual aliases: [a=Randall Jones] AKA [a=Randall Sims] [a=Chris Clarke (2)] AKA [a=Deep C] [a=B. Bathey] AKA [a=Hito]

artist.name: Digital Boogie
artist.profile: House project.

artist.name: Terry Francis
artist.profile: English DJ and producer born in Epsom on 28/7/1966. His career began by co-promoting an acid house night at Hamilton's wine bar in Leatherhead in 1988 whilst working as a roofer. In 1990 he became a resident at Sterns in Worthing. He also used to work in Swag Records. Also co-owned the [l=Eye 4 Sound] & [l=Wiggle] labels. Promoter and resident DJ of Groove Kitchen & Wiggle in the mid-1990s. Also resident DJ for Freaky Disco. Also hosted shows on Energy 87.9 FM.

artist.name: Those Norwegians
artist.profile: Norwegian (!) house group originally comprising Rune Lindbæk, Ole Johan Mjøs and Torbjørn Brundtland. They released some singles and the acclaimed album "Kaminzky Park" on Paper in this incarnation. The producers in the original line-up then went their separate ways, and Rune Lindbæk continued with the project with Gaute Drevdal. The project now seems to be inactive.

artist.name: Sweet Exorcist
artist.profile: Seminal "Bleep Techno" collaboration between Richard H Kirk and DJ Parrot, taking the name from the title of the [r=554601] LP by Curtis Mayfield. Their [i]Clonks Coming[/i] became the first album release on Warp.

artist.name: The Dice Man
artist.profile: Born: August 18, 1971, Limerick, Ireland

artist.name: Speedy J
artist.profile: Electronic music producer from Rotterdam, The Netherlands (born 10 August 1969 in Rotterdam). After two albums of electronic listening music on the label Warp, he released two albums with a more experimental sounds in 1997 and 2000. The album Loudboxer (2002) saw a return to a more minimal style of techno. He collaborated with Mike Paradinas on the project Slag Boom Van Loon and also released two ambient albums for the FAX +49-69/450464 label under his real name. In 2008, Speedy J founded his own record label, Electric Deluxe, releasing his own music as well as records by Terence Fixmer, Gary Beck, Tommy Four Seven and others.

artist.name: Jaco
artist.profile: Based in Huddersfield, UK. Collaboration between Gilpin & Shamshad (a.k.a. [a=Solitaire Gee]) and local duo [a=Hotline]. According to the CD artwork on 'Show Some Love', Jaco is pronounced "Ya-Koh".

artist.name: THK
artist.profile: Thomas Kukula, born 8 February 1959 in Düsseldorf, Germany, better known under the name DJ Red 5, or "General Base", is one of those who successfully produced electronic music in the 1990s. He began in 1986 to work in the smaller clubs of Düsseldorf environment as a DJ. From 1988 on he worked for over 11 years in the Club "Tarm Center". Soon there was the opportunity to work as a producer.

artist.name: Solitaire Gee
artist.profile: Duo from the Fourth Wave record shop in Huddersfield. They also recorded on Warp as [a=Black Mojo] (as a pair), as [a=Lex Loofah] and [a=Resoraz] (individually) and as [a=Jaco] (with Hotline). They also owned the Fourth Wave record shop in Huddersfield, as well as previously working together at the Big Tree record shop.

artist.name: DiY
artist.profile: Do It Yourself was a Nottingham-based veteran free party collective formed in 1989. The DJs comprised Digs and Woosh, Harry, Jack, Simon DK, Pezz and Emma, as well as engineers and sound people. DIY also ran their own record label, [l=Strictly 4 Groovers], and released at least 30 12" records and 2 albums and also had releases on Warp. A history of the DiY sound system, written by founding member [a=Mark Harrison (8)] aka Harry Harrison ([a=Harry (33)]), was published by Velocity Press in 2022.

artist.name: Coco Steel & Lovebomb
artist.profile:
British electronic group.
Members: Chris Mellor (Coco), Lene Stokes (Steel), and Craig Woodrow (Lovebomb).
Though Coco, Steel & Lovebomb grew out of the techno community, the group blends elements of garage, soul, and disco with only an ambient-techno blueprint.
After Coco (aka Chris Mellor) began DJ-ing at the Zap club in Brighton, his acid-house sets grew popular in the wake of the late-1980s house explosion. With partner Steel (aka Lene Stokes) and Lovebomb (aka Craig Woodrow), he formed Coco, Steel & Lovebomb.
Originally growing out of the Zap club, their first headline single was "[m=80282]" (1991), later releasing follow-ups, "[r=326558]" (1991), "[url=http://www.discogs.com/Coco-Steel-And-Lovebomb-You-Cant-Stop-The-Groove-Parts-1-4/master/80283]Hold It[/url]" (1992), "[url=http://www.discogs.com/CSL-Work-It-Run-Free/master/80293]Work It[/url]" (1993). Taking three years from their initial single to record debut album "[m=80284]" (1994).
Two ambient outings followed with albums "[m=80287]" (1997), followed by "[r=87274]" (1998), as well as a remix album "[r=178575]" (1999) – all released on [l=Other Records].

artist.name: Eternal
artist.profile:
Australian progressive house/techno artist.
[b]For the RnB/Soul group, please submit entries to [a=Eternal (2)][/b]

namevariations.name: e t e r n a l

artist.name: RAC
artist.profile: RAC was Sheffield duo Richard Benson and Chris Duckenfield (although the final RAC release [i]Structures[/i] was credited to Duckenfield only); the name RAC is simply an acronym of "Richard And Chris". They put out several releases on Warp and sublabel Nucleus between 1992 and 1996. Regular engineer for RAC (and several other Warp releases) was [a=Richard Brown] who went on to form [a=Swag] with Duckenfield.

artist.name: F.U.S.E.
artist.profile: F.U.S.E. stands either for "Futuristic Underground Subsonic Experiments", "Futuristic Underground Sound Experiments" or "Further Underground Sound Experiments".

artist.name: Richie Hawtin
artist.profile:
Canadian (British born) producer, label manager and DJ, born June 4, 1970; brother of [a185883].
Although mainly influenced by Detroit techno, Hawtin has developed his own style ranging from harder noisy techno to deep minimal abstract acid, particularly under his best known alias Plastikman. He was involved in the development of the Final Scratch digital DJ technology and has continued to use new technology as it becomes available.
Founded the [l=Plus 8 Records], [l=M_nus] and [l=Probe Records] techno labels.
Before becoming a DJ worked in a video game shop & at McDonald's.

groups.name: Frederic Galliano And The African Divas
Frédéric Galliano Electronic Sextet
Frederic Galliano Kuduro Sound System

artist.name: Move D
Ludovic Navarre
artist.profile: French House/Nu Jazz artist and producer. Best known under the alias of [a74], under which he has developed a distinctive melancholy sound.

artist.name: Tikiman
artist.profile: Due to legal problems Tikiman had to drop this alias in 2003 and use his real name instead.

artist.name: Andy Caine
artist.profile:
UK session vocalist and musician, songwriter and producer.
After a brief stint as a solo recording artist in the mid 80s, Caine became involved as a songwriter and producer with acts associated with [l370482] and [l616], often in collaboration with [a=Youth]. He later worked with many of the Boy Bands of the 90s and remains an in demand session vocalist. As a featured vocalist he is perhaps mostly known for [m686863] and [r577], two seminal tracks of the mid 90s German deep house scene. His songwriting credits are indexed under Andy or Andrew Robert Caine and Andrew Robert Cunningham in mechanical rights databases such as BMI and GEMA.

artist.name: Choice
artist.profile: One-release collaboration between [a750] & [a162], with the help of [a74] as sound engineer.

artist.name: Orange
artist.profile: One-shot alias used by Shazz for a single release and several other remixes.

artist.name: Suburban Knight
artist.profile:
A crucial figure on the outskirts of Detroit techno since the mid '80s, James Pennington's recordings as the Suburban Knight pioneered the moodier side of the Motor City with classics like "The Art of Stalking" and "The Groove." And though he collaborated on the biggest hit to come out of Detroit, the techno-pop monster "Big Fun" by Inner City, he later recorded with subterranean mainstays [a=Underground Resistance]. It was through his connection with another Detroit mainstay, [a=Derrick May], that Pennington began recording. May had set up his own [l=Transmat] label in 1986, and Suburban Knight debuted the following year with the third release for the label, "The Groove." He had also been working with [a=Kevin Saunderson] (who was also his room-mate for 3 years), and co-wrote "Big Fun" with Saunderson and [a=Arthur Forest]. After vocals were added by [a=Paris Grey] and the record was released, it became a worldwide hit and virtually defined early Detroit techno for many foreigners. Though Pennington only released one more single for Transmat, it was the dark classic "The Art of Stalking." After the commercial success of 'Big Fun', he signed a deal with [l=Paragon] but released very little as they wanted a more commercial sound. This deal prevented him from released anything for around 3 years.
With the rise of Detroit's second wave in the early '90s, Pennington became a mentor for Mike Banks and the Underground Resistance crew. With UR, he released the singles "Nocturbulous Behavior" and "Dark Energy." He also featured on the [l=Submerge (3)] label compilation Depth Charge, Vol. 3, and produced tracks for Underground Resistance's 1998 full-length Interstellar Fugitive. James' Debut album 'My Sol Dark Direction' was released to critical acclaim including both classics and new, unreleased productions.

artist.name: Robert Armani
artist.profile: American DJ and producer born 6 August 1970 in Chicago. His DJ career began at the age of 14; followed by remixing from the age of 18. He has released material on several labels—including [l314], [l351906], [l937], [l2720] and [l739].

artist.name: Hardfloor
artist.profile: German acid/techno and tech house duo - formed 1991. Well known for distinctive acid sound credited to their virtuoso skill at manipulating Roland TB-303 bass synthesizers. They reputedly use up to six of these machines at once, some of which are highly modified.

artist.name: Coma Club
artist.profile: Dutch DJ and producer.

artist.name: Mike Dearborn
artist.profile:
Mike James Dearborn was born October 7th, 1972 in Chicago Illinois, USA.
Owns [l=Majesty Recordings] and used to promote the United Nations club night in Chicago. Published via [l=Mike Dearborn Music]

artist.name: Random XS
artist.profile:
Dutch acid/house/techno outfit from Utrecht, The Netherlands, composed by DJ Zero One and Frank de Groodt. Releasing & performing live since 1992.
Nowadays, Zero One still performs live together with Frank de Groodt, who is now an established electro/techno producer himself.

artist.name: Paul Johnson
artist.profile: American house DJ and producer Born: 11th January 1971 Chicago, IL - died: 4th August 2021 Chicago, IL. He released a vast amount of music for some of the most consistently underground dance labels before hooking up with [l=Peacefrog Records]. 12" releases on the likes of [l=Cajual Records] / [l=Relief Records] and [l=Dance Mania] led to his debut album release "Bump Talkin'" in 1996. This and the follow up "Feel The Music" became two of the most talked about releases of their time. He lost the use of his legs as a result of a shooting accident in 1987, and required a wheelchair for mobility. Co-founded [l=Dust Traxx, Inc.] in 1997 and ran his own label [l=Nocturnal Interludes].

artist.name: Storm
artist.profile: Project by [a=Markus Löffel] & [a=Rolf Ellmer], credited as [a=Trancy Spacer & Spacy Trancer].

artist.name: Mike Dunn
artist.profile: DJ, producer and vocalist from Chicago. Ran [l=Dance Mutha Records] between 1988 and 1990 (relaunched in 2024). Ran [l=Warehouse Records (2)] and its sub-label [l=Muzique Records] with [a=Armando Gallop] between 1988 and 1990. Ran [l=Deep Soul] with [a=Byron Stingily] and [a=Mike Weiss] between 1998 and 2000. See [l=The Dunngeon] for Dunn's studio. See [l=Blackball Muzik] for his current production company and publisher. Since August 2025, he has also been using the company Entile as his label, starting with release [r34934699].

artist.name: Steve Poindexter
artist.profile:
DJ & producer born in Chicago in 1965.
His father used to drum for [a=Duke Ellington] and his mother used to sing with [a=Mahalia Jackson]. He began his DJing career at the age of 10, DJing at parties at Mendel High School.

artist.name: Armando
artist.profile: Acid house producer of Afro-Cuban descent, releasing music under the name Armando. He was born on February 12, 1970, in Chicago, Illinois, and died on December 17, 1996, of leukemia. Ran [l=Warehouse Records (2)] and its sub-label [l=Muzique Records] with [a=Mike Dunn] between 1988 and 1990. Some of his most famous tracks are "House Music All Night Long", "Land of Confusion", "100% of Dissin' You", and "World Unknown".

artist.name: Carl Craig
artist.profile:
Born: 22 May 1969 in Detroit, USA.
Carl Craig is a Detroit electronic music pioneer and the founder of Planet E Communications, a platform that has supported a diverse array of international artists for over 30 years, spanning hip-hop, jazz, funk, and esoteric electronic sounds.
Operating under multiple aliases—69, Paperclip People, and C2, among others—Carl has produced six albums and over 600 remixes for artists like Depeche Mode, Tori Amos, Kenny Larkin, and Kevin Saunderson. In 2008, his remix of Junior Boys’ “Like A Child” earned him a Grammy nomination. He also launched the Detroit Love event series and played a pivotal role in the city’s electronic music festival at the millennium’s turn.
Carl created the “All Black Vinyl” Instagram series, which evolved into the “All Black Digital” partnership with Beatport and Qwest TV in 2022, featuring discussions with Black artists and live streams from South Africa. This partnership continued with a Black History Month DJ mix for Apple Music and educational talks at NYU and Michigan State University.
In 2020 Carl created Party/After-Party, an immersive sound and light installation that premiered at DIA:Beacon before moving to MOCA in Los Angeles, where it became part of the museum’s permanent collection. The installation was accompanied by performances featuring Carl alongside artists like Moodymann, DJ Minx, and DJ Holographic. In 2022, Carl performed his Synthesizer Ensemble at Carnegie Hall’s AFROFUTURISM Festival and at the 59th Venice Biennale to honor Stan Douglas’s work in the Canada Pavilion.
In 2024, Carl’s life and career became the subject of Desire: The Carl Craig Story, which premiered at the Tribeca Film Festival. Directed by Jean-Cosme Delaloye, the documentary features interviews with family and notable music figures, chronicling Carl’s journey from Detroit’s middle-class roots to global renown.

artist.name: Edge Of Motion
artist.profile: Duo from Amsterdam (The Netherlands) into techno music since 1990.

artist.name: DJ Skull
artist.profile: [a=Ron Maney] also known as DJ Skull lived in the Southside of Chicago (IL, USA). His musical career started during the summer of 1986, while recovering from a broken bone. During the following years he homed his craft, DJing first at basement parties, building his reputation to go on to larger events such as, loft and hotel parties, and eventually the infamous Music Box and Power Plant clubs. At the age of fifteen Ron shopped his first demo at [l=Trax Records]. There he first met Chicago's legendary DJ [a=Ron Hardy], working as A&R for Trax Records. Two years, and a new alias later (DJ Skull) later while at the Power Plant, Chicago's own [a=Steve Poindexter] heard a DJ playing one of Rons tracks, and met Ron. Steve was now A&R at [l=Saber Records] and invited Ron to release some tracks. So in October of 1991 DJ Skull's first record "N.R.G. Music" was released. In 1993, he released the EP "The Stomping Grounds" on [l=Djax-Up-Beats], which was more noted than the first one. Over the years that followed DJ Skull released lots of EP's on several labels. He has become well-respected producer and DJ in the Techno industry. He was playing at famous events in the USA and Europe such as: Planet Djax 1 (Chicago, IL), Mayday 8 (Germany) and The Lollipop (Sweden) just to name a few.

artist.name: Akilah Bryant
artist.profile:
Akilah Bryant also know as Black Widow has been on the Chicago Party Scene since the late 80’s. Hailing from the Northside of Chicago, Akilah started her journey at Medusa’s, where Alternative music was the popular genre. Soon after hearing DJ Skull spin at an underground party, she attended all of the legendary clubs, such as the Power Plant, the Musik Box, and numerous hotel parties. In 1991, she met DJ Skull, where they became a powerhouse team, with Skull producing, and Akilah singing. After spending countless hours in the studio, Akilah decided it was time for her to come out with her own style of tracks, something that would encompass femininity, techno, and the soulful disco that was played at all of the Chicago clubs. With this in mind her first E.P. Urban Electronics on DJAX, hit the ground running. After this release Akilah started performing live P.A.’s, with the first being at Planet DJAX, the DJAX tour. From there she released another classic E.P. titled Black Widow, which contained the Acid anthem “Eye Trip”. Also released by Akilah, and co-produced by Skull was Arachnophobia on the Swedish label Hybrid. Black Widow went on to perform all over the country, and abroad, unique tracks that embody her style.
Back to the drawing board Akilah decided to go more into computers, and has designed the website for their new record label Hypnotic Tones. With Skull on the tracks and Black Widow on the web, this is sure to be one of the hottest Chicago Techno labels around. Also, they will debut Akilah’s label Urban Electronix, which will allow female artists that have a love for Chicago Techno to showcase their talent. Stay tuned for all that Black Widow and DJ Skull has to offer.

artist.name: Claude Young
artist.profile: Techno producer and DJ from Detroit (USA). He started as a DJ in the early 1990s and in July 2023 he decided to retire from his professional career.

artist.name: Miss Djax
artist.profile: Founder of the Dutch label [l=Djax Records] and associated labels [l=Djax-Up-Beats], [l=Djax-X-Beats] and [l=Djax-Break-Beatz] in 1989.

artist.name: Marko Laine
artist.profile:
Marko Laine is living in Finland and produces uncompromising dark techno music. He got picked up by Djax in 1995 and in 1996 his first record got released.
Marko is a producer that only believes in the underground. He started his own record shop because he was unsatisfied with the local record stores.

artist.name: Zero One
artist.profile: Producer, DJ, Engineer from Utrecht, The Netherlands. Founding member of [a=Random XS].

artist.name: Ismistik
artist.profile: [a=Bjørn Torske]'s early '90s techno project.

artist.name: R.E.C.
artist.profile: [b]R.E.C.[/b] stands for [b]R[/b]hythmic [b]E[/b]nergy [b]C[/b]orporation.

artist.name: The Morning Glory Seeds
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Amsterdam, Netherlands.

artist.name: Trance Induction
artist.profile: Utrecht-based Trance Induction is a project of former philosophy and artificial intelligence student Tjeerd Verbeek [1968], who left university for a career in dance music. Since 1990 Verbeek has been developing his own eclectic style - a blend of acidhouse, ambient, techno, world music, breakbeats and industrial new wave. From 1995 until 1998, Trance Induction did several club tours, combining live- and DJ performances. For these, Tjeerd was joined by DJ Zero One of [a=Random XS] fame. Over the years Verbeek released several 12"es under different artist names, some of them collaborations with others.

aliases.name: Fastgraph
Pieces Of A Pensive State Of Mind
The Operator
Sonar Base
Frank De Groodt

artist.name: Joey Beltram
artist.profile:
Born: 6 November 1971 in Queens, NYC, USA.
Joey Beltram is widely acknowledged as a leading innovator in the early development of underground dance music.
Joey began DJing as a hobby back in 1983 at the age of 12 playing mostly early electro and hip-hop. He made the switch to house music in 1985 when the first wave of Chicago house hit NYC that same year.
In 1989 he started releasing some of his early productions on several small New York record labels whilst he was working as a courier. A year later he caught the eye of [l=R & S Records] in Belgium and by mid 1990 released his first major record with that company titled "Energy Flash". Beltram soon followed with some equally impressive releases. All through the 90's he has created some of the most timeless music on some of the most cutting edge labels of the decade.
In 1999 he formed [l=STX Records], which he currently records for under both JB³ and Joey Beltram.

artist.name: Jaydee
artist.profile:
Robin Albers obtained a degree in commercial studies and was a member of the Dutch national baseball team and triple Dutch wrestling champion (in the category up to 70 kilos).
In the seventies Albers was one of the first mixing DJ's in Holland. He played in clubs all over the country and later went on to Dutch national radio, where for 11 years he had his own show. Five times he organized the annual Dutch Mixing Championship, and in the past years he hosted various music and sports programs on national TV. He performed in Denmark, Italy, Germany and Belgium, spun at Amsterdam's renowned Club, and at many major raves in Holland.
For three consecutive years his radio show For Those Who Like To Groove got elected Best Dutch Dance Show - a unique accomplishment for which in 1992 he received an industry award. Besides his musical activities as Jaydee, Robin also has his own label, First Impression, and his Club in Vianen (near Utrecht).
robinjaydee [at] gmail [dot] com

artist.name: Melt
artist.profile: Melt is one of the performance names of Belgian bassist/keyboard player/producer [a=Marc Requilé].

artist.name: Helium
artist.profile: European trance/ambient group from the early 90s. For the American indie group, please use [a=Helium (3)].

artist.name: The Rapino Brothers
artist.profile: House and pop music production duo from Italy. In 1988/89, they relocated to London, England.

artist.name: Space Cat
artist.profile: Goa trance - psychedelic trance music solo project from Tel Aviv, Israel.

artist.name: Nemesis (12)
artist.profile:
Nemesis was another of producer Toyin's alter egos. Whilst DarKnight handled the more soulful garage cuts, Nemesis produced more hard hitting tracks. Ironically this identity was created after a track released on the b-side of a soul record became a massive cult hit in house clubs worldwide.
"After the storm" was a 'simple' house cut released at a time when house was rapidly moving into techno. As "Nemesis" Toyin released several albums, remaining a cult hero to this day.

artist.name: DJ Bubba
artist.profile: Born: 4 october 1964 Lokandu (Congo) Died: 26 June 2019 Brussels (Belgium) Hard trance producer.

artist.name: Airscape
artist.profile:
Airscape is a trance project, most known as an alias of the two famous DJ's and producers [a=Johan Gielen] and [a=Sven Maes], the latter worked on the project from 1997 to 2005 while Johan Gielen (together with [a=Jos Borremans]) started the project in 1993. Sven Maes left Airscape in 2005 leaving the project in a hiatus. Johan Gielen and [a=Ben Huijbregts] revived the project in 2009 with the single "My Love" featuring US vocalist [a=Jes Brieden]. In 2014, [a=Peetu Sainio] (aka [a=Peetu S]) replaced Ben Huijbregts in the project.
[I]Previous productions partners:[/I] Jos Borremans (1993-1998) Oliver Adams (1995, 2013) Sven Maes (1997-2005) Ben Huijbregts (2009-2014) Jan Vervloet (2013)

artist.name: Sonic Animation
artist.profile: Australian dance, techno musical group; formed in 1994 in Melbourne by [a547755] (DJ), [a547752] (drums, keyboards and programming) and [a547751] (vocals and programming) All tracks on their debut album were written, produced and engineered using a 386 computer. Bertschik left the group in 1998. The band broke up in 2006, reforming in 2011.

artist.name: Project JPO
artist.profile: Alias for [a=Pertti Grönholm]'s solo projects from the late 1980s to the late 1990s.

artist.name: Cavestar
artist.profile: Kevin Crosslin produces and remixes music under the name Cavestar. After releasing his first self-titled project and co-piloting NoCal electro-label, Mindspore Records, he now lends his talents as a producer, art director and mastering engineer to LA-based Abstrakt Reality Records. Crosslin owns and operates Aligned Design, a small multimedia production kitchen in Los Angeles. A complete discography can be found at his website.

artist.name: Cydonia
artist.profile: Goa Trance project from UK

artist.name: Plankton
artist.profile:
Belgian Trance producer active in the 90s.
For French DJ/producer please [a=Plankton (16)].

artist.name: Another Fine Day
artist.profile: London based ambient, downtempo, & dub producer, active since the early 90s.

artist.name: A Positive Life
artist.profile: Ambient / Downtempo / Trance / Dub project based in UK Now is a solo project of Stefan Pierlejewski joined by + Patrick Morsman 1991 - 1994 (2nd founder member) + DJ Liam Farrell 1996

artist.name: Original Rockers
artist.profile: In 1994 they changed their name to [a=Rockers Hi-Fi]

namevariations.name: Original Rockers At The Elephant House
The Original Rockers At The Elephant House

artist.name: Banco De Gaia
artist.profile: Producer from Leamington Spa, UK. Banco De Gaia [World Bank] began as a solo project of Toby Marks producing a variety of dance and chill-out music often featuring tribal samples. After several tape-only albums, Banco was signed to Planet Dog where he received much wider recognition. Banco expanded into a band in 1997 and later set up the Disco Gecko label.

artist.name: Insanity Sect
artist.profile: Insanity Sect were brothers Kenny and Martin Wright from Grimsby, North England.

artist.name: Digital Jesus
artist.profile: Nick Philpin & John Odonnel

artist.name: The Starseeds
artist.profile: THE STARSEEDS - REAL BIO - The Critically Acclaimed AMAZING British DUO THE STARSEEDS are the Canadian Regina Dannhof (composer, lyricist, singer, co producer) & British Alex McGowan (composer, co producer) They Reached CULT STATUS with Their Groundbreaking TRIP HOP CHILL OUT Debut Album "PARALLEL LIFE" *Parallel Life Hit the British Press like a Bomb! *US BILLBOARD MAGAZINE "the hottest Import since Massive Attack" *The ROLLING STONE Magazine "Simply Beyond Your imagination" *NUMBER ONE in the US COLLEGE CHARTS *MUSIC WEEK MAGAZINE "Album of The Week" *MIXMAG "One of those records so beautiful inspired and so original that words fail to do it justice" *Contributions to Film & US TV Smash Hit's such as "6 Feet Under". *Regina Dannhof's songwriting & her gorgeous sexy vocal performance combined with the DUO's UNIQUE PRODUCTION TRADEMARK made this PIONEERING Album a strong contender for the Mercury Music Prize! BACK TO THE NOW: *NEWSFLASH February 2025 Our 2nd album Just Got Reborn by Regina: A BRAND NEW VIBE IN A DIFFERENT TIME renamed: "STRANGE PLANET" out now! OUR STORY: THE STARSEEDS, Founded by Regina & Alex in London, UK when we lived together as a married couple... Creating...Experimenting Day & Night... Developing our OWN UNIQUE STYLE Producing together & recording both of our Albums PARALLEL LIFE & STRANGE PLANET in our Beloved Legendary “Orange Blue" Central London Recording Studio, Fulham Broadway called Universal Feedback. Welcome to the Just Launched OFFICIAL WEBSITE February 2025: Exclusively Download original releases from our 3 albums, very soon we have Vinyls and CD's for sale! thestarseeds.net contact: thestarseeds@yahoo.com

artist.name: Coldcut
artist.profile: Influential UK DJ and production act working broadly in the field of electronic music. They also founded the eclectic label [l=Ninja Tune].

artist.name: Sandoz
artist.profile:
Sandoz was one of the most prominent solo monikers of [a=Richard H. Kirk] since 1992, who produced - after twenty years of listening to Jamaican music - contemporary Electronic Dance music with Jamaican Reggae and Dub influences and other ethnic styles. The first album [m=412314] is a collection of previously released 12" tracks.
Note: Sandoz Laboratories was a Swiss pharmaceutical company, best known for inventing LSD in 1938 and later marketing it as a psychiatric drug under the trade name [i]Delysid[/i].

artist.name: The Sabres Of Paradise
artist.profile: Dub-influenced leftfield electronic trio comprising [a=Andrew Weatherall], [a=Gary Burns], and [a=Jagz Kooner], established in 1992. The group formed out of a series of fruitful remix collaborations done before the name "Sabres Of Paradise" was used — including "Papua New Guinea" by [a=The Future Sound Of London], [a=Flowered Up]'s epic baggy journey "Weekender" (renamed "Weatherall's Weekender" over two drastically reworked and extended balearic house/dub remixes), and [a=The Moody Boys]' "Centre Of The World". The group released its own material starting in 1993 but was known more for a series of adventurous remixes of other artists under the Sabres name. When the trio disbanded in 1995, Weatherall went on to form [a=Two Lone Swordsmen] with producer/engineer [a=Keith Tenniswood], while Kooner and Burns joined [a=The Aloof].

artist.name: David Holmes
artist.profile:
Northern Irish DJ, musician and (soundtrack) composer, born in Belfast on 14 February 1969.
[b]For the New Zealand/UK-Engineer (active from 1999) please us [a201936] For the UK-Engineer (active from 1977-1992) please use [a=David Holmes (3)] For the US-Engineer (active from 1976-1993 ?) please use [a=David Holmes (7)][/b]

artist.name: Jimi Tenor
artist.profile:
Finnish musician, composer, and artist.
Born: 1965 in Lahti, Finland.

artist.name: The Irresistible Force
artist.profile: Psychill / Ambient / Downtempo / Dub project from London, UK

artist.name: Broadcast
artist.profile:
Formed in Birmingham in 1995.
Original members: [a=Trish Keenan] - vocals [a=Roj Stevens] - keyboards [a=James Cargill] - bass [a=Tim Felton] - guitar [a=Steve Perkins (2)] - drums
They first appeared on [l=Wurlitzer Jukebox] in 1996. Their experimental pop music references the work of [a=BBC Radiophonic Workshop], [a=Ennio Morricone] or [a=John Barry]. At the time they were frequently associated with [a=Stereolab].
Lead singer Trish Keenan died of pneumonia on 14 January 2011 at the age of 42.

artist.name: Plone
artist.profile: Plone formed in late 1996 in Birmingham, where they started gigging, supporting other local bands Broadcast and Pram. In September 1997 they released the single "Press A Key" after being approached by [a4698176] of Wurlitzer Jukebox. Snapped up by Warp on the strength of this single they introduced themselves with "Plaything" on the "WAP 100: We Are Reasonable People" compilation and played live at John Peel's Meltdown festival. In September 1998 "Plock", their first single release for Warp, brought about much critical acclaim. They began to build collections of old drum machines, analogue synthesizers, and effects. Their musical influence came from music that has shown an imaginative and loose sense of technological experimentation whilst retaining a pop sensibility.

artist.name: Red Snapper
artist.profile:
Red Snapper are a British instrumental band founded in London in 1993 by Ali Friend (double bass), Richard Thair (drums), and David Ayers (guitar). The three core members are also joined by various guest musicians and vocalists on different records.
In early 2002, Red Snapper split up and went their separate ways focusing on solo projects. In 2007 Red Snapper regrouped. Since the 2007 reunion Tom Challenger (saxophone) has also been a member of the band. In 2008 they started touring and then released a new album in 2011. They were also joined live by vocalists [a=Anna Haigh] & [a=Alison David], & keyboardist [a=Jake Williams (2)] & MC [a=MC Det].

artist.name: Two Lone Swordsmen
artist.profile: British duo, formed in 1995 by DJ and producer [a2657] (1963-2020) and studio engineer [a23899]

artist.name: Rabbit In The Moon
artist.profile: American electronic music group, formed 1992 in Tampa, Florida. Their style draws from psychedelic trance, house music and breakbeat, along with other diverse influences.

artist.name: Morgan Geist
artist.profile: This New Jersey native (born 5 November 1972) was initially inspired by England's take on the sound of Detroit, but eventually tracked techno back to its Motor City innovators. When he began listening to music, however, it was the sound of early-'80s synth heroes like Devo, Severed Heads and New Order alongside early hip-hop, jazz and 60s Hindi film soundtracks. Geist released his first 12" (via Dan Curtin's innovative Metamorphic label) while studying at Oberlin College in 1994. He formed the label [l=Environ] soon after, releasing powerful statements of purpose which showed his affinity for the more emotive strains of disco, techno, soul and all flavors in between. His debut album was released in 1997 and licensed to EastWest/Warner. Geist continues to run Environ today. A highly-regarded remixer and producer both solo and as half of Metro Area, Geist has lent his touch to a wide range of artists including Hugh Masekela, Telex, Erlend Øye and The Rapture.

artist.name: Deep Dish
artist.profile: Deep Dish are the duo of Dubfire and Sharam. Originally from Iran, they took root in the Washington, DC area in the early 1990s. They run [l43373], [l=Yoshitoshi Recordings] as well as its sister labels [l=Shinichi] and [l=Yo!].

artist.name: Seed
artist.profile: US producer,remixer & collaborator. Owner of [l=Ritual Sounds]. Director at Everytone Music & Post.

artist.name: Stillpoint
artist.profile: Ambient group using a variety of ethnic instruments.

artist.name: The Herbaliser
artist.profile: English jazzy hip hop group formed in 1995 in Twickenham, London (UK). In 2020 the relationship of the production duo came to a conclusion and in 2021 Jake Wherry solely declared The Herbaliser finished. See also [a66309].

artist.name: Continuous Mode
artist.profile: One half of Porter Ricks

artist.name: Substance
artist.profile: Solo project of [a=Peter Kuschnereit]. He collaborated with [a=René Löwe] (aka [a=Vainqueur]) as [a=Substance & Vainqueur] aka [a=Scion] aka [a=Tyros].

artist.name: Vainqueur
artist.profile: Solo project of [a=René Löwe]. He collaborated with [a=Peter Kuschnereit] (aka [a=Substance] / [a=DJ Pete]) as [a758178] aka [a969] aka [a24837].

artist.name: Porter Ricks
artist.profile: Named for a character on the '60s TV show Flipper, Porter Ricks specialize in subaquatic dub techno, providing the closest touchstone to the static hum and fuzzy beatwork of their quasi labelmates Basic Channel. A collaboration between ambient maestro Thomas Köner and beatmeister Andy Mellwig, the duo debuted with three 12" releases on the Basic Channel sublabel Chain Reaction during 1995-1996. Filtering out any of the harder effects from Detroit techno and Plastikman material with what sounded like an affinity for undersea sonar frequencies and wind-tunnel ambience, the singles became quite popular with the growing experimental techno scene of the mid-'90s (and even influenced Plastikman in return). By the time of the 1996 CD collection BioKinetics on Chain Reaction, Porter Ricks had moved on to Force Inc. with the double-pack Redundance EP, plus the singles "Spoil" and "Exposed." The duo also recorded a single for Barooni before collecting their Force Inc. material onto a self-titled 1997 CD. Two years later, the Techno Animal collaboration Symbiotics appeared. Following the album's release, Mellwig released a full-length as Continuous Mode, and Köner continued releasing innovative ambient recordings on labels like Mille Plateaux, Touch, and Die Stadt. In 2016, Porter Ricks reunited for a performance at the Berlin Atonal festival. This was followed by the release of the Shadow Boat EP (on Tresor), the duo's first material in 17 years. The full-length Anguilla Electrica arrived in 2017.

artist.name: Conemelt
artist.profile: Group from Tonbridge, UK.

artist.name: Fluxion
artist.profile: Fluxion is a pseudonym of Konstantinos Soublis (aka K. Soublis), an electronic music producer from Athens, Greece.

artist.name: Vulva
artist.profile: Tim Hutton and Thomas Melchior formed Vulva in 1993 in England. The experimental techno duo signed with the Rephlex label (home of Aphex Twin) and released From the Cockpit in 1993. Vulva's first domestic release was the Mini Space Vulvette EP, for Reflective Records. After a side project for the German Source label called Yoni ("Yoni" and My Little Yoni), Hutton and Melchior released their second full-length for Rephlex, Birdwatch, in 1996. Vulvic Yonification followed in late 1997.

artist.name: Bochum Welt
artist.profile:
Bochum Welt is the stage name of the Italian electronic musician Gianluigi Di Costanzo.
The name Bochum Welt is a combination of the German word for "world" (welt) and the name of a high-powered astral telescope.
Di Costanzo has released records on established record labels. His first single for Aphex Twin's Rephlex label, "Scharlach Eingang" (1994), published by Warner/Chappell Music, was so accomplished that the British music magazine NME ran an article claiming that Bochum Welt could be a ghost project by Aphex Twin. Over the following years, Aphex Twin released eight of his records on Rephlex and included his tracks in the label’s two compilations.
His debut album for Sony Music Japan, "Module 2 / Desktop Robotics", collected early works and was followed by the double CD "Robotic Operating Buddy", released by Rephlex and nominated in 2009 as a top 15 electronica album by The Wire (UK).
BMG signed him to a long-term worldwide publishing deal in 2009. Originally founded as Bertelsmann Music Group, BMG is the music division of the German media company Bertelsmann AG, established in 1835 by Carl Bertelsmann in Gütersloh, Germany.
Rephlex Records released his new full-length in 2013, “Good Programs (To Be Coloured in Yellow)”, as well as a collaborative EP with Heinrich Muller aka Gerald Donald from the Detroit group DREXCIYA, nominated in 2013 by Fact (UK magazine) in the 50 greatest 10ʺ records of FACT’s lifetime. Both of the records were published by BMG.
"April", a 17-track studio album, is one of the official Record Store Day releases in April 2017, released by Studio K7 and published by BMG.
After a Bochum Welt show on Halloween on the roof of the Ace Hotel Los Angeles, formerly the United Artists Theatre, Joshua Eustis (Telefon Tel Aviv / Nine Inch Nails) and Bochum Welt met and began collaborating. More live performances followed at the Ace Hotels in New York, Chicago, Palm Springs, and London.
In 2019 and 2020 "Seafire", an album and remixes released by CPU Records / BMG, included collaborations with Joshua Eustis and EOD.
CPU and BMG Records released “F.O.A.S / Desktop Robotics” and “Module 2” special edition albums in 2022 and 2024.
In addition to releasing his own music, Di Costanzo has worked with Thomas Dolby's Headspace and Beatnik Inc., Hollywood companies that produced multimedia content for Steven Spielberg, David Bowie, and others.
In 2025, Bochum Welt teamed up with Jil Sander on "Wanderlust", a multidisciplinary launch project including a vinyl EP, a digital album, a music video directed by Christopher Simmonds, and a live event held at OHG Hamburg, a former factory turned cultural venue. Wanderlust was described as reflecting Jil Sander’s duality of “strictness and lightness, rigour and abandon”. The project also celebrated a symbolic return to Hamburg, Jil Sander’s city of formation, with the album artwork based on photographs from Christopher Simmonds’ campaign shot in the city. Presented as Jil Sander x Bochum Welt, the collaboration received international coverage in Vogue, Wallpaper, Hypebeast, Icon, and others. The EP and album are published by BMG and distributed worldwide.
In September 2025, Bochum Welt composed and performed the original soundtrack for Jil Sander’s fashion show in Milan, marking Simone Bellotti’s debut as creative director. The event took place in the brand’s historic headquarters, whose white interiors opened toward Castello Sforzesco — a spatial dialogue Bellotti described as emblematic of the tension between historic Milanese identity and modern minimalism, and of the conceptual axis linking Milan and Hamburg, Jil Sander’s city of origin. A black curved runway cutting through the stark white space amplified this contrast, while the music, entrusted to Bochum Welt, shaped the atmosphere rather than merely accompanying the garments. In continuity with Wanderlust, Bochum Welt’s soundtrack emphasized Bellotti’s theme of Jil Sander’s duality: “strictness and lightness, rigour and abandon.”

artist.name: DMX Krew
artist.profile: Artist DJ producer performer engineer and label owner from London (UK). He is the founder of [l=Breakin' Records] and [l=Fresh Up Records]. Additionally to his artist career, he has joined [l=Curve Pusher] mastering team, in 2018.

artist.name: 9 Lazy 9
artist.profile: 9 Lazy 9 joined Ninja Tune when [l=Ninja Tune] was just one grey metal cupboard in a run down London loft. Operating out of Rome (Italy) in 1992 they soon gained respect for their ground cracking first album "Paradise Blown". Their sound, distinctively more sun than North Sea, is a smoky Mediterranean mix of vines and good times. Keir John Fraser and Giacomo Braddellini have been making music for many years in different guises before combining forces in Rome in the early nineties. They have been working with some of Italy's finest musicians on their latest album, "Sweet Jones", recorded in the Umbrian Hills near Orvieto. Gianluca Petrella plays trombone, Mishael Levron guitar; Manu Bandettini plays flute with Adriano Tirelli on bassoon. The combined force brings you one of the freshest records to come out of Italy for some time.

artist.name: Diferenz
artist.profile: Swiss DJ.

artist.name: Rollercone
artist.profile: DJ/Producer from Geneva, Switzerland.

artist.name: Beamish & Fly
artist.profile:
Triphop / Big Beat act.
Members: Christopher Bemand (Beamish) Max Skinner (Fly)

artist.name: Chugga
artist.profile:
Hailed as the funkiest rebels to ever flunk out of air-conditioner repair school, Memphis based duo Chugga (a.k.a. Lester Fuero and Jeff Hanes) combine the straight-on breaks of old-school hip-hop with 70's funk and 90's jeep dub. Their stripped-down approach to making music pays tribute to the mixing styles of 70's DJ's such as Kool Herc and Afrika Bambaataa, while maintaining a relaxed flow that works just as well for deep listening.
When asked what they were currently up to, Jeff quipped, "Use your time machine to send that question back 15 years, when somebody might have cared."
Chugga continue to reside in Memphis.

artist.name: Knowtoryus
artist.profile: Hip Hop group in parts with ragga-flavour, rare groove and funky street elements. Based in Germany (Nürnberg / München) the players are Free Justice, an english MC from Paramount California formerly of G.S.P. (Ghetto Style Poets), Cee Justice, Now B, one of the english style MCs from Belle Glades Florida and DJ Jaazz. In 1994/95, Jaazz was Bavarian DJ-Champion and in fact he is [a8665] on [l657].

artist.name: Raucous a/k/a Sabotage
artist.profile: Rapper from Harlem, New York, USA.

artist.name: DJ Krush
artist.profile: Japanese alternative hip hop record producer, DJ, and remixer, born July 29, 1962 in Tokyo. General manager: [l305096].

artist.name: Roey Marquis II
artist.profile:
Roey Marquis II. (also known as Ming or RM2, born Calogero Randazzo; *1971 in Frankfurt am Main, Hesse) is a German/Italian hip-hop producer and DJ.
Shortly after playing DJ-sets at Larry's Inn at the age of 18, Calogero Randazzo became that clubs manager. In 1991 he was the co-founder of [l=S.Y.A. Records], focusing on eurodance and rap music, and went on to open the record store Sounds Of Blackness the same year.
In 1992 he created the name Roey Marquis II. while spinning at the Funkadelic Club, founding the jazz influenced label [l=Loose Ends Records], together with Kerstin Lamb in February of 1993, and the hip-hop label [l=Ruff-N-Raw] together, shortly thereafter. Producing for such artists as [a=Da Germ], [a=Otropic T(h)ree] and [a=Diferenz], as well as his own projects, and those with [a=Jazz Con Bazz].
From 1993 to 1996, Calogero successfully ran his record labels, while heading the promotions agency Tribes Of The Underground. Performing as a support-DJ for the likes of [a=Run-DMC], [a=Onyx], [a=Fugees], [a=Grandmaster Flash], [a=Nas] and [a=KRS-One] as well.
From 1997 he began working for the Frankfurt based production company Fun De Mental, from where he operated [l=Quiet Force Records (Germany)], and the rap/R&B heavy Division Genuine Productions. Touring with The Marquis Club.
A year later he began on the project [a=MK2], together with [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/Kabuki]DJ Kabuki[/url] releasing the album Prepare To Be Educated, and he collaborated with MC's [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/Da+Germ]Germ[/url] and [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/Matrix+(6)]Matrix[/url] to form the group [a=Spiritual Warriors], releasing their debut, a concept album, in 1999.
Roey Marquis II. released his first solo album [b]Ming[/b], a producer album and the first of a RM2 Trilogy in 2000. That same year, he started a new production company, called Shen Productions, and introduced his new label [l=Ming Dynasty], signing such artists as [a=Jonesmann], [a=Jeyz], [a=Profan78], [a=Pal One] and [a=Lunafrow] amongst others. In 2002 he released [b]Herzessenz[/b], the second part of his RM2 Trilogy, followed by [b]Samsara[/b], his third installment a year later. He has since been involved in numerous other projects, having had his autobiography "Lebenslinien" published in 2008, which he co-wrote with Tobias Hoeft.
Roey Marquis has produced songs for a large part of the German rap scene, having worked with the likes of [a=Absztrakkt], [a=RAG], [a=Stieber Twins], [a=Samy Deluxe], [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/Curse+(3)]Curse[/url], [a=Azad], [a=Kool Savas], [a=Olli Banjo], [a=Brixx], [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/Nicodemus+(2)]Nicodemus[/url], [a=Schivv], [a=Tatwaffe], [a=Archetype], [a=Al' Tariq], [a=Problemz], [a=Lin Que], [a=Finsta], [a=Tony Touch], [a=Evil Dee], [a=DJ Spinna], amongst many more.

artist.name: Empirion
artist.profile: British Industrial techno band founded in 1993 in Essex.

artist.name: Dave Clarke
artist.profile:
English DJ, producer and radio show host. Born in Brighton, on 19 September 1968. Based in Amsterdam. Founder of [l1441] and [l127481].
Started as Hip-Hop DJ in the mid 80's, shifting to Acid House and later to Rave near the end of the decade. He began recording under the alias [a=Pig City]) in the early 90's. He became active as radio presenter in 2000, hosting "Technology" on Studio Brussels in Belgium where he promoted new Techno and Electro artists. In September 2006 he launched a new radio show White Noise, originally broadcast on 3FM in the Netherlands.

artist.name: Secret Knowledge
artist.profile: House/Techno act from Aylesbury, UK, and Ohio, USA. Kris Needs and Wonder Schneider were a couple as well as recording together.

artist.name: Azzido Da Bass
artist.profile: German DJ and producer based in Hamburg, born 29 August 1971. He is the founder of [l=Luscious Sounds].

artist.name: Muse
artist.profile:
Muse are an English rock band from Teignmouth, Devon, formed in 1994. The band consists of Matthew Bellamy (lead vocals, guitar, keyboards, piano), Christopher Wolstenholme (bass guitar, backing vocals) and Dominic Howard (drums, backing vocals). Dan Lancaster joins Muse on live stage since 2022 (previously Morgan Nicholls) for additional instruments and vocals.
Muse signed to Maverick Records and released their debut album, Showbiz, in 1999, showcasing Bellamy's falsetto and a melancholic alternative rock style. Their second album, Origin of Symmetry (2001), incorporated wider instrumentation and romantic classical influences, and earned them a reputation for energetic live performances. Absolution (2003) saw further classical influence, with orchestra on tracks such as "Butterflies & Hurricanes", and became the first of five consecutive UK number-one albums.
Black Holes & Revelations (2006) incorporated electronic and pop elements, displayed in singles such as "Supermassive Black Hole". The album brought Muse wider international success. The Resistance (2009), loosely based on the novel 1984, and The 2nd Law (2012) explored themes of government oppression and civil uprising and cemented Muse as one of the world's major stadium acts. Their seventh album, Drones (2015), was a concept album about drone warfare and returned to a harder rock sound. Their eighth album, Simulation Theory, featuring a retro 1980s style, was released on November 9th, 2018. Will of the People, their latest to date album, was released on August 26th, 2022 and is inspired by the current years events, such as the Covid-19 pandemic, riots, Black Lives Matter movement, and so on.
Muse have won numerous awards, including two Grammy Awards, two Brit Awards, five MTV Europe Music Awards and eight NME Awards. In 2012 they received the Ivor Novello Award for International Achievement from the British Academy of Songwriters, Composers and Authors. They have sold over 20 million albums worldwide.
When marked as a copyright holder, use [l621514].

artist.name: Jan Driver
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Germany.

artist.name: Big Ron
artist.profile: House DJ from London, United Kingdom.

artist.name: Lustral
artist.profile: Lustral is a British electronic music project, incorporating the production duo of Richard Louis Simmonds (Ricky Simmons) and Stephen Christopher Jones (Steve Jones). They were also billed as The Space Brothers and Chakra.

artist.name: Poseidon
artist.profile: Artist which appears in a 1992 compilation with a track called "Strange Harmony (End Of The Party Mix)".

artist.name: Orinoko
artist.profile: House/Trance DJ project from Germany.

artist.name: Timo Maas
artist.profile:
German producer, label manager and DJ and remixer born in July 27, 1969.
Maas bought his first set of turntables at the age of 17 and played his very first DJ set in 1982 at a party in his friend's home. In 1992, he started to DJ in the European rave scene, earning a name in the electronic underground world.
Most of the records and remixes attributed to Timo Maas are in reality collaborations between more artists than Timo himself, regularly with [a12995] and [a12994].
In more than 30 years of career, Timo and his team has been collaborating with and remixing many artists such as [a2725], [a3101], [a12332], [a8029], [a8760], [a1031], [a1661], [a1003], [a9436], [a4719], [a9902], [a35301] & [a145074]
Maas' first record, "The Final XS", together with [a17209], was released in 1995. His second record release was a collaboration with another producer, [a195631], "Die Herdplatte", which was a bigger success than his first. Gary D also gained Maas a residency at Hamburg's famous club, The Tunnel, between 1994 and 1996, playing also at the historic Bristol club Lakota.
