artist.name: R. Solution
artist.profile: One of many aliases used by Marc Mac active between 1991-1993. The first release R.Solution - Blowing My Mind was by both Mark Clair (Manix) & Dennis McFarlane (Tek 9) after that R.Solution was Mark on his own.

artist.name: USI
artist.profile: Underground Sound Inc.

artist.name: Tek 9
artist.profile: One of the earliest active aliases of Dego from 4 Hero, originally used for his solo breakbeat and hardcore work, but has taken a more downtempo and hip hop slant over time.

artist.name: Sudden Def
artist.profile: UK Hardcore producers signed to Reinforced Records. Previously Keith Montague & Darren Dooley up to the end of 1992. Then from 1993 just an alias of Keith Montague.

artist.name: Simon "Bassline" Smith
artist.profile:
He started DJing at the age of 17. At this point in his life he was setting the stage for the rest of his musical career by pioneering the sound of the underground all over the UK at many all day parties which was the basis of the rave scene as we know it today. He was given the name "Bassline" after destroying speakers at these early parties through his heavy sub bass sounds.
It was around this time that Simon started one of the first D&B labels [l=Absolute 2 Records].
His son is [a=Youngman MC].

artist.name: Special K
artist.profile: UK Jungle producer. Played on Don FM with [a=Ghostrider]

artist.name: Randall
artist.profile:
Born 2nd April 1970. Died 31st July 2024.
Hardcore/Jungle/Drum'n'Bass DJ/producer from London. In the mid 1980s he was part of the Limited Edition sound system. Began attending raves whilst working as a messenger in the World Trade Centre in London. His first bookings, after sending out tape demos, was at Delirium in London, playing Thursdays and Saturdays. He then began his own show on Centreforce FM. Resident DJ at A.W.O.L., Orange at the Rocket, Innersense and Together As 1 and also had shows on Rave FM and Pressure FM.
Co-owned the De Underground record shop in Forest Gate. Later co-founded the [l6144] label. His daughter is the recording artist [a=Amber Simone]. His brother Mark was a professional footballer in England and Sweden.

artist.name: System 3
artist.profile: System 3 is a hardcore/gabber producer from Italy active since 1995, having released several classic hits, and having been part of many projects showcasing his contribution to the hardcore music scene. In time and through many collaborations with other artists such as Dj Dano, The Sickest Squad, Dj Sirio and many more, he co-wrote and recorded vocals for releases on labels like D-Boy Black Label, Industrial Strength, Mokum Records, Megarave, Aeon Tracks, Psychik Genocide and others. This gave him the possibility to perform at some of the most well known parties and festivals throughout Europe. System 3 has been associated with groups like Crime Lab and Max E-Crew, and is a co-founder of italian hardcore label Aeon Tracks. Dario Benetti was invlolved with the first release in 1996, in 2001 Daniele Baraldo left the project and since then System 3 became a solo project of Piero Scarato.

artist.name: Paul Mac
artist.profile: [b]NOT TO BE CONFUSED with Australian producer and songwriter [a=Paul McDermott (2)] alias [a=Paulmac][/b]! Techno DJ, producer and mixing / mastering engineer from UK. Runs and records for [l=Stimulus Recordings] and [l=C-Side] as well as a host of other labels. He has said in an interview that it was necessary to use a number of aliases during more productive phases of his career. Some of his pseudonyms are unusual names, but they are all copied from real people; for example, Eddie Fiola was a famous BMX stunt rider in the '80s. Co-owner of [l=Hardgroove Mastering] with [a=Ben Sims].

artist.name: The Hacker
artist.profile:
Musician / DJ from Grenoble, France. He has been producing music since 1989, with a high influence from Techno, Electro, Italo, EBM & New Wave.
He is also known for his collaboration as duo [a8767].

artist.name: India
artist.profile:
India – also known as [i]La India[/i] in the latin music industry. Born on March 9, 1969 as Linda Viera Caballero, in Río Piedras, San Juan, Puerto Rico, and raised in the Bronx, New York City, USA.
Worked as backing vocalist on latin-freestyle artists [a=TKA]'s track "[r=98844]" (1986), even featuring on its back cover, when she was 14. Then under the tutelage of producer [a=John "Jellybean" Benitez] on a couple of tracks including her track "[r=264534]" (1988) (with co-producer [a=Louie Vega] who she had been introduced to in her mid-teens, and would later go on to marry in 1990, though divorce in 1996).
Signing briefly to [l=Reprise Records], who supposedly planned on making her the Latin version of [a=Madonna] –Jellybean's other main find earlier in his career– where both Jellybean and Louie Vega produced her album "[r=1458694]" (1990).
However, it wasn't until her major involvement in the music business working with [a=Louie Vega] and [a=Kenny "Dope" Gonzalez] on various projects mainly in the mid-1990's whilst in her twenties, for which she achieved great success. The three tracks of note during this time were as [a=Masters At Work] with "[r=18862]" (1993), and "[r=1218441]" (1997), along with their [a=Nuyorican Soul] project track "[r=42801]" (1997).
Alongside work on latin-house music into the early 2000's, she also became more involved in the more purist latin arena around the late 1990's, and in fact on these projects the name La India was generally used.
Whilst moving her career and concentrating on latin music, she built quite a reputation as 'The Princess Of Salsa' garnering three Grammy Award and two Latin Grammy Award nominations. She has collaborated with her godmother [a=Celia Cruz], and many other artists like [a=Marc Anthony], [a=Gloria Estefan] and even [a=Tito Puente], releasing several albums of her own.

artist.name: Doc Scott
artist.profile:
As well as DJing and releasing his own tunes, Scott runs the label [l=31 Records].
Was monthly resident at Speed in London.

artist.name: King Britt
artist.profile: American DJ, producer/remixer, and artist. Born: 1968 in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, USA. Originally from Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, in 1992 Britt was the DJ for the Grammy winner hip hop-jazz-fusion band [a=Digable Planets] (alongside [a=Ishmael Butler], [a=Doodlebug (2)], and [a=Ladybug Mecca]). Britt's career pointed to different directions as he started to travel the world with [a=Digable Planets] tours, and with his own ones on the international house music scene as a DJ. At the very beginning of his journey as solo DJ, he settled into a partnership with the well-known Philadelphia DJ, producer, and owner of the label [l=Ovum Recordings], [a=Josh Wink], who also contributed for the development of his career. One of their first projects together was [a=E-Culture], behind the classic track "[url=https://www.discogs.com/E-Culture-Tribal-Confusion-Unification/master/212691]Tribal Confusion[/url]" (1990) released by [l=Strictly Rhythm]. Founded [l=The Buddy System Project] in 2014. Runs his own label [l=KingBrittArchives].

artist.name: The Sunburst Band
artist.profile:
Dave Lee (programming & production), Michele Chiavarini (keys), Frank Tontoh (drums), Julian Crampton (bass), Tony Remy (guitar), Pete Simpson (vocals), Donna Gardier (vocals), Alex Bennett (keys), Nick Cohen (laptop)
Plus Taka Boom, Diane Charlemagne (RIP), Linda Clifford, Henry Thomas, Thomas Dyani-Akuru
Formed in 1998 by Dave Lee (aka Joey Negro), The Sunburst Band is a collective born out of Mr Lee’s passion for boogie, funk, disco and jazz.
Veteran UK electronic producer Dave Lee (aka Joey Negro) is one of the few people who can glide effortlessly from the chilly depths of the "underground" to the moneyfied glitz of commercial music, while keeping both his dignity and his wide-ranging fan-base intact. Having moved on recently from the cool ad-friendly, chill-out throb of his Jakatta moniker, Dave has embarked upon a series of production projects including developing and producing new electro-rockers The Like Me's due to release their first album at the end of this year. Alongside this, the ever prolific Mr Lee, has also regrouped with his supa-talented muso mates to form London's latest nu-funk and boogie hybrid, The Sunburst Band. With members culled from the funkiest ends of Incognito, Jamiroquai and the Kyoto Jazz Massive, The Sunburst Band is unashamedly musical.

artist.name: Tuomas Salmela
artist.profile: Finnish DJ and producer based in Tampere.

artist.name: Daniel Ibbotson
artist.profile:
Balancing his affinity for new-school deep house beats with an early fixation for jazzy textures, indie-rock and Warp's bleep techno, Daniel Ibbotson is a producer-by-accident, a home-production aficionado who put together a demo tape during 1995 while studying at the Glasgow School of Arts for his photography degree. After sending it to the electro label Clear Records (whose buzzed-about résumé included incarnations of µ-Ziq, Black Dog and Global Communication), the label released some of the material as the Souped-Up EP in August 1996. More than enough material was left for an entire album, released in 1998 for the Clear spin-off Reel Discs. Ibbotson also remixed Friend & Doktor Kosmos, and appeared on the Compost collection Future Sound of Jazz, Vol. 3; he returned in 1999 with Frequency and Phase.
Daniel lived with Multiple Myeloma for over a decade and died in July 2025.

artist.name: Slow Supreme
artist.profile: Early moniker of Lindström. Style ranged from Deep house, lounge grooves and disco experiments. "Granada" scored a minor hit and was featured on several bar grooves and lounge compilations.

artist.name: Tilt
artist.profile:
British electronic music producers and DJs, originally consisting of [a=Michael Park] and [a=Michael Wilson (3)] in Coventry, England. The line-up has changed many times over the years, and has also spent some time in hiatus.
1993-2002 Michael Park, Michael Wilson, [a=John Graham] 2002-2004 Michael Park, Michael Wilson (3) 2004-2005 Michael Park, Michael Wilson (3), [a=Andrew Beardmore] 2005-? Michael Park, Andrew Beardmore 2011-2012 Michael Park, [a=Nic Britton] 2012-2013 [a=Michael Park], [a=Nic Britton], Alex Brown 2012- Current [a=Michael Park], [a=Nic Britton]

artist.name: Cass & Slide
artist.profile:
British production and remix duo consisting of members Christopher Andrew Stuart Bank Cutbush and Peter John Martin. They met in 1999 while they both were working at London-based Flying Rhino Records. They released "Diablo" on Flying Rhino before relocating to Fire Records, owned by Cass and fellow Englishman Lee Burridge. The duo were given world-wide exposure when the Evolution remix of "Diablo" appeared on John Digweed's 'Hong Kong Global Underground' mix CD, and "Perception" was featured on Sasha's 'Ibiza Global Underground' mix CD.
Cass & Slide released a full-length album in 2002, which turned out to be a departure from their usual sound with a larger focus on the breaks and down-tempo sounds of the electronic music spectrum. The CD received mixed reviews, and the duo's production/remix output halted when Cass's label folded in early 2003. They have remained largely inactive since.

artist.name: Space Manoeuvres
artist.profile: Production project of British DJ / Producer [a=John Graham] (with sometimes co-writer [a=Lea Kenny]) responsible for the progressive trance classic "[m=146805]".

artist.name: Breeder
artist.profile: Breeder was a production duo of [a113477] and [a44552]. They met through a mutual friend and swiftly headed into the studio to remix one of Simon's compositions with [a75741], "Make It Funky," which they'd recorded as [a16458]. They ended up making a brand new track, which was "The Chain," and the duo's first single. Along with their second single "Twilo Thunder," the duo had commercial success, having first appeared on [m37695] compilation. Breeder split in 2001.

artist.name: Planisphere
artist.profile: Belgian Progressive Trance project by DJ & producer [a=Airwave] (aka Laurent Véronnez) & [a=Inflexion] (aka Georges Petkidis).

artist.name: Bedrock
artist.profile: Electronic music duo from London, UK, active since 1992.

artist.name: Nils Petter Molvær
artist.profile:
Norwegian trumpet player, composer and producer, born September 18, 1960 in Langevåg on the island of Sula, Norway.
His musical role models include Miles Davis, Don Cherry, Billie Holiday, Brian Eno, Jon Hassell, Joni Mitchell and Bill Laswell. He is considered a pioneer of the fusion of jazz and electronic music.
At the beginning of his career he played in the formation Oslo 13 led by Jon Balke. The quintet with Lotte Anker, Mette Petersen, Klavs Hovman and Ole Rømer released the album Being on Stunt Records in 1993. He became known to a wider audience through his debut album Khmer, on which Molvær contrasts his ethereal playing and his feverish, hoarse trumpet tone with powerful electronic beats. Khmer received a great deal of attention and excellent reviews; for example, Molvær received the German Record Critics' Award and the American newspaper L.A. Weekly named Khmer the Jazz Album of the Year in 1998. Rolling Stone magazine ranked the album 92nd in its 2013 list of the 100 best jazz albums.
This was followed in 2000 by the album Solid Ether and the following year by the pure remix album Recoloured, on which various guest musicians and DJs participated, including Bill Laswell and Funkstörung. The album NP3 was released in 2002 and Streamer in the summer of 2004. Streamer was released on Molvær's own, newly founded label Sula. In 2005, Molvær released another studio album, ER, on which, in addition to himself, the singers Sidsel Endresen and Elin Rosseland, but also guitarist Eivind Aarset and pianist Magne Furuholmen (formerly of a-ha) set important accents.
He also wrote the music for the German film Stratosphere Girl by M. X. Oberg. In 1998 he received the Norwegian Gammleng Prize in the jazz category. In 2003 he was awarded the Buddyprisen from the Norwegian Jazz Forum. In 2021, Molvær received the European Film Award together with Peter Brötzmann for the music in the historical drama Große Freiheit.
He is the son of [a2867663] and nephew of [a3191426].

artist.name: Lemongrass
artist.profile: Electronic music project from Germany.

artist.name: Marcel
artist.profile:
Marcel is the artist of Good Looking Records' Cookin' Records imprint. He had two albums at the German company UCMG/Mole Listening Pearls (Viginti etduo, Cirrus maximus). In 2004, the album "Gambler's Delight" came out on Good Looking's downtempo sublabel Cookin' Records. You can find his latest releases on the Good Looking Records' cd Earth vol.6., "Jealous" and "Solitudo 3".
Marcel also records as Carmel alterego. He released a 12" under this jazzy Carmel alterego titled "Nujazzkiller" at Fluid Ounce in 2002, from which "Harakiri blues", a crazy acid track with opera samples was a favourite of many djs, nevertheless Gilles Peterson used to play this tune out frequently.
More recently the Carmel releases will come out at Tru Thoughts Records (Quantic's record label), where the last release was actually the Quantic: Off the beaten track (Carmel remix).
Marcel use to play unique live acts with samplers, synthesizers etc.

artist.name: Nobukazu Takemura
artist.profile:
Japanese electronic DJ, producer, and artist.
Born: 26 August 1968 in Osaka, Japan.
Takemura started his DJ career during high school, spinning in the clubs of Osaka, Kyoto, and Kobe. In 1987, he formed the DJ team "Cool Jazz Productions", and in 1988, joins "Audio Sports" the now legendary hip-hop unit headed by Eye Yamatsuka of the Boredoms.
Around 1993 Takemura first branched out as a solo artist under the moniker [a=Child's View]. The name highlighted one of the key themes in his music that is still prevalent today; trying to create melodies that musically capture a sense of wonder, a childlike innocence and curiosity.
He also has a huge catalog of collaborations with critically acclaimed artists from every corner of the world. From designer [a=Issey Miyake], who had him create music for two seasons of designs, to company [l=Sony] who had him create all the sounds for their futuristic "Aibo" toy dog.
From artists [a=Steve Reich] to [a=DJ Spooky], from [a=Yo La Tengo] to [a=Tortoise], his wide range of collaborative partners reflect the wide range of his musical talents.
He was managed by [a=Shinsuke Yamada (2)].

artist.name: Gábor Deutsch
artist.profile:
Budapest/Berlin based Hungarian music producer and mixing/mastering engineer.
His live project is called [a=Anorganik] and uses other pseudonyms like [a=Raster] and [a=Gary Blade] for different genres. Debut album "Contrast" was released on [l236], later he co-founded Hungary's first drum and bass label, Subscope Recordings, and established a house label called "LIP Recordings / Dublife Music" with DJ Soneec. His tracks and remixes have been released on more than 100 CD, vinyl and digital compilations worldwide.
In the last years he has been working as a mixing and mastering engineer, co-producer, audio-doctor and remixer for Hungarian electronic, rock & jazz bands and producers, also composing music for films and commercials.

artist.name: Anima Sound System
artist.profile:
Created in 1993 by Zsolt and Szabolcs Prieger and shortly thereafter joined by Gergely Németh, Anima Sound System is the premier electronic act in Hungary. Blending a unique fusion of Hungarian folk music and modern electronic styles, Anima provides a unique experience amongst today's crowded techno cornucopia.
The band's line-up is often changing and has variously featured: Szilvia Bognár, DJ Cadik, Tamás Németh, Ákos Angler, Judit Németh, Tamás Soós, Jeremy Thomson and others.
Latest line-up includes Fanni Prieger, daughter of Zsolt Prieger.

artist.name: Bullitnuts
artist.profile: Round 1999 Bullitnuts split up. [a=Murray Clarke] started recording as [a=Leggo Beast] and [a=Robert Ellerby] started [a=Momma Gravy] together with [a=Remko Schouten]. Bullitnuts recorded 3 albums for Pork Recordings.

artist.name: Jungle Brothers
artist.profile: Jungle Brothers were formed through the high school friendship of Nathaniel "Afrika Baby Bam" Hall and Michael "Mike G." Small. Mike brought family friend DJ Sammy B into the mix, and the Jungle Brothers were born. Members: Nathaniel Hall, Michael Small, and Sammy Burwell embraced a range of styles -- including house music, Afrocentric philosophy, James Brown, and the use of jazz samples, as one of the original but most underrated members of the loose collective of 'alternative' rap artists known as the Native Tongues Posse, formed by hip-hop legend [a=Afrika Bambaataa], which included [a=Queen Latifah], [a=De La Soul], [a=A Tribe Called Quest], and later [a=Monie Love] and [a=Black Sheep]. The group's far-out yet most successful single was "I'll House You," collaborating with house producer [a=Todd Terry] in an early experiment in what later became hip-house.

artist.name: Yonderboi
artist.profile: Hungarian electronic music artist. He was born in Mernye, Somogy, Hungary in 1980.

artist.name: Misstress Barbara
artist.profile: Italian DJ & producer, born 3 December 1975 in Catania (Sicily) but moved with her family to Montreal (Canada) in 1983. She is the founder of [l=Relentless] - renamed to [l=Iturnem] in 2003. Misstress is combination of words "miss" + "stress".

artist.name: Rino Cerrone
artist.profile: Italian techno / tech house DJ, producer and label owner based in Naples. He started artistic career in 1989/1990. Co-founder of [l=Rilis] and sublabels. On 25 September 2024 it was announced that he has passed away.

artist.name: Reload
artist.profile:
Reload was originally a Mark Pritchard solo project. Later Tom Middleton joined Mark on some of his Reload releases, and "& E621" (the name of a chemical flavour enhancer) was added to specify Middleton's involvement/flavour enhancement on a track (similar to the set-up with "Link & E621"). As Tom became more heavily involved on the Reload "A Collection of Short Stories" album, the "& E621" was scrapped, for simplicity, and Reload came to mean both Mark Pritchard and Tom Middleton. All Reload Remixes (except for the two on the Mystic Institute "Cyberdon EP") were done by both Mark and Tom together.
In '97 Pritchard released The "Reload Archives" EP, as a solo endeavor.

artist.name: E621
artist.profile: E621 is a Chemical Flavour Enhancer. Both Link and Reload were originally Mark Pritchard solo projects/aliases. Then, Tom Middleton joined Pritchard on some of these releases and "& E621" was added to specify his involvement or "flavour enhancement" of the project. (ie: Reload & E621 / Link & E621) With Middleton's heavy involvement on the Reload "A Collection of Short Stories" album (with no specific credit as "E621"), Reload came to mean both Pritchard and Middleton, and the "E621" alias was scrapped. No tracks were ever released by "E621" alone.

artist.name: Chameleon
artist.profile: Melodic, ambient drum 'n' bass project of [a=Tom Middleton] & [a=Mark Pritchard].

artist.name: Calm
artist.profile: Calm is the main alias of Japanese producer Kiyotaka Fukagawa. Sometimes Calm uses aka "Farr", though he (largely) doesn't record under this name.

artist.name: Little Big Bee
artist.profile:
Little Big Bee is a sound producer team by Eitetsu Takamiya. As a club DJ, he started his career in the end of 1980’s, and had played 6 times in a week in the night clubs in Tokyo as a regular DJ with releasing lots of remixes and producing various artists. Thence, he founded an Independent label, Flower Records in 1995. In 1993, Little Big Bee published its first work by providing to S-KEN’s compilation album "JAZZ POWERS”, and this song was also released with vinyl in UK with receiving great responses from the market. Accompanies with Kenji Jinguji (Bass) and PLAZA Fujisaki (Keys), brought out remixes and produce works for worldwide artists like Atjazz (UK), KENNY BOBIEN (US), SUGAR HILL GANG (US), Pasta Boys (ITA), and many other local artists such as PE'Z, COLDFEET, Seiko Matsuda, Miyazawa Kazufumi through his own label besides releasing its own original works constantly. In 2006, Little Big Bee released its first album "WATERMAN". “K.G.O (Jazztronik Remix)“ which is included in the same album was played by Tom Middleton, and this became the opportunities that Danny Howells played on BBC (UK) and WMC (US) as well. Immediately after the distribution on Beatport, won Single Of Win The Week. Further more,”K.G.O. (Tom Middleton Remodel)” which was edited by Tom Middleton himself was also a big hit through his own mix CD "One More Tune" released from Renaissance. The first song in the album “Scuba” was also released with vinyl and has got premium price in the record market all over the world year by year. In addition, its original song "Searchin'" released from Yellow Production in France was used in the popular drama series "Sex And The City" and well received all over the world.
[b][i]Members:[/i][/b] [a=Eitetsu Takamiya]: DJ, producer, engineer and label owner of [l=Flower Records] [a=Plaza Fujisaki]: keyboardist [a=Kenji Jinguji]: bassist
[b][i]Former members:[/i][/b] [a=Kei Horiguchi]

artist.name: Chari Chari
artist.profile:
Japanese electronic project from Kaoru Inoue.
Kaoru Inoue started his DJ career in a small clubs in Tokyo in early 1990s under the influences of Acid Jazz, after participating in a compilation called "Listen Up" (1994). And it was aiming for a fusion of breakbeats and exoticism.
The second album "In Time" (2002) was released on Toy's Factory with the 12" release of "Aurora EP" (2002) on Bonjour Records, he gained a solid reputation both domestically and internationally, but since then Kaoru has been absent from the scene under this name for a long time.
In 2013, 5ive (cos/mes)'s label "Snaker" released the collection of rare tracks as a very limited LP, then Chari Chari returned once as a live band in 2014 to perform a live set at RA festival in Tokyo, and released 12" EP "Fading Away / Luna de Lobos" (2016) recorded with members of the band on Kaoru's independent label Seeds And Ground.
The third album "We Hear The Last Decades Dreaming" (2020); the first time in 18 years.

artist.name: Salomé De Bahia
artist.profile:
Brazilian vocalist, living in Paris, France.
Her musical career started in 1958. She met Parisian DJ and producer Bob Sinclar in 1997 in Paris jazz café Chez Felix

artist.name: Kyoto Jazz Massive
artist.profile: Japanese jazz and electronic recording duo. The two brothers Shuya and Yoshihiro Okino make up the production team known as Kyoto Jazz Massive. They are based in Kyoto, Japan, where they ran a record shop, and their own label [l=Especial Records] (and offshoot [l=Especial Distribution]).

artist.name: Jazztronik
artist.realname: Ryōta Nozaki (野崎 良太 / Nozaki Ryōta)
artist.profile: Jazztronik is a freeform music project by DJ, keyboardist, and producer Ryota Nozaki. In 2017 he launched his own label called "[l1384733]".

artist.name: Waiwan
artist.profile:
Wai is second-generation British-born Chinese raised in Hull by Hong Kong Chinese parents. As an artist, producer and remixer, he has released music for major and independent labels, internationally for over twenty years, receiving support from the likes of Gilles Peterson (BBC Radio 6), Mixmaster Morris (NTS Radio). His single Goddess was ranked amongst the all-time top twenty Nu-Jazz records on Worldwide FM. Following the release of his album Distraction, Wai has since performed across venues in Leeds, Manchester, New York, and London, including the legendary Plastic People, Hacienda, Band on the Wall and Big Chill festival. He has also worked on collaborative commissions for Castlefield Gallery, Studio Eleven as part of the BBC Contains Strong Language, PRS for Music Foundation’s New Music Biennial.
Wai’s recent explorations into music composition involves modular synthesis and the manipulation of field recordings, juxtaposing familiar sentiments in unfamiliar settings through the notion of imagined nostalgia. He currently lectures and works as the Technical Resource Manager at the University of Hull’s Music Department.

artist.name: Eric Kupper
artist.profile:
Eric Kupper got his start two decades ago working as a keyboardist and guitarist for such producer/remixers as David Morales, Frankie Knuckles, Arthur Baker, Peter Rauhofer, and Richie Jones to name a few. Since 1986 he has played on, remixed, and/or produced over 1400 records for artists spanning all contemporary musical genres.
Eric also creates a great deal of music outside of the dance realm, including unashamed pop, heavy rock, punk, folk, indie rock, or whatever floats his musical boat. Artists include John Wesley Harding, Julee Cruise, Coolbone, Drill, PM Dawn, The Butterflies of Love, Saint Etienne, Bonnie Bailey, Robina, Peyton, Gaz Brookfield, McQueen, Ofra Haza, Jaymes Bullet, Laura Pausini, and Gloria Estefan.
A few years back Eric was bitten (hard!) by the DJ bug as well and became a resident at London's Pacha with Hed Kandi for four years, as well as DJing all around the globe as an independent DJ or as a Hed Kandi resident. Eric is now a resident DJ with Tokyo Disco, a new venture created by Mark Doyle, the former creator of Hed Kandi. With Tokyo Disco he presently enjoys a residency at Neighbourhood in London, Enclave in Chicago as well as numerous spots throughout the U.K. and the world.
Owns the [l=Hysteria] label and [l=Hysteria Recording] studio.

artist.name: Bill Summers
artist.profile: Percussionist born May 20, 1948 Note: Some songs with his band Summers Heat are released or filed under "Bill Summers"

artist.name: Da Lata
artist.profile: Brazilian-influenced British group formed in the early 1990s by multi instrumentalist Christian Franck and DJ Patrick Forge.

namevariations.name: B. McCarthy & Rasoul
DJ Ra Soul And B. McCarthy
DJ RaSoul & B. McCarthy
DJ Rasoul & B. Mc Carthy
DJ Rasoul + McCarthy

Rasoul + McArthy

artist.name: Joey Negro
artist.profile: Joey Negro was the main alias [a=Dave Lee] (Born 18 June 1964, Isle of Wight, England, UK) worked under from 1990 to July 2020, when he announced he was dropping it. In 1990 he was given an opportunity for his first solo release on [l608]. Believing his own name to be somewhat mundane, he decided an alias was required. Unable to think of a suitable name and under pressure to meet a deadline, he took inspiration from the artist names that were present on two records amongst a pile upon his desk. Elements from [url=/artist/4451-Pal-Joey]Pal [b]Joey[/b][/url] and [url=/artist/281120-J-Walter-Negro]J Walter [b]Negro[/b][/url] were used to form the main artist alias used on "[m79550]".

artist.name: Lisa Stansfield
artist.profile:
Born April 11, 1966 in Rochdale, Lancashire, United Kingdom. British female vocalist whose 1989 debut album 'Affection' spawned pop and club hits such as 'All Around The World' and 'People Hold On'. 1991's 'Real Love' was another success. The singer enjoyed success on the British pop charts as well as the U.S. R&B charts well into the nineties, scoring a #1 hit on the latter with 'All Woman' and continues to record and perform well beyond the millennium. The career of Stansfield starts already in 1980, in the then successful TV casting show "Search for a Star". She wins the show's contest and starts her career as singer and TV presenter in the age of 14. In her TV show "Razzmtazz", she meets other young stars, for instance the young [a721977]. After problems to stay on the ground due to this early success, she quits the TV show and founds her first band [a131788] together with schoolmates [a49715] and [a1191285]. The band did not manage to be successful and splits up in 1988. But her former bandmates continue to work as producers and songwriters in production studios. They meet [a946] in a collaboration and by chance, the UK electronic duo connects with Lisa as a feature vocalist. This leads in consequence to her successful solo career, which is accompanied by the production and songwriting activity by her two former bandmates for her solo work. Andy leaves the trio around 1993. [a49715], who is in relationship with Lisa for the last years, marries her in 1998.
She is married to schoolmate [a49715], with who she recorded her first demo tape.

artist.name: Ortin Cam
artist.profile: Electronic music producer from Hasselt, Belgium. Tom Macoye (8/4/1982) got his first taste of techno during the nineties through the "Teknoville" radioshow on Studio Brussel. Caught up in this music, at the tender age of 15 he started making beats with an old school computer.

artist.name: Futureshock
artist.profile: British electronic duo mainly active from 1998 and 2003. Not to be taken for [a53058].

artist.name: Cameo
artist.profile:
[b]Funk - electro band[/b]
Formed in 1974 in New York, USA. Originally called New York City Players, but changed to The Players after a lawsuit against them, by the [a=Ohio Players]. Has released 17 albums between 1977 and 2000. Their definitive hit is 1986's "Word Up". In the '00s, Cameo-bassist Aaron Mills started collaborating with [a=OutKast].
Current/past/side/tour-members:
Larry Ernest "Mr. B" Blackmon (1975-present) Thomas Michael Jenkins (1976-present) Nathan David Leftenant (1976-1991) (1995-'00s) Charlie L. Singleton (1981-present) Jonathan Moffett (1980s) (2000-present) Anthony A. Lockett (1978-1981) ('00s-present) Aaron Ellis Mills (1979-1981) (1986-present) Arnett G. Leftenant (1976-1981) Damon Mendes (1979) Eric R. Durham Snr. (1976-1978) Gary Dow (1976-1978) Gregory B. Johnson (1975-1982) Jeff Nelson (1992-1996) Jeryl Bright (1980-1981) John Blackwell (1996) Kenni Hairston (1986-1987) Kevin Kendrick (1982-2000) Kurt Jeter (1977) Melvin Wells (1984-1996) Michael "Calamari" Burnett (1982) (1984-1988) Stephen Moore (1980-1981) Thomas Delmar "TC" Campbell (1980-1981) Vincent Wilburn Jr. (1981) Wayne Cooper (1977-1980) Willie "Chill Factor" Morris (1986-1995) William Revis (1977)

artist.name: Peter Funk
artist.profile: Alias used by US producer/DJ Kevin Yost

artist.name: Recloose
artist.profile: American electronic music producer, DJ and musician hailing from Detroit, Michigan, US.

artist.name: Gamat 3000
artist.profile:
Gamat 3000 are Daniel Scholz and Matthias Tanzmann. For a few years they produced various electronic tracks, published under different synonyms. In Daniel's early days he got a lot of publicity for his remix for the Swiss pop duo Yello.
For the last two years Gamat 3000 have been publishing sweet, deep and sensible house music and electronic tracks on the German record labels Freude Am Tanzen, Dessous Rec., Lo-Fi Stereo and FM Recordings. "Feeling Love" on Freude Am Tanzen got a real club hit and was licensed for various compilations like "Für Dich" on Kompakt (by Tobias Thomas). Don't forget the two electronic tracks on C-Rock's "Stereography" - Sampler (Lo-Fi) and the two erotic deep house tracks on Steve Bug's "Erotic Moments In House" - Compilation (Dessous).
Style: House, Deep House, Minimal Electronic and Techno-House

artist.name: Jackmate
artist.profile: Jackmate (born 1972, ✝ may 23, 2021, aged 49) was a Stuttgart based, German DJ and producer. He was a co-founder of [l=Philpot].

artist.name: Ben Nevile
artist.profile: Artist, DJ and producer located in Montreal, Canada. Ben is also the label manager of [l=Power Trio], since 2019.

artist.name: Jon Delerious
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Calgary, Canada.

artist.name: Sunkissed
artist.profile:
Canadian-born (Owen Sound, Ontario) Peter Grove started creating music in his early teens. He played guitar for several bands (including Liberation Symphony) before trading his "traditional" instruments for the electronic variety. Armed with a plethora of synthesizers, samplers, and drum machines, and a penchant for Aphex Twin, Richie Hawtin, and Moby, he dove headfirst into the rave scene. Following a short stint with Max Rebo (later Vegasphere), Grove began developing his solo career as Sunkissed.
His wraithlike fusion of surreal chords and dark beats caught the attention Richie Hawtin (Plastikman), who duly signed a release for Definitive, the house-centric sub-label of Plus 8. Simultaneously, Grove set up his own label, Woodwork Recordings, to further expand his catalog

artist.name: Jay Tripwire
artist.profile: Canadian tech-house / deep house DJ and producer.

artist.name: The One
artist.profile: German House and Hip Hop producer. [b]Do not confuse with [a475477] aka [a3833342]![/b]

artist.name: A Forest Mighty Black
artist.profile: A Forest Mighty Black is one of the few outposts for jazzy downtempo in Germany. Fronted by producer Bernd Kunz, it gained praise for their refreshing blend of Brazilian and fusion influences with trip-hop, and appeared on several compilations from the Acid Jazz label.

artist.name: Diabolo
artist.profile: French harmonica player and composer. Born: June 29, 1962 in France.

artist.name: DJ Phantasy
artist.profile: He began as a ticket seller for Dave Lambert's Phantasy rave in the late 80s, as well as DJing on the rave scene as DJ Steve. When the owners of Phantasy were arrested he asked them if he could use their name as his DJ name. Used to work at Lucky Spin record shop.

artist.name: Flytronix
artist.profile: Drum & Bass producer.

artist.name: Chaos A.D.
artist.profile: Born : 1975 Chelmsford, Essex, United Kingdom

artist.name: SAS
artist.profile: UK Progressive House producers duo, from the early 90's.

artist.name: Kubrick
artist.profile: Dutch Techno / Trance duo.

artist.name: Leftfield
artist.profile: Originally a solo project by [a=Neil Barnes] in 1990, Leftfield quickly became a collaboration with [a=Paul Daley], one that lasted until 2002 when they split. During that time, 2 albums were released - [m=66073] in 1995 and [m=100010] in 1999. In 2010 Neil restarted the band without Paul and has toured a great deal since with many of the original other collaborators such as [a=Adam Wren], [a=Earl Sixteen] & [a=Cheshire Cat]. A third album [m=844564] was released in 2015 and a fourth [m=2890702] (in collaboration with Adam Wren) in 2022, one that was rapidly followed by the [m=3174141] remix album.

artist.name: Sine
artist.profile: Duo consists of Charles Webster & David Thompson from Nottingham (UK).

artist.name: Dr. Robotnik
artist.profile: Producer from Finland.

artist.name: Puola
artist.profile: An electronic rock band from Jyväskylä, Finland, founded in 1999.

artist.name: Tero
artist.profile: Finnish electronic music producer. He made music using an old Commodore 64 computer.

artist.name: Messiah
artist.profile:
Messiah was formed in 1988 during London's acid house craze by college friends Ali Ghani and Mark Davies. When the pair met at an Iggy Pop concert, they decided to purchase some electronic equipment and make music for fun. When musical chemistry became evident, they began to get into the English techno and rave scene. The duo's brand of techno encompasses the aggression and volume of punk and the diva vocals of house music.
They had three UK Top 40 hits - Temple of Dreams (20, 1992), I Feel Love (19, 1992) and Thunderdome (29, 1993) and were one of the two non-[a=MNO] acts to be signed to Rick Rubin's [l=WHTE LBLS] - his failed attempt to popularise dance music in the US.
Following the collapse of Rubin's label, their final releases was the single [r=715630] in 1996 and the album [r=185422] and single [m=421997], released under the name Messiah Presents Progenitor, in '97. Their sound had changed drastically and failed to gain the attention their debut had. The band split soon after.

artist.name: Dark Syndicate
artist.profile: Dutch Hardcore Techno producer.

artist.name: DJ Nex
artist.profile: Jon "J-Bass" McDonald (one of the original [a=Altern 8] dancers) gave Mark the nickname DJ Nex which he used for these releases.

artist.name: DJ Ze Mig L
artist.profile: Techno DJ & producer from Lisboa, Portugal. Contact: djzemigl@gmail.com

artist.name: Notsignal
artist.profile: German Techno project.

artist.name: Fairground People
artist.profile: Mursel Azakli, Christian Vincenzo Kruse

artist.name: Four Ears
artist.profile: Ambient, jungle and drum n bass project from Berlin, Germany.

artist.name: Putte
artist.profile: Lives in Stuttgart, Germany. Also composer of filmscores.

artist.name: Benjamin Brunn
artist.profile:
The Hamburg-based producer Benjamin Brunn has been working with electronic music since 1996. His sound signature can be described as "soothing, yielding, close to the physics of how fluid move", "with luminescent tones that can be therapeutically easy to listen to" (Resident Advisor).
Brunn's first records were released in 1999. In 2003 he started performing his music live. He has played shows in Brussels, London, Paris, Dublin, Moscow, Kyiv, Tokyo.
Between 2005 and 2015 he concentrated on house music styles, which resulted in various record releases on labels like Smallville, Third Ear, Woodwork, Arma, Wake Up!, Black Wall. In 2013 he published a multi-style album called Colour Tracks with four colour-coded vinyl records in a box matching various techno-related electronic music styles.
His ambient-related music has been released on Pudel Produkte and Kimochi.
He started self-releasing his ambient-ish music under the name Chrome Plated Diamonds in 2017. He chose cassette tapes as physical medium.

artist.name: DJ Spinna
artist.profile: DJ and producer, born and raised in Brooklyn on January 30, 1973. He began his recording career in 1994 as a member of the underground hip hop group [a=Jigmastas]. Throughout his career, he has broken down musical barriers between the worlds of hip hop, deep house, soul, and jazz working with everyone from [a=Mos Def], [a=Masta Ace], [a=Pharoahe Monch] and [a=Eminem], through [a=Eric Roberson], [a=N'dea Davenport], [a=Mary Mary], and all the way to [a=Stevie Wonder], [a=Roy Ayers], and [a=Sergio Mendes]. He has other notable side projects to his credit such as the [a=Polyrhythm Addicts], [a=The Free Radikalz], and his alter ego [a=Chopp Master Flopp]. Runs [l=Wonderwax].

artist.name: Jel Ford
artist.profile: DJ and producer based in London, UK.

artist.name: Simbad
artist.profile: French DJ and producer, based in Cape Town, South Africa.

artist.name: Pete Namlook
artist.profile:
Born on November 25, 1960 in Frankfurt. Passed away on November 8, 2012, at the age of 51 from a heart attack.
Pete Namlook was one of the most influential figures of electronic and ambient music in the 1990s. Inspired by [a=Oskar Sala], one of the pioneers of electronic music, Namlook focused on the untapped potential of analogue synthesizers, often developed or extended in his laboratory.
Freshly educated in composition from Goethe University, Pete Namlook (Peter Kuhlmann) started his career in Frankfurt playing in the electronic ensemble [a=Romantic Warrior] that was loosely part of the new age and electronic scene. They cut Romantic Warrior (Frog, 1985), Himalaya (Frog, 1986), Planet (Blue Flame, 1988).
In 1991 he began a second life as a disc jockey playing techno music under the moniker [a=Sequential].
The single True Colours changed his career and a good chunk of modern music. Kuhlmann assumed the identity of Pete Namlook, opened his own Fax label and made his first album, Silence (Fax, 1992), a collaboration with Dr Atmo (and one of the first albums released only on compact disc).
Namlook understood that producing was more important than recording and he set out to discover, encourage and promote music by ambient electronic artists. During the first year alone, his label released over 100 records and CDs. The label name refers to the "fax" as a medium for fast and cheap distribution of ideas. Its humble weekly releases are equivalent to sending a fax to a group of friends.
His double albums are containers of lengthy ambient suites reminiscent of Germany's kosmische school of the 1970s and of [a=Brian Eno]'s late 1970s "discreet" music, but also influenced by his two true love: eastern and classical music. No musical genre was a direct influence, though: Namlook has always claimed that Nature was his main teacher.

artist.name: Lance DeSardi
artist.profile: DJ, producer, and mix engineer based in London. Originally from Dallas, Texas, DeSardi relocated to San Francisco, California before he moved to London in 2012. Runs his own label ([l=Legwork]) and studio ([l=Builders Of Paradise]).

artist.name: o9
artist.profile: South Florida based electronic musician.

artist.name: Disjecta
artist.profile: Solo project of Mark Clifford of Seefeel with releases on Warp and his own Polyfusia label. Named after [i]Disjecta: Miscellaneous Writings and a Dramatic Fragment[/i] by [a=Samuel Beckett] (the Latin term "disjecta membra" means "scattered fragments").

artist.name: John Howard
artist.profile:
US West Coast DJ.
Note: For the Australian actor, go to [a=John Howard (12)].

artist.name: Matmos
artist.profile:
Matmos is an American experimental electronic duo of [a31065] and [a=Drew Daniel] active since the mid-1990s and based in Baltimore, Maryland.
The band is named after a "seething lake of evil slime" from the '68 cult sci-fi film [r=1632360]. Matmos gained prominence for its postmodernist approach and the ability to create "listenable" music from various bizarre objects. On most releases, Matmos scrupulously list all samples and sources, ranging from amplified crayfish nerve tissue, cow uterus, snails and contact microphones on human hair to life support systems, inflatable blankets, solid gold coins, and plastic riot police shield.
Daniel and Schmidt create "concept albums," taking turns to decide on the next record's topic. Matmos has explored various diverse concepts: "surgical procedures" on 2001 [i][m=17057][/i], a "Western/country" genre ('99 [i][m=17044][/i]) and "Medieval music" (2003 [i][m=17073][/i]), "various types of plastics" ([i][m=1510156][/i] in 2019), "Whirlpool washing machine" ([i][m=958262][/i] from 2016), "Ganzfeld experiment & parapsychology" ([i][m=521821][/i] in 2013).
Martin Schmidt met Drew Daniel in San Francisco in the early 1990s, and they soon began making music together, self-releasing an 8-bit mono "mixtape/demo" cassette [i][r=4314385][/i] circa 1993. Schmidt and Daniel were also members of a glitch project [a=Disc] with [a=Miguel Depedro] in 1997-98, and played in occult-avantgarde band [a=IAO Core], appearing on '98 [i][r=975281][/i] album. Between 1999 and 2008, Matmos were signed to the [l=Matador] label. The duo joined [l=Thrill Jockey Records] in October 2012 and released its 12th studio album, [i][m=2637398][/i], in May 2022. Matmos also runs [b][l=Vague Terrain][/b] private imprint and has published over dozen of collaborative albums and EPs on other prominent international labels, including [l12114], [l264], [l13047], [l552], [l130482], [l25455] and [l2507].
Matmos developed a rotating cast of guest musicians throughout the career, regularly working with [a=Lesser], [a=Jon Leidecker] ([a=Wobbly]), [a76860], [a=Keith Fullerton Whitman], [a7495395], [a=Dan Deacon], [a=Jason Willett], [a=Owen Gardner] and other members of [a=Horse Lords], [a=People Like Us], and [a=Matthew Herbert]. In 1998, an Icelandic superstar [a=Björk] invited Matmos to remix her [i][m=34355][/i] single. The duo further collaborated on her 2001 [i][m=37082][/i] album and toured internationally as core members of Bjork's live band on [i]Vespertine[/i] (August–December 2001) and [i]Greatest Hits[/i] (May–September 2003) world tours. From 2011 to 2013, Matmos played in the stage ensemble for [a290134]'s production [i]The Life and Death of Marina Abramovic[/i] with [a333056] (guitar, clarinet), [a1239196] (piano) and [a268659] (guitar, bass).
The band contributed music to a few soundtracks, including Daria Martin's "Minotaur" film and works of Berlin-based choreographer [a=Ayman Harper]. Drew and Martin also made music for hardcore gay fetish porn, with selected tracks released on 2002 [i][r=60953][/i] CD-R. They recorded a song with [a=David Grubbs] for [i][r=624804][/i], a 2012 documentary about elderly French gays and lesbians, and appeared on [i][r15990805][/i] video game soundtrack.

artist.name: Mount Florida
artist.profile:
Project from Glasgow, Scotland.
Now inactive.

artist.name: Rennie Pilgrem
artist.profile:
Rennie Pilgrem is a British electronic music producer. He is the son of the jazz trumpeter, [a=Geoff Pilgrem].
He started playing tenor saxophone in his youth immersing himself in 'black' music he formed various funk bands. In the late 80's after hearing the music coming from the Detroit scene, Rennie started to make house techno records with Rhythm Section aka Ellis Dee, Richard Thake and Nick 'Newton' Scott.
Their third release 'Comin' On Strong' was a anthem and would have charted top 40 if they'd known about 'bar codes' on records. With Ellis Dee, the crowned king of rave DJs, Rhythm Section played at pretty much every main party between '90-92'. On the same bill as The Prodigy, Moby, Altern 8, Shades Of Rhythm and Bizzare Inc.
In 1993 Rennie founded TCR Recordings as a medium for putting out his own material. His first few releases were funky house with a smattering of breaks lurking in the background. Recording now as 'Thursday Club' he had a big tune with 'Thursday Theme' which was chased by Virgin. Rennie followed this up with 'A Place Called Acid' in 1995. He also co-promoted the Friction night with [a=Adam Freeland].
Rennie has remixed for a wide range of people from Jazzy Jeff & the Fresh Prince to Freq Nasty and Arthur Baker.

artist.name: Mfon
artist.profile:
Chicago House producer.
Mfon Akpan joined the Soma stable after his buddy Gene Farris recommended that he send some tracks. He duly responded to his friend's request, and the rest is Soma history. The first release on Soma was the discofied "Ron Hardy's Resurrection" (Soma 72) which entered into the DJ Magazine Hype Chart. At the beginning of 1999 Mfon relocated from his home town of Chicago to Norway. Soma 84 "Dance of the Drunk Mantis" was his second Soma release and was another Chicago twisted disco stormer, which came with a Studio 54 remix. Mfon also produced material for Strictly Rhythm.

artist.name: DJ Jiggy
artist.profile: Jiggy was the pioneer of minimal electronic music in Portugal. Today he still is one of the most innovative djs . Since the early nineties - when his professional career got started as resident dj in Alcântara Mar and then in Kremlin ( the same mythic clubs where DJ Vibe started his career ) – to the present days, playing all over the world together with the greatest international names, he's been consolidating an unique position as producer and dj, recognized by some of the world's best artists (Carl Cox were referring to Jiggy , on JockeySlut magazine as one of the best Djs in the world ) . After winning the Dance Club Award for the “best national compilation” in 2004, he won in 2005 the same prize for “best national techno dj”. Now after several remixes, some bestsellers on Beatport like Lula-Twilo in partnership with his mate Carlos Fauvrelle,is always innovating.

artist.name: DJ Dainskin
artist.profile:
Dainskin is one of the country’s most successful house DJs and is regarded as a pioneer of the Swiss house scene. His vinyl collection consists of more than 10’000 discs. The final breakthrough came in 1997 with the mega rhythmic track „Move your Body“. He has produced this together with Steven F., Andre B. and Thomas H., as a label acted the still young High House Records, to whose co-founders DJ Dainskin belongs. „Move Your Body“ has been released on countless mix CDs and compilations, including the „39 Flavors of Tech Funk“ compilation by scene star Dave Angel. Until 2001 DJ Dainskin produced several house mix CDs on Energetic Records.
In recent years, Dainskin has withdrawn slightly from the producer business to take care of his son Alessandro born in 1996. In this phase of life you devote yourself to other professional projects.
Since 2015, he has been active in the music industry with his newly founded label d / Records. In order to carry out this demanding job in a professional manner, he completes various training courses at various schools throughout Switzerland. He is also working on a musical development in his studio, bringing new tech / house dance tracks to the market.
With new label Differenz Records and new distribution digdis.de will be set in the future on new emotions and heart blood.

For any submissions as "Bad Company UK", "BC UK", "BCUK" etc, please use this artist entry with the appropriate ANV.
Owners of labels: [l=BC Recordings], [l=BC Authorised], [l=BC Halo], [l=BC Presents...], [l=Square One].

artist.name: Sachiko M
artist.profile: From 1994 to 1997, as a member of [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/Ground+Zero+(3)]Ground Zero[/url] (which disbanded in March 1998), Sachiko M performed at festivals and concerts around the world. She played an important role in the group's chopped-up, "plunderphonic" (or "plagiaristic" sampling) sound. Since the disbanding of [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/Ground+Zero+(3)]Ground Zero[/url], Sachiko M has pursued a unique performance style which brings to the forefront the sampler's own test tones and noise, to develop nearly memory-free sampling works. Her solos consisting entirely of sine waves have attracted a great deal of attention. In addition to playing solo, she often collaborates with musicians around the world as well.

artist.profile:
[b]R&B Group[/b]
Members;
Jason Turner, Darnell Alexander, Dave Booker, Leland Allen
2nd Nature was a male R&B quartet from Seattle, Washington. The group was discovered by local producers Don and Oman Quijano in Seattle, at an audition for the TV syndicated talent show, Star Search in 1993, and helped to produce their only album, What Comes Natural, released in 1995.

artist.name: Plain Lazy
artist.profile:
Plain Lazy are Robert Webb and Ian Lloyd. The duo met at graffiti meetings in 1988 when we were both involved in a legal spraycan arts project (having previously been on the not-so-legal side of things).
"Musically, we've been working together on tracks for over six years, mostly producing laid back house tracks with a jazzy, late night feel." An album of material was recorded for Alola records but never released, although some of the tracks were featured on compilation albums, including More Space To Dance, Slo Mo 2 and Disco Connections.

artist.name: Trevor Loveys
artist.profile:
British electronic DJ, producer, and artist.
Loveys started recording in the early 1990s after being inspired listening to producers such as [a=Ron Trent], [a=Abacus], [a=Blaze], and [a=Masters At Work]. He taught himself keyboards and drum programming after buying a synth and Akai sampler and after spending a year developing his sound and production skills he released his first single, "[m=184602]" (1996) under pseudonym [a=Soul Motive] on the [l=Nuphonic] label. This release caught the attention of producers and music press alike, gaining respect from [a=Deep Dish], [a=Kevin Mckay], and [a=Everything But The Girl]'s [a=Ben Watt].
[a=House Of 909] is the production outfit Loveys is best known for, and it was on the eponymous [l=House Of 909] label that he released all his early productions. Loveys' music caught the attention of [a=Richard Breeden], manager at the legendary [l=TRIBAL United Kingdom] label, and then the UK arm of [l=Pagan], and it was this label that gave Loveys the chance to record his first album "[m=144469]" (1997). The album put Loveys on the deep house map, and it attained album of the month in publications [l=Muzik Magazine] and [l=DJ Magazine], not to mention features in the [url=https://www.discogs.com/label/15335-New-Musical-Express]NME[/url], [l=Music Week], and [l=i-D Magazine].
In 1998, after having some personal and creative differences with his partner [a=Nigel Casey], Loveys decided to go solo and started producing for his friend [a=Omid Nourizadeh] (aka [a=16B])'s label [l=Alola]. He then started to collaborate with his bass playing friend [a=Chris Pedley] to create the album "[m=11157]" (1999), which crossed the boundaries from deep house and electro, to broken beat and downtempo – being made album of the month in magazine [url=https://www.discogs.com/label/4162-Ministry-Magazine]Ministry[/url].
Loveys continues to develop his sound, and working on projects for various labels including [l=Ephemoral Recordings], [l=Loungin' Recordings], as well as the [l=Freerange Records], along with his [a=Soul Sides] project that he is working on with producer [a=Solid Groove].
Loveys also runs a monthly "Soul Sides" club night in the Consortium Club, Bournemouth, UK as well as with guesting at [l=Freerange Records] and [l=Front Room Recordings] club nights across London, UK.

artist.name: Lil Louis And The Diamond Corp.
DJ Pierre
artist.profile: DJ, producer, club owner and promoter from Chicago, Illinois. Pioneered the acid house sound with [a=Phuture] in the mid-to-late 1980s. Signed to [l=Jive] in the late 1980s by former [l=Trax Records] A&R [a=Wayne Williams]. Moved to New York in 1990 where he created the looped-up, layered 'wild pitch' style inspired by the parties thrown by DJs [a=Greg Daye] and [a=Bobby Konders]. A&R at [l=Strictly Rhythm] from February 1992 to April 1994. Founded [l=DJP Records] in 1999 and Afro Acid Muzik ([l=Afro Acid Plastik]) in April 2007. Moved to Atlanta, Georgia in 2008 and currently based in Duluth. Brother of Reuben Jones ([a=R. Jones (5)]) aka [a=King Maurice].

artist.profile: Born in Hitchin (UK)

artist.name: 2 Bad Mice
artist.profile:
Formed in 1991, 2 Bad Mice (the name originating from The Tale of 2 Bad Mice by Beatrix Potter) are widely credited as among the first U.K. hardcore acts to begin heavily incorporating breakbeats into the style. Consisting of members Sean O'Keeffe, Simon Colebrooke and producer Rob Playford (owner of Moving Shadow records), 2 Bad Mice were a staple on the early-to mid '90s hardcore scene and were instrumental in the music's steady mutation into jungle/drum'n'bass. The group's influential approach to sampled beats -- cutting them up into shards of rhythm and rearranging them in novel combinations (first appearing in mature form on the 1992 hit "Bombscare") is generally considered the blueprint from which jungle's characteristic manipulation of breaks was assembled.
Although 2 Bad Mice for the most part stopped producing as the hardcore scene began to wane (Playford focusing his energy on Moving Shadow, now one of the most influential jungle labels), a best-of compilation of the group's work appeared on the American Sm:)e Communications label in 1995. These days Playford continues to work in production, while O'Keeffe, Colebrooke (and Paul Rhodes) currently DJ worldwide as 2 Bad Mice.

artist.name: Delerium
artist.profile: Side-project of [a=Front Line Assembly] members Bill Leeb and Rhys Fulber, formed 1986. Originally releasing voiceless industrial soundscapes with militant drumming, Delerium gained wide recognition with a shift towards ethnic electronica and New Age influences in a similar manner to [a=Enigma] (of "Sadeness" fame) but with less Gregorian chanting. This period created their chart-topping single "Silence" (feat. [a=Sarah McLachlan]), which garnered significant attention after being remixed by various trance acts. Delerium have operated mainly as an electro pop band since then, featuring many famous female and male vocalists.

artist.name: Dr. Tommy
artist.profile: Japanese singer, songwriter, producer and DJ.

artist.name: Younghead
artist.profile:
DJ & producer from London.
Resident DJ at Roast and also worked in the Lucky Spin & Section 5 record shops.

artist.name: Fizzarum
artist.realname: Dmitry Dubov (Дмитрий Дубов) & Albert Rasyulis (Альберт Расюлис)

artist.name: Solid State
artist.profile:
By late 1994 Dextrous hooked up with 2 other guys, Paul and Jon who then went by the names of 'Phaze 3' and 'Klass A' respectively, that he had met whilst DJing on the London pirate station 'Kool FM'. They all shared the same artistic ideas and the frustrations of the way in which the scene was over looking a more musical approach in the production of Drum'n'Bass. So Dextrous decided to start 'State of the Art Recordings', as a vehicle to put out music that wasn't confined to what the so-called top club DJs would play, but to allow a little more freedom of expression and with that reach a whole new audience. The 3 of them together recorded under the name 'Solid State' and christened their sound 'Urban Fusion' which encompassed many elements i.e. Funk, Jazz, Techno, Dub as well as Drum'n'Bass.
Well as they say "all good things come to an end!" and by the summer of 2000 the 3 guys parted company. Their last project together was the album entitled "Point Of No Return" on the 'Renegade Recordings' label.

artist.name: Paradox
artist.profile:
The musical career of Dev Pandya dates back to 1990 when Mixrace was formed with partner DJ Trax. From their first releases on leading hardcore label Moving Shadow, the pair went on to set up and record for their own labels before Dev’s two alter-ego guises emerged in 1996. Exploding across the scene with a string of 12" releases on Renegade Hardware, Total Science’s CIA, Certificate 18, and Reinforced Records, Alaska/Paradox soon became a name synonymous with abstract atmospherics and intricate breaks. His Alaska alias appeared at the same time yet focused on more ambient productions, releasing on labels such as Good Looking, Renegade Recordings, Vibez, Blame’s 720 Degrees, and Tayla’s Nexus label. Pioneering a style that channels the 70’s funk of breakbeats, Paradox has always focused on the raw elements of drum and bass within his music. Appealing to an audience beyond the confines of a club environment, his production credits include work for ESPN, Konami Arcade platforms and Sony Playstation games in the USA and Japan. Often choosing to position himself as an outsider, Paradox’s success has never been limited to that of the UK-orientated drum and bass scene. He has collaborated with some of greatest contemporary musicians the world has to offer, including Herbie Hancock, Shobha Gurtu, Toshinori Kondo and Bernie Worrell.
2011 saw Dev release the Ramifications LP (astonishingly Dev’s 9th vinyl studio album to date) and the follow up to 2002’s Reinforced Records What They Don’t Know LP. Like all Paradox albums the packaging was stunning, featuring triple-marble vinyl plus a solid-black disk for music lovers and record collectors looking for something extra special.
As a bonus, the solid-black disk version included extra tracks and more from the infamous Sample Me series - specially edited breakbeat samples for aspiring producers to loop and splice – and shows Paradox’s commitment to the grass roots of the scene, giving something back to the fans and those he’s inspired. When he’s not in the studio, Paradox is inevitably on tour and continues to entertain crowds with his live shows truly worldwide. Armed only with his trusty Amiga, synths and a mic, he performs a truly live PA, reconstructing tracks from his back catalogue and editing beats on the fly. He has performed over 200 live PAs covering Australia, USA, Asia, Europe and beyond, and continues to venture into unchartered territory to represent his sound. Into 2013 and Dev Paradox is releasing 12”s at a fast rate. Singles on Arctic, Paradox and Samurai Red Seal have all surfaced and the 150th landmark 12” is on the horizon. Work has also begun on the 10th studio artist album confirming Paradox as the most consistent drum programmer over the last 23 years.

artist.name: Moss
artist.profile: Moss is Mancunian flute and sax player Bernard Moss. He worked with a lot of bands in the Manchester area like: Kalima, Swamp Children, Sub Sub, A Certain Ratio and Mark Brydon's Cloud Nine. He has been a contributor to the Pork sty for many years (Moss moved to the Hull area for a couple of years), working on albums by Fila Brazillia, Bullitnuts, Solid Doctor, Leggo Beast and Baby Mammoth. He released his first solo material in 1998, with the Bullitnuts produced 'Fly Ball EP'. Three solo albums have been released on Pork Recordings so far.

artist.name: Odyssey
artist.profile: UK Drum 'n Bass artist. [b]For the 70s and 80s New York soul/R&B trio, please use [a=Odyssey (2)].[/b]

artist.name: Pariah
artist.profile: Drum n Bass producer group from UK active since 1997.

artist.name: Carsten Jost
artist.profile: Co-Founder of [l=Dial] Records

artist.name: Jacek Sienkiewicz
artist.profile:
Polish DJ and producer born in Warsaw in 1976. He started playing as a DJ in 1995. He is the founder of [l=Recognition] in 2000.
Jacek Sienkiewicz (b. 1976) is considered to be one of the pioneers of current Polish electronic music. As a teenager, he started DJ’ing at the first wave of underground parties in early 90s Warsaw, playing mostly ambient music. Soon after, he decided to try his hand at proper music production. His live debut took place in 1996 at the Centre for Contemporary Arts as an accompaniment to a video performance. Three years later, his first self-released album appeared which was received enthusiastically worldwide, giving impetus to the idea of starting a record label Recognition. Since then, Sienkiewicz has released more than 40 vinyl EPs, either for his own imprint, or for other respected labels such as Klang Elektronik, Trapez, WMF, Smallville. Between 2002 and 2012 he collaborated with Frankfurt’s Cocoon Records, releasing four albums and several EPs for this then-important label. His records were played and strongly supported by renowned artists such as Sven Väth and Richie Hawtin; legendary producers like Ricardo Villalobos, Uwe Schmidt and recently, Max Loderbauer collaborated with Jacek during last decade. He has played all over the world, from big festivals like DEMF Detroit, Mutek Montreal, Transmediale Berlin to small, intimate gigs in underground clubs and bars. There is no doubt - Sienkiewicz knows all of his Detroit Techno/Chicago Acid House musical roots yet he creates signature sound that results from his highly original approach to modern electronic music production. Keeping far from current trends and fashions, he definitely chose the hard path to follow, fusing concrete beats with his organic and highly detailed soundscapes of melancholy. Apart from his brand new 5th album Drifting planned to be released on Recognition in June ’15, he is presently preoccupied with a search for new means of expression. Some of his forthcoming works were clearly created in more experimental and open-ended vein (he remains a fan of Coil, Autechre and vintage Polish Radio Experimental Studio sound). Through numerous events organised in his native Poland, he continues to promote quality music of all stripes, inviting high-valued international artists to play and collaborate, while a string of releases on Recognition by up-and-coming Polish talents is a substantial proof that a scene he co-created is developing into a right direction.

artist.name: Jasper Street Co.
artist.profile: Gospel-oriented house music group. The group took its name from the address of the [a279580]' recording studio in Baltimore, 510 Jasper Street. The singers have changed throughout the years with [a=Vonita White] and [a=Thomas "Fruity" Roberts, Jr.] having been with the group the longest.

artist.name: Ilios
artist.profile: Previously based in Barcelona, currently based in Athens. Runs the [l=Antifrost] label.

artist.name: Thurston Moore
artist.profile:
American guitarist and vocalist born on 25 July 1958 in Coral Gables, Florida and raised in Bethel, Connecticut. He lived in New York City for most of his music career and was taught guitar by [a=Glenn Branca]. He has since relocated to Stoke Newington, in the United Kingdom.
Thurston Moore is best known as the guitarist and vocalist of [a=Sonic Youth] which he co-founded in 1981. In 1984 he married [a=Sonic Youth] bassist [a=Kim Gordon]. The couple separated in 2011 and divorced in 2013.
In addition to performing music, he also runs the [l=Ecstatic Peace!] label and [l=The Daydream Library Series].

artist.name: DJ Kane
artist.profile:
DJ/producer from Kilburn, London.
Pioneering Jungle/Drum & Bass Producer DJ who began on the Radio with a show on Don FM in 1992 before joining Kool FM also in 1992 where held a legendary show until 2017. Also starting Producing in 1992 he went on to be the first signing to Trouble On Vinyl and had an early couple of releases on No U Turn. His sister is vocalist [a=Vannessa Simon].

Ghetto house DJ/producer on the Dance Mania roster
After numerous releases and mixtapes, Milton moved from Chicago to Cedar Rapids, Iowa in the late 1990s. (This is referenced in the title of the 1998 release [url=http://www.discogs.com/DJ-Milton-Chi-2-Da-I/master/717973]"Chi To Da I"[/url].) He released [url=http://www.discogs.com/DJ-Milton-Get-That-Cash/master/300492]"Get That Cash"[/url] while in Iowa as well.

artist.name: Playground
artist.profile: Rick and Rob made their records secretly because Rick already signed up an exclusive record contract during that time elsewhere

artist.name: DJ Duke
artist.profile: DJ, producer and label owner based in Los Angeles, California. Born in Denmark in the late 1960s, Duke (FKA [a=Ken Larsen]) competed in [l=DMC]'s World DJ Mixing Championship in London in March 1987. His first release, Genius At Work, created on reel-to-reel at [l=SweMix Studios] in 1986, was released on [r=308199] following his move to New York in 1987. Duke founded his own house label [l=Power Music Records] in 1990 and had chart success with [m=102197] in 1993. His related company [l=Power Music Distribution, Inc.] was registered in 1994. Relocated to Los Angeles in 1999 where he started [l=Earth, Moon & Sun] in 1999 and [l=Superbad Music]. in 2008.

artist.name: Richard Harvey
artist.profile:
Richard Harvey is a pseudonym used on [l=User] / [l=00A] Releases.
[b] For the classical multi-instrumentalist and composer please use [a=Richard Harvey (2)]. [/b]

artist.name: Gemini
artist.profile: As one of Chicago’s most mysterious and revered characters, the story of Gemini, aka Spencer Kincy (born June 6, 1969) has become something of a myth in recent years – blazing a trail throughout the 90’s, prolifically releasing over 200 tracks from 1994 to 1999, Spencer’s music had shades innovation and soul few of his peers could match. Then, suddenly, at the peak of his career, he disappeared. Rumours and gossip of Spencer’s whereabouts and situation have been rife in the music press, and rarely on the money. Spencer is alive and well, and wants his music to be heard.

artist.name: Glenn Wilson
artist.profile: Swedish techno producer Glenn Wilson has been at the forefront of techno since 1992 when he opened his own record shop and founded his Planet Rhythm label to promote Swedish artists. Glenn also founded the Compound & Template labels.

artist.name: Joe Lewis
artist.profile: Joe Lewis is the president, C.E.O., and founder of Target Records since 1986.

artist.name: Chuck Cogan
artist.profile: Swedish musician.

artist.profile: vocalist, musician, composer, dj, producer and label founder/ owner of [l=Origu] and [l=Our Label Records], based in Germany

artist.profile: CVO stands for "Chicago's Very Own"

artist.name: Mike Clark
artist.profile: A native of Detroit. DJ and producer true to his craft, founder of [l=Strictly Beatdown] and founding father of the Beatdown sound. He has worked with major dance labels such as [l=Underground Resistance], [l=Night Groove Records], [l=Happy], [l=Simply Soul], [l=Planet E], [l=Gotsoul Records] and [l=Third Ear Recordings].

artist.name: Spirit
artist.profile:
Prolific British drum & bass producer, who sadly passed away on 26th August 2018. Often collaborated with [a=Digital]. Owned/managed the [url=https://www.discogs.com/label/7597-Inneractive-Music]Inneractive Music[/url] label.
[b]NOTE: For the 1960s prog-rock band, please use [url=https://www.discogs.com/artist/270578-Spirit-8]Spirit (8)[/url][/b].

artist.name: Toni Aalto
artist.profile: Helsinki, Finland

artist.name: Torstein Stretch
artist.profile: Producer from Copenhagen, Denmark

artist.name: JB
artist.profile: JB first started out as a Rap DJ in the 80's when he teamed up with rapper Mr Senior and formed a small group called Heist. Rap music was very much the influence in JB's earlier career scratching and deck to deck chopping 2 of the same instrumental tracks while the MC rapped over it. Then JB started building his studio while studying a sound engineering course at Sandwell College. This led to him getting into the Rave/Hardcore scene as a lot of his college friends were in to this music. "This music really caught me because of the technology being used to make these tracks" JB hungry for production, then got to meet and release tracks on [l=Fokus Recordings] in London with [a=Tim Taylor] & [a=DJ Taktix], JB started piercing his way into this amazing and ever moving futuristic scene. JB opened his own record shop and together launched his own record label of the same name, Back 2 Basics Recordings was born. B2B only in it's very early days was already making a huge impact on the scene, JB collaborating with DJ Taktix had produced one of B2B's first classics "The Way". This classic pushed the label into full recognition, with every DJ lucky enough to get it on dub plate playing it, and all the rest hungry for it.

artist.name: Mampi Swift
artist.profile:
Starting off his career as a DJ in the early nineties, DJing his first ever set on New Years Eve 1992 at Innersense at the Lazerdrome. He got the gig as his friend's father was the head of security at the venue. 1992 saw him also play on Kool FM for the first time, guesting on The Weedkillers show. He would later on take over his childhood friend, DJ Buz's show.
Swift Landed a Job at the now defunct Lucky Spin record shop. This was very helpful, as full access to the studio next door, with in house engineer Pete Parsons (aka Voyager) was part of the package. This became a valued learning ground for the inspired artist. After a few releases on various well known labels, his hunger grew and he set up his own labels [l=Charge] & [l=Blade].
Also had a show on Kool FM, and it was after one of his shows that he acquired the "Mampi" part of his name after MC Navigator, Crazy Legs & MC Foxy started calling him it.

artist.name: Renegade
artist.profile: Drum n Bass artist from the UK.

artist.name: Twisted Individual
artist.profile: Drum & Bass DJ & producer born in Croydon, UK. Contact: twistedind@hotmail.com

artist.name: Chris Fortier
artist.profile: American Trance / Progressive Trance DJ & producer known as a precursor to the electronic dance music movement in the USA. Most famous for being one-half of trance production duo [a=Fade] with [a=Neil Kolo], with whom he subsequently co-founded eponymous record label [l=Fade Records] in 1994.

artist.name: Neil Kolo
artist.profile: Producer - Sound Engineer & Editor and Designer from US. Born in 1969.

artist.name: Gant Garrard
artist.profile: American DJ/producer and emcee from Chicago, born 1979.

artist.name: Craig Alexander
artist.profile: Craig Alexander has been a staple in Chicago House Music for 30 years. His love for the music began in the early 80s when he attended The Muzic Box where he heard the Legendary Ron Hardy play for the first time. Listening to Ron helped him develop a style all his own that has rocked dance floors all over the world. Initially starting out as a promoter doing parties at the Legendary AKA's with Ron Hardy he quickly began manning the decks as a opener for his parties as more people caught his sets he began to get offered headlining sets. He's played alongside everyone from Glenn Underground, Paul Johnson & Dj Rush to Lil Louis and Thomas Bangalter(Daft Punk).His musical journey has taken him to several places including France, Switzerland, & Germany to name a few. He is relaunching Oblique Records Digital in 2012. For Bookings: obliquerecords@gmail.com Contact:www.facebook.com/djcraigalexander or www.facebook.com/craigalexanderII

artist.name: Mandrake
artist.profile:
One of many aliases for techno artist Willie Griffin.
For the Brazilian percussionist use [a83734] (with ANV).

artist.profile: DJ and producer from Chicago.

artist.name: Lester Fitzpatrick
artist.profile: Techno producer from Chicago.

artist.name: Gene Hunt
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Chicago.

artist.name: Robert Owens
artist.profile: House music vocalist, DJ, producer and label owner. Born in Ohio in 1961, Owens worked extensively with [a=Larry Heard] in the mid-1980s and formed [a=Fingers Inc.] with [a=Ron Wilson]. One of his best-known features, Tears, was with [a=Frankie Knuckles] and [a=Satoshi Tomiie]. His debut solo album, Rhythms In Me, was released in 1990. Runs [l=Musical Directions].

artist.profile: Argentinian techno producer based in Munich, Germany
