artist.name: Roots Manuva
artist.profile: British rapper from Stockwell, South London, born 9 September 1972, London, England.

artist.name: East Flatbush Project
artist.profile: East Flatbush Project is the brainchild of Spencer Bellamy, who was originally a DJ for rap pioneer Howie Tee. The project was developed to feature local Brooklyn rappers, and the debut single, "Can't Hold It Back," was released in 1994. But it was the next single, "Tried by 12," that took the hip-hop community by storm, as well as the electronic world. Ninja Tune and Chocolate Industries licensed the tune and released an EP featuring remixes by Squarepusher, Autechre, Funkstörung, and Herbaliser. Bellmany released three more singles -- "Ruste Juxx" (featuring Ruste Juxx of Boot Camp Clik), "Everything We Spit Is Hard," and "Head to Head" -- but never equaled the success of "Tried by 12."

artist.name: Sluta Leta
artist.profile:
Sluta Leta was formed by Bengt Liljstad and Jonas Bergkvist in the small town of Umeå in northern Sweden in 1994, with the name (which is Swedish for "Stop Searching") taken from the scooter rental shop that they ran during the summer months on the Greek island of Corfu. It was in Corfu that had a chance meeting with the people behind the Mego label, and common interests and a lasting friendship was fused, which led to their debut 12" release for Mego in November 1995, followed by releases on Cheap Records, Chocolate Industries and Uptight.
The band then went on hiatus for a few years, during which there were several line-up changes. Over that time, all of the original members - Bengt Liljstad, Jonas Bergkvist and later Yngwie Moskowich & Anders Rydberg - left the band and were replaced by the current line-up of Andi Pieper, Ramon Bauer & Gerhard Potuznik.

artist.name: Rec Center
artist.profile: Rec Center is a Hip Hop group formed in Elgin, IL (just outside of Chicago) in the mid 90's. The group originally consisted of 9 MC's - Bad Luck, G-NO, Prophet, Ikon, Reality, Maintain, Naugh-T, Mozes Gunn, Xclusive, and 1 DJ / Producer - DJ Emmaculate. The first single from the group was released under the name Monogenesis, which included Bad Luck and G-NO. They went on to release another 12" single and then a full length album titled "Lonely People" in 1999. The group disbanded in the early 2000's, but various members reunited over the years to create and release new music. The album Lonely People has been regarded as an underground classic of Chicago Indie Hip Hop. In 2022, Berlin based label, HHV, reissued Lonely People on limited edition double vinyl, CD, and Cassette.

artist.name: EL-P
artist.profile: Born March 4th, 1975 in Brooklyn, New York. EL-P, aka El Producto, is one of hip-hop's most obstinate and adventurous pioneers, combining a mid-'80s lo-fi old school aesthetic with a progressive rock musician's inclination to push boundaries. He has never succumbed to the whims of corporate hip-hop, instead choosing to pursue his own decidedly uncommercial leanings. In the mid-'90s, he developed a strong reputation with the groundbreaking trio [a12470], a band whose achievements include the first LP, [m=51754], on [l1216], a label that is considered by many the best label for intelligent hip-hop. Over the group's auspicious stint together, he proved he was himself capable of intense lyricism and sonic production so powerful it could stand on its own. In the latter part of the '90s, El-P was also a collaborator with [a3270], [a5648], and [a24205]. In 2001, after releasing one last album with Harlem rappers [a28077], the group chose to amicably pursue their own directions. El-P has started his own label, [l23482], and was also selected to work on former [a12212] frontman [a64348]'s first solo album. Somehow he found the time to work on his own solo release during all of this, [m=113798], which saw the light of day in May of 2002.

artist.name: Melf
artist.profile: Located in minneapolis, MN.

artist.name: Benjamin
artist.profile: French singer/song-writer. Active in the mid-60s.

artist.name: Ilkae
artist.profile: ilkae is a catch-all term for a variety of music written by either [a=Aaron Munson] (Canada) or [a=Krystian Lubiszewski] (Poland) (or both!) since 1999. Munson has been the sole active member of the group since 2004. Following the closure of Merck records in 2007, Munson's work has largely been published by [l=Eerik Inpuj Sound]. Continuing to release as ilkae, Munson has made physical media releases on [l=The Irrational Media Society], [l=Field Hymns] and [l=Onibaba records].

artist.name: Oblique
artist.profile: Oblique from Barcelona Spain are: Juan Gonzalez, Sonja Oblique, Carlos Garcia Bayona. Synthwave and Synthpop are the sounds of the Oblique's universe, inspired by 80's soundtracks. Analog synths and programming with the voice of Sonja create this project.

artist.name: Soundstate
artist.profile: Ambient producer

artist.name: Eu
artist.profile: Experimental noise/industrial project from southern Brazil, dedicated on the recording of cassette tapes based on obscure matters.

artist.name: Kustota
artist.profile: idm / experimental

artist.name: Ambidextrous
artist.profile:
[a=Nick Zavriev], better known by the name Ambidextrous, can be easily considered a veteran of Russian electronic music, having had almost half a dozen releases (including solo and group work) and participated in numerous club and festival gigs. At the start of his career, Nick preferred making IDM, appearing on various European labels, but lately has been into experimenting with dance music. Possessing encyclopedic electronic knowledge, he is able to combine the style and sound of several epochs.
Ambidextrous (from Latin: "ambi" - two, both; "dextrous" - right) means "a person who can use his both hands". In accordance to his name, Nick manages to do several activities at once: give lectures to students, write for musical journals, promote indie music with friends and colleagues from the promo group Avant, organize parties with Rave Diggers formation and have three more music projects at his side: ethno-trip-hop band [a=Jermook], psychodelic downtempo band [a=Dream Mechanics] and guitar ambient band [a=Light Under Water].

artist.name: SCSI-9
artist.profile: Electro/techno duo from Russia, comprised of Anton Kubikov & Maxim Milutenko. Formerly started as a solo project by Anton in 1990s, later joined by Maxim in 2001.

artist.name: The Fall
artist.profile:
Post-punk band from Greater Manchester, UK. 1976 to 2018.
Mark E. Smith was the lead singer, lyricist, frontman and only constant member of the formation. He died on the morning of Wednesday January 24th, 2018.
Formed in 1976 through a poetry-style collective beginnings, The Fall released around 50 singles, 34 studio albums, and well over 50 live and compilation albums as well as releasing a play 'Hey Luciani' and a Ballet 'I am Kurious Oranj'. They also recorded 24 sessions for John Peel's Radio 1 show between June 1978 and August 2004.
The band went through numerous personnel changes over the years (with well over 30 different line-ups). From July 2007 The Fall comprised: Mark E. Smith, vocals; Pete Greenway, guitar; Dave "The Eagle" Spurr, bass; Elena Poulou, keyboards; Keiron Melling, drums. For a year (2014-15), Daren Garratt became a full-time member as a second drummer. Poulou's (Smith's then wife) last gig was 28th April 2016. Mike Clapham joined on keyboards from 23rd May 2017 until their final gig.

artist.name: Martian Tin Can
artist.realname: Colin Laird, Matt Smith, Mark Pilkington
artist.profile: Martian Tin Can (Colin Laird, Mark Pilkington, Matt Smith) are a production trio who have previously recorded for SKAM and Guidance and have also self-released works using various pseudonyms. Their latest release is MTC LP1 via Bandcamp.

artist.name: Yunx
artist.profile: Manchester electronic duo Iain Law and Darren Taberner producing under different names, but primarily focussed on Yunx.

artist.name: Mark Franklin
artist.profile:
DJ and producer based in West London, UK, Original From Leeds, Uk,
He started DJ'ing in Leeds when he was 15. After two years of producing music his first EP was released in 1992.

artist.name: B12
artist.profile: [a=Michael Golding] and [a=Steve Rutter] have provided the UK's intelligent techno and early electronica scene with both original material and also a homebase for productions from [a=Stasis] to [a=As One] on the [l=B12] Records imprint. After [r=30723] was issued on Warp in 1998, the duo retracted from the public eye until 2007, with a bevy of new releases.

artist.name: Beaumont Hannant
artist.profile:
An eclectic producer who released four LPs during 1993-94 but then all but retired his solo work for a place in the trip-hop duo, [a=Outcast]. Beaumont Hannant began DJing in 1986 after attending several hip-hop mixing competitions.
During the late '80s he was heavily into electro, techno and hip-hop as well as indie-rock (he even managed fellow natives of York [a=Shed Seven] for a while). Hannant began working on production in 1993 with the EP "Tastes and Textures Vol.1" for GPR Records. One track, "Awakening the Soul", appeared on the Positiva Ambient Collection and Hannant released his debut album "Basic Data Manipulation" (Tastes and Textures Vol.2) that same year.
In 1994 Hannant recorded three full-lengths (Texturology, Bitter Sweet, Sculptured) for GPR, each possessing a variety of styles. By 1995, however, Hannant had begun to diversify his talents, providing remixes for [a=Autechre], [a=Björk] and [a=Ned's Atomic Dustbin], producing indie-folkie [a=Lida Husik] and founding the group [a=Outcast] with his engineer [a=Richard Brown].
The duo signed to One Little Indian and released their debut album "Out Of Tune" in 1996. Hannant appeared to have returned to his solo guise as well at the end of the 1996 with the release of a GPR double-pack EP titled Notions of Tonality.

artist.name: Richard H. Kirk
artist.profile:
Richard H. Kirk (born 21 March 1956, Sheffield, England - died 21 September 2021) was an English musician who specialised in electronic music. He was a member of the industrial music band [a=Cabaret Voltaire], formed in 1973. He released his first solo album "[m=66247]" in 1980, and developed a solo career parallel to the band until its dissolution in 1994. His music featured a fusion of diverse musical genres from dance-orientated Techno and House to Experimental Electronica and Ambient.
Kirk resurrected the Cabaret Voltaire name in 2009 as the sole member.

artist.name: Seefeel
artist.profile: British electronic / shoegaze / post-rock group formed in the early 1990s. Starting out as a quartet on Too Pure, they are currently signed to Warp Records.

artist.name: D'Arcangelo
artist.profile: Italian duo.

artist.name: Pluxus
artist.profile:
Band from Stockholm, Sweden that plays mostly instrumental electronic music where rhythm and melodies play a big role. The band consists of Sebastian Tesch, Adam Kammerland and Anders Ekert.
Until 2003, Björn Carlberg was also a member. Pluxus is now releasing its material on its own record company Pluxemburg.

artist.name: Ovni
artist.profile:
OVNI are a three-piece "folktop" ensemble whose unique listening electronics primarily spanned the period 1997-2005.
Hailing from Boulder & Denver, Colorado, and Boston, Massachusetts, the artists were also instrumental as the collaborative trio behind Techno iRadio webcaster RadioValve.com in the mid-1990s.
Part experimental downtempo, part electronic noise, the group took influences from the Industrial and Ambient/IDM sub-genres, with live VJ synchronization a component as important to their work as the audio production. Their largely underground productions also gained a brief audience through Addictive TV’s UK series.
In 2002 the group were honored as artists-in-residence at the Morris Graves Foundation as part of their North American West Coast “Future of Kitchen Design” tour.

artist.name: Tom Churchill
artist.profile:
Tom was born in Cardiff, Wales, in 1978 and is currently based just outside Glasgow, Scotland. He ran the Headspace Recordings label, founded in 1997 with partner Raeph Powell, together with its sister label Emoticon, until 2006.
As well as releasing several of his own productions on Headspace and Emoticon, he produced a number of deep house EPs for labels such as DiY, Nordic Trax and Deep Departures in the early 2000s, and held DJ residencies at both Radius in Cardiff and Traxx in Glasgow.
After taking a sabbatical from production for several years he is currently working on new material, both solo and as part of The Nuclear Family.

artist.name: I'm Sore With Shift-option And Beck Uk
Max
artist.profile: Performed "Jazzy Rhymes" on [r1714] in the year 2000.

artist.name: Les Elles
artist.profile: French band from Caen, Normandy, founded in 1992 by [a1651567] (voice, text and music) with Sophie Henry (piano, accordion, harmonization), Sarah Auvray (choir, sound effect) and Christine Lapouze (cello).

artist.name: Sybarite
artist.profile: After touring and recording with the Silver Apples for the better part of the mid-'90s, Xian Hawkins began writing and recording music on his own. A brilliant multi-instrumentalist and songwriter, Hawkins donned the Sybarite moniker and has been releasing captivating records ever since. Resting comfortably between down-tempo electronic and modern chamber ensemble, Sybarite's compositions are wonderfully sophisticated, yet elegant and catchy enough to make even the most wooden legs sway. Ladies be warned: this will go straight to your hips!

artist.name: Amorphous Androgynous
artist.profile:
One of the Future Sound Of London's most well-known aliases. Used for their early-1990's album [r=1719] and their more recent releases [r=44376] (2002), [r=525492] (2005) and [r=1394575] (2008). The later psychedelic releases generally add a 'The' to the name to differentiate from the '90s electronic material.
The liner notes for FSOL's album [r=2512] make mention of an Amorphous Androgynous album with a plain black cover called [r=1145416], which after 14 years of mystery was finally released under the FSOL name.

artist.name: Jocelyn Brown
artist.profile:
American vocalist, lyricist, and artist.
Born: 25 November 1950 in Kinston, North Carolina, USA.
Brown has been a versatile vocalist to this day, singing on many R&B, Disco, Pop, and House productions.
Her aunt [a=Barbara Roy] inspired Jocelyn to spread her musical wings. She began her career in the 1970s as a studio singer for many groups and artists like [a=Musique], [a=Inner Life], [a=Bad Girls], [a=The Salsoul Orchestra], [a=Disco Tex & His Sex-O-Lettes], [a=Van McCoy], [a=Evelyn King], [a=Gloria Gaynor], [a=Phyllis Hyman], [a=Vicki Sue Robinson], [a=Dazzle], [a=Revanche], [a=Melba Moore], [a=Roberta Flack], [a=Cerrone], [a=Chic] and [a=Change].
In 1984, she started her solo career gaining great success with the single "Somebody Else's Guy", which reached #2 on the Billboard R&B Singles chart (#75 on the Hot 100). In 1985-87 she worked with [a=Culture Club] and toured with them. In 1990, some of her records started to be sampled by House and Rap producers; among them [a=Snap!] (who sampled her vocals from "Love's Gonna Get You").
Since then, her contribution to the world of dance music has been well recognised, working with countless artists of the genre, such as [a=Todd Terry], [a=Martha Wash], [a=Kym Mazelle], [a=Jamestown], [a=Cassius], [a=Right Said Fred], [a=DJ BoBo] and many, many others.
Niece of singer [a=Barbara Roy].

artist.name: Roy Ayers
artist.profile: American funk, soul and jazz vibraphone player, composer and producer. Born September 10, 1940 in Los Angeles, California. Died March 4, 2025 in New York. Ayers began his career as a post-bop jazz artist, releasing several albums with [l681], before his tenure at [l1610] beginning in the 1970s, during which he helped pioneer jazz-funk. Among his hits are Everybody Loves The Sunshine, Searching and Running Away, as well as collaborations with [a=Fela Kuti]. Founded his own label [l12536] in 1980. Father of [a747956] and [a550856].

artist.name: George Benson
artist.profile: American guitarist, vocalist, composer. Born: 22 March 1943, Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, U.S.A.

artist.name: Phoenix
artist.profile:
French electronic pop band from Versailles (Yvelines, West suburb of Paris) formed in 1997.
Thomas Croquet (aka [a=Thomas Mars], vocals), Frederic Moulin (aka [a=Deck D'Arcy], bass), and Christian Mazzalai (guitars) were the original trio who would become known as Phoenix. Mazzalai's older brother Laurent Mazzalai (aka [a550175] aka Branco) joined the trio when his band broke up in 1995 (see [a=Darlin']), and two years later they called themselves Phoenix. At this point they were still making rock music without the synth-rock sounds, and released a single on their own label [l=Ghettoblaster]. Shortly there-after, they were signed to the Paris-based [l=Source]. While there, they got aquainted with label-mates [a=AIR], and were the backing band for a number of TV appearances. From this connection, Phoenix created their first French house track in 1999, "Heatwave." This was the precursor to their debut album, "United". From this, they appeared on a number of compilations, but went on a creative hiatus until 2004.

artist.name: Cosmo Vitelli
artist.profile: French DJ, producer and manager, currently based in Berlin where he relocated his studio. After spending his childhood between West Africa and Central France, he moved to Paris in the early 1990's.

artist.name: Rob
artist.profile: Has played keyboards and percussion as both a studio and tour support musician for [a2269].

artist.name: Bertrand Burgalat
artist.profile:
French singer, composer, producer, remixer and manager & founder of [l=Tricatel].
He was born in 1963 in Bastia, Corsica, France.

artist.name: Bosco
artist.profile:
Stéphane Bodin and François Marché were both born in 1970, in the French city of Niort. The two first connected in their teens when both attended a gig by Ptose, a local band in the out-there spirit of the Residents. In 1990, the pair rekindled their friendship and began creating tracks around a sampler and a eight-track recorder. Bosco quickly emerged as one of the most inventive bands on the Parisian electronica scene. The duo became known for their voluminous sonic palette, merging infinite musical styles into single tracks. Bosco released their self-titled debut in 1997, followed by «PARAMOUR» in 1999. When the time came for «ACTION», both Bodin and Marché were anxious to strip their far-flung sound down to its essence, retaining the imaginative musical cubism, but with an increased focus on songwriting. Melding such disparate inspirations as Devo, AC/DC and the Sugarhill Gang, Bosco concocted 11 eclectic and irresistible tracks, blending rock n’ roll hooks with house beats, full-blowing brass with utterly unique synthesized melodies. They even managed to create room for Texas-based country crooner Templeton Thompson, who lends her voice to the chicken-fried «Hey! Hey! Hey!» As if that weren’t enough, the «ACTION»-closing track known as «Runk Pockers» incorporates a sample from the notorious UK hardcore thrash combo, the Exploited.
Bosco have now emerged as among our wittiest and most innovative musical architects, fashioning myriad influences into their own inimitable vision

artist.name: Mellow
artist.profile: French pop outfit fronted by [a=Air] associate [a=Patrick Woodcock].

artist.name: Sébastien Tellier
artist.profile:
French electronic artist born in Paris in 1975. Sings in French, English and Italian. His third album, Sexuality, was produced by [a=Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo] of [a=Daft Punk].
Tellier represented France in the 2008 Eurovision Song Contest.

artist.name: Drum Club
artist.profile: Split up in 1996.

artist.name: Charlie Hall
artist.profile:
Ex-member of [a=Spiral Tribe]. Co-promoter of The Loop club nights in London. Was resident DJ at TransLantic at the Wag club alongside [a=Noel Watson].
Co-owned the [l=M.C. Projects] label & ran [l=Pro-Jex] and [l=Yume].

artist.name: The Chemical Brothers
artist.profile: Tom & Ed met in history class at Manchester University in 1989. They started in 1992 as DJs known as "The 237 Turbo Nutters" (named after the number of their house on Dickenson Road in Manchester and a reference to their Blackburn raving days). They then opted for "The Dust Brothers" later in 1992, which they nicked from the L.A. producers of the Beastie Boys' 1989 Album "Paul's Boutique", as they thought they would never be famous. In 1995 they changed their name to "The Chemical Brothers", after the real [a=The Dust Brothers] threatened to sue.

artist.name: Kraft
artist.profile: UK Drum 'n Bass producer.

artist.name: D'Cruze
artist.profile: Hardcore/Jungle/Drum 'n' Bass producer from Essex. When doing live PAs he was assisted by MC Tony.

artist.name: Stratus
artist.profile:
Stratus started producing electronic music in 1990. Throughout the years he has released on various labels and contributed to some of the premier drum & bass compilations in the US.
In 1996 Stratus started one of the first drum & bass labels in the USA, SuperCell Records. While SuperCell is gone, Stratus continues to work on new tracks and new styles. His influence draws from the rawness of the old skool hardcore sounds and extends the boundaries of the newer styles of drum & bass.

artist.name: Jagged Edge
artist.profile: Drum & Bass collaboration of Optical & Ryme Tyme from the United Kingdom. [b]Not to be confused with the R&B group from the United States [a=jagged edge (2)][/b]

artist.name: Carlito
artist.profile: UK Drum n Bass producer.

artist.name: Q Project
artist.profile: Prolific drum and bass producer for labels like Legend, C.I.A., and Hospital.

artist.name: Tango
artist.profile:
Midlands based UK Hardcore, Jungle and Drum&Bass DJ and producer. (27 May 1971 - 31 Jan 2018) R.I.P
Tango released some massive tunes in the early days of Hardcore on labels including Formation, F-Project and Creative Wax. He has also collaborated with artists such as Essence Of Aura, Ratty & DJ Pulse. Tango was a trained chef & electrician. After taking a break from the music scene for a few years he returned for producing and DJing Drum&Bass.

artist.name: Essence Of Aura
artist.profile:
Essence Of Aura were formed in Coventry in the early 1990's. One of the UK's first Drum & Bass producers cutting up breaks and playing them out at raves even before releasing vinyl. They performed at many raves between 1990 and 1995, including Amnesia House, Quest, The Eclipse, The Edge, Fibre-optic, New Age and Pandemonium. They were named as the Midlands number 1 live PA by Pandemonium. [a=Jumping Jack Frost] pointed them out in Muzik Magazines 10 years of Acid House article, along with [a=Carl Cox] as the most important Jungle artists of 1994.
They also featured MC Hi-Fi who was resident MC at the famous Edge club in Coventry.
Essence Of Aura held parties including the first ever party in Kenilworth in January 1991 under the name Insomnia and featured DJ Ratty, DJ Pepsi and gave DJ Fallout of Tango & Fallout fame her debut gig.
They were regulars at the Eclipse and The Edge in Coventry, were an active part of the rave scene in Coventry's hey day and pioneers of early forms of the Drum & Bass movement. They disbanded in 1996.

artist.name: DJ Breeze
artist.profile:
Hardcore DJ/producer from London, UK
Breeze was originally a house DJ before he met Dougal who converted him to hardcore. He also spent a couple of years sharing a house with Dougal and looking after his DJ bookings. Used to work in the Essential Vinyl record shop in Northampton.

artist.name: Vext
artist.profile: Drum and Bass producer from Coventry, UK. Born in 1970. Gavin Watton was a former member of FBD Project, a studio based Drum and Bass outfit from 1990-1994.

artist.name: Spinback
artist.profile:
Drum and bass producer from the UK better known as one-half of Total Science and the Funky Technicians.
Was fortnightly resident DJ at Speed in London.

artist.name: Reflection
artist.profile: Reflection was the techno duo of brothers Ichiro and Kenji Taniguchi. They've released some 12"s, were featured on the Child's View remix comp, and were the first Japanese signings to London's Clear Records. They dealt in intelligent electro, incorporating everything from bossa nova spice to acid squelching -- basically any style of exotica within reach of the last few decades. The duo's first release for Clear was 1997's Transparent EP, after which came their debut album, The Errornormous World.

artist.name: As One
artist.profile: Mixer / producer / DJ often incorporating jazz, soul, and funk elements into electronic tracks. Degiorgio's sound is warm techno with soul and often based outside of the constraints of the straight 4/4 beat. His style can be partly traced to his main musical influences: P-Funk, Motown, early hip hop, Detroit techno, and mid-60's Blue Note.

artist.name: Markus Nikolai
artist.profile:
Markus Nikolai is a German electronic music producer, deejay and performer, co-founder of labels [l=Perlon] and [l=More Down Than Out]. Markus also contributes to countless other projects, including [a=Florida (14)] with [a=Thomas Brinkmann], [a=Darren (8)] with Chris Rehberger, Pico Rocco with [a=Max Loderbauer] from [a=Sun Electric]. He also started the band [a=Morane] with acoustic musician [a=Theo Krieger] and Annika Müller de Vries. From 1998 through 2000, Markus hosted the Peak Club on Sundays in Frankfurt. Apart from his musical activities, Nikolai is also an optometrist, owner of MarkusNikolaiOptic stores and his own design company MarkusNikolaiEyewear.
Born in Frankfurt, Nikolai learned several musical instruments from his grandfather, a conductor, incl. piano, guitar, drums, percussions and, most prominently, accordion. In 1983, Nikolai gave up this instrument and found his first EBM / industrial band [i]Whetzon Churchill[/i]. The group performed regularly in Frankfurt area, and played a concert at a small venue No Name, where a legendary weekly Techno Club party was held. Nikolai also started DJ'ing in 1986-87. In 1992, [a=Thomas Franzmann] (mostly known as [a=Zip]) joined one of Markus' bands [a=Bigod 20] (which also included [a=Front 242] vocalist [a=Jean-Luc De Meyer]), and their collaboration ends up being of the most resourceful and symbiotic for the artist. In the mid-nineties, along with graphic artist [a=Chris Rehberger], the trio founded audio-visual project [a=Pile], an experiment in melding music, graphic design, philosophy, film and architecture. In 1997, they released [m=504881] album on Sony / Epic and 2 years later the group toured in Europe as part of Daimler Chrysler futuristic art exhibition [i]Lab 0.1[/i]. Later that year, Markus and Zip launched their own label, Perlon.

artist.name: Total Science
artist.profile:
The pair first met in 1987, both living on Blackbird Leys estate in Oxford, England and brought together through a mutual love of hip-hop. Distracted by the arrival of hardcore in the early nineties, record collecting was quickly followed by DJing and eventually production.
Greenhalgh was the first into the studio, with "[r=319804]" as [a=Q Project] launching his career in 1991. However it was the establishment of the [url=http://www.discogs.com/label/Legend+Records+(UK)]Legend[/url] imprint and specifically Greenhalgh's track "Champion Sound" from the "[r=84127]", which really took their profile above and beyond the slew of anonymous white labels that were dominating the music at the time.
In 1994, the pair started recording together, with "[url=http://www.discogs.com/release/84159]Got To Believe[/url]" the first in a series of records which pushed the label away from the fizzling darkside movement and more towards the epic string sections and rolling beats which started to appear on labels like [l=Lucky Spin Recordings], [l=Basement Records] and [l=Good Looking Records]. They also worked Saturdays in the Record Basement shop in Reading.
After six years of recording singles for others, the pair established the [l=C.I.A.] label in the closing months of 1997 with the help of Brillo from [l=Timeless Recordings]. The release of "[r=5570]" for [a=Goldie]'s label [l=Metalheadz], breathed a new lease of life into the project, and the pair achieved a further boost with a well-timed [a=Bad Company] remix of "[url=http://www.discogs.com/release/10368]Champion Sound[/url]" which conjured further interest in what they were doing at their own label.
Their debut album "[r=129273]" exposed a further field of interest, with downtempo experiments logged as a series of 'breaks' on the majority of their early EP's extended to some superb deep house, this breadth taken a step further with their [l=Skindeep] imprint which has forged broken beat to considerable acclaim.
Their pioneering of a revival in old-school sounds and arrangements, together with labels [l=Digital UK] and [l=Reinforced Records], dominated drum & bass throughout 2001 and beyond.

artist.name: DJ Hype
artist.profile: DJ and record producer, particularly well known as a competive battle DJ. also the founder of the pirate radio station Fantasy FM.

artist.name: Rascal & Klone
artist.profile: Steve Ward (Rascal) and Greg Lomas (Klone) met in 1994 at Kenilworth’s legendary ‘Euro Dizzy’ rave partys. Klone was the live P.A. and Rascal the resident DJ. Rascal has been DJing since the age of eleven, growing up on diet of early electro and hip hop. Klone started producing at the early age of ten, growing up with a musical family - his father a session drummer for Decca recordings in the 60s & 70s and his Uncle producer for Bad Manners and many of the early 80s Coventry Ska bands. From then on the two combined their skills - appearing as a DJ duo and working together in the studio. This led to their first release as Rascal and Klone on their own label, the now defunct ‘Project F80 Recordings’ pressed in small quantities and distributed from the boots of their mates' cars. Although lasting for only three releases, Project F80 enabled them to gather enough funds together to revamp their studio and spend some serious time on their sound. Relying purely on feedback from Dubplates and DATs given to DJs, Rascal and Klone felt that their sound was starting to make an impact. In 1999 their track ‘Twister’ was licensed to Moving Shadow for their ’Killa Bites’ compilation. In 2000 they made their full debut for the label with the highly acclaimed “Phoenix EP” (MSX004) scoring top marks in reviews and with many of the DJs. This followed with their single on Audio Couture ‘Get Wild’/’Winner Takes All’ (AC042) which went on to be liscensed to the game ‘Grand Theft Auto 3’ and the Moving Shadow compilation ’01.1’. Rascal and Klone were then asked to compile and mix CDs such as ‘Atmospheric Drum and Bass Vol.6’ and an Audio Couture compilation for Knowledge magazine cover CD and asked to remix tracks for downtempo label ‘Kingsize Records’. In 2001 they went on to secure deals with ‘Allied’ (a division of Dune), ‘Killerhertz’ (a division of Ram), ‘Emotif’, and more importantly ‘Urban Takeover’ - watch this space……. With releases on Urban Takeover, Shakedown, Allied, Emotif and Moving Shadow, these guys are not to be missed. Their tracks are currently receiving support from DJs such as Andy C and Ed Rush & Optical and you'd be well advised to keep a close eye on them as DJs and producers.

artist.name: Alias
artist.profile:
Drum n Bass artist from the UK.
Duncan Busto only released two tracks under this name.

artist.name: Mulder
artist.profile: Allen Doe is the man known as Mulder, his nickname for his resemblance to the 'X-File's' Fox Mulder. Based in Bristol he started as a Drum and Bass producer for Urban Takeover using an Amiga 1200. In 2002 he progressed to making Nu-Skool Breaks briefly before quitting production due to illness. In 2006 he resumed production making 'Hardcore Breaks' aka 'Nu-Rave'. In August 2008 he started his own digital label for his own output called [l=Cue Burn Digital].

artist.name: Ray Keith
artist.profile:
DJ/ producer from Colchester, UK. From rare-groove to acid-house to hardcore to jungle, Ray Keith remained one of the most steady DJs on England's breakbeat scene during the 1980s and '90s. After a remix of Orbital's "Chime," he moved into production with apparent ease and became a respected drum'n'bass label-head as well. Keith began DJing soul, hip-hop and rare-groove around Essex and Suffolk in the mid 1980s until the acid house and hardcore explosion of the late '80s and early '90s. His first break playing house was at Oscars in Clacton-On-Sea. He then became a resident at The Crazy Club at the Astoria and latterly Innersense & Movement, as well as now owning the Best Of British organisation. He has had his own radio show on Vibe FM, alongside occasional shows on Kiss FM. By the middle of the '90s, he had become one of the most inspired drum'n'bass DJs and remixers. His own productions, including the jump-up anthem "Terrorist" began taking center stage by the mid-'90s and Keith formed three separate labels -- Penny Black, Dread and UFO -- in 1996. He has worked in close association with Gavin Cheung (aka Nookie) and also works at one of the premiere dance shops in Britain, Black Market Records (after leaving City Sounds). In 1997 he set up a new label -- Dub Dread --, and by 2003 had co-founded a DJ agency, Dread Entertainment Agency alongside his partner, Lena. Keith released the Penny Black collection Breakage, Vol. 1 in 1997, followed by a solo debut of sorts, 1999's UFO Presents Contact.
His nephew is [a=Joy Orbison].

artist.name: Pleasure
artist.profile: Drum n Bass artist.

artist.name: Chris Cowie
artist.profile: Techno / Progressive House & Trance DJ & producer from Aberdeen, Scotland, UK. Started record labels [l=Hook Recordings] (1993) & [l=Bellboy Records] (1994).

artist.name: Ultrahigh
artist.profile: Performing live acts since 1993.

artist.name: Herman Orphey
artist.profile: DJ and producer based in Jacksonville, US.

artist.name: Darien
artist.profile: American R&B, soul vocalist

artist.name: Sirko Mueller
artist.profile: one half of the tokomak records company and former a main founding member of tokomak. he is well known for his diversity in music, where he’s drawing much influences from tha big d(etroit). several high quality releases for some exciting labels like the well known mojuba sublabel wandering, verdant recordings or the london based rewired records have proven his skills and taste. a master of funky rhythm patterns without losing the sight of deepness and power in his productions.

artist.name: Don Williams
artist.profile:
[b]NOTE: For the American Country Singer, please use [a=Don Williams (2)][/b]
Founder and owner of Mojuba & A.R.T.less Records and one half of the Tokomak Records company. He is the creative force behind these imprints and Tokomak's main founding member. His musical influences ranges from Classic, Funk and Jazz via Drum & Bass and Trip Hop to his beloved Detroit Techno and US House, which can be still enjoyed throughout his DJ-Sets. After a few releases on the legendary Pure Plastic imprint, he delivers tracks of his own brand of funk on labels like Rewired, Styrax Leaves and 100% Pure.

artist.name: Yutanie
artist.profile: Owner of Taigatrommel and D&D Records as well DJ

artist.name: Wax Master Maurice
artist.profile: Chicago house producer.

artist.name: DJ Chip
artist.profile: Chicago juke producer.

artist.name: Gant-Man
artist.profile:
American DJ/producer and emcee from Chicago, born 1979. Gant Garrard (pronounced Jerrod) is a disc jockey who was introduced to mixing in the early 1980s by his older brother and learned how to count beats and blend records at the young age of 5 with hip hop, New York garage, Chicago house, electronic and disco music.
At age 10 he was ready to share his talent with the world and began mixing on a college radio station in Chicago WKKC 89.3 FM where he mixed from 1989 through 1997. At age 15 in 1995, Gant produced his first EP for Dance Mania called "It's About Time" which spawned the Chicago Underground smash "3 Blind Mice", which was built around the theme music from The Three Stooges television show. He became known in Chicago as the Youngest Professional DJ and is attributed to being one of the main key innovators of Ghetto Juke House.
At age 18 he began to travel, playing both the Rave and club circuits in the U.S. as well as in Europe. In 1998-99 Gant was playing in Paris at Le Queen and Gibus as well as in Geneva, Switzerland at the Lake Parade prepared him for his Summer 2000 residency in Ibiza, Spain at El Divino alongside Paul Johnson and DJ Skip. You Gotta Get Up was a huge success on Dust Traxx Chicago that was even reissued with Paul Johnson remixes and continues to be repressed due to relentless demand. Gant has recently teamed with 2004 Grammy award winning producer Maurice Joshua to work on remix production for Beyoncé, Destiny's Child and other major artists as well as submitting Juke House remixes to major labels hoping the A&R's will give Gant a chance to exploit this long awaited underground Chicago sound that has been around since 1992.
As of December 2005, Gant is the first DJ/producer to have a Chicago Juke house remix for a major artist on a major label with his remix for Beyonce's "Check On It" featuring Slim Thug on Columbia/Sony Records. For the past couple of years multi-talented Gant has silently leaked his hip hop producing skills to the rap industry. 2006 his Juke remix of "Shake & Pop" is one of highest downloaded tracks in Juke House music. 2007 Gant-Man teamed with A-Trak and new artist Kid Sister and released Control/Damn Girl Juke Remix. Now with 17 years of being a DJ, 12 years of producing tracks and releasing production on labels like Columbia, Riviera, Dust Traxx, Treehouse, Bunchlox, R&R Records, Fools Gold, and Dance Mania, his career has become more prolific with each passing year. He is one of the up and coming producers to watch in 2008. Gant has the experience to move crowds all over the world with his House, Ghetto Juke, old school & Hip-Hop sound and will move any crowd he is given the chance to.

artist.name: DJ Bass
artist.profile: Dutch born dwarf Bas Van Tilburg founded the Danger Hardcore Team. Despite the group name they only produced jump and happy hardcoremusic and where therefore not taken seriously by the major party organisers. However they had some succes in Belgium and today DHT is the name for his hardstyle productions while he still tries to make hardcore tracks under his DJ Bass alias.

artist.name: DJ Thadz
artist.profile:
Thaddeuz Anderson a.k.a. DJ Thadz is a renowned Chicago-based DJ and producer who has made a name for himself within the city’s vibrant juke and footwork music scene. He is affiliated with the Chicago-based label Clownhouse Muzik and has been featured on iconic Chicago label Dance Mania with his vinyl releases co-produced by Stacy Kidd & Paul Johnson.
DJ Thadz’s passion for music began in 1997 when he started producing his unique blend of juke and footwork. His music is characterized by its fast-paced beats, frenzied rhythms, and innovative sound design. Over the years, DJ Thadz has captivated the Chicago dance community with his massive juke parties such as “Juke Jam” and heart-throbbing footwork tournaments like “King of the Circle” that drew thousands of spectators.
DJ Thadz is best known for co-producing juke anthems such as “Bang Skeet” and “Chicken Headz,” which have become staples in the Chicago juke and footwork scene. His music has the ability to instantly drive a gathering to the dance limit, and his live performances are renowned for their energy and enthusiasm.
DJ Thadz extensive music catalog is a testament to his dedication to the art form and his willingness to experiment and push the boundaries of what is possible within the Chicago juke and footwork genres. His music is available on all major streaming platforms, making it easy for fans to access and enjoy his work.
If you’re a fan of Chicago juke and footwork music or just looking for something new and exciting to listen to, DJ Thadz is definitely a name to watch out for. With his innovative approach to production, his captivating live performances, and his extensive catalog of music, DJ Thadz is a key figure in the Chicago music scene and a true pioneer of the juke and footwork genres.

artist.name: Mystic Bill
artist.profile: DJ and producer from US.

artist.name: Michael Fakesch
artist.profile: Music producer, sound designer, DJ, lecturer and film music composer from Rosenheim, Germany.

artist.name: Commercial
artist.profile:
Electronic project from the USA.
[b]Do not use this artist for radio / television advertisements. Use [a=No Artist] instead.[/b]

artist.name: Esophagus
artist.profile: Austrian band from Vienna.

artist.name: Kettel
artist.profile: Kettel is Reimer Eising (born 1982), a self-described “elixir-brewing, camping-loving and musicmaking sorcerer-to-be”. He is well-known for his playful, spontaneous melodies and organic folk sounds similar to Aphex Twin, Jega, and Plaid.

artist.name: L'usine
artist.profile: Texan Jeff McIlwain produces ambient/experimental techno that harks back to the era of classic early-'90s British techno (itself inspired by American sources in Detroit). Highly melodic though usually highly abrasive as well, his self-titled debut album was released in 1999 on the Isophlux label. He also performs under the name Lusine.

artist.name: Shad T. Scott
artist.profile: Electronic music producer from Miami, Florida. Active since the early 1990's, focused mainly on Electro and Techno music. Owner of [l492], [l1471319] and [l1841491].

artist.name: Marinkovic
artist.profile: Japanese DJ and producer

artist.name: Gosub
artist.profile: Project by electronic music producer, from Miami, Florida. His music combines classic Electro with Techno.

artist.name: Dakota Suite
artist.profile:
Dakota Suite is a slowcore/modern classical/ambient/folk project from Leeds, England, UK. Formed in 1996 by Chris Hooson (guitar, vocals), Keith Shotton (drums), Andrew Thrower (bass) and Guy Cave (spoken word).
Dakota Suite experienced some lineup changes, since 2002 became mostly a trio of Chris Hooson, Colin Dunkley and David Buxton, and since 2012 became a duo of Chris Hooson and David Buxton.
Dakota Suite also records with Quentin Sirjacq
Current line-up: Chris Hooson - vocals, guitar, acoustic guitar, piano (1996-) David Buxton - bass, guitar, pedal steel guitar, piano, clarinet, percussion, sampler, synthesizer, effects
Former members: Guy Cave - spoken word (1996) Andrew Thrower - bass (1996-1999) Keith Shotton - drums (1996-1997) Richard Formby - sitar, acoustic guitar, electric guitar, lap steel guitar, piano, bouzouki, harmonium, sitar, electric organ, synthesizer, autoharp, oud, banjo, electronics, effects (1997-2000) Elliot Reuben - drums (1997-1998) Colin Dunkley - piano, cello (1998-2012, session in 1996) John Shepard - drums (1998-2002, 2012)

artist.name: Mus

artist.name: Bear
artist.profile: Bear was a band based in Sheffield, England that existed between 1993 and 2002, with various line-ups the only constant of which was singer-songwriter-guitarist Chris Trout. They released three CD albums, two CD EPs, one 7” single and two split 7”s.

artist.name: Lazerboy
artist.profile: Band from Leeds, England

artist.name: Quigley
artist.profile:
English, songwriter and vocalist of the Montgolfier Brothers (R. Quigley together with Mark Tranmer). He also recorded as "At Swim Two Birds".
Born: 17 March 1969 Salford, England, Died: 18 August 2020

artist.name: Joe Louis
artist.profile: Joseph Louis Barrow (May 13, 1914, La Fayette, Alabama, USA – April 12, 1981, Las Vegas, Nevada, USA), better known as Joe Louis, was an American heavyweight boxing champion.

artist.name: Bandit
artist.profile: 2000s House, Techno act.

artist.name: Moonman

Alias used by Ferry Corsten from 1996-1999. Known for "Don't Be Afraid" & "Galaxia".
He also used the Moonman name for remixes, many of which have become classic Trance anthems.
For example: [r=26595], [r=70706], [r=146449] & [r=2277609].

artist.name: Vick Lavender
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Chicago. Runs [l=Sophisticado Recordings].

artist.name: Proswell
artist.profile: Proswell is Joseph Misra from Chicago, IL.

artist.name: Gotham City
artist.profile: American House producer.

artist.name: Mr. Fingers
artist.profile: Born: 1960, Chicago, Illinois, United States

artist.name: Frankie Knuckles
artist.profile:
American DJ, record producer and remixer, born 18 January 1955 in South Bronx, New York City, died 31 March 2014 in Chicago, Illinois, due to complications from diabetes.
Knuckles was the DJ at the Warehouse nightclub in Chicago from 1977 to 1982. It is widely accepted that his style of DJing and his selection and the appeal of the Warehouse gave house music its name, although in the beginning, the word 'house' was used only in Chicago to denote something which was cool, hip, fresh or bad. Knuckles had been long time friends with [a=Larry Levan], they had had their musical upbringing together from going to clubs like Loft and the Gallery.
Due to his importance in the development of house, Knuckles was often known as "The Godfather of House Music", and as such the city of Chicago named a stretch of street and a day after Knuckles in 2004. His accomplishments earned him a Grammy Award in 1997. As well, Knuckles was inducted into the Dance Music Hall of Fame for his achievements in 2005. He founded his own label [l=Noice! Music] in 2006.
Knuckles' record collection was preserved, catalogued and made publicly accessible in 2015 by the Stony Island Arts Bank in Chicago following his death at the age of 59 the previous year.
See [l=FKF] for the Frankie Knuckles Foundation, a not-for-profit organization established in his memory.

artist.name: Ural 13 Diktators
artist.profile: Duo based in Helsinki (Finland), formed in 1997. Their style was a crossover between the music of 80s (hi-NRG, disco, electro, synth-pop), the sound of 90s (techno, house) and videogame music. After the release of the second full-length in 2002, "Techno Is Dead", they left the music scene. They come back in October 2011 and December 2013 as a live act for a special event.

artist.name: Marumari
artist.profile: Marumari is a duo which began in 1999 as a solo electronic music project by Josh Presseisen, with contributions from his partner Sasha Ellms (now Sasha Presseisen). Sasha became an official member of Marumari in 2001. The music of Marumari is primarily in the IDM/electronica category.

artist.name: Goem
artist.profile:
Goem = A Russian term for shops whose products were available only to Communist Party members. Now abolished.
Goem originated with [a=Frans de Waard] in September 1996 when he got hold of a Student Stimulator (a measuring device used in hospitals). After exploring the machine for some time, [a=Roel Meelkop] joined him and together they recorded the album 'Stud Stim'. In May 1997 [a=Peter Duimelinks] also joined their ranks and they have since recorded and performed extensively in numerous venues, including Barcelona, Berlin and Amsterdam.
Goem founded and runs the [l=Audio.NL] label.

artist.name: Ultra Milkmaids
artist.profile: Experimental and Ambient project from Nantes (France).

artist.name: Warmdesk
artist.profile:
Warmdesk is the project of Chicago-based producer William Selman. Named after an obscure single by an early 90s group out of Cologne that inspired him initially, the sound of Warmdesk has evolved from his earliest work of musique concrete and skewed rhythms into combining these elements into stripped-down minimal techno. Still present are the dubby textures and samples from everyday life, but now they coexist with smooth chords and rhythmic grooves. Selman has released material on many labels, including A Touch of Class, Deluxe, Bip-Hop, Static Caravan and Fallt. His first album was the "Guero Variations" cd for the well-regarded Deluxe label (home to Electric Birds, Blechtum from Blechdom and Matmos's early endeavors). The record brought together the musique concrete and techno as it is composed almost completely with sounds from the interior of the piano without actually playing the keys; according to the Portland Mercury's Dave Segal, it "makes musique concrete detritus shimmy and shake with glee." The album made it into several critics' top lists for records of 2003.
More recently, he has released two 12"s on Andy Vaz's Background Records' "House" sublabel A Touch of Class that showed off more dancefloor-friendly cuts (while retaining the sampling aesthetic) that quickly found their way into top tens of DJs like Steve Bug and Mathias Schaffhäuser as well as receiving the highest accolades from critics. Currently in the works are more releases for several labels and a collaboration of dancefloor cut-and-paste with Forte/Neuton artist Matthew Mercer called 2400W. He also runs A Posteriori Recordings which releases occasional one-off records of a more conceptual theme--the last being “Guero Variations Variations.” During the day, Selman is a software and database engineer.

artist.name: Laurent Pernice
artist.profile: French musician Laurent Pernice (ex-member of French band Nox) embraced the sampler and electronics and crafted the ethic/industrial collage of Releases Details (Permis de Construire, 1988) and Sortie Vers la Ville (Permis de Construire, 1994), both further eroded by his jarring vocals. In between, he also released a more conventional work, Axident (Permis de Construire, 1992). The experiment continued with Sept Autres Creatures (Odd Size, 1996), Yppah (Moloko+, 2001), Infrajazz (2003) , Drosophiles & Doryphores (2004). In 2014 Atypeek Music republish the complete discography on digital platform.

artist.name: Tim Baker
artist.profile:
Tim Baker(* November 10, 1967 - † April 4, 2021), a highly respected international DJ and producer emits a striking devotion to the music he produces, plays and remixes. This native Detroiter has a creative style of Djing, which fuses the funk with menacing percussion for a dramatic and emotional experience. His insight and passion for Detroit techno and house music began advancing 17 years ago in 1984 when he received his first turntables. The music which motivated his early phase of Djing included Cameo, Parliament, the B-52's, Bauhaus, Tangerine Dream, and Brian Eno. In the city of Detroit , the creative energy of the times was just beginning to manifest itself into the musical phenomena of ‘techno'.
Tim Baker's frequent visits to several Detroit underground clubs such as, the Majestic Theater and the Music Institute to hear Derrick May left a lasting musical impression on Baker. The uninhibited presence of new music fueled his eagerness to DJ. As a result, Baker acquired his first residency at the famous Nectarine Club in 1987-1988, where Jeff Mills was also a resident.
In 1990, Tim Baker graduated from Eastern Michigan University with a business degree in marketing. He started his Elephanthaus clothing line in 1993 and began manufacturing ‘club wear'. The Detroit nightlife was his inspiration for his first T-shirt designs. After much success with his Elephanthaus line, he decided to open a clothing store. Over the next six years, he continued to DJ parties while he was designing fashion.
In 1994, Tim Baker played in Europe for the first time at Berlin 's Tresor club. His Djing gigs began to accumulate, so he stopped making clothes and focused on the music. In 1997, he started Elephanthaus Records and Baker released his first EP, Black Machines . His musical career has since then evolved and taken him to the likes of the UK , Germany , France and more. He has spun at world famous clubs such as the Tresor Club in Berlin , Florida135 in Spain , The Rex Club in Paris , and he has performed at festivals around the world including The Love Parade held annually in Berlin , the Detroit Music Festival, and the Sonar Festival in Spain .
While constantly striving for a better understanding for the future of his music, Tim moved his home base from Detroit to Chicago in 1998. The move motivated Tim to launch his house label, Real Estate Records . This label combines the forward thinking future funk of Detroit with the soulful edge of Chicago . This combination brings out the purest form of house music, and has lead to the great success of their “Future House Society” series. Tim Baker has also diversified his discography with releases on Spain 's Minifunk Label, Ovum in the U.S. , BML in the U.S. , and several others. In spring 2003 the release of Tim Baker's first single on Nova Mute entitled “Addicted”.
For the 21st century, Tim Baker will continue to uphold the repertoire of his labels by discovering innovative and creative talent. Tim extends his music capacity to new territories acquainting the world with his unique sound and vision. Tim's undeniable propelling force is to provide World Class DJ performances, incredible production and quality entertainment for the masses.
(Taken from the Elephanthaus Records website.)

artist.name: Russian Roulette
artist.profile: Techno and house DJ and producer from Chicago, Illinois. Also known as [a=DJ Rush].

artist.name: Richard Grey
artist.profile: DJ and producer. He contributed to the world success of the "French Touch" of House music. With an early love of music, heavily influenced by his father’s record collection, Richard grew up listening to classic Jazz, Funk and Disco acts such as [a=George Duke], [a=Crusaders] and [a=Chic]. He began to record solo in 1999 with 'I Need You'. He is the founder of [l=G-High Records] and Pyramidal Records.

artist.name: Grudge
artist.profile: Techno.

artist.name: The Kick Inside
artist.profile: The Kick Inside is [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/Laurent+Collat]Laurent Collat[/url], who is best known for his productions on French labels [url=http://www.discogs.com/label/F-Communications]F-Communications[/url] and [url=http://www.discogs.com/label/Brique+Rouge]Brique Rouge[/url]. Collat is also a well-known sound engineer, having co-produced Laurent Garnier's last album.

artist.name: Midnite City Soul Affair
artist.profile: Alias used by [a739], a Finnish DJ and a producer of house music, born 28 September 1973, Kemi, Finland.

artist.name: Thunderball
artist.profile: Belgian Techno / Hardcore Techno duo.

artist.name: Liftoff
artist.profile: Liftoff is: Steven Albert, Steve Raskin, Johnna Raskin, Rob Myers.

artist.name: Exodus Quartet
artist.profile: Formed in 1991 by Eric Hilton and Fari Ali, Exodus Quartet sought to recreate the sound of Washington, DC's Exodus acid jazz club. Their only album, 1996's [i]Way Out There[/i], featured many guest musicians and was released following a plethora of compilation appearances. Hilton went on to co-found [a=Thievery Corporation].

artist.name: Isabelle Antena
artist.profile:
Isabelle Antena is a French pop and jazz singer. Born on 1960-05-28 in Villepinte near Paris, Isabelle Powaga began her career in the trio [a24163] on the label [l=Les Disques du Crépuscule] (based in Brussels, Belgium), touring with [a=Paul Haig], [a=Tuxedomoon] and [a=Cabaret Voltaire]. She then recorded her first solo album in London with Martin Hayles. As a solo artist she became particularly popular in Japan. She has recorded a string of solo albums as Isabelle Antena, as well as under the names [a=Antena], Powaga Sisters and [a=Pause Cafe]. In 2003 she featured on [a=Buscemi]'s album Camino Real, and released a best of, L'alphabet Du Plaisir. In 2006 her song Nothing To Lose appeared on the [m960634]. She slipped away after her French Riviera album, which was a collaboration with producers from the japanese nu-jazz scene, [a=Kyoto Jazz Massive], [a=Jazztronik], [a=Yukihiro Fukutomi], [a=Tatsuo Sunaga]. In 2017 she made a comeback album. Her daughter [a5068143] has chosen the same pseudonym family name. Fun fact: she used to work as an au pair for Rick Wakeman somewhere in the 1970s.
(For group membership, see [a=Isabelle Powaga].)

artist.name: Batu
artist.profile: Formed in early 1992, Brighton, England and disbanded in late 1994, this group featured the madcap talents of Bebeto De Sousa, a native of Sao Paolo, Brazil, and the vocals of Sharon Scott.

artist.name: Red
artist.profile: UK trip hop act from mid nineties on Cup Of Tea Records.

artist.name: Shantel
artist.profile: German DJ and producer of Bukovina German Romanian descent, born in Frankfurt am Main January 1, 1968.

artist.name: Waldeck
artist.profile: The act known as Waldeck is the work of one Klaus Waldeck, a producer for Vienna's [l=Spray Records], home to work by [a=Kruder & Dorfmeister] and [a=Count Basic]. Waldeck's work is similar earthy downtempo, though many of his productions reflect the recruitment of vocalists, similar to Massive Attack. After contributing a track to the Spray compilation The Eclectic Sound of Vienna, Waldeck released his debut full-length Balance of the Force in 1999. Along with an American distribution deal through E-Magine, an accompanying remix album was released in the United States as well. Who Is Waldeck, his second LP, was released in fall 2000. His electronic daydream continued with The Night Garden, issued in spring 2002. - John Bush

artist.name: Five
artist.profile: Five is an English boy band formed in 1997 (formerly stylised as 5ive), consisting of two rappers (Abz & J) and three singers (Scott, Sean, Rich). Setting Five apart from other boy bands, all members were heavily involved in the process of writing their songs and raps. Five released several hit singles around the turn of the 21st century, particularly in their native United Kingdom. The group has sold over 20 million records worldwide. All five members reformed in 2025 for the Keep on Movin' tour in November.

artist.name: Joe Silva
artist.profile:
Joe Silva is a Canadian house music producer, DJ and composer.
He was in the group They Never Sleep. He also produces with DJ Freestyle using the name Big Pond, and with Ali Khan as Tonepushers.

artist.name: Steve Frisco
artist.profile:
DJ and producer.
stevefrisco@yahoo.fr

artist.name: DJ Boss
artist.profile: Slovakian techno producer and DJ who started experimenting with making music in 1995. His first release was on [a=DJ Rush]'s [l=Kne' Deep] label.

artist.name: Phil Weeks
artist.profile: French DJ and producer based in Paris. Founder of [l=Robsoul Recordings].

artist.name: Alex Palmer
artist.profile: DJ and producer born in 1961 and raised in Tottenham. Lived in Flatbush, Brooklyn, with his American father between 1979 and 1983 where he was exposed to early hip hop. Co-founded [l=V4 Visions] in 1990 on which he released several records. Moved to Frankfurt, Germany when the label folded in 1994.

artist.name: Volga Select
artist.profile: French electronic music producer duo.

artist.name: Funk 198
artist.profile: Techno DJ from Barrie, Ontario, Canada.

artist.name: The New World Order
artist.profile: Acid house producer of Afro-Cuban descent, releasing music under the name Armando. He was born on February 12, 1970, in Chicago, Illinois, and died on December 17, 1996, of leukemia. Ran [l=Warehouse Records (2)] and its sub-label [l=Muzique Records] with [a=Mike Dunn] between 1988 and 1990.

artist.name: Drowsy Birds
artist.profile: Drowsy Birds were a Chicago-based group with connections to various experimental and post-rock groups active in the city around the turn of the millennium. They pioneered a subgenre they termed "16 style", which entered around the interpolation of records played at 16rpm into their tracks.

artist.name: Science Park
artist.profile:
Science Park is the nom du disque of a nomadic pop musician who has made his home in Boston, Chicago, and other points unknown.
Science Park music is distilled from late-night short-wave radio broadcasts, highway lines, satellite transmissions, voices of unknown origin, electromagnetism ... and the works of Stina Nordenstam, Penguin Cafe Orchestra, Crammed Discs, and Zang Tuum Tumb.
Science Park has performed and/or toured with the Clientele, the Delgados, Solex, Barcelona, Flare, Mono Puff, and others.
Vancouver, British Columbia, Canada

artist.name: Lesser
artist.profile: Indie sampler / electronic artist Stranded halfway between the heavily fraternal scenes organized around drum'n'bass and noise.

artist.name: Jack Dangers
artist.profile:
Born: 1965, Swindon, England
Electronic musician and vintage synthesizer enthusiast. The mastermind behind [a=Meat Beat Manifesto].

artist.name: Mouse On Mars
artist.profile: Electronic pop and leftfield band from Düsseldorf & Cologne, Germany. Since 2011 located in Berlin.

artist.name: Tracey Thorn
artist.profile: English singer and songwriter, born 26 September 1962 in Brookmans Park, Hatfield, Hertfordshire, England, UK. In 2009 she married [a=Ben Watt].

artist.name: MC Conrad
artist.profile: British drum and bass vocalist and former collaborator with [a321]. Born in Abingdon, Northampton on April 16, 1972, he was initially inspired by Electro, breakdancing and Hip Hop. He later became an MC in the early 90's rave scene, and moved into hardcore, jungle and drum and bass. Started label "Resonance" in 2022. Conrad's agency, Clinic Talent, confirmed on April 30, 2024 that he had passed away.

artist.name: DJ Magic Mike
artist.profile: American Miami bass producer from Orlando, La Florida state (USA).

artist.name: Bayaka
artist.profile: Japanese electronica producer duo. Composed of DJ Mitsuru (Mitsuru Matsuki / 松木充): DJ and Teruo (Teruo Seno / 瀬野晶夫): programming.

artist.name: The Future Sound Of London
artist.profile:
British duo who met in the 1980s in Manchester. Their very influential discography shows off influences spanning acid house, hardcore techno, ambient, krautrock, '60s psychedelica, and more. Previously Dougans, as [a=Humanoid], had a UK Top 10 hit with [r=13628]. Together as FSOL, they scored a major crossover success with [r=6352]. Their first full-length [r=27552] charted their dancefloor-friendly early career, after which FSOL moved into deeper, more album-oriented world. [r=83318] was a double-disc set spanning long stretches of breakbeat-flavored ambience. It was followed by [r=35401] which added hip-hop, trip-hop, industrial textures, and bleak urban imagery into their mix of influences. Their [r=154267] album compiled music from their ISDN-uplinked radio broadcasts from the mid-1990s. After Dead Cities came out in 1996, they felt like they were moving in the wrong direction and they dropped off the radar after the [r=1633800] mix in mid 1997.
After a 4 year long hiatus in which they were surrounded by rumors of insanity and drug addiction (whereas the real situation was mercury poisoning from teeth fillings and some soul-searching travels), the [r=97752] concept remix album came out in 2001, followed by the full-length [r=93342] and more material under the Amorphous Androgynous name.
In March 2007 they finally went independent and launched FSOLDigital.com releasing dozens of unreleased material (under the [l=From The Archives] series), as well as old and new material from their other aliases. One of the most anticipated albums in ambient history, [r=1145416] got released 13 years since its recording, and then Environments continued as a series, the first volumes were reproduced old material blended with new neoclassical material. By the time they reached [r=6066849] in 2014, all recorded material was brand new.

artist.name: Braxton Holmes
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Chicago.

artist.name: E.O.G.
artist.profile: Duo from The Netherlands. E.O.G. stands for Elements Of Grief.

artist.name: Phako
artist.profile: Dutch producer.

artist.name: Funckarma
artist.profile:
Funckarma is a Dutch electronic music group made up of brothers Don & Roel Funcken.
Since 1999 Funckarma has been the main mode of output for the brothers album releases. Over time they have grown in their diversity of style from an almost purely IDM core to a wide array of genres of music - both through their exploration of new styles as they develop and collaborations with other artists.

artist.name: Duplex
artist.profile: Duplex are John Matze & Chris Callahan and Hailing from Rotterdam. They started making soulful techno music ‘together apart’ in 1995. Their music has been spread into the world through labels such as Dolly, Clone Records, Frantic Flowers, Klakson, Field Records, Dub Recordings and Harbour City Sorrow. Their sound combines elements of Disco, Funk, (Detroit) Techno, Electro and House.

artist.name: Duracel
artist.profile: Producer from The Netherlands.

artist.name: Nu Science
artist.profile: Electronic project from Helsinki, Finland, started in 1996.

artist.name: Stardust
artist.profile: Short-lived collaborative project formed by Alain Quême (aka [a=Alan Braxe]), Benjamin Cohen (aka [a=Benjamin Diamond]) and Thomas Bangalter (aka 1/2 [a=Daft Punk]). Famous for their worldwide-hit "Music Sounds Better With You".

artist.name: Funky People
artist.profile: Collaboration between Kevin Hedge, Josh Milan, Tee Alford, Cassio Ware and Allen Jeffrey. The ensemble produced four of the early releases on the Funky People label.

artist.name: Heaven And Earth
artist.profile: Collaboration between Michael Kandel (Tranquility Bass, High Lonesome Sound System, Exist Dance, Astralwerks) and Matthew Lux (Isotope 217, Thrill Jockey). LD3 of Phuture's classic "We Are Phuture" makes a guest appearance on vocals on "The Business".

artist.name: Romanthony
artist.profile:
House producer from New Jersey renowned for his resemblance, vocally, to [a=Prince]. Launched his own label, [l=Black Male Records], in 1991 on which he released records under a variety of aliases. Founded [l=World Records (60)] in 1995 and [l=DigiTalent] in 2004. In 2000 he co-wrote and performed the vocals on [m26637].
Born in Long Branch, New Jersey on September 5, 1967. Died in Austin, Texas on May 7, 2013, aged 45.

artist.name: Phortune
artist.profile: Depending on the releases, Phortune is either [a=DJ Pierre] or collaborations between [a=Roy Davis Jr.], [a=Victor Blackful] and [a=Earl Smith Jr.] with executive production by [a=DJ Pierre].

namevariations.name: "Dynamic Duo" T. Sozzi/T.Musto N.Y.
"Dynamic Duo" Tommy Musto & Tommi Sozzi

artist.name: Bobby Konders
artist.profile: DJ and producer based in Brooklyn, New York City. Runs the [l=Massive B] label which was a division of [l=Nu Groove Records] for the house music Konders produced in the early 1990s.

artist.name: Stanny Franssen
artist.profile: Stanny Franssen aka. G-Force from Belgium took over together with artists like Marco Bailey a pacesetter function with their functional technotools in the technoscene of Belgium. In the meantime Stanny is beside Marco Bailey and Dj Redhead one of the most important artists of Belgium. Since he has released on Monika Kruse’s Terminal M-imprint is the star of the Belgian radiating as brightly as never before. Raised with EBM and the sound of detroit, Stanny inspires the dark futuristic detroit feeling for Techno.

artist.name: Marco Carola & Adam Beyer
artist.profile: Italian/Swedish duo of DJs and producers.

artist.name: Sandals
artist.profile: Will Blanchard, John Harris, Derek Delves, Ian Simmonds

artist.name: Christian Wünsch
artist.profile: Techno DJ, producer from Spain. Born in Monaco, he spent most of his life in the North of Spain. He started his own labels Tsunami Records and Nine Records.

artist.name: Fanon Flowers
artist.profile: Fanon Flowers (pronounced Fuh-nahn) is a Techno music producer and DJ based in Los Angeles, California.

artist.name: Paris Angeles
artist.profile: French House quatuor composed of [a=Jean-Sébastien Ciano] AKA [a=Demon Ritchie], [a=Jean-Baptiste Millet], [a=Mike Brank] and [a=Rodolphe Lejeune].

artist.name: Didier Sinclair
artist.profile: Pioneer of french "FM", Didier Sinclair started his career in 1982 on different local radios of Montpellier as animator and DJ. In 1984, he starts to work for NRJ radio station in Montpellier and two years later for FUN Radio. From 1987 to 1991, Didier has a residency in the gay club in the south of France: "Le Phébus". He becomes, then, one of the precursors of the House music movement. All these reasons in addition to his success makes him coming to Paris at the end of 1991. In 1992, Radio FG, the first radio that adopted an electronic music concept in France and worldwide, propose him a daily mix show. In 1993, Didier Sinclair is in charge of radio FG musical programmation. In October 1994, Didier leaves Radio FG to contribute to the development of Going Global Series (G.G.S), an electronic music label created and owned by BARCLAY (UNIVERSAL Group) which is today Sound of Barclay (S.O.B). In march 1997, Didier is elected the 4th best french DJ according to referendum's results of CODA Magazine. In October 1997, He joins back the Radio FG Djs' team and takes in charge, in september 1998, the musical programmation. Didier Sinclair is from that moment on, one of the principal DJ of the French house scene exporting his talent worldwide to every major city. He sadly passed away from lung cancer on Oct. 30, 2008.

artist.name: LX DJ
artist.profile: Italian artist

artist.name: Stetsasonic
artist.profile:
American hip hop group formed in 1981, in Brooklyn, New York. They broke up in 1991, soon after the release of their last album "Blood, Sweat & No Tears". The crew were known for including live instrumentation in their shows.
In 1998, French DJ [a=Dimitri From Paris] remixed the song "Talkin All That Jazz", awakening some new interest in the group. Stetsasonic reconvened over a decade later and, in the early 2020s, released four new singles with promise of a new album in 2024.

artist.name: Master H
artist.profile: Techno/house DJ and producer from Marseille, France and based in Paris. Founded [l=Komplex De Deep] in September 2008. Contact: info@master-h.com

artist.name: Intruder
artist.profile: House music act.

artist.name: DJ HMC
artist.profile: [b]H[/b]ouse [b]M[/b]aster [b]C[/b]am. One of the early pioneers of house and techno in Australia. Based in Adelaide and one of the main players behind [l1532111] which helped put Australian techno on the map from around 1991.

artist.name: Nocturne
artist.profile: Drum n Bass act of the late '90s.

artist.name: Apex
artist.profile: Matt Jarrett is the mastermind behind the mighty Apex (Renamed Chōten). Jarret hit Marine Parade hard with his debut release Bangin' back in 1999. In 2000 Jarrett was comissioned to remix the massive club hit "The Red Pill" [MAPA008], originally released on Freakin' Music by Florida Breaks masters Scratch D vs. H-Bomb. Followed on Marine Parade by the seminal Symmetry ep, Other tracks on the double-pack EP were "Our Nation", "Jump Funk" and a NuBreed remix of "Symmetry".

artist.name: Elementz Of Noize
artist.profile:
Alan Clark, Justin Maughan
Drum n Bass producers from Newcastle upon Tyne, UK.

artist.name: James Christian
artist.profile: New York City-based DJ/producer. Founded the [l=Floorwax] label in 1995.

artist.name: G-Pal
artist.profile: [a=George Pallikaris] originally hails from Thessaloniki, the largest city of northern Greece. He was first introduced to music at the very young age of 4, when he was already training to play the piano! After developing his skills composing and producing his own tracks, George moved to New York City to pursue a degree in music technology. While attending courses to obtain the NYU's degree, he concluded his first commercial record release on UK's [l=Low Pressings] around the beginning of 1999. Very soon, he joined the [l=UCMG New York] artist roster, decision that allowed him to release various singles for [l=Plastic City] - including his critically acclaimed debut album "Life". Meanwhile, George had to return to Greece to do his military service for the Greek army! It was however this obligation that gave him the opportunity to found [l=Swift Records] in 2001. Since then, things got in a row for G.Pal who still manages to compile various tasks at the same time. The monthly residency at LUV club in Athens, alongside his wife [a=Anna Maria X].

artist.name: Clatterbox
artist.profile:
During the mid-nineties, Dave secured himself a considerable following while recording releases for the seminal UK label Clear, recently made available again under his DJK Productions brand. Clatterbox' 'Debut' 2x10" and subsequent 'Clatterbeats' releases defined a unique sound by fusing electro with heavy dope beats and are now sought after collector's items.
He went on to produce music under various guises including the solid house sounds of Cartel Productions, a project that has recently resurfaced on Cheap Records, and a successful collaboration album project with Metamatics (Lee Norris) on Neoouija.
Evolving his sound to a more sinister and electronic electro-style, David set up his own record label called Z-Bop in 2003, which is co-run by long-time friend 'Even Steven' Horne (a.k.a. The Horn from Universal Language) and a family of artists who all hail from the South West of England.

artist.name: Outside
artist.profile: British jazz-funk group

artist.name: Jol
artist.profile: Jol is the Gothenburg based producer [a=Joel Eriksson]. He was one of the founders of Gonkyburg with [a=Quant]. He released two singles for the Gothenburg based [l=Dot] label, before signing with the Danish imprint, [l27219]. For April, Jol released the Moody Aow album and Going Down single. Going Down was remixed by fellow Gonkyburg artists; [a=Plej], Quant and [a=Swell Session].

artist.name: Bobby Pruit
artist.profile: Disco - soul - house singer - songwriter.

artist.name: Funky Green Dogs
artist.profile: Special project by the [a4656] duo with female vocal support.

artist.name: Youth
artist.profile: English producer, songwriter and bassist (also plays percussion and keyboards).

artist.name: Andrew Weatherall
artist.profile:
British DJ, producer, musician, and remixer.
Born: 6 April 1963 in Windsor, Berkshire, England, UK. Died: 17 February 2020 in London, England, UK (aged 56).
Weatherall worked in and showed enthusiasm for a wide variety of genres. He moved into visual art later in life. Labels he previously ran include [l=Sabres Of Paradise], [l=Emissions Audio Output], [l=Bird Scarer Records], and [l=Moine Dubh].
He died suddenly from a pulmonary embolism.
Brother to [a=Ian Weatherall].

artist.name: Sven Van Hees
artist.profile: Belgian DJ & producer. Born in Antwerp on 1968-05-26. Label owner and manager of [l=Your Lips]. Former label manager of [l=Global Cuts] and co-founder of [l=Wonka Beats].
