artist.profile:
American R&B/Soul Singer (born May 24, 1944 in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, USA).
Also known as "The Godmother of Soul".

artist.name: The Edge
artist.profile: British guitarist and keyboard player with the Irish rock band, [a=U2], born 8 August 1961 in East London, England, UK of Welsh parents. He moved to Dublin in his youth. In 2002 he married [a=Morleigh Steinberg].

artist.name: Jah Wobble
artist.profile: English bass guitarist, singer, poet and composer, born August 11, 1958 in Stepney, London. Jah Wobble originated from a drunken, slurred pronunciation of his real name, John Wardle, by his friend and fellow musician Sid Vicious. Wobble officially began his recording career as bassist, and founding member of the hugely influential post punk group [a=Public Image Limited] (PiL). After two LP's, Wobble parted with Lydon and PiL in 1980 to pursue a successful and prolific solo career, and has since collaborated with a wide variety of musicians and recording projects over the years. Some of his best known work is published with [a37723]. His explorations into World music predated much of the genre's popularity. His music has spanned a number of genres, including ambient music and dance music, and in 2003, reworkings of traditional English folk songs. Though he has released recordings since the early 1980s, Wobble has been quite prolific from the mid-1990s to the present. He ran his own label, 30 Hertz Records, and tours regularly throughout England and Europe. His daughter is actress Hayley Angel Wardle and he is married to the renowned harpist [a=Zi Lan Liao].

artist.name: Chas Jankel
artist.profile: British musician, born 1952, London, who went from keyboard player and guitarist with Ian Dury and the Blockheads to a solo career in the early 1980s. He had several club/chart hits and also appeared on a few soundtracks. His son Lewis now produces and records music as [a=Shift K3y].

artist.name: Visage
artist.profile:
Pioneers of the New Romantic movement, the synth-pop group Visage emerged in 1978 from the London club Blitz, a neo-glam nightspot which stood in stark contrast to the prevailing punk mentality of the moment. Spearheading Blitz's ultra-chic clientele were Steve Strange and DJ Rusty Egan, one time drummer with the Rich Kids. Seeking to record music of their own to fit in with the club's regular playlist (a steady diet of David Bowie, Kraftwerk, and Roxy Music), Strange and Egan were offered studio time by another Rich Kids alum, guitarist Midge Ure. In late 1978, this trio recorded a demo which yielded the first Visage single, an aptly futuristic cover of Zager & Evans' "In the Year 2525."
Adding Ultravox keyboardist Billy Currie as well as three members of Magazine -- bassist Barry Adamson, guitarist John McGeoch, and keyboardist Dave Formula -- Visage signed to Martin Rushent's Genetic Records to release "Tar," in September of 1979, followed a year later by their self-titled debut LP. The album yielded a major single in "Fade To Grey," an instant club classic which heralded synth-pop's imminent commercial breakthrough. The follow-up, "Mind of a Toy," was a Top 20 hit. Next came their sophomore album, 1982's "The Anvil." Midge, John, Dave and Billy left in 1983. "Beat Boy," was Visage's 1984 release, the band now consisting of Steve Strange, Rusty Egan, Steve Barnacle and Gary Barnacle. In 2013, Visage returned with the line-up of Steve Strange and Steve Barnacle, with new members Robin Simon (ex-Ultravox guitarist) and Lauren Duvall (on vocals).
Steve Strange died on the 12th of February, 2015.

artist.name: Holger Czukay
artist.profile:
German krautrock musician, [b]born[/b] 24 March 1938 in the Free City of Danzig (today Gdansk, Poland), [b]died[/b] 5 September 2017 in Weilerswist (near Cologne), Germany, [aged 79].
He studied under [a=Karlheinz Stockhausen] from 1963–1966, and in 1968 co-founded the German rock group [a=Can], for whom he played bass guitar, as well as undertaking most of their recording and engineering. Czukay also recorded some early ambient albums, and was influential for his utilization of radio sounds and sampling.
He played bass, guitar, keyboards, percussion, drums, vocals and French horn.

artist.name: Tom Moulton
artist.profile:
American engineer, mixer and Disco record producer.
Born: 29 November 1940 in Schenectady, New York, USA.
Moulton is the originator of the remix, the breakdown section ("Disco Break"), and the 12-inch single vinyl format. He has humbly maintained that the last two innovations were pure accidents. Perhaps contrary to expectation, Moulton's early successes in "mixing down" dance records were the result of insistently taking away elements from the original multi-track.
Although not a disc jockey, Thomas Jerome Moulton was more essential to the rise of disco than any other single person in its history. Born in Schenectady, New York on November 29, 1940 music was in his life from a very early age. Moulton even quit high school to work in a record store in the late 1950s. With aspirations to become a radio DJ, he quickly found that he didn't have what it took to be a DJ in the turbulent payola-ridden radio scene of the 1950s.
His career started in the late 1960s with a self-made tape of overlapping songs created for the Fire Island bar and restaurant The Sandpiper. As the club in question was very high profile, it was only a matter of time before his skills were noticed and put to use pre-release by those in the music industry. Moulton preferred R&B and dance music, but actually mixed a wide range of popular recordings. A noteworthy quote has him saying, "I never made a dance record, I made records you can dance to".
Moulton worked as a model at the Bookings and Ford agencies before beginning his production career. Before that, he had worked in the music industry, holding a sales and promotion job at King Records (from 1959 to 1961) and similar positions at RCA and United Artists. He eventually left due to his disgust at the industry's dishonesty.
His notable achievements in recording technique include extending the high frequencies and tightening the bottom for better sounding play at high volume and lengthening for greater musical and emotional impact by repeating key passages.
He was responsible for the first continuous-mix album side ever, on the [a=Gloria Gaynor]'s seminal disco album "Never Can Say Goodbye", earning him the title the "father of the Disco Mix" . Among some of his other success in mixing songs are [a=The Three Degrees]' "Dirty Ol' Man", [a=MFSB] featuring [a=The Three Degrees] "Love Is The Message", [a=B.T. Express]' "Do It (Til You're Satisfied)", [a=The Trammps]' "Disco Inferno", [a=People's Choice]'s "Do It Any Way You Wanna", [a=Andrea True Connection]'s "More, More, More" [a=First Choice]'s "Doctor Love", and [a=Claudja Barry]'s album "The Girl Most Likely".
Between 1977 and 1979, he produced [a=Grace Jones]' first three albums, including one of the singer's biggest hits, her rendition of Édith Piaf's "La Vie en rose".
Tom Moulton's innovative work was honored at the 2004 Dance Music Hall of Fame ceremony in New York City when he was inducted for his achievements as a Remixer. He is the official archivist of the Bethlehem Jazz and Salsoul music catalogues, and has overseen all of the digital remastering for the entire catalog. In late 2006 Moulton would remix the [a=The Brand New Heavies] (featuring [a=N'Dea Davenport]) single "I Don't Know (Why I Love You)", a cover of the Stevie Wonder and Jackson 5 hit.
[b]A Tom Moulton Mix[/b] is a phrase indicating that a dance record had been mixed by Tom Moulton. Prior to Moulton (and throughout his active years), no major player had prefaced their name with "A" and followed it with "Mix". One will find examples of this usage today, but in Mr. Moulton's heyday such usage was unheard of for those seeking respect in the industry. Moulton's use of the phrase led to similar usage by his contemporaries within the Salsoul Records family. Such usage would have given him the status of "mentor" rather than infringe his territory. In a late 1990s interview, Mr. Moulton himself cast aspersion upon Vincent Montana Jr.'s attempt to use this titling (Montana and Moulton were known to have had a difficult professional relationship).
"A Tom Moulton Mix" was later used as the title of a compilation of Moulton's remixes on Soul Jazz Records. Tom's remixes, both past and present, continue to be played on radio stations today, and more recently aired on Retro Soul Radio London, are Tom's outstanding Philly Regrooved Albums. Retro Soul Radio presenters Dave Simons and Neil Winter had the pleasure of speaking with Tom about his career, his music and his passion for the music industry. The interview was aired on Retro Soul Radio with his fans from all over the world locked in for over 2 hours listening to his tracks and what he had to say about them.

artist.name: Danny Krivit
artist.profile:
American disco & electronic DJ, producer/remixer.
Born: 10 April 1957 in New York City, New York, USA.
Krivit grew up in in the 1960s in Greenwich Village, New York City, and was surrounded by music. His mother was an accomplished jazz singer and his father was the manager of legendary jazz trumpeter [a=Chet Baker] before going on to open up "The Ninth Circle"; a Village hot spot, where Danny worked as a boy. Here, Krivit met many influential music scene people; [a=Janis Joplin], [a=Jimi Hendrix], [a=Charlie Mingus], [a=John Lennon], and [a=Yoko Ono], amongst others.
Krivit's home was also a hotbed of musical inspiration, with [a=The Mothers Of Invention] living down the hall, and [a=Sid Bernstein] (manager of [a=The Rascals]) living upstairs, regularly popping down to the Krivit household to practice their future hits on the piano. At school a close friend and classmate was the son of [a=Creed Taylor], the production genius behind many artists who recorded on the [l=Verve], [l=CTI], and [l=Kudu] labels, and another close friend was legendary soul guru [a=Nile Rogers].
By 1970 Krivit was already a vinyl junkie and an amateur DJ, but DJ-ing as a profession wasn't realised until 1971, after another neighbor and friend of his (vice president of [l=Polydor Records]) introduced him to one of his artists (the legendary [a=James Brown]), who gave Danny white-label promo copies of his "Get On The Good Foot", and "Think" by [a=Lynn Collins]. Soon after he started DJ-ing at "The Ninth Circle" which by now had been transformed into a disco. In 1975, his father opened his second club called "Ones", with Krivit being their sole DJ through 1977. From this he started promoting and DJ-ing at his own after-hours club down the street. By now, Krivit was becoming part of the underground scene, club-hopped a lot, met some of the greatest DJ names of the time, including [a=Nicky Siano] (of the Gallery), [a=Walter Gibbons] (of Galaxy 21), [a=Tee Scott] (of Better Days), [a=David Rodriguez] (of The Limelight), [a=Richie Kaczor] (Club Hollywood), and [a=Bobby DJ] (of Le Jardin), et al. – although one party host stood out amongst the rest: [a=David Mancuso] (The Loft parties).
The Loft was a musical center and mecca for DJs, a unique place and home of the original Record Pool. Here Krivit began his long time friendships with DJs [a=Larry Levan] and [a=François Kevorkian] (aka François K). In 1977 when he started DJ-ing at "Trude Heller's", he also become an avid roller skater, and with his girlfriend ([a=Daphne Rubin Vega]) would regularly skate over to the Paradise Garage club, where Levan would let them skate around the club while he would check out some of the new records that week on the sound system. The Garage remained Krivit's main stomping ground until it closed in 1987.
His list of DJ residencies steadily grew throughout the 1970s, and in 1979 after DJ-ing at the opening of the Roxy club, he became their resident DJ for the next 4 years. In the early 1980s the Roxy was the home of some of the best hip hop DJs of the day like "D.S.T", [a=Grand master Flash], and [a=Africa Bambaata]. Being one of the few white boys who could scratch mix, so he became known as 'Danny Rock'. Throughout the 1980s, the list of residencies and guest spots expanded: Area, Danceteria, The Ice Palace, Laces, The Limelite, Red Zone, Save The Robots, Studio 54 (Virgin Islands), Tracks, The Tunnel, The World, and many others, including one guest spot at the legendary Paradise Garage.
Since the late 1970s, trade magazines including Billboard, Cash Box, Record World, Dance Music Report, have listed Krivit's playlists and his mix tapes had been getting airplay on radio stations WBLS, WKTU, and Kiss FM.
The 1980s saw the beginning of Krivit's studio work, working on tracks featuring [a=James Brown] "Soul Power", "The Funky Drummer", "Give It Up And Turn It Loose"; [a=Ecstacy, Passion & Pain] "Touch And Go"; [a=Brenda And The Tabulations] "Let's Go All the Way"; and [a=Gloria Gaynor] "Casanova Brown".
Moving into the 1990s, he inherited The Ninth Circle club night and continued to DJ at clubs such as Mars, Tracks, The Choice, Palladium, Shelter, Sound Factory Bar, Twilo, and The Warehouse. At its inception in 1996, Krivit joined Francois K and [a=Joe Claussell] at their "Body And Soul" night at club Vinyl and has regularly DJ'd both in the US and internationally in various clubs in New York, Japan, and elsewhere. In 1999, a highlight was DJ-ing for a 15,000 strong crowd at one of three special Body And Soul event for "Summer Stage" in Central Park, NYC.
Krivit's reputation outside New York has grown immensely, and he has hosted two special international Body And Soul events in London for "Angels Of Love" and [a=Giorgio Armani]. His reputation continues to grow, much like his 50,000-strong record collection, and he also VJ's with a collection of over 5000 rare soul performances on video from the 1960s to 1990s.
Married to [a=Akemi Kakihara] aka [a=AK (2)].

artist.name: Larry Levan
artist.profile:
American DJ and producer. Born: 20 July 1954 in Bedford Stuyvesant, Brooklyn, NYC, NY. Died: 8 November 1992 in Beth Israel Medical Center, Manhattan, NYC, NY (aged 38).
Levan is revered primarily as the DJ and driving force of the famous gay-friendly disco [l=Paradise Garage]. With engineer [a=Richard Long], he custom-designed the Garage's sound system and DJ booth, complete with audiophile Thorens turntables.
Levan's brilliance lay not only in his technical skill and audio expertise, but also in his eclectic taste. He broadened the 'rules' of what dance music could be, mixing everything from gospel, reggae, Philly soul and Euro-disco to rock (e.g. [a=Stevie Nicks] "Stand Back", and [a=The Who] "Eminence Front"), post-punk (e.g. [a=The Clash] "The Magnificent Seven", and many [a=Talking Heads] tracks), ambient/environmental music (e.g. [a=Klaus Schulze] and [a=Manuel Gottsching]) and just about everything else. He augmented his sets with disorienting sound effects and audio manipulations, working the crossover and balance controls to throw sound around the room as if it had a will of its own.
Cutting his musical teeth at The Loft; essentially the first underground after-hours disco, which was started by [a=David Mancuso] at the advent of the 1970s. The Loft combined psychedelic culture with proto-disco music, which then consisted of long-form, psychedelic-influenced soul (e.g. [a=Booker T & The MG's] "Melting Pot", [a=The Temptations] "Papa Was a Rolling Stone", et al.), jazz-funk (e.g. [a=The Blackbyrds]), funky rock (e.g. [a=Barrabas] "Woman") and trippy head music like [a=Pink Floyd] "Dark Side Of The Moon". When club Paradise Garage opened in 1976, Levan added gospel and R&B-flavored disco to his musical menu.
After the Garage closed in 1987, Levan kept a considerably lower profile, doing guest spots at various clubs, including Studio 54, Palladium, and Mars, and DJ-ing regularly at The Choice – arguably the inheritor of the Garage's underground legacy. The Choice didn't have the grandeur of the Garage, but Levan made it his home, casting his psychedelic spell on a diverse crowd of devoted Garage fans and various other after-hours types.
Levan was voluntarily hospitalized at Beth Israel Medical Center after returning to New York following a Japanese tour. Three days after being released into the care of his mother, he was re-hospitalized at Beth Israel with hemorrhage symptoms. He died, aged 38, of heart failure caused by endocarditis at 18:15 EST, Sunday 8 November 1992.

artist.name: Fretless AZM
artist.profile: Producer from Cowes on the Isle Of Wight.

artist.name: Flare
artist.profile: Alias of Japanese producer [a=Ken Ishii]. First used in 1995.

artist.name: Locust
artist.profile: Mark Van Hoen project which often includes guest performers. Located in Los Angeles, California.

artist.name: Jake Slazenger
artist.profile: Born: 1971 in Wimbledon, United Kingdom

artist.name: Silent Poets
artist.profile: Japanese electronic solo project (former duo).

artist.name: Steve Alexander
artist.profile: Steve Alexander is a drummer and composer, who was born on November 20, 1962. He has worked with artists like [a=Jeff Beck] and [a=Duran Duran] in the past.

artist.name: UFO!
artist.profile: Drum & Bass dj and producer from San Francisco.

artist.name: Subcode
artist.profile: Drum & bass producer from Los Angeles.

artist.name: Juju
artist.profile:
California-born artist Juju aka Neil Zarama started his DJ career as a dancehall DJ in 1992. Soon he had his first DJ residency in San Francisco at the first drum'n'bass club called "The Jungle" in 1994. By 1996 he became a co-founder of the [a=Phunckateck] collective. He also held residencies in Hong Kong, Venezuela and his own weekly club “Phuturo” which ran for 10 years in the Lower Height District of San Francisco. By the mid 2000s, Juju had been signed by several top UK Labels and also had founded 3 of his own record labels running out of his Phuturo music studio in San Francisco. He was one of the leading American drum and bass artists touring extensively worldwide and the Phuturo weekly Club in San Francisco ran for 10 years straight, seeing hundreds of top global artists throughout the years. He retired from music around 2010, not before leaving his mark during the early years of Dubstep with collaborations with Don Carlos (Black Uhuru) from Jamaica on his imprint Narco Hz.
Neil Zarama is currently a Senior UX Producer still living in San Francisco building old motorcycles as hobby.

artist.name: Future Beat Alliance
artist.profile:
Matthew Puffett's production career began in 1996 with a string of releases on Void Records, under aliases including Mode-M, Soul Electrik, and the pseudonym that would go on to host the majority of his work: Future Beat Alliance, where he continued to record under this moniker & released music for some of the most respected electronic labels across Europe including Tresor, Delsin, Rush Hour & Versatile.
​Matthew has also been the main Programmer & Engineer for James Lavelle`s UNKLE project since 2012, The body of work includes co-writing & programming UNKLE's 5th studio album, The Road Part I , Various film projects & advertising campaigns including Paul Bettany’s directorial debut Shelter , H&M`s winter fall 2012 campaign, scoring music for the short film "Erebus" & also remixing high profile bands including Queens Of The Stone Age, Bombay Bicycle Club, London Grammar, Mark Lanagen, The Duke Spirit & Ten Fe, During this period UNKLE were winners of the MPG Remixer Of The Year award in 2016 & 2018.

artist.name: Domu
artist.profile: [a=Dominic Stanton]'s main alias for his broken beat productions.

artist.name: Kaidi Tatham
artist.profile:
British multi-instrumentalist, producer/remixer, DJ, and artist.
Once nicknamed the 'Herbie Hancock of the UK' by DJ [a=Benji B] due to his keyboards prowess, Tatham is a true innovator and virtuoso in sound production, as one of the original creators of the West London broken beat sound.
Born and raised in Redditch, just south of Birmingham, UK and classically trained from a young age, Tatham moved to London in 1993 to study for a BTEC in Performing Arts. Here he met [a=Kester Lydon], a fellow keyboard player, who went on to connect him to a new world of musical minds and ideas. Of many people he was introduced to, [a=Jake Wherry] of [a=The Herbaliser] was his first performance credit on release "[url=https://www.discogs.com/master/353042-The-Herbaliser-The-Real-Killer-Blowin-It]Blowin' It [/url]" (1995), and while spending time at Lydon and Wherry's West London studios, Tatham met some of the original members of what went on to become [a=Bugz In The Attic] and 'the rest is history' as they say.
BITA member [a=Alex Phountzi] remembers, 'I met Kaidi when I was 19 years old. We were working on an early track from our [a=Neon Phusion] project and Kaidi, who was working in [a=The Herbaliser]'s studio next door, came in to check out what we were up to. I think we needed a new bass line on a track we were working on, Kaidi put his hands on a keyboard and started playing something and locked in immediately. We all clicked musically and as friends, and at this point, the [a=Bugz In The Attic] crew was just taking shape.' Immersing himself in this newfound creative scene, Tatham soon went on to make another invaluable connection through fellow BITA member [a=Mark Lipka] (aka [a=Mark Force] and [a=G Force]), who connected him with [a=Dego] ([l=Reinforced Records] co-founder and one half of [a=4Hero]) where the two formed a strong musical bond, collaborating to this day.
Along with his earlier work with BITA and continued work with Dego and extended [l=2000 Black] connections, he also DJ's globally, and has done pioneering work for London production outfits like [a=DKD], [a=Silhouette Brown], [a=Blakai], [a=Likwid Biskit], [a=Neon Phusion], and under [a=Agent K] (his own alias); quietly proving himself as one of the most influential British musicians of his time.
Over the years, his musical prowess has gone-on to bless many projects, including with DJ [a=Jazzy Jeff], and [a=Andrew Ashong], along with session work for artists such as [a=Amy Winehouse], [a=Mulatu Astatke], [a=Slum Village], [a=Soul II Soul], and [a=Leroy Burgess], as well as [l=First Word] label mates such as [a=Eric Lau], [a=Children Of Zeus], and [a=Darkhouse Family], with later work on labels like [l=Eglo Records], [l=First Word Records], and [a=Theo Parrish]'s [l=Sound Signature], et al.
His second solo album "[m=199306]" (2008), and the first under his own name following [a=Agent K]'s "[m=204655]" (2002), pushes the musical boundaries of electronic dance music rarely heard today, retaining its contemporary London influences it allowed Tatham to stretch out musically in a way he hadn’t done on record before, with the majority of the tracks not in the standard 4/4 time signature that most contemporary dance music follows, and some switch between a handful of different time signatures over the course of a few minutes, his virtuosity never gets in the way of coherent groove, melody, harmony, and arrangement. Originally released on Japanese label [l=Freedom School], from Tokyo, and recorded on a modest set up at his flat in south London, it became a holy grail record for dance music fans and jazz heads alike, with its mythical status spurred-on by not being available digitally until 2020.
Influencing countless musicians/producers who heard the album, in more recent years Tatham's efforts there have been felt outside his immediate musical realm, epitomised when track "Orbit" from album "[m=204655]" (2002) was sampled on [a=Freddie Gibbs] & [a=Madlib]'s track "[m=1516239]" (2019) from album "[m=1571782]" (2019). Madlib said, 'Kaidi was ahead of his time with that album, and he still is. I love that loop and I’m happy we could use it. Kaidi is my brother, he loves what we do, and we love him.'
Along with more 12"/singles projects, he continues to grow on later albums. From "[m=2198146]" (2018), on to "[m=2198146]" (2021) (via "[m=2389594]" (2021) with cohort [a43597]), and latterly with "[m=2746763]" (2022).

artist.name: Fourth World
artist.profile: Formed in 1990, Fourth World features the legendary Brazilian jazz percussionist Airto Moreira, the six-octave voice of Flora Purim, guitarist José Neto, keyboardist/flutist Jovino Santos Neto and Gary Brown on bass and back-up vocals.

artist.name: Electric Soul
artist.profile: American DJ and producer. Born in 1961.

artist.name: Aaron Munson
artist.profile: Aaron Munson, best known as the Canadian member of [a=ilkae], uses his birth name primarily for Mastering and Production work on other artist's releases, as well as attribution for his work on independent video games. Aaron Munson has been the curator of [l=Eerik Inpuj Sound] since 2011, when he assumed the role from from his ex-merck label-mate [a=Proswell]. For Audio/Visual work by the Canadian experimental filmmaker of the same name, see [a5133866].

artist.name: Xhale
artist.profile:
Experimental electronic project from Norway.
Other unlisted releases: 1999 - Genious ([l=Miasmah Music], Mia001) [* released in .XM file format] 1999 - Distance Farm ([l=Miasmah Music], Mia004) [* released in .XM file format]

artist.name: Nicky Blackmarket
artist.profile:
Major UK drum & bass / jungle DJ.
Nicky Blackmarket is one of the original pioneers of drum and bass, and he is still the most faithful soldier for the scene around the world.
His early influences came from his mother's background in the jazz scene, and it is improvisation and variety that characterise his DJ performances throughout his career. At the start of the eighties, Nicky was 14 and playing electro at youth clubs in London. He played with long-time collaborator Clarky and began to mix all forms of dance music, at house parties and on the radio with Star FM. He left Star and joined Friends FM, run by Mad B, which gave Nicky a good education and the momentum to start his own shop in London. At this time he played under the name DJ Magical E. In 1990, he and partner [a=David Piccioni], bought [l=Black Market Records (4)] in Soho. In 1992, as the house scene moved towards breakbeat, Nicky created a separate department downstairs to cater for the sound that would eventually be called Drum and Bass.
In the mid-nineties Nicky pushed the hardcore scene with a regular show on Pulse FM and performances at the Roller Express. He moved into production, making his first record "Spam EP", along with various remixes and releases on his label [l=Gyroscope Records]. He also hosted shows on Eruption FM around this time.
As the jungle scene exploded Nicky formed his legendary partnership with the late Stevie Hyper D. These performances are still regarded as the standard by which all others are judged, and mp3s of those sets will probably be on the internet forever.
Blackmarket Records supported the scene as a focus for the community, an essential component in an world without the internet. Nicky started another label [l=Kartoons] featuring the anthem "Two Degrees", confirming his status as a central force in the scene.
His daughter is now DJing drum 'n' bass under the name DJ Millz.

artist.name: Brockie
artist.profile:
UK drum & bass producer and DJ from Hackney, London. He began DJing rare groove on the BBM sound system and on Eastman's sound system.
He has hosted a show on Kool FM since 1991, and co-owns Undiluted Records with Ed Solo.
Resident DJ at Jungle Fever.

artist.name: Phantasy
artist.profile: * Disco producer (not to be confused with UK Hardcore / Drum & Bass [a=DJ Phantasy]

artist.name: Pram
artist.profile: Post-rock band from Birmingham, England, incorporating elements of dissonance, dub and electronica.

artist.name: Four Tet
artist.profile:
British post-rock/electronic musician, DJ, producer, and label boss.
Born: September 2, 1977 in Putney, London, England, UK.
Main alias of [a=Kieran Hebden], who apart from being a recording artist, also runs his label [l=Text Records]. Starting out in post-rock band [a=Fridge] in 1994, he later moved into more electronic sounds under name Four Tet. Hebden is also known for his extensive work with jazz drummer [a=Steve Reid (2)], late in the drummer's life.

artist.name: John McEntire
artist.profile: Multi-instrumentalist, engineer, and mixer born in Portland (OR, USA) on the 9th of April 1970. He's now living in Chicago (IL, USA). His first instrument was the drums. He did a percussion major at Oberlin College (OH, USA) in 1987 and later Technology in Music and Related Arts at the same school. In 1988 he joined his first band [a311148] but left in 1989 to join [a=Bastro]. In 1991 he relocated to Chicago (IL, USA) together with [a=David Grubbs], another core member of [a=Bastro], where they renamed the band to [a=Gastr Del Sol] after some changes in the line-up of the band. After the debut of [a=Gastr Del Sol] the band became more of a collaboration with [a=David Grubbs] and [a=Jim O'Rourke]. He then shifted focus to his band Mosquito (founded in 1989) which was renamed to [a=Tortoise] in 1993. In 1998 [a=Gastr Del Sol] came to an end. In 1993 he also founded [a50982]. From the mid-nineties he started to do more engineering work while working with bands like [a=Stereolab], [a=Eleventh Dream Day] and [a=Trans Am (2)]. He built his own studio [l269529] (Chicago, IL, USA). In 1999 he also started to compose film music.

aliases.name: 1987 Oregon State Rudimentry Snare Drum Champion
John Bondage

artist.name: Labradford
artist.profile: Labradford is a U.S. post-rock musical group from Richmond, Virginia, founded in 1991. They have released six full albums from 1993 to 2001. Though not disbanded, its members have since been active with separate projects.

artist.name: Oval
artist.profile: Founded in Darmstadt, Germany in 1991 by Markus Popp, Sebastian Oschatz, Frank Metzger and Holger Lindmüller. Holger Lindmüller left about 1993, and Oval became a trio, then Sebastian Oschatz and Frank Metzger left in 1995, and since that Oval became a solo project of Markus Popp. For copyright credits, see [l462181].

artist.name: Andy Votel
artist.profile:
Andrew Shallcross, better known as Andy Votel (November 4, 1975, Manchester, UK) is an English producer and musician who co-operates [l=Finders Keepers Records], [l216214] & [l398688].
Votel name derived from the acronym VOTEL for [a3422938], a hip-hop crew on the Manchester scene at the time of early releases on the Fat City and now defunct Grand Central imprints.
Andy Votel is the long-time partner of [a=Jane Weaver].

artist.name: Jim O'Rourke
artist.profile:
Jim O'Rourke (January 18, 1969, Chicago, Illinois) is a Tokyo-based American performer, composer and record producer.
O'Rourke collaborated with a long list of musicians including [a=Illusion Of Safety], [a=Christoph Heemann], [a=David Grubbs], [a=Loren Connors], [a=Wilco], [a=Henry Kaiser], [a=K.K. Null], [a=Günter Müller], [a=Voice Crack], [a=Evan Parker], [a=Phill Niblock], [a=Mats Gustafsson], [a=Tony Conrad], [a=Faust], [a=Space Streakings], [a=Loose Fur], [a=Sonic Youth], [a=Fennesz], [a=Diskaholics Anonymous Trio], [a=Carlos Giffoni], [a=Keiji Haino], [a=Masami Akita], [a=Masaya Nakahara], [a=Takehisa Kosugi], [a=FIRE!], [a=Akira Sakata], [a=Oren Ambarchi] (among others...)
O'Rourke scored several films and ran [l=Moikai] & [l=Dexter's Cigar] labels.

artist.name: Spiritualized
artist.profile: British space rock band founded in 1990 in Rugby, Warwickshire. Spiritualized has always been Jason Pierce plus a rotating cast of supporting musicians.

artist.name: Alan Braxe & Fred Falke
artist.profile: French electronic music producer duo.

artist.name: Hans Nieswandt
artist.profile:
German DJ since 1987, Producer and author.
He was born 1964 in Mannheim, Germany.

artist.name: Negrocan
artist.profile: Negrocan is a multi-ethnical group that comprises Andres Lafone (from Uruguay; bass, vocals), Liliana Chachion (from Brazil; vocals), Davide Giovaninni (from Italy; drums, vocals), Neil Angilly (from England; keyboards) and Carlos Fuentes (from Chile; percussion). The band was formed by Andres Lafone in London in 1991. They performed at the WOMAD festival in 1992.

artist.name: Gusto
artist.profile: Born in the Bronx, New York, Ed Greene started rapping in the mid-1980's, establishing and running the [l=Soul Power Records (2)] label, before moving over to house music in the early 1990's. Prior to attaining success with his "Disco's Revenge" track he worked as a residential specialist helper for people with disabilities.

artist.name: Grant Nelson
artist.profile: Prolific producer, remixer and DJ from London, born 27 April 1971. Produced under the name [a=Wishdokta] from 1990 to 1993 when he was making breakbeat hardcore/techno for [l=Kickin Records]. Heralded as an influential producer on what became the UK garage scene due to his numerous releases on [l=Nice 'N' Ripe], a label he co-founded in 1993. In 1997 he started using the alter-ego [a=Bump & Flex] to release 2-step garage. Nelson has remained a house/garage producer since his early Nice 'N' Ripe days and continues to do so with his [l=Swing City] (1995-) and [l=Freeze Dried] (2012-) labels. Partner of [a=Kate Ross]. Cousin of [a=David Thackeray].

artist.name: The Plastic Avengers
artist.profile:
Plastic Avengers is George Thomson Hailing from Edinburgh, George is fast becoming a household name in the capital of Scotland for his production work and his exemplary DJ skills at Edinburgh's Emporium of House, 'Tribal Function'.
Spurred on by the Chicago sounds of DJ Sneak, Derrick Carter and Gene Farris, George has become an ambassador for the American sound. His club night Tribal Function has been running for ten years and production-wise, George records as Plastic Avengers for NRK. Three EPs have now been released under this guise, namely "Sideburns EP", "Oddsox EP", "All I Want" and "The Strutt" as well as a CD compilation of the EPs greatest moments, "42 Minutes Of Plastic". Plastic Avenger¹s debut album "Space Grafitti", is out now. The album is a collection of 13 brand new tracks that span filtered disco, electro, tech-house and deeplatin grooves. The album is already a favourite with press and radio across Europe, and its release will see the Plastic Avengers name and profile raise substantially.
George has also honed a production career in North America and Canada by recording for Stickman Records and Aquarius records. He has also remixed for Sham's Jinx label. George also records as Shutterbug for Quality Control and has remixed for Sneak's Defiant label, Glasgow Underground and Monkey Fruit. He counts Ian Pooley and DJ Sneak as big fans of his work, and with mates like that, George Thomson is set for greater things. (taken from the NRK Sound Division website)

namevariations.name: George
George T For The Plastic Avengers
George T.
George Thomson

artist.name: Buck
artist.profile: Rock artist on the 1996 compilation Deep Volume I.

artist.name: Deep Burial
artist.profile:
Instrumental hip hop project.
Originally a solo project by Richie Howard. Deep Burial (Howard) released his debut album, "Blood Samples", on Ultramack in 1998. George Brennan joined shortly after and the duo (akas Evil Dick da Mentalist and Buck Horrors) released "The Undead EP" on Fresh Killer in 2001. They released a second and final full-length, "Black Music," on Penny Dreadful in 2006. They broke up in 2008 and Brennan went on to form [a1601091].

artist.name: Tim Song Jones
artist.profile: American boom operator, sound designer, electronic musician, composer, and engineer who worked at [l263971]. Born August 27, 1957 in Knoxville, Tennessee, he was raised in San Diego and received a B.A. in Composition from the University of California San Diego. He moved to Los Angeles in 1988 to work in feature film post production.

artist.name: Minimal Vision
artist.profile: Italian house project.

artist.name: Planet 6
artist.profile: Collaboration between Spukkin Faceship and No.e Sunflowrfish, also known as Primordial Sound. Part of the S.P.A.Z. collective from the San Francisco Bay Area.

artist.name: Deanne Day
artist.profile: Artist name is a conflation of the initials of the two contributing musicians: "D and A"

artist.name: Atomic Dog
artist.profile: Another alias of T Power used for his jump up jungle releases on Deep Thought.

artist.name: Hijack
artist.profile: Jackin' / Fidget / Electro House project from the UK, initially comprising Jonathan Chant (Vukasin) and [a=Daniel Carro]. Daniel Carro left the project in the second half of 2008. Founded digital label [l=Jack Union Records] in 2007, and [l=Realoop Records] in August 2022. Contact: hijacktracks@gmail.com

artist.name: Mr. Scruff
artist.profile:
British DJ, producer, and artist.
Born: 10 February 1972 in Macclesfield, Cheshire, England, UK.
Carthy lives in Stockport, Greater Manchester, and studied fine art at the Psalter Lane campus of Sheffield Hallam University. Before he could make a living from his music alone, he worked as a shelf-stacker in the Burton Road branch of Kwik Save.
His DJ name was inspired by his scruffy facial hair, as well as his trademark loose-lined drawing style. He has been DJ-ing since 1994, at first in and around Manchester, then nationally, and internationally. Known for his marathon DJ-ing sets (often exceeding six hours), eclectic musical taste, love of a "nice cup of tea", and the quirky home-produced visuals and animations associated with his music.
In an interview Carthy said: "It’s about putting a lot of effort in and paying attention to detail. I get annoyed if I don’t take risks. I’m very hard on myself."

artist.name: Statik Sound System
artist.profile: Statik Sound System formed in the early part of 1994 after band members had been in a variety of noise, dub and breakbeat acts, Statik went on to become a premier act on Bristol’s own [l506]. They produced 2 albums, a Remix album, 6 singles/EPs and appeared on countless compilation albums such as [a1434]'s [r=2951]. They were remixed by the likes of Mr Scruff, Cut La Roc, Spring Heel Jack, Smith and Mighty, More Rockers, The Egg, Tao, Ian Simmonds, The Bullitnuts, Aim, and toured and played around the globe with a bizarre mixture of bands as diverse as Spiritualized, Black Francis, through to Receiver and Smith and Mighty. After putting together a studio in 1998 they released their last album – ‘[r=760186]’ in 2001.

artist.name: James Bong
artist.profile:
Trip hop / experimental music producer duo.
for drummer see [a=James Bong (2)] for techno artist see [a=James Bong (3)]

artist.name: J.M. Silk
artist.profile:
J.M. Silk is Steve "Silk" Hurley and Keith Nunnally.
[b]J.M. Silk[/b] stands for [b]J[/b]ack [b]M[/b]aster [b]Silk[/b]. As a DJ, Steve Hurley was known around 1983-1984 as [a=Steve "Silk" Hurley]. Starting his producer career, he thought about changing his name to the Chicago House styled [i]Jackmaster Silk[/i] alias. But [a=Farley Keith Williams], at that time Steve's roommate, changed his name from [a=Farley "Funkin" Keith] to [a=Farley "Jackmaster" Funk] a week before Steve was able to launch a radio promo tape as [i]Jackmaster Silk[/i].

artist.name: Daniel Wang
artist.profile: Chinese-American DJ, producer and label owner who releases music in the disco-house genre. Originally from Northern California, but residing in Berlin since 2003. He started [l127470] in 1993 from Chicago, and continued releasing Balihu Records from New York until about 2001. His more recent reissues and releases are now handled by Rush Hour in Amsterdam.

artist.name: Abfahrt Hinwil
artist.profile: Abfahrt Hinwil is Chris Cunningham ( AKA Point 7, owner of Toytronic and no relation to the music video director) and Martin Haidinger (AKA Gimmik). Num Num is another project of this same duo.

artist.name: M
artist.profile:
[b]M[/b] aka [a=Joachim Muno] and [a=Udo Niebergall], German [b]Techno/Hard Trance[/b] duo.
If looking for the artist who sang "Pop Muzik", please use [a=M (2)] as PAN.

artist.name: Num Num
artist.profile: Num Num was a project devised by Martin Haidinger and [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/Chris+Cunningham+(3)]Chris Cunningham[/url] to get around the exclusive contract restrictions placed upon Gimmik by Worm Interface records. Toytronic records spread the rumor that the project was both Martin and Chris but in fact all Num Num tracks were created by Martin alone. Besides helping the disinformation campaign, Chris’ involvement in the project was limited to helping Martin come up with the project name, which was inspired by the ‘birdy num num’ scene in the film [b][i]The Party[/b][/i] with Peter Sellers.

artist.name: Digitonal
artist.profile: Modern Classical & Electronica, accessible yet thoughtful & beautiful music. Violins, percussion and guest vocalists with electronics, Digitonal live shows are magnificent to say the least - famous festival appearances at The Big Chill, Mor, Cheltenham International Festival of Classical Music, the National Theatre, the ICA, and the British Arts Council Selector have cemented their position as one of the most respected live acts in the electronic world today.

artist.name: Darrell Fitton
artist.profile: Abstract, downtempo and IDM producer from Manchester, England. Active since 1994.

artist.name: Hell Interface
artist.profile: Alias used by duo Mike Sandison & Marcus Eoin (aka Boards Of Canada).

artist.name: DJ Puncho
artist.profile: Chicago juke producer.

artist.name: Dirty Harry
artist.profile: Another new beat formation featuring Praga Khan & Co.

artist.name: Arj Snoek
artist.profile: German House producer from Mönchengladbach who lived in Köln. He passed away in November 2025.

artist.name: DJ Wally
artist.profile: Underground Hip Hop & DnB producer from Queens, NY stormed the scene in 1995 with his signature gritty sample-based sound and was signed to the legendary Rawkus records in 1997. Wally is responsible for composing theme songs for various successful TV shows such as "Basketball Wives" and "Hip Hop Honors" on VH1 and "Gangland" on The History Channel.

artist.name: Jon Tye
artist.profile: Founder in 1995 and frontman of [l=Lo Recordings].

artist.name: Gorodisch
artist.profile: Gorodisch is guitarist Stephen Cracknell. Gorodisch released one mini album full of folky electronics for The Leaf Label before going on as (core member of) The Memory Band. The name Gorodisch (but with an extra C) is taken from Delacorta's novel and JJ Beneix film 'Diva'. Both Stephen Cracknell and this character share the love of a good sandwich.

artist.name: Goio
artist.profile:
Marko Laine is living in Finland and produces uncompromising dark techno music. He got picked up by Djax in 1995 and in 1996 his first record got released.
Marko is a producer that only believes in the underground. He started his own record shop because he was unsatisfied with the local record stores.

artist.name: Rob Tissera
artist.profile: House/hard dance DJ & producer, DJ tuition and studio engineer from Leeds, United Kingdom

artist.name: Electroliners
artist.profile: Dan Wherrett & Jim Hopkins

artist.name: Purveyors Of Fine Funk
artist.profile: Tracks produced by Dan Curtin, Mike Filly & Tatsuro Hayashi.

artist.name: Death In Vegas
artist.profile:
Electronic rock band from the United Kingdom - a brainchild of Richard Fearless (Richard Maguire). Over the years a lot of collaborators have come and gone, making Death In Vegas essentially Fearless' project.
Influenced by a wide range of musical genres including psychedelic rock, electronic, krautrock, dub and industrial, the band's sound has constantly been changing, moving between live rock sounds and electronic. The band was formed in London in 1994 by Fearless and Steve Hellier and signed to Concrete Records under the name of Dead Elvis. But objections from the [a=Elvis Presley] estate forced them to change their name, and Dead Elvis became the title of their first album instead.

artist.name: Yves Deruyter
artist.profile: Belgian DJ and producer born May 4, 1970 in Antwerp, Belgium. Started as a DJ in 1985, and as a producer in 1991.

artist.name: Manual
artist.profile: Manual aka Jonas Munk Jensen is a producer from Odense, Denmark and after more than seven years of playing guitar and piano he started to combine electronic with 'analog' instruments. Manual doesn't improvise very much (you can guess it from the name!), all his songs are composed without using a computer. Manual uses old-school synthesizers and sometimes samples in combination with guitars and pianos to create well structured songs and soundscape-like compositions. Later he took artists like To Rococo Rot, Jim O'Rourke or Boards of Canada as a starting point and since his sound has evolved to a strong and fragile character of its own.

artist.name: Sarcoblast
artist.profile: Duo from UK.

artist.name: Denki Groove
artist.profile: Japanese synthpop group formed in 1989.

artist.name: Acorn Arts
artist.profile: DJs and Progressive House and Techno producers duo from UK. They are the founders of [l=X-Gate Records].

artist.name: Mijk Van Dijk
artist.profile:
German (his father is Dutch) producer since 1990, DJ since 1992, working in the German club Dubmission (Berlin), but also worldwide. He performs Live Acts as [a=Microglobe] since 1991.
Brother of Matthias van den Nieuwendijk ([a=DJ Mat Mushroom]).

artist.name: Bizz O.D.
artist.profile: Artist based in Germany.

artist.name: Christopher Just
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Vienna, Austria. Brother of [a=Raphael Just].

artist.name: Co-Fusion
artist.profile: Japanese duo of DJ Wada and Heigo Tani, one of the pioneering acts of the Japanese techno scene. They went on a hiatus after 2009, but returned ten years later with a series of new releases, teaming up with a younger producer, Toiza71. Co-Fusion stands for Community Fusion.

artist.name: Schatrax
artist.profile: Producer from the Isle Of Wight (UK) who owned the [l=Schatrax] and [l=Forever Grey Records] labels.

artist.name: Adam Beyer & Lenk
artist.profile: Swedish duo.

artist.name: Jim Masters
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Cambridge. Worked for [l=Ministry Of Sound] from 1991 to 1996. Ran [l=Open].

artist.name: A Number Of Names
artist.profile: Early 80s electro/disco group from Detroit, MI, USA.

artist.name: Martin Circus
artist.profile: French band formed in early 1969 playing psychedelic/progressive rock, changing to pop in 1971 and moving with the trends of disco and new wave later.

artist.name: Convextion
artist.profile: DJ and Producer from Dallas, Texas USA. Born in 1972. Main styles are Deepness, Minimalistic and Soul. Co-founded Hardsync magazine with [a123564] in 1994. Following his first 12" release in 1995 he released 7 more EP's, after which he withdrew from the industry until 2006.

artist.name: Reade Truth
artist.profile: One of New York's early pioneers of techno and DJ culture, Reade Truth has been an integral part of the cities' techno history. His first of over two dozen releases came out in 1994 on the Brooklyn underground acid label, Direct Drive. Since then, his releases have made many a mark on labels like Planet E, Strictly Rhythm, Sonic Groove, Instinct, Pomelo, Wurst, N-Syde, Path Records and so on.

artist.name: Freestyle Orchestra
artist.profile: American house and garage project by Little Louie Vega, who worked with Todd Terry a lot around 1990. Little Louie used this name again in 1998, but now together with his partner Kenny Dope Gonzalez, for a release on their Masters At Work Label.

artist.name: Mutiny
artist.profile: DJ and producer duo.

artist.name: Stephanie Mills
artist.profile: Stephanie Mills is an American Rhythm & Blues, Soul, Disco and Gospel singer and songwriter, born on March 22, 1957, in Brooklyn, New York, U.S. Mills began her career in 1966, when she received a role in the Broadway play Maggy Flynn. In 1973, Mills signed at [l=Paramount Records] by [a=Michael Barbiero], releasing her first single. Her role as Dorothy in "The Wiz" made her a star. She has been produced by [a=James Mtume & Reggie Lucas], [a=Ashford & Simpson], [a=George Duke], [a=Angela Winbush], [a=Gene Griffin], [a=Nick Martinelli], [a=Marc Gordon] and [a=Gerald Levert], [a=LeMel Humes], [a=Robert Brookins], [a=Paul Laurence], [a=Wayne Brathwaite], [a=Russell Simmons] etc. She has dueted with [a=Teddy Pendergrass] ("Two Hearts", 1981).

artist.name: Kenlou
artist.profile: [b]KenLou[/b] stands for [b]Ken[/b]ny Gonzalez & [b]Lou[/b]ie Vega. Original KenLou releases from I to IV are on orange label with the MAW totem logo. Represses are on light blue labels.

artist.name: Frankie Feliciano
artist.profile: DJ and producer from New York. Ran [l=Ricanstruction Records] (1997) and [l=Ricanstruction Label] (2003 to 2005). Current label: Ricanstruction Brand.

artist.name: Benji Candelario
artist.profile: Began his career working on edits at [l=Shakedown Sound Studios]

artist.name: Unreleased Project
artist.profile: Masters At Work used this name on six releases on their MAW Records label. All releases contain unreleased Masters At Work productions or unreleased remixes of tracks which were previously released on their own or other labels.

artist.name: Dennis Ferrer
artist.profile: DJ and producer from New York. Founded [l=Sfere] with [a=Kerri Chandler] in 1998. Runs [l=Objektivity] (formerly a Sfere sub-label). Mentored [a=The Martinez Brothers]. Based in Union City, New Jersey.

artist.name: Points Proven
artist.profile: Members are Dennis McFarlane, Billy Ntimih and Kevin Tompkins.

artist.name: Crossfire
artist.profile: Crossfire started producing music at age of 17. After a couple of years, of complete focus, he finally got some recognition from infamous [a=DJ Trace] ([l=DSCI4]). [a=DJ Trace] signed his track "Hydra" (on [r=52393]) for his label, [l=DSCI4]. The track "Hydra" crawled into the playlists of [a=Ed Rush & Optical], [a=D. Kay], [a=Black Sun Empire], [a=Paul B] and many others. Quickly a release on [a=Ryme Tyme]'s [l=1210] label followed with the tunes "Hideout" and "Swordfight" ([r=76357]). Soon thereafter [a=DJ Trace] decided to give [a=Crossfire] his own 12" on [l=DSCI4], "Counterforce" and "Firebolt" ([r=125946]). The tune "Counterforce" also got played out a lot by the ones like: [a=DJ Bailey], [a=Loxy], [a=DJ Ink], [a=Klute], [a=Paul B] etc. Bookings came in and the name [a=Crossfire] started to ring a bell in the mind of a lot of drum&bass lovers. He played in Swizz, Austria, Germany, Holland, France, Italy, UK, Estonia, Portugal, Hungary, Czech, Slovakia, Poland, Italy, Belgium, Moscow, Latvia...

artist.name: Mark Taylor
artist.profile: [b]For the British keyboardist, see [a=Mark Taylor (7)] who also worked for [a=Belinda Carlisle] For the British jazz drummer, see [a=Mark Taylor (10)][/b] British record producer and songwriter born in Windsor, England. Worked With [a=Cher] and [a=Enrique Iglesias]. Associated with producer [a=Terry Adams]. Worked at [l=Studio House, Wraysbury].

artist.name: ADULT.
artist.profile:
ADULT. is the Detroit duo Nicola Kuperus and Adam Lee Miller. They played their first lives show together in Germany in 1997 under the name [a=Artificial Material]. In early '98 they released their first 12" under the moniker [a=Plasma Co.] Later that year, they released their first 12" under the name ADULT. Currently, they have released 5 albums and 19 EPs or singles on [l=Ghostly International], [l=Thrill Jockey], [l=Clone] and on their own label [l=Ersatz Audio], (which currently has over 40 releases since '95) They have remixed over 20 acts as well, including [a=Tuxedomoon], [a=John Foxx], [a=Death in Vegas] and [a=Pet Shop Boys] (for Moog Music). Before forming ADULT., Miller was half of the band [a=Le CAR].
Kuperus and Miller also make sculpture, paintings, films, photographs, performances, videos and installations. They strive to intersect the lines between art and audio. They have exhibited their work at institutions such as the Austrian Cultural Forum (NY), Mattress Factory (Pittsburgh), Detroit Institute of Arts (Detroit), MOMAS (Saitama, Japan), and Centre d'art contemporain de Meymac (FR). They have shown their film The Three Grace(s) triptych at places such as Anthology Film Archives (NY), Distrital Film Festival (Mexico City), and Grey Area for Art and Technology (SF).

artist.name: G.D. Luxxe
artist.profile: Vienna-based Gerhard Potuznik has as many side projects. He has recorded solo as Potuznik, Gerhard Deluxxe, and G. D. Luxxe, is also a member of Maeuse, Private Lightning Six, and Cube & Sphere, and has collaborated with an extensive list of electronic artists including such luminaries as Patrick Pulsinger, Tunakan, and Solvent.

artist.name: Prefuse 73
artist.profile: Music producer, who has been based in Atlanta, Barcelona and New York.

artist.name: Chris Morris
artist.profile:
British comedian, writer, director, actor, voice actor, and producer.
Born: 15 June 1962 in Colchester, Essex, England, UK.

artist.name: Tripform
artist.profile: Electronic music duo based in Santa Barbara, CA consisting of Pablo Manzarek and Norman Reed. Tripform's earliest releases were made by Pablo Manzarek working solo.

artist.name: Adrien75
artist.profile: Electronic musician from Bend, OR, United States.

artist.name: AEC
artist.profile: Tyler Newman (Informatik) and Stacey Purcell

artist.name: Etherealities
artist.profile: Dub artist based in London, UK.

artist.name: S.E.T.I.
artist.profile: Back in 1992 "S.E.T.I." stood for the ‘Search for Extraterrestrial Intelligence’, however in 2024 [url=https://www.discogs.com/artist/33339-Lagowski?anv=Andrew%20Lagowski]Andrew Lagowski[/url] declared that it now represents the ‘Self Extermination of Terrestrial Inhabitants’.

artist.name: Lobe
artist.profile:
Since his early days ('88) at the Pelican club in Aberdeen, Ian Hartley has been experimenting with sound, texture and melody. A graduate of Edinburgh's Blue Room scene he has retained his 'career' as a psychiatric nurse, a fact that grounds the man (he is modesty incarnate) and tempers his response to the praise which has been heaped upon him from certain quarters.
Lobe was founded in 1995.

artist.name: Supanova
artist.profile: House music act.

artist.name: Big Hair
artist.profile:
Big Hair's patent brand of tech-house funk with a twist of wry humour has won them many fans. Chris and Aaron started Big Hair in 95, and started releasing on Kontraband in 97. They swiftly received dancefloor recognition with such Tech House anthems as Dougie At the Ockie, their ‘le grande boofont’ work on Wiggle, their string of remixes including the Manor Maus mix for Terry Francis and Nathan Coles, and collaborations with Ben Chapman (for Visitor, Belgium) and Nathan Coles (for Surreal).
Big Hair's own sound - grounded in good ol' 4/4 beats - is surrounded by a palette of different influences including afro beat, tech-house, chicago house, funk, hip hop and various charity shop oddities. Their recent album 'Imagine Being Chased by a One Man Band', covers the whole spectrum over it's 12 tracks. With a conscious aim to challenge the preconception of a 'dance' album, Big Hair created a parallel world, fuelled with dancefloor dynamite - broken up with psychedelic interludes and sleazy instrumental segways. "Imagine Being Chased By A One Man Band' remains funky and entertaining to the last.
Recently, the duo have signed to u-freqs, a new label with a music policy aimed to freq out both the dance floor and the bedroom listener. Already causing quite a stir, with releases and remixes from the duo’s other, more dancefloor monikers, Le Grande boofont., and with Big Hair collaborations with Dave Coker (Pig Bwoy, Smart Alec) and Timmy S (Surreal), the future of Big Hair noises looks blissfully twisted. The first 3 releases have won many fans already including Terry Francis, Seb Fontaine, Laurent Garnier, Eddie Richards, Kenny Hawkes and Nathan Coles.
Alongside their production duties, live is where Big Hair need to be experienced. Working back to back alongside DJs, with a blend of laptops, FX, decks, sequencers, live MCs and video, the Hair have been regularly rocking The End with their bi-monthly live spots at Subterain, and with the Underground Freqs crew (Tak Records, DIY, Drop music) at a whole host of partys and club dates, including Turnmills, Jax, Nitsa, OTV, and Kozzmozz.

artist.name: Walter Wanderley
artist.profile: Walter Wanderley (May 12, 1932, Recife, Brazil - September 4, 1986, San Francisco, USA) was a Brazilian pianist, organist and arranger. He moved to São Paulo in 1947 where he reportedly learned to play organ with his aunt and also studied harmony at Lyceu de Artes. Wanderley started performing and recording professionally around 18. He recorded over 18 albums in Brazil. He gained notoriety during the 1960s with his band, Walter Wanderley e Seu Conjunto. He accompanied João Gilberto for whom he did arrangements for Gilberto's third album. Married to singer Isaura Garcia, Wanderley moved to the USA in 1965, invited by [a=Creed Taylor] to sign with [l=Verve Records] after [a=Tony Bennett] recommended him to Taylor. Walter's first album for Verve, with [a=José Marino] on bass and drummer [a=Claudio Slon] plus guests [a=Bucky Pizzarelli] and [a=Urbie Green], "Rain Forest," included the Billboard Top 50 hit "So Nice" (Summer Samba). After several albums for Verve (featuring such musicians as [a=Dom Um Romao], [a=Paulinho Magalhaes] and [a=Marcos Valle], he signed to A&M/CTI, recording albums orchestrated by [a=Don Sebesky] and [a=Eumir Deodato], featuring [a=João Palma], [a=Airto Moreira], [a=Hubert Laws] and [a=Marvin Stamm]. He later settled in California until he passed away after a long battle with bone cancer.

ワルター・ワンダレイ

artist.name: Boozoo Bajou
artist.profile: Downtempo and dubtronica duo from Nürnberg (Nuremberg, Germany). Active since 1997.

artist.name: Cal Tjader
artist.profile: American latin jazz musician, artist, and composer, born 16 July 1925 in St. Louis, Missouri, died 5 May 1982 in Manila, Philippines. He worked from the 1950s onwards until his death. Best known for his talents on the vibraphone, but also accomplished on drums and bongos.

artist.name: Montefiori Cocktail
artist.profile: Future Jazz / Easy Listening producer duo from Italy, formed by a couple of twins: Francesco (Kekko) on keyboards and Federico (Kikko) on saxophone, flute and voice. The two brothers, sons of saxophone player [a=Germano Montefiori], a talented 60’s and 70’s musician, before becoming Montefiori Cocktail, have worked separately. Francesco was a producer of house and breakbeat music, while Federico played saxophone in many bands, such as [a=Ladri di Biciclette] and [a2011936].

artist.name: Nicola Conte
artist.profile:
Latin-jazz artist, composer, producer, and DJ from Bari, Italy. Born in 1962 or 1964 in Bari, Apulia, Italy. He DJ's internationally and has compiled several compilations, most notably the [l=Viagem] and the [l=Deep, Afrocentric, Modal Jazz Series].
Apart from recording many albums of his own, Conte has recorded and produced for a great many other artists, including [a=Rosalia De Souza], [a=Barbara Dennerlein], [a=Stefania Di Pierro], and more. He also has several regular musicians he has worked with, including [a=Gianluca Petrella], [a=Fabrizio Bosso], [a=Pietro Lussu], [a=Sandro Deidda], [a=Pietro Ciancaglini], [a=Daniele Scannapieco], [a=Lorenzo Tucci], et al. Many of these artists are associated acts on his regular Italian label [l=Schema] of which many albums have received international distribution by the major [l=Universal Music Group] under several of their labels.
More recently, Conte has worked with [l806]. He has also been quite prolific remixing, providing several each year, including remixes for Japanese artist [a=Tatsuo Sunaga] (aka [a=Sunaga T Experience]) and for jazz band associates [a=The Five Corners Quintet].

artist.name: Zimpala
artist.profile:
Nu-Jazz, Downtempo Project directed by [a=BNX].
ZIMPALA band is : Noémie Brosset (Singer & Vocals) Anthon Giraud (Guitars) bordono (Programming / Rhodes / Soprano Sax) Arnaud Pierret (Bass) Ant-One (Drums) Fred BNX (A&R, DJ)

artist.name: Marcos Valle
artist.profile: Brazilian singer, songwriter and record producer. Born on 14 September 1943 in Rio de Janeiro. Brother of [A=Paulo Sérgio Valle]. He was married to the Brazilian vocalist [a=Anamaria Valle] from 1965 to 1969. Later married singer [a583054].

artist.name: Alphawezen
artist.profile: Electronic duo from Aachen, Germany founded in the early 2000s

artist.name: Q-Point
artist.profile: [b]For technical credits, please consider using [a=Q Point] in Hilversum[/b]

artist.name: Geb.el
artist.profile: Born in Austria. Currently resides in London.

artist.name: Naomi
artist.profile: German pop duo, based in Berlin.

artist.name: Dublex Inc.
artist.profile: Electronica DJ project from Stuttgart, Germany.

artist.name: Bad Meets Evil
artist.profile: [b]Bad Meets Evil[/b] is an American hip-hop duo composed of Detroit-based emcees [a=Royce Da 5'9"] (Bad) and [a=Eminem] (Evil). Bad Meets Evil was formed in 1999, thanks to the duo's mutual friend, Proof. The group's discography consists of one EP and four singles.

artist.name: Taxi
artist.profile: Taxi are Simon Lovejoy, Paul Cullen and Jo Laundy. They met in Norwich. Simon & Paul would write the music and Jo would sing. Alex Findlow was the owner of the studio in Norwich.

artist.name: Earthbound
artist.profile: Euro-/Progressive dance act from Borås, Sweden. Earthbound was originally three members: Anders Kilbrandt (Eriksson), Fredrik Johansson and Carloscar Andréasson. Carloscar Andréasson left the group after the first 2 singels.

artist.name: Stryke
artist.profile:
Techno & House Producer/DJ, hailing from Miami, Florida. Releases on labels such as Plastic City, Ovum, Tronic, Droid, & many more. He has produced & remixed such artists as Depeche Mode, Murk, Danny Howells, Nic Fanciulli, & hosts of others.
Publishing: [l=Stryke Songs]

artist.name: Aphrodite
artist.profile:
The Drum 'n' Bass DJ and producer Gavin King, who was born in Aberystwyth, Wales, but raised in Eltham, is one of the original rave scene and Jungle pioneers. He runs the label [l=Aphrodite Recordings] and has founded [l=Urban Takeover] with Michael Hearn aka [a=Micky Finn].
He took his artist name from the name of a house night he used to co-promote from 1988 1990 whilst at Warwick University. He held a regular Sunday show on pirate station Pulse FM until the station shut down in 1995.

artist.name: Le Knight Club
artist.profile: The band The Knight Club was founded in 1997 by Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo (1/2 Daft Punk) and by the founder of the hard house label Pumpking Records, Eric Chédeville (Rico The Wizard). A ‘filtered house‘ style centered around the built from disco/funk samples.

artist.name: Thomas Bangalter
artist.profile:
French musician, record producer, singer, songwriter, composer and DJ. Born in Paris on 3 January 1975 as son of [a683919] and [a2603400]. Married to French actress [url=https://www.discogs.com/artist/166722-Elodie-Bouchez]Élodie Bouchez[/url] with whom he has two sons: Tara-Jay (born 2002) and Roxan (born 2008).
Bangalter is most famous as one half of the duo [a=Daft Punk], which initially started out as [a225348], with a 3rd member playing the punky thrash that gave birth to the Daft Punk name. While the duo debuted with a single in 1993 and an album in 1995, Bangalter also did solo work, viz. the two famous Trax On Da Rocks EPs which blended various samples set to funky house and techno beats. He released Music Sounds Better With You on his own label [l=Roulé] as part of [a=Stardust] as well as his remix for Rock Shock by [a=Roy Davis Jr.]. In 1998, his track Gym Tonic was the center of controversy as Chris The French Kiss (Chris LeFriant, later known as [a=Bob Sinclar]) released it as a single (caving to the pressure of his distributor Warner) on his album Paradise (which was initially supposed to be a compilation/concept album of tracks produced anonymously by various artists, all hidden behind the moniker "Bob Sinclar") without clearning the samples & breaking a promise made to Thomas. This resulted in a lawsuit from fitness guru and actress Jane Fonda (whose voice had been illegally sampled on the record) & Thomas cutting all ties with Chris.
Keen on his privacy and anonimity, Bangalter nonetheless has given a few interviews in and out of the [a=Daft Punk] phenomenon. Although a lot of people credit him for the invention of 'filtered house', he has cited Throw by [a=Carl Craig] aka [a=Paperclip People] and [a=Kenny "Dope" Gonzalez] as precursors of the style. Outside Daft Punk, Bangalter used to DJ but he stopped his DJing career due to hearing issues. In 2022, he started his own [l3221819] label (its name is an anagram of Thomas Bangalter).

artist.name: Ontoni
artist.profile: DJ and producer from US

artist.name: George Morel
artist.profile: DJ and producer from New York. A&R for [a=Strictly Rhythm] from 1992. Established his own label [l=Groove On] in 1993.

artist.name: Sébastien Léger
artist.profile: French DJ and house music producer from Paris. Owner of the record label [l=Lost Miracle]. Previously founded the labels [l=Cyclik], [l=Mistakes Music] and [l=Temple Of Lions].

artist.name: Edu Lobo
artist.profile: Eduardo de Góes "Edu" Lobo (born August 29, 1943) is a Brazilian bossa nova singer, guitarist, and composer. He achieved fame in the 1960s as part of the bossa nova movement. His compositions include Pra Dizer Adeus (with Torquato Neto), Choro Bandido (with Chico Buarque), Beatriz (with Chico Buarque), Arrastão (with Vinicius de Moraes), Upa Neguinho (with Gianfrancesco Guarnieri) and Ponteio (with Capinam).

artist.name: Jamie Hodge
artist.profile: Originally a young 17 year old techno producer on Ritchie Hawtin's +8 label, Jamie has since released very few of his own works. Mostly they are very obscure, yet dazzling pieces of music, or in the case of the Conjoint sessions they are seminal incursions into the future of jazz. His love for old, rare vinyl and funk music brought him to start the re-issue label Aestuarium Records, which like Jamie has had few, but brilliant releases.

artist.name: The Bug
artist.profile: Electronic music producer from London, UK, influenced by grime, hiphop, ragga, dubstep and dub techno.

artist.name: Daniel Pemberton
artist.profile: British composer, born on 3 November 1977.

artist.name: Jupiter Jam
artist.profile: French rapper living in London, UK.

artist.name: Bowery Electric
artist.profile: American indie rock/shoegaze band founded in New York in 1994 and disbanded in 2000.

artist.name: Jerome Sydenham
artist.profile: DJ, producer and record label executive from New York. Former A&R at [a=Atlantic] and [l=EastWest] (under [a=Merlin Bobb]) and label manager at [l=Pallas Records]. Founded [l=Ibadan] in 1995, [l=Kraftmatic Records] in 2018 and [l=Royal Babylon] in 2021. Relocated to Berlin in the 2000s.

artist.name: Jerome Sydenham & Dennis Ferrer
artist.profile: DJ and producer duo.

artist.name: Imani Uzuri
artist.profile: American vocalist and composer raised in North Carolina.

artist.name: Seiji
artist.profile: Sometimes credited as Paul Seiji or Paul "Seiji" Dolby.

artist.name: Charles Dockins
artist.profile: DJ, producer and keyboardist from Baltimore, Maryland. Ran [l=Soulboy Records] in the late 1990s/early 2000s. Established the EarthShaker Digital platform in the early 2010s. His [a=Project 4007] alias and publishers [l=4007 Music] / [l=4007 Loch Raven Music] were named after his family home on 4007 Loch Raven Blvd in Baltimore. Possibly married to [a=Lynette Smith].

artist.name: Mijan
artist.profile: House vocalist. Born and raised in New York, now based in California. Previously lived and worked in Germany and Italy.

artist.name: Ursula Rucker
artist.profile: American female poet & activist, born and raised in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, USA, where she was a graduate of Temple University’s journalism program. Rucker had been writing poetry since adolescence but kept her writings to herself until she read poetry in 1994, at Philadelphia's Zanzibar Blue, which is credited as her debut. That same year, Rucker was invited to collaborate with numerous recording artists, and producers including Wax Tailor, King Britt, 4hero, Jamaaladeen Tacuma, Josh Wink, Alix Perez and most notably The Roots. Their collaboration The Unlocking led to subsequent invitations to close The Roots follow-up albums Illadelph Halflife and the certified gold record Things Fall Apart. Rucker garnered a reputation for unique poetry techniques and stage presence which were described as "strong, vulnerable, wounded and raging". King Britt gave Rucker recognition and invited her to create her first spoken-word recording, the 1994 club hit Supernatural. She appears in the 2008 film The Black Candle, directed by M.K. Asante, Jr. and narrated by Maya Angelou.

artist.name: Frank Bretschneider
artist.profile: Frank Bretschneider (born 1956 in Obercrinitz, East-Germany) lives and works as a composer in Berlin. Still in the GDR he founded the tape-label [l=klangFarBe]. Later he co-founded the [l=Raster-Noton] label in 1996.

artist.name: Pan Sonic
artist.profile:
Pan Sonic (known as Panasonic from 1993 until 1998) were an internationally renowned Finnish electronic music project consisting of [a4725], [a11329] and [a549915].
In 1994, they began releasing on [l985], the experimental techno label of their friend [a99452]. On their first tour outside of Finland, while in London, they got a record deal with [l2500] (the [l26391] sub-label). In 1995, their first release on Blast First was "Vakio" which received international recognition. In 1996, Sami Salo left Pan Sonic and the project continued as a duo until disbanding in 2009. The pair briefly reformed in 2015 to create the soundtrack for the documentary film [i]Atomin Paluu[/i] ("Return of the Atom").
[b]When credited as a company please use [l1165253][/b]

artist.name: Alva Noto
artist.profile: German artist, musician and label owner, born 18 September 1965 in Karl-Marx-Stadt. In 1994 he founded [l887200].

artist.name: Curd Duca
artist.profile: Curd Duca (born 14 March 1955) is an Austrian musician, composer and producer of electronic music.

groups.name: Auch Wenn Es Seltsam Klingen Mag
8 Oder 9

artist.name: Ester Brinkmann
artist.profile: Ester Brinkmann is the experimental project of Thomas Brinkmann.He uses his sister's name.

artist.name: All
artist.profile: Born Cologne, Germany ALL is a concept between M:I:5 and Gas

artist.name: Dettinger
artist.profile: Dettinger is a German producer signed to [l715], Cologne.

artist.name: Ultra-Red
artist.profile:
missionstatement In the world of modern electronic music and sound art, Ultra-red distinguish themselves for their intrepid blend of political commitment and innovative sound. Rejecting both self-satisfied formalism and convenient political posturing, Ultra-red have for over ten years pursued a precarious but dynamic exchange between art and political organizing. Collectively, the Los Angeles-based group has produced radio broadcasts, performances, recordings, installations and pubic space occupations (ps/o).
Working within a variety of urban ambiences, Ultra-red have investigated the spaces of needle exchange (Soundtrax, 1992 - 1996), public sex (Second Nature, 1995 - 1998), public housing (Structural Adjustments, 1997 - Present), resistance to global capital (Value System, 1998 - Present), labor (Social Factory, 1997 - 2002) and education (School of Echoes, 2001 - Present). Throughout each of these efforts, Ultra-red have pursued two aesthetic-political objectives: to explore acoustic space as enunciative of social relations and, secondly, to radicalize the conventions of electro-acoustic art.
Founded in 1994 by two AIDS activists, [a=Dont Rhine] and Marco Larsen, Ultra-red have expanded over the years to include activists and organizers from a variety of social movements both in Los Angeles and abroad. In 2004, on their tenth anniversary, the four members of the new group launched a restructuring of Ultra-red into an aesthetic-political organization. The newly reorganized Ultra-red permits a greater diversity of projects and campaigns on a local and international level, with multiple groups and alliances taking up the Ultra-red moniker.
Ultra-red has established partnerships with community-based organizations like the Union de Vecinos in East Los Angeles and, internationally, with Ballymun Women's Resource Centre in Dublin, Ireland and the Germany-based migration and anti-racist network Kanak Attak. In addition to these groups, a number of artists have entered into tactical and provisional alliances with Ultra-red for purposes of specific and on-going cooperation. Some of these artists include [a=Eddie Peel] (of [a=Sony Mao] and [a=Needle]), [a=Elliot Perkins] (fka [a=Phonem]), [a=Terre Thaemlitz], and others. To offer an outlet for the release of projects to come out of these alliances, in 2004 Ultra-red launched Public Record, a fair-use on-line archive for audio, text, image and video documents.
Since the late 1990s, Ultra-red have featured the following four artist-activists: Elizabeth Blaney (artist, organizer, [a=Union De Vecinos]), Pablo Garcia (musician, organizer, Woodcraft Rangers), [a=Dont Rhine] (artist, AIDS activist), and Leonardo Vilchis (artist, organizer, [a=Union De Vecinos]).
More recently, the band has expanded further with new members Manuela Bojadzijev, Janna Graham, [a=Chris Jones (46)], Elliot Perkins, Robert Sember, and Ceren Tuerkman.

artist.name: Reinhard Voigt
artist.profile: As a member of Kompakt since the early nineties, Reinhard Voigt has been a key player in the development and subsequent success of this legendary Cologne-based record label. Taking early notes from his famous older brother [a=Wolfgang Voigt], Reinhard has refined and re-tooled his sound in a direction that has become unmistakeably his own in its dirty, subversive (yet charming) minimal techno pop. His latest releases on Kompakt's "Speicher" project have been highly sought, and have helped bring a more radical dancefloor sound to the typical Kompakt releases, which also sell out around the globe. Everyone has heard his tracks, as most of them are wide-ranging enough to find their way into many different DJ sets.

artist.name: Ihan
artist.profile:
Experimental electronic project from France.
Started in 1999, in 2020 renamed to [a=Lingering Echoes].
Yan Arexis is a French musician and visual artist, also a writer (Editions Denöel). He started his works in 1991 with one of the pioneers of french Heathen metal SUS SCROFA, then with the band STILLE VOLK, pioneers of Heathen Folk whose records are published by Holy Recods (ORPHANED LAND, SEPTIC FLESH, ELEND; NIGHTFALL, S.U.P, GODSEND...) and Prophecy productions (ALCEST, AMBER ASYLUM, AGALLOCH, DOOL, ARCTURUS, FAUNA, NEGURA BUNGET..).
In 2000 he turned towards electronic-Glitch music with the IHAN project published on the prestigious label MILLEPLATEAUX records (ALVA NOTO, ALEC EMPIRE, KID 606, THOMAS KÖNER, OVAL, GAS, VLADISLAV DELAY...).
He then creates the duo LA BREICHE with Patrick Lafforgue whose records are published by COLD SPRING RECORDS (LAIBACH, LARS VON TRIER, CORRUPTED, PSYCHIC TV, COIL, SPK, MERZBOW, MZ 412, ZENI GEVA & STEVE ALBINI, GODFLESH...).
He is also the co-founder of the festival l'HOMME SAUVAGE since 2017 (HEXVESSEL, SATURNALIA TEMPLE, ARKTAU EOS, VISIONS, THROANE, JESS & THE ANCIENT ONES...).

artist.name: Johnny Klimek
artist.profile: Bassist, drummer and electronic music composer born in 1962 in Melbourne (Australia). In 1983 he moved to Berlin (Germany) where founded [a=The Other Ones]. In 1987 he started to work in his own studio and did several successful remixes. He toured with [a=Nina Hagen Band] and he (co-)produced the soundtrack of Wintersleepers (1996) and Run Lola Run (1998). Related studios are [l=Klimax Studios, Hollywood] and [l=Klimax Studio, Berlin].

artist.name: Fennesz
artist.profile: Christian Fennesz (Vienna, Austria, 1962) started his career as the singer/guitarist/songwriter in the group [a=Maische], one of the more interesting bands to occupy the Viennese underground during the late eighties.
