artist.name: Savath & Savalas
artist.profile: Side-project of American producer Guillermo Scott Herren (better known as Prefuse 73), Catalonian singer Eva Puyuelo Muns and American musician Roberto Carlos Lange. Started in 1998.

artist.name: Radiohead
artist.profile:
Electronic and alternative rock (modern rock) band from Oxfordshire, England (United Kingdom).
The name Radiohead comes from the [a=Talking Heads] song, "Radio Head", from the "[url=http://www.discogs.com/Talking-Heads-True-Stories/master/39386]True Stories[/url]" album.
Formed by school friends in 1985, Radiohead did not release their first single until 1992's "[r=767600]". The cathartic "[url=http://www.discogs.com/Radiohead-Creep/master/21481]Creep[/url]", from the debut album "[url=http://www.discogs.com/Radiohead-Pablo-Honey/master/13344]Pablo Honey[/url]" (1993), became a worldwide hit as grunge music dominated radio airwaves.
Radiohead were initially branded as a one-hit wonder abroad, but caught on at home in the UK with their second album, "[url=http://www.discogs.com/Radiohead-The-Bends/master/17008]The Bends[/url]" (1995), earning fans with their dense guitar atmospheres and front man [a=Thom Yorke]'s expressive singing. The album featured the hits "[url=http://www.discogs.com/Radiohead-High-Dry-Planet-Telex/release/199387]High & Dry[/url]", "[r=1463625]" and "[url=http://www.discogs.com/Radiohead-Fake-Plastic-Trees/master/21526]Fake Plastic Trees[/url]".
The band's third album, "[url=http://www.discogs.com/Radiohead-OK-Computer/master/21491]OK Computer[/url]" (1997), propelled them to greater attention. Popular both for its expansive sound and themes of modern alienation, the album has been acclaimed by critics as a landmark record of the 1990s, some critics go as far to consider it one of the best of all time. "[url=http://www.discogs.com/Radiohead-Kid-A/master/21501]Kid A[/url]" (2000) marked further evolution, containing influences from experimental electronic music.
"[url=http://www.discogs.com/Radiohead-Hail-To-The-Thief/master/16962]Hail To The Thief[/url]" (2003) was seen as a conventional return to the guitar and piano-led rock sound. After fulfilling their contract with EMI, Radiohead released "[url=http://www.discogs.com/Radiohead-In-Rainbows/master/21520]In Rainbows[/url]" (2007) famously via a pay-what-you-want model. Their latest album, "[url=https://www.discogs.com/Radiohead-A-Moon-Shaped-Pool/master/998252]A Moon Shaped Pool[/url]", was released in May 2016.
Radiohead's original influences were cited as alternative rock and post-punk bands like [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/Smiths,+The]The Smiths[/url], [a=Pixies], [a=Magazine], [a=Joy Division] and [a=Siouxsie & The Banshees], who Thom Yorke claims inspired him to become a performer, and [a=R.E.M.] (with lead singer of the band, Thom Yorke, referring to himself as an 'R.E.M. groupie').
Band members: Thom Yorke – vocals, guitar, piano, keyboards, laptop (1985–present) Colin Greenwood – bass guitar (1985–present) Ed O'Brien – guitar, effects, backing vocals (1985–1990, 1990–present) Philip Selway – drums, percussion (1985–present) Jonny Greenwood – guitar, keyboards, ondes Martenot, glockenspiel, laptop, orchestral arrangements (1988–present)
Current live members: Clive Deamer – drums, percussion (2011–present)
Former members: Rasmus "Raz" Peterson – tenor saxophone, alto saxophone (1985–1989) Liz Cotton – alto saxophone (1988–1989) Charlotte Cotton – alto saxophone (1988–1989) Nigel Powell – drums (1990)
Previous names: On a Friday (1985–1990, 1990–1991) Shindig (1990)

artist.name: UI
artist.profile: Ui (pronounced ooo-eee) was an American post-rock/funk band based in New York City, which started in 1990. The group was started by Sasha Frere-Jones and Clem Waldmann. Wilbo Wright joined the group in 1993. The group's songs are often described as "bass-heavy", as the band frequently used two bass guitars. Other instruments used by the band include banjo, synthesizers, tuba and timpani. Their musical style is described to range between funk, dub, post-rock and electronica. The group had a high reliance on samplers and other studio equipment to overdub and articulate rhythms. The group disbanded in 2003 shortly after the recording of the album Answers.

artist.name: The Beta Band
artist.profile: Scottish band from St Andrews. Formed in 1996 and disbanded 2004. Reunited in 2025. Genres: indie pop / rock, downtempo, folk & folktronica, experimental music ...

artist.name: Day One
artist.profile: Day One consists of vocalist [a=Phelim Byrne] and multi-instrumentalist/producer [a=Matthew Hardwidge]. The duo began collaborating in the mid-’90s, blending the storytelling traditions of hip-hop and British folk along with a slew of musical influences. They were fortunate enough to find a collaborator in [a=Mario Caldato Jr.] ([a=Beastie Boys], [a=Beck], etc.) to work with on their projects he has co-produced all of their output to date. The duo was originally signed to [a=Massive Attack]’s label [l=Melankolic], on which they released their debut album "Ordinary Man" in 2000 to great international success. They released their 2nd album, "Probably Art", in 2007. The group paved the way for artists such as The Streets in pioneering a unique new British rap style. Day One’s diversity is reflected in the various TV and movie soundtracks in which their music has featured, including: dark, teenage epic "Cruel Intentions", Emmy Award-winning US series "Six Feet Under", Channel 4 hit "Skins" and most recently the hit US movie "Easy A". The group's third album was recorded in Los Angeles in early 2015.

artist.name: Horace Andy
artist.profile: Horace Andy (born Horace Keith Hinds, February 19, 1951, Kingston, Jamaica), is a roots reggae songwriter and singer, best known for his distinctive vocals. After having recorded for many Jamaican producers and labels through the 1970's and 1980's ([l=Studio One], [a=Bunny lee], [a=Phil Pratt], [a=Derrick Harriott], [a=Keith Hudson], [a142107], [a=Tapper Zukie], [a343837], [a300922], ...), he became known by the mainstream public with his collaborations with Bristol's trip hop band [a=Massive Attack]. Cousin of [a=Justin Hinds], he was renamed by [a194190], a reference to [a=Bob Andy]. His second wife was [a=Caroline Williams (2)]. They split up soon after the release of [m=32618].

artist.name: Carter Burwell
artist.profile: Carter Burwell (born November 18, 1954, in New York City) scored a lot of soundtracks for films by Ethan and Joel Coen. Fargo, Blood Simple, Barton Fink and Raising Arizona are just some examples.

artist.name: Primal Scream
artist.profile:
Alternative rock band formed in 1982 in Glasgow, Scotland.
Bobby Gillespie ('elemental noise', 1982–84; vocals 1984–present) Andrew Innes (guitar, 1986–present) Darrin Mooney (drums, 1997–present)
Former members: Jim Beattie ('elemental noise', 1982–84; guitar 1984–88) Judith Boyle (vocals, 1984) Martin St. John (tambourine, 1984–87) Robert 'Throb' Young (bass, 1984–88; guitar, 1988–2006; died 2014) Thomas McGurk (drums, 1984–86) Paul Harte (guitar, 1985–86) Stuart May (guitar, 1986) Gavin Skinner (drums, 1987–88) Martin Duffy (keyboards, 1989–2022; died 2022) Henry Olsen (bass, 1989–94) Philip 'Toby' Tomanov (drums, 1989–94) Denise Johnson (guest vocals, 1990–96) Steve Sidelnyk (drums, 1994–96) Paul Mulreany (drums, 1996–99) Gary 'Mani' Mounfield (bass, 1996–2011; died 2025) Kevin Shields (guitar, 1998–2006) Barrie Cadogan (guitar, 2006–15) Simone Butler (bass, 2012– 2026)

artist.name: Sunna
artist.profile:
Metal/Alt. rock band from Bristol, UK. After the release of 'One Minute Science' many of the members left the group, leaving Jon Harris as the main driving force of the project. This led to a rotation of musicians, including drummer Mark Richardson, who almost took of a full time role but instead went to play for Feeder after the death of their drummer Jon Lee. The group's follow-up 'Two Minute Terror' was released in 2009 with Harris and Ian Maclaren as the two focal forces at the helm, gracing fans with the single 'One Of A Twin' and a cover of David Bowie's 'Ashes To Ashes', as well as the song 'Spider', which had been originally written for 'Spider-Man 2'. By the time the album 'After The Third Pin' was released Jon Harris had taken full command of the group, not only writing and recording all the songs, but also provided instrumentation for the majority of the record's run time. Drummers were brought in, including the likes of Josh Clark and Mark Richardson, with guest slots on 'Feel The Blade' featuring Sid Harris & Alex Clemetson on guitars & bass respectfully. Sunna's last LP '4 Global Mourning' carried on the same tradition, with Jon Harris providing the vast majority of the runtime's input. Since then the odd single/ep has popped up on the project's bandcamp, with rumours of a 5th album always being whispered on the official FB page.
Past Members: Tim Harris - Drums (1996-2000) Ian Maclaren - Lead Guitar Mark Richardson - Drums (2001-2002) Dave Jenkins - Electronics Alex Clemetson - Bass Guitar Shane "Shane-O" Goodwin - Bass (2000-2001) Jim Harris - Bass (2001-2002) Mark "Flatline" Cahill - DJ (2000-2002)

artist.name: Doves
artist.profile:
Alternative rock band from Manchester, England, which started out as the dance band [a26531].
The band comprises twin brothers [a=Jez Williams] (guitar, vocals) and [a=Andy Williams (4)] (drums, vocals), and [a=Jimi Goodwin] (bass, vocals, guitar).
Years active: 1998 – 2010 (hiatus), 2018 – present
During the band's hiatus, Jimi Goodwin released a solo album, while the Williams brothers regrouped as [a=Black Rivers].
They began life as a Haçienda inspired dance band until their studio burned down and they lost all of their equipment, and then reformed as Doves.

artist.name: King Biscuit Time
artist.profile: Genre blending solo project aka KBT, active 1998-2006.

artist.name: Nine Inch Nails
artist.profile: Industrial rock band Nine Inch Nails (abbreviated as NIN and stylized as NIИ) was formed in 1988 by [a27457] in Cleveland, Ohio. Reznor has served as the main producer, singer, songwriter, instrumentalist, and sole member of Nine Inch Nails for 28 years. This changed in December 2016 when [a259284] officially became the second member of the band. Nine Inch Nails straddles a wide range of many styles of rock music and other genres that require an electronic sound, which can often cause drastic changes in sound from album to album. However, NIN albums in general have many identifiable characteristics in common, such as recurring leitmotifs, chromatic melodies, dissonance, terraced dynamics and common lyrical themes. Nine Inch Nails is most famously known for the melding of industrial elements with pop sensibilities in their first albums. This move was considered instrumental in bringing the industrial genre as a whole into the mainstream, although genre purists and Trent Reznor alike have refused to identify NIN as an industrial band. In 2020, Nine Inch Nails was inducted into the Rock And Roll Hall Of Fame.

artist.name: Muffler
artist.profile:
Based in Helsinki, Finland, Mr. Mikkonen has been producing music since the age of 9, starting back in 1991. He produced a few house tunes which were released in Finland, but in 1994 he discovered jungle/drum 'n bass -- and it was love at first hearing. Ever since he has been mostly doing drum 'n bass, but back then he was limited to using only an Amiga 500 and a simple tracker program. Later he got some more equipment and finally could start doing music a bit more seriously. His first 12" was released on Urban Takeover (URBTAKE 19 - Somber / Fury).
Runs the label [l=Unique Music Records].

artist.name: Twilight 22
artist.profile: Electro-funk project led by keyboardist/producer [a=Gordon Bahary], with [a=Joseph Saulter] and [a=Errol Moore] on vocals.

artist.name: Guru
artist.profile:
American (of Trinidadian descent) rapper, producer and a founding member of [a=Gang Starr]. His stage name "Guru" is a backronym for "Gifted Unlimited Rhymes Universal".
Born: 17 July 1961, Roxbury, Boston, Massachusetts, USA Died: 19 April 2010, New York, New York, USA, aged 48

artist.name: Cybotron
artist.profile:
Detroit-based electro formation launched by [a=Juan Atkins] and [a=Richard Davis (3)] in 1981. One album was released by this duo, the other two albums are solely [a=Richard Davis (3)]. In 2019, Cybotron reshaped. In place of the members Richard Davis and John Housely, Juan Atkins was joined by Laurens von Oswald, and by fellow Detroiter Tameko Williams aka DJ Maaco.
Not to be confused with Australian [a=Cybotron (2)], which also released electronic music.

artist.name: Aleem
artist.profile:
Boogie-funk, r'n'b and dance music trio formed in New York City.
Identical twin brothers [a=Taharqa Aleem] and [a=Tunde Ra Aleem] began their career as [a403107] with covers of [a=Jimi Hendrix]'s "Cry Of Love" and "Rain Bow Bridge" (they also shared the same apartment with Jimi). They formed Aleem with [a=Leroy Burgess] as a vocalist during the early '80s and originally released dance songs on their own label, [l3692] ("Hooked On Your Love", "Get Down Friday Night", "Release Yourself". 1985's "Confusion", resulted in a deal with [l681] where they released two albums.
They worked as producers on acts including [a=Fonda Rae], [a=Caprice], [a=Captain Rock] and [a689608]. The NIA label also became home to such notable rappers as [a=Marley Marl] and [a=MC Shan] and helped develop the careers of MCs such as [a=Sparky D]. After [l3692] folded, the twins opened up their own recording studio in the late '80s.

artist.name: Herbie Hancock
artist.profile: American jazz pianist, keyboardist, composer, band leader (born April 12, 1940, in Chicago, Illinois, USA). Hancock is one of the best-known modern jazz composers, creator of “Watermelon Man” (which has been a reference point throughout his career), “Maiden Voyage”, “Dolphin Dance”, right through to the dance grooves of “[r=3173760]”. Learned the piano from the age of 6, performing piano concertos by [a95546] with the [a837562] aged 11. He came to wider attention via his work with trumpeter [a20956] who introduced him to [l281] co-founder [a252962]; he recorded for Lion's label as both leader and sideman throughout the 1960s while he was a member of trumpeter [a23755]' regular working group from 1963 to 1968. Entered a period of working in jazz fusion from the late 1960s, initially with Davis, and then in his own groups in the 1970s; the "Headhunters" album was a best seller. Around the same time, he worked with the acoustic [a813480], effectively the Davis second quintet with [a55745] substituting.

artist.name: Newcleus
artist.profile: Electro/boogie/hip hop group from Brooklyn, New York. In 1976, a DJ collective known as Jam-On Productions developed in Brooklyn, and was composed of Ben "Cozmo D" Cenac, his best friend David "Dr. Freeze" St. Louis, and Cenac's cousins Monique "Niqu D" Angevin and her brother Pete "Master Quadro" Angevin. In 1981, Cenac created a recording group as an extension of Jam-On, which included his wife Yvette (Lady E), Niqu D, and her future husband Bob "Chilly B" Crafton. This foursome originally went by Positive Messenger, but changed their name to Newcleus.

artist.name: Inner City
artist.profile: American house and techno group formed in Detroit in 1988. The group was originally composed of producer and composer Kevin Saunderson and Chicago native Shanna Jackson, a.k.a. [a=Paris Grey]. Kevin Saunderson is renowned as one of the Belleville Three (along with [a=Juan Atkins] and [a=Derrick May]), the main originators of the Detroit Techno sound. [a=Ann Saunderson] (Kevin's wife) has also been involved in the group. The group is currently Kevin, [a=Dantiez Saunderson] (their son), and vocalist [a=Steffanie Christian].

artist.name: Cevin Fisher
artist.profile: House music DJ, producer, and artist, born 26 October 1963 in East Orange, New Jersey. Former road manager for [a=New Kids On The Block]. Worked in the late 1980s/early 1990s at [a=Arthur Baker]'s [l=Shakedown Sound Studios]. Shakedown studio head [a=Tim Scott (14)] went on to became Cevin's manager in the late 1990s.

artist.profile: aka Simon Pearson, [b]Electronic / House[/b] artist, has some releases on for example [l8272]

artist.name: Doc Martin
artist.profile: DJ and producer from San Francisco, California. Held numerous residencies in New York (Roxy, Twilo, Tunnel), L.A. (Metropolitan) and London (The End). Runs [l=Wax Records].

artist.name: Cornelius
artist.profile: Japanese guitarist, musician, audio / video artist. Born January 27, 1969 in Setagaya-ku, Tokyo. He was married to [a=Takako Minekawa] from 2000 to 2012. He is a second cousin to [a=Miki Berenyi] and Joi Ito.

artist.name: Money Mark
artist.profile: [b]Money Mark[/b] is the primary alias of the American producer and musician, [a=Mark Ramos-Nishita] (b. 10 February 1960, Detroit, Michigan), best known for collaborating with [a=Beastie Boys] from 1992 until 2011.

artist.name: Buffalo Daughter
artist.profile: Japanese rock group formed in 1993.

namevariations.name: bd
バッファロー・ドーター

artist.name: Damon Albarn
artist.profile:
British vocalist and musician born 23 March 1968 in Whitechapel, London. The family moved to Leytonstone, London not long after he was born and then to Colchester, Essex.
Best known as a founding member and vocalist in the British Band [a=Blur] in the 1990s.
He formed also [a=Gorillaz] in 1998 as a side-project with old housemate [a=Jamie Hewlett], and would provide the singing vocals for fictitious lead singer [a470634]. Damon also used this alias outside of Gorillaz on a handful of occasions (see credits).
Incredibly prolific, respected and busy musician releasing and collaborating with artists from all over the world since 2000.
Was once engaged to [a=Justine Frischmann], Blur's album "[url=http://www.discogs.com/Blur-13/master/34446]13[/url]" is supposedly written about their breakup.
Once acted in a British gangster film called "Face", alongside actors [a=Ray Winstone] and Robert Carlyle, also voiced [a=George Harrison]'s character "Bull" in a radio play "Up Against It" by Joe Orton.

artist.name: Megamind
artist.profile:
Italian project
This Megamind is used in cases when it's intended as a group by [a5775] and [a47582], mostly as releasing artist and remixer.
In most other cases (e.g. when credited as author), use the [a5775] alias [a82094].

artist.name: Terence Fixmer
artist.profile: French EBM/Techno producer and live performer. He is the founder of [l=Planete Rouge Records].

artist.name: Black Lung
artist.profile: Australian electronic and industrial music project by David Thrussell, active since 1994.

artist.name: DJ Randy
artist.profile:
Dutch DJ and producer born 14 March 1965.
for Italian DJ/producer see [a=Randy].

artist.name: Justin Berkovi
artist.profile: British Techno DJ and producer based in London. He is the founder of [l=Predicaments] and [l=Nightrax]

artist.name: Pizzicato Five
artist.profile:
Pizzicato Five (1985-2001) was a Japanese pop group best known to audiences in the West in their later incarnation as a duo of [a=Maki Nomiya] and [a=Yasuharu Konishi].
The group was formed in 1985 by Konishi and fellow founding bandmates Keitarō Takanami, Ryō Kamomiya, Mamiko Sasaki, and Shigeo Miyata. Miyata left the group almost immediately but the four remaining members kept the name Pizzicato Five.
Last members: Yasuharu Konishi (1984–2001) - Songwriter, bass guitar, guitar, keyboards, vocals. Yasuharu Konishi (小西 康陽 Konishi Yasuharu?, born February 3, 1959 in Sapporo, Hokkaido) was the only founding member to stay with the group until the end. He is a music producer now. Maki Nomiya (1990–2001) - Lead vocal. Maki Nomiya (野宮 真貴 Nomiya Maki?, born March 12, 1960 in Kushiro, Hokkaido) was the third vocalist of this group. She is also a solo singer, a narrator, a fashion model and a dress designer now.
Former members Keitarō Takanami (1984–1994) - Guitar, keyboards and vocals. Keitarō Takanami (高浪 慶太郎 Takanami Keitarō?, born May 6, 1960 in Nagasaki, Nagasaki) was one of the founding members, and he is a guitarist and a music producer now. He changed his name to 高浪敬太郎 (Keitarō Takanami) later, and was also known as "K-taro." Mamiko Sasaki (1984–1987) - Lead vocal. Mamiko Sasaki (佐々木 麻美子 Sasaki Mamiko?) was one of the founding members and was the first vocalist of this group. Ryō Kamomiya (1984–1987) - Keyboards. Ryō Kamomiya (鴨宮 諒 Kamomiya Ryō?, born March 23, 1962 in Shinjuku, Tokyo) was one of the founding members, and is a composer and an arranger now. Takao Tajima (1987–1990) - Lead vocal, guitar, harmonica. Takao Tajima (田島 貴男 Tajima Takao?, born April 24, 1966 in Ota, Tokyo) was the second vocalist of this group. He had already come out as Original Love's vocalist then. He is a solo singer and is also a music producer now.

artist.name: Joy Division
artist.profile: British punk/post-punk/new wave band formed in 1976 in Salford, Greater Manchester. They were incorrectly named [a695777] on the flyer for their first gig alongside Buzzcocks and Penetration but announced themselves as [a310128] at the start of their first performance. Throughout 1977 they continued under this name up to the recording of the first EP but changed their name to Joy Division at the start of 1978, before releasing their first 7" EP "[r=498855]". Shortly after the suicide of Ian Curtis on May 18, 1980 – following troubles with grand mal seizures (a form of epilepsy), depression, and other contributing personal factors happening in his life – the band was renamed [a=New Order], following a pact between all four members made long before Curtis's death that should any member leave, the remaining members would change the name of the group. Ian Curtis (vocal, occasional guitar), Bernard Sumner (guitar, keyboards), Peter "Hooky" Hook (bass guitar, backing vocals), and Stephen Morris (drums, percussion).

artist.name: Christian Smith & John Selway
artist.profile: DJ and producer duo.

artist.name: Tim J
artist.profile: Steady as a rock, Tim J owns a special spot in the dance scene. His tribal techno and groovy beats are very recognizable and appreciated by numerous big dj's. Tim J is producing and playing for several years now with lots of different artists, like [a=Joff Roach], [a=Miss Melody], Erwin v/d Bliek, [a=Warren Fellow], [a=Stephen J. Kroos], [a=Jellisimo] and many others. Tim J is co-owner of [l=Smoke Records].

artist.name: New Order
artist.profile: Formed 1980 in Manchester, United Kingdom shortly after the suicide of [a=Ian Curtis] ([a=Joy Division], [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/Warsaw+(3)]Warsaw[/url]) Current members: [a=Bernard Sumner] (vocals, guitar, keyboards), [a=Stephen Morris] (drums, keyboards), [a=Gillian Gilbert] (keyboards, guitar, 1981–2001, 2011–present), [a=Phil Cunningham] (guitar, keyboards, 2001–present), [a=Tom Chapman (2)] (bass, 2011–present). Former members: [a=Peter Hook] (bass, keyboards, 1980–2010).

artist.name: The Arc
artist.profile: UK electronic duo consisting of Dave Froud & Matt Strellis, who plays an ambient/chillout music in mid 90's.

artist.name: Avalon
artist.profile: American eIectronic music producer based in Belgium. Avalon is a music project started in 1993 by Tim Handels, signed during those years to Brussels-based Crammed Discs (SSR Records, Freezone, Made To Measure) and recently reinstated since 2022.

artist.name: Kosmos
artist.profile: Drum & Bass group consisting of [a500020] & [a557391]. For all other Kosmos entries, please check your submissions.

artist.name: David Byrne
artist.profile: Scottish-American singer, songwriter, artist, film and record producer born May 14, 1952 in Dumbarton, Scotland and based in New York City. He was married to [a=Adelle Lutz] from 1987 to 2004 (divorced). Best known as the lead singer of [a=Talking Heads], David Byrne has also collaborated with many artists from [a=Brian Eno] to [a=X-Press 2], and has also released several solo albums on his own [l=Luaka Bop] and [l=Todomundo] labels.

artist.name: Baaba Maal
artist.profile: (* 12 November 1953 in Podor) is a Senegalese singer, guitarist and percussionist. Under the influence of his friend and family griot [a=Mansour Seck] he devoted himself to learning music from his mother and his school's headmaster and then went on to study music at the university in Dakar and postgraduate on a scholarship at Beaux-Arts in Paris. Baaba sings primarily in Pulaar and is the foremost promoter of the traditions of the Pulaar-speaking peoples who live on both sides of the Senegal River.

artist.name: Edson Cordeiro
artist.profile: Edson Cordeiro (born February 9, 1967) is a Brazilian sopranist countertenor and pop and jazz singer.

artist.name: Hooverphonic
artist.profile: Hooverphonic is a Belgian band established in 1995. Although they were initially characterized as a trip-hop group, they quickly broadened their sound to the point that they could no longer be categorized in a single genre. The band's original name was Hoover, but they eventually changed it to Hooverphonic because other bands were already using the Hoover moniker, they also wanted to avoid any legal concerns with the popular vacuum cleaner company. Hooverphonic's lineup has consisted of bassist [a=Alex Callier], guitarist [a=Raymond Geerts], and a number of lead singers. Among them were [a=Liesje Sadonius] (1996-1997), [a=Geike Arnaert] (1997-2008, 2020-2025), [a=Noémie Wolfs] (2010-2015), and [a=Luka Cruysberghs] (2018–2020).

artist.name: Avatars Of Dub
artist.profile: Dubby downtempo and ambient d+b project of Mike Reger + Phil Brooks (Washington, DC). Early signing to Thievery Corporation's Eighteenth Street Lounge Music. Broke up 2001. Mike Reger currently records under De Rigueur and Mikey Dubs. Phil also recorded with Desmond Williams as Browntempo.

artist.name: Black Uhuru
artist.profile: Roots Reggae group, formed 1972 in Kingston, Jamaica. One of the most popular reggae acts ever and the first one to win a Grammy. Founded by [url=https://www.discogs.com/artist/357958]Derrick "Duckie" Simpson[/url], [a=Don Carlos (2)] and [a=Rudolph "Garth" Dennis], they rose to international fame in the '80s, when they were joined with the rhythm section of [url=https://www.discogs.com/artist/5064]Sly Dunbar & Robbie Shakespeare[/url] and with [a357957] and [a=Michael Rose] as leadsingers. After his departure Rose was replaced by young singers like [a=Junior Reid] and [a=Andrew Bees].

artist.name: Ursula 1000
artist.profile: Ursula 1000 is the brainchild of New York producer and multi intrumentalist Alex Gimeno. Inspired by 60s soundtracks, the 90s lounge revival, Japanese shibuya-kei, British bigbeat, funk and disco, Gimeno fused these varied sounds to create what he calls his "retro/future" sound. He’s toured the world and performed in over 30 countries and collaborated with the likes of Fred Schneider of The B52s, Lady Bunny, Puddles Pity Party and had his music used in Sex And The City, Grey’s Anatomy and for ads for Verizon, Adidas and Grand Marnier. His 8th album Esoterique marks his 20th anniversary since his debut.

artist.name: Denim
artist.profile: English singer, songwriter and guitarist, born 12 August 1961 in Birmingham, England, UK. Formed [a=Felt] in 1979.

artist.name: Beck
artist.profile:
American musician, singer-songwriter and multi-instrumentalist. born July 8, 1970, in Los Angeles, California, USA. Son of [a=David Campbell] and [a=Bibbe Hansen], grandson of [a=Al Hansen]. He was married to [a=Marissa Ribisi] from 2004–2019.
When his parents split, he and his younger brother stayed with their mother. Beck dropped out of school at the age of 14 and drifted around for about a decade, ending up back in Los Angeles. While playing some open-mike events, he got noticed by the little local label Bong Load Custom Records. In 1993, "Loser" was released to the world in a limited pressing of 500 copies and sent out to radio DJs across the U.S. The song became quite popular, and subsequently DGC/Geffen/MCA signed Beck in November of 1993, while giving him the freedom to work with independent labels and to make uncommercial music. The DGC release of Loser in January 1994 became an international hit.

namevariations.name: A.P.E
APE
Ape
Paul Conboy And Adrian Corker For A.P.E. Productions

artist.name: No Se
artist.profile: For the German Acid Jazz act, please use [a=No Sé] and ANV if necessary.

artist.name: Taran
artist.profile:
Born: 27 Sept 1966
In 1986 he graduated from the Kiev Art College and appeared on stage jamming with Eliott Sharp, showing no mercy for the audience's ears. In 1988 he joined the Ukrainian Academy Of Art, got up after a heart attack and headed a strange electro-acoustic chamber band Sugar-White Death, laying bare the Hearts Of Darkness of the Ketamin Junkies and Post Chernobyl Psychos. The rest of his verbal energy went into the production of Lo-Fi-videos.
In 1992 Taran finally started to pay attention to the HipHop groovies, destroying ultimately his father's record collection and releasing the project The Tenth Victim. At the same time he was co-producing for the German cult-indie-label What's So Funny About... the first CD-Sampler of underground-experimental Ukrainian bands. In connection with this project Taran appeared at the 1993 Pop-Komm festival in Cologne, where he was deeply disillusioned by the massive misconcetions he discovered about the indie-scene in the Ukraine.
1994 saw him involved in a variety of club projects in Kiew, Warsaw and Odessa, mostly roaming through the multimedia jungle. In late 1995 he codirected the first Ukrainian big rave (Torba Sound) and as a guest of the Dutch Institute Of Tactical Media, started work on Mod And Mini And Space Age. In 1996 Taran signed to Compost Records in Munich, and still riding down the "Down Escalator Of Opportunity" in the capital of his nation...

artist.name: S-Tone Inc.
artist.profile:
Stefano Tirone's primary music project since 1992. His first music experiences date back to the early 1980s, during the new wave era (with tracks included into “Gathered” and “Body Section”, two compilations released by the magazine Rockerilla), but who, as a producer, starts working in the early 1990s, alternating house production to funky productions.
The influence of the British acid jazz scene of the early 90’s and his deep love for the 70’s soul and jazz music are the foundations of the S-Tone Inc. project, that starts in 1992 with the track Get Freaky Now, that received great appreciation in the UK and represents one of the earliest examples of "made in Italy" acid jazz music. His debut album was released in 1996 on the Milano 2000/Ishtar label under the title Love Unlimited and contains the track Ainda Sonhar, a composition that was one of the forerunners of the nu bossa genre and that got included into tens of compilations all over the world.
In 1997 he releases under the name Cosmorama a monothematic drum and bass project entitled “Space Odyssey”. Mainly through the third album under the name S-Tone Inc., released in 2002 on Schema/Ishtar and entitled Sobrenatural, he refines his style, made of Brazilian, soul and jazz influences, without forgetting the experiences that he’d made with various Italian and British DJ’s in the house music scene.
In 2003 he starts to work with Toco ( Tomaz Di Cunto ) a brazilian songwriter. Stefano Tirone produces his three albums Instalaçao do samba, Outro lugar and Memoria and together they write and perform several tracks for the S-Tone Inc project in all the following releases (Luz y sombra, Moon in libra, Lost & Found, Onda and Body & Soul).

artist.name: Le Gooster
artist.profile: Le Gooster, born Nguxi de Carvalho in Geneva, Switzerland, is known for his work as a DJ, musician and record producer. Goo founded Synchrovision Records in 1999.

artist.name: Ben Sims
artist.profile: DJ, producer and label owner from London, UK. Founder of [l=Theory Recordings], [l=Hardgroove], [l=Machine (4)], [l=Ingoma] and [l=Symbolism]. Co-owner of [l=Hardgroove Mastering] together with [a=Paul Mac].

artist.name: Sosa
artist.profile: Italian DJ.

artist.name: Chipi
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Tuzla, Bosnia&Herzegovina.

artist.name: That Kid Chris
artist.profile: DJ, producer and remixer from the United States.

artist.name: Redhead
artist.profile: Techno DJ and producer from Belgium.

artist.name: Aedena Cycle
artist.profile: Aedena Cycle were a unique outfit: an ambient techno quartet! When the classic "Traveller's Dream" EP on Apollo was released in 1994, the band comprised no less than four talented youngsters from Tromsø (Norway's Detroit - musically speaking, that is). Two of them were Torbjørn Brundtland and Svein Berge, who eventually became the hugely successful Röyksopp five years later. The other two were Kolbjørn Lyslo and Gaute Barlindhaug. The group disbanded shortly after their debut EP: Brundtland went on to work with Rune Lindbæk and Ole Johan Mjøs - together, they comprised Alanïa, Drum Island and Those Norwegians as well as the Flamingo Records team that was responsible for chart smashes by DJ Malin and Oki-Man. Then he joined forces with Svein Berge again and made pop history with the "Melody AM" album. Lyslo is now a house producer under the name Doc L. Jnr., whereas Barlindhaug continued Aedena Cycle as a solo project and released two ambient albums on Beatservice, "Albite" and "Cargo Cult" (allegedly recorded with the some of the crappiest equipment in the history of electronic music). In the early nougthies he had to change the name to Kolar Goi, as the company that owned the rights to Aedena Cycle (a series of graphic novels) didn't approve of the use of the name.

artist.name: Biosphere
artist.profile: Biosphere is the main recording name of Geir Jenssen (born 1962), a Norwegian musician who has released a notable catalogue of ambient electronic music. He is well known for his "ambient techno" and "arctic ambient" styles, his use of music loops, and peculiar samples from sci-fi sources. His track "Novelty Waves" was used for the 1995 campaign of Levi's. His 1997 album Substrata was voted by www.hyperreal.org as one of the all time classic ambient albums.

artist.name: Snooze
artist.profile: French electronic musician and film composer born in 1964 in Beirut, Lebanon.

artist.name: Afterlife
artist.profile: Steve Miller a Chillout producer and re-mixer who has worked with established vocalists such as Danni Minogue and Cathy Battistessa as well as Chillout DJ's Chris Coco and Pete Gooding. He is also part of Lux a collaboration with James Bright and The Normalities with Chris Coco that takes a more dub chill orientated approach. His work has featured on various labels and compilations including the prestigious Cafe Del Mar.

artist.name: Bush
artist.profile:
Bush are an English rock band formed in London, England in 1992 under the name Future Primitive.Their current lineup consists of lead vocalist and rhythm guitarist Gavin Rossdale, drummer Robin Goodridge, lead guitarist Chris Traynor, and bassist Corey Britz. In 1994, Bush found immediate success with the release of their debut album, Sixteen Stone, which is certified 6× multi-platinum by the RIAA. They went on to become one of the most commercially successful rock bands of the 1990s, selling over 10 million records in the United States. Despite their success in the United States, the band were less well known in their home country and enjoyed only marginal success there. Bush have had numerous top ten singles on the Billboard rock charts and one No. 1 album with Razorblade Suitcase in 1996. The band broke up in 2002 but reformed in 2010, and have released three albums since then: The Sea of Memories (2011), Man on the Run (2014), and Black and White Rainbows (2017). In Canada, the band is credited as Bushˣ, as there was already a 1970s Canadian rock band with the same name.
They were managed by [a=Dave Dorrell].

artist.name: UKO
artist.profile: The Austrian outfit UKO is sometimes simply Jürgen, but can also the duo of both brothers.

artist.name: Bent
artist.profile:
Electronic duo from Nottingham, England. They are the founders of [l=Sport] Records.
Friend and vocalist [a=Katty Heath] features on a few of their albums and has toured frequently with the live group.

artist.name: Pounding Grooves
artist.profile: He is working at Curve Pusher as a cutting engineer.

artist.name: Xentrix
artist.profile:
Originally from Liège, Belgium Eric bought his first pair of turntables in 1994 and started touring Chicago house music and Detroit techno in local parties and clubs. His ability to get a dance floor moving developed at the legendary Montini Rave Zone in Sint-Truiden, where he started playing at the age of 19.
A year later, he started producing music and learning the basics of the studio with Jean-Louis Kollash . Their first album was released in 1997 on the Antwerp label Minimalistix. It follows with several techno releases under the name "djxentrix & jlk" on Reload records - Bipolar - News - Intense records-Pornographic rec.
His main asset is his sharp ear and his collection of records that he has built up over the decades has expanded and diversified to create atmospheric sets, full of soul and flavor . He worked for Audiopolis record shop and remains an unconditional fan of vinyl.
Few "DJs" are able to set up an atmosphere or get a crowd moving on the dance floor like he can do ,no one can stand still. He has released tracks under several different labels including his own Kroma Recordings (2002-2006), a funky techno oriented label.
His tracks have been played and promoted by many big names in the techno scene.
While the market is overloaded with monotonous music, he decides to explore new horizons where he starts to express himself more freely with an emphasis on jamming and imperfections. The hypnotic and groovy side of techno is what interests him the most.
He recently launched his new label NEUTRE RECORDS ( 2017 ). Through this new project, he is developing a new alias PRSPCTV.
With more than two decades of experience, some sound signatures have influenced him a lot
( AXIS , UR , Indulge , Steve Rachmad , Jacques Lueder ,DVS1, Jeroen Search, Dimi Angelis, Planetary assault system , Heiko laux , James Ruskin , Surgeon , john tejada , Damon wild, Steve Bicknell , Aubrey & Solid groove label, Echoplex ,Donato Dozzy , Mark Bell , Mark Broom .....) These last ones allowed him to develop his own groove and identity as a dj and producer .
A complete and discreet artist who makes people dance since 1994.

artist.name: Simpson Tune
artist.profile: Italian House project.

artist.name: The Riders
artist.profile: Italian project.

artist.name: BZS
artist.profile: BZS stands for Black, Zotti & Simioli

artist.name: Max Duley
artist.profile: West London based producer & DJ. Founder of the ARCart labels.

artist.name: Tony Thomas
artist.profile:
UK based producer and owner of several labels, including Telepathy Records, Moxi Records, Ambiosphere Recordings, New Era Recordings, Cubic Records, Progress Recordings, Shiva Records. Has produced countless releases and remixes on over 100 labels worldwide under at least 40 different aliases over a 20 year period, as well as many collaborations. Has performed and DJ'd in over 40 countries.
[a291813] Credited as guitarist and vocalist on black music releases, for example Parliament, Funkadelic ... [a355269] Reggae musician, credited for playing percussion, keyboard, as producer and so on. [a752662] Lead guitarist of the UK punk band Impact (14), active in the early 80s. [a846803] Producer & credited for Liner Notes [a1218727] Piano player from Memphis, TN. [a1453016] Has one writer credit [a1505232] Credited as a video producer 2002 in the UK. [a2577557] Member of [a383337], sometimes credited as co-writter for 'Been So Long'. [a2747162] Released one US funk 12" EP in 1989 [a2779704] Was a member of the Minneapolis punk band [a1248886]. [a2944194] US - Texas / Oklahoma - country musician from the 60s.

artist.name: Alexander Kowalski
artist.profile: Techno DJ, producer and live performer based in Germany. Founder of [l=Damage Music Berlin]. He was born in 1978 in Greifswald and moved to Berlin with his family in 1986. He started making music on his Amiga and in 1997 he bought equipment with a friend and arranged a studio. His first production "The Wide Theatre" was then released on [r=29278] compilation under the name DisX3. At the end of the nineties he became known through his first live acts in Berlins' techno club, Tresor. Kowalski fuses elements from classic Detroit and Berlin techno sound.

artist.name: DJ Freedom
artist.profile: Drum n Bass artist.

artist.name: Voda
artist.profile:
Sound engineer, mastering engineer, remixer and composer.
Not to be confused with [url=https://www.discogs.com/artist/147690-Jonathan-More]Jonathan More[/url] from [url=https://www.discogs.com/artist/946-Coldcut]Coldcut[/url].

Jon Voda

Right Dishonourable Sir Jon Voda, Private Secretary For Mastering And Final Fiddles (MAFF)
Sir Jon Voda

artist.name: Mixmaster Morris
artist.profile: Long-running champion of Ambient music and everything related.

artist.name: Virginia Astley
artist.profile: British singer-songwriter and classically trained musician (piano, flute), born 26 September 1959, in Watford, England, UK. She is the daughter of [a=Edwin Astley] and Hazel Balbirnie, twin sister of [a=Alison Astley], youngest sisters of Karen Astley (ex-wife of [a=Pete Townshend]), Gareth Astley and [a=Jon Astley]. Mother of [a=Florence Astley]. Since late 2020, she has started making her music and art available through Bandcamp.

artist.name: Anomolies
artist.profile: Anomolies is an all female collective with members spanning across the hip-hop spectrum including emcees, deejays, b-girls, producers, visual artists, and beyond. There are 5 core members of the crew: [a249336] (MC), [a192836] (MC/Producer), Kuttin Kandi (DJ), Big Tara (B-girl/MC), and [a80687] (MC).

artist.name: Terry Hall
artist.profile: British singer/songwriter (born March 19, 1959 in Coventry England; died December 18, 2022), whose dour image belies a sharp wit and musical talent. He first came to prominence as lead singer of [a=The Specials]. In 1981 he went on to form [a=Fun Boy Three], in 1984 [a=The Colourfield] which lasted until 1987, one Album as [a=Terry, Blair & Anouchka] followed in 1990 before he collaborated with [a=David A. Stewart] as Vegas. He released two solo Albums in the 1990's and another collaborative effort with [a=Mushtaq] in 2003. He was regularly collaborating with the [a=Lightning Seeds] and more recently with the Dub Pistols before performing with the The Specials again since 2009.

artist.name: Underwater
artist.profile:
Underwater was an Atlanta, GA based trip-hop band formed in 1996 with Jeremy Wilkins on keyboards/programming/guitar and Melissa Mileski on vocals. Both Wilkins and Mileski were previously in the Florida based ethereal-goth band, Rosewater Elizabeth. The band later added Alec Irvin on drums and keyboardist/programmer/remixer, Matthew Jeanes.
The group's first album, I Could Lose, produced by Chris Vrenna of Nine Inch Nails, was released on Risk Records in 1998. The band also released an independent single, RED., and a second full-length on their own label, sub:marine, entitled, This Is Not A Film. The band broke up in 2001 after various personal developments and tragedies. In 2007 Wilkins and Mileski temporarily reunited and released a new EP, Welcome Home, with an additional multi-instrumentalist, George Lewis III, on board.
Members went on to [a=allegra gellar], [a=hawks do not share], and [a=larvae]

artist.name: The May Family Reunion
artist.profile:
May Family Reunion was a folk group from Lake Lure, NC and later Tampa Bay, FL. The band works with a Russian painter named Elena Klimenko, and Japanese painter named Maeko Yoda. Lena lives in Moscow and Mae lives in the U.K. Their art is the visual representation of the May Family sound. Band Members: Dan Rosen, Lee Wiggins, Austin Hill, Alexander J Caruso, Lena Klemenko and Alex Moutsko
Releases: Some may be unreleased. Before The War With China E.P War In The World Of Animals Voices From The Tower Flyin' Over Lake Towns Outside The Law, 2007

artist.name: Larvae
artist.profile:
Electronic project from the USA. Formed in 1997 in Atlanta, Georgia, USA as a solo project of Matthew Jeanes, later expanded to the duo with Matthew Jeanes' longtime friend and collaborator Bryan Meng, who focused on the film and video aspect of Larvae live shows.
Later relocated to Austin, Texas, USA.
In 2004 expanded to the trio with the addition of Christopher Burnett.
Since late 2008 Larvae again became a solo project of Matthew Jeanes.
In 2009 and 2012 respectively Larvae has shortly became a duo with A. Reeves, but became a solo project of Matthew Jeanes again.
Since 2022 Larvae became a band again as a duo of Matthew Jeanes and Christopher Burnett.
Lineup: Matthew Jeanes - electronics, guitar (1997-present) Christopher Burnett - electronics (2004-2008, 2022-present)
Former members: Bryan Meng - live visualization (2000-2008) A. Reeves - electronics (2009, 2012)

artist.name: Tony D
artist.profile:
Italian-American Trenton, NJ, hip-hop luminary whose career as an emcee was overshadowed by his skills and fame as a producer. Produced many obscure NJ records before signing with [l158452] around 1990 for his own album, then forming [a242183] in 1991, and even later producing abstract hip hop tracks for the UK [l738] label. Later in his career, he founded the [l4784] label, re-releasing random Philly hip-hop compilations and several of his own productions, as well as a retrospective of his own work. Was killed in an automobile accident in Hamilton, NJ, where he lived, at the age of 42, on 4 April 2009.
"Weighing in at 16 stone of Italian American heritage Tony Depula a.k.a Tony D is a man who has left an indelible mark across the history of the hip-hop beat, wrestling his way round some classic LPs from [i]Poor Righteous Teachers[/i] (acting as producer on classics like [i]Shakiyla[/i]) - to his own [i]Pound For Pound[/i] release on [i]Grand Central[/i]. Tony has always kept his productions tight and full. The hard drums and bass generated courtesy of his EMU SP1200 drum machine have been laced with some classic raps courtesy of YZ, Lo-key and some amazing vocals from Veba and Spikey T. Tony caught the hip-hop bug in 1985 travelling up to the Bronx from his Trenton, New Jersey base and buying drum machines from a producer who had moved on to drug dealing. It was not long before Tony was making classic beats some which were subsequently robbed by the likes of [i]Naughty By Nature[/i] and [i]Heavy D[/i]; all cases which were settled out of court. Responsible for [i]Grand Central[/i]'s first release and first artist solo album, ([i]Pound For Pound[/i] back in 1997) he hooked up with [i]Grand Central[/i] when [i][a16820][/i] stumbled across Tony D's [i]Central J Parlay EP[/i] in a N.Y. basement." -from [i]Grand Central[/i]'s website

artist.name: Fingathing
artist.profile: Instrumental jazz/hip-hop group from Manchester, England, comprising classically trained double bass player [a43766], DJ Turntablist Peter Parker and visual artist Chris Cavetroll Drury.

artist.name: Riton
artist.profile: Electronic DJ and musician.

artist.name: Microbots
artist.profile: First tracks on [l=Overdrive] (mainly Chip tracks) as well produced by [a=J.D. Wood].

artist.name: Urban Soul
artist.profile: House/Deep House from Atlanta, United States.

artist.name: Victor "Overdose" Sanchez
artist.profile: House producer and recording engineer from the Bronx, New York City. A&R and production manager at [l=BPM King Street Sounds] from 1995 to 1997.

artist.name: Nick Sentience
artist.profile:
Hard dance producer, remixer, engineer & DJ from London, UK. He has played at Ministry of Sound, Gatecrasher, Cream, Space to mention a few clubs.
Officially the youngest DJ and producer in the world to enter the top 100 DJ list at the age 20 in 2000 (voted as 74th).
Awards: Tune of the Year 2003 - Cause and Effect | Mixmag Remix of the year 2003 - Never lost His Hardcore Remix Winner HDA - remix of the year Hostile Best Newcomer | Musik Magazine 2001

artist.name: DJ Tandu
artist.profile: Trance music project founded in 1994 by German songwriter, producer and DJ, [a35231].

artist.name: Safri Duo
artist.profile:
Uffe Savery (born April 5, 1966) & Morten Friis (born August 21, 1968) met in 1977 in Copenhagen, Denmark, where they were both members of [a=Tivoli-Garden]. They later formed a classic ensamble/percussion duo, while attending the Royal Danish Conservatory of Music, and released 6 albums throughout the 1990s, until they were discovered by producer & DJ Michael Parsberg in 2000. This resulted in the track "Played-A-Live (The Bongo Song)" which became the most popular song in Europe that year. The mix of classical elements fused with trance, tribal and pop resulted in major mainstream success and two more albums the following three years.
The 2008 album "Origins" saw the duo returning to their classical roots, inspired by world music and artists such as Mike Oldfield.
Safri Duo have worked with prominent names such as [a=Michael McDonald] (featured on the track "Sweet Freedom") and [a=Youssou N'Dour] (three tracks on the "Origins" album). They performed live with [a=Aqua] at the Eurovision Song Contest 2001 in Copenhagen and [a=Jean-Michel Jarre] at his AERO concert in Denmark, September 7, 2002.

artist.name: Matthew Dear
artist.profile: Matthew Dear's most recent excursions have found him at the doorsteps of the minimal-but-funky regime of techno music. Inspired by pioneers like Mike Ink, Thomas Brinkmann, and Ricardo Villalobos, Dear has ingested, manipulated and re-introduced his version of this always changing and innovative realm of sound creation. Recent acceptance into the legendary Plus 8 roster and ever-popular Berlin-based Perlon label has solidified Dear as a viable source for pushing the boundaries of modern dance music while at the same time remaining focused on the simple goal of making people dance.

artist.name: Kurtis Mantronik
artist.profile: Born September 4, 1965, in Jamaica to a Black Jamaican mother and a Syrian Arab father. Raised in Canada and in New York.

artist.name: Stock, Hausen & Walkman
artist.profile:
No other artist's name makes quite as much sense as Stock, Hausen & Walkman (1991-2001) - a triple pun embracing pioneering electronic composer and theorist Karlheinz Stockhausen, the '80s pop-by-numbers production team Stock, Aitken & Waterman, and the Sony Walkman.
At the time of the group's formation in the late '80s, they were an experimental/improv duo consisting of Matt Wand ( who named and started the band ) & guitarist Rex Casswell. Cellist Dan Weaver, drummer Richard Harrison, Andrew Sharpley & others contributing in live and studio roles along the way, They played at Company Week at Derek Bailey's request in 1990. The project was continued with arguably the most successful albums Organ Transplants vol1 & 2 conceived & designed by Wand with musical contributions from Sharpley after Casswell moved to Denmark and Weaver began taking recording time-outs to perform for dance and theatre. Wand started and ran Hot Air Records to get the S,H&W records ‘out there’ and began releasing a wide range of albums, also by other artists over that decade and continues conceiving, designing and producing albums as that label to the present. Matt Wand wrapped the project up in 2001 but continues to release archive S,H&W material of “some interest” on Hot Air. There are vague rumours that new material might see the light in the near future.
Stock, Hausen & Walkman (now Matt Wand, Andrew Sharpley and Daniel Weaver) is known for making sample-heavy, subversively funny collages with a healthy disregard for copyright issues: "taking pot shots at anything that moves, be it high art music, low pop or cheesy listening" (The Wire 163). Distinctive packaging has been prevalent in most SHW's releases.
The duo's music (Daniel Weaver leave them before the "Ventilating Deer" CD-ROM came out) has a hands-on, DIY approach, consisting of obscure samples and messed up beats, collaged into short, quirky songs. Wand and Sharpley continued on with an album-per-year release schedule until their split in 2001.

artist.name: Billy Nasty
artist.profile: DJ and producer from the United Kingdom. Ran [l=Zoom Records (2)] with [a=David Wesson] and his own label [l=Tortured Records].

artist.name: The Aloof
artist.profile:
Line up includes: Ricky Barrow (Vocals), Gary Burns (Keyboards, Bass Guitar; ex-[a=The Sabres Of Paradise]), Jagz Kooner (Programming, Engineering; also ex-[a=The Sabres Of Paradise]), Richard Thair (Drums, Percussion), Dean Thatcher (Keyboards).
When they played live they were joined by [a=Nick Abnett] (bass), Dave Stone (guitar), [a=Nick Strasburg] (keyboards) & [a=Will Blanchard] (drums). They were managed for a time by [a=Simon Bentley].
They also owned the [l=Flaw Recordings] and [l=Screaming Target] labels.

artist.name: Monkey Mafia
artist.profile:
Monkey Mafia's brand of big-beat "Brit-hop-amyl-house" is similar in respects to that of the Chemical Brothers, Fatboy Slim, and artists associated with the Skint and Wall of Sound labels. The production arm of Essex-born DJ Jon Carter's many club-related activities, the project began in 1995, following a string of tracks for Wall of Sound under the name Artery and coinciding with his residency at ...
When playing live they were joined by the MC [a=Dougie Reuben], DJ [a=First Rate] & [a=Tom Symmons].

artist.name: Psychonauts
artist.profile:
Originally a trio based in Yeovil, UK.
Lionel left before being involved in any Psychonauts singles, EPs and albums, except for involvement in their live DJ sets and the two [a=James Lavelle] credited mixes on the [r=207457] & [r=161518] releases, as well as their outstanding BBC Radio 1 Essential Mix, 15th Dec 1996 (never commercially released).
Former members:
Lionel Kisnorbo (1996-1997)

artist.name: Primax
artist.profile:
Primax was the "nom de plume" of producer/remixer Ian D Smith who worked full time for Media Records UK in the early 1990s. He met label boss Peter Pritchard through his writing for the American Dance Music magazine "Dance Music Authority" (aka DMA) while DJing at "Europe's biggest gay nightclub" Heaven under the name "Ian D" for 4 years in the 90s at "A Saturday Night at Heaven" in the early 1990s.Ian played the main floor every Saturday night for the best part of 4 years.
Before starting at Heaven Ian promoted his own night at The Paradise Club in Islington and as part of the club promotion produced a monthly music review newsletter "Prime Cuts" which was distributed through record stores in London, Birmingham, Blackpool and Edinburgh. The tag line for the mag was "Prime Cuts - To The Max". The name "Primax" for Ian's remix and production work came from shrinking that tagline down to a single word!
Most Primax mixes were produced with Media label boss Peter Pritchard who worked mostly with Ian and Stu Allan (under the name "Clock") However Ian also produced four remixes (one unreleased) with famous ex-Stock Aitken Waterman producers Phil Harding and Ian Curnow under the name "Primax and The Power Syndicate" after being hired by their manager Tom Watkins to help his band 2wo Third3 and then being rehired several times when a radio hit of his remix of a 2wo Third3 track gave them their first (and only) Top 20 hit.

urls.url: Currently DJs under the name "DJ Fast and Fluid"

artist.name: Davy DMX
artist.profile: American musician, DJ/producer, born in The Bronx, NYC on October 3, 1960. Also known as Davy D., he got involved with hip-hop by DJ'ing around neighborhoods in Queens. In the early 1980s, as a guitarist, he joined with bassist Larry Smith and drummer Trevor Gale to form Orange Krush, a group who recorded a handful of singles, in addition to several backing tracks for Run-DMC, Sweet G, and Lovebug Starski. After the group separated, he worked with Kurtis Blow as a DJ and guitarist, providing significant contributions to "If I Ruled the World" and "The Breaks." Scattered singles like 1984's "One for the Treble" and 1985's "The DMX Will Rock" put him on the map as a solo artist; in 1987, a full-length album called Davy's Ride followed for Def Jam. Apart from occasional behind-the-scenes work on records, Davy DMX also appeared as a member of the Afros, a comical group who sprouted from another group called Solo Sounds. Tuff City released a collection of four-track recordings called FFFresh, albeit without his knowledge.

artist.name: Crooklyn Clan
artist.profile: The Crooklyn Clan is a New York based Hip-Hop and Mash Up production team. The members are Brooklyn NY natives DJ Sizzahandz & DJ Riz. The group was formed in 1993 with their first release on Nervous Records. They produced Fatman Scoop’s hit single, "Be Faithful". The single went platinum in Europe and hit number one in various countries. They also produced the follow-up single, "It Takes Scoop". The song went gold in Europe and entered the Top 40 in Australia, United Kingdom and Ireland. The Duo has produced tracks for artists including DJ Kool, Funkmaster Flex, Sean Paul, & Stik-E & The Hoodz, as well as featured music in the movie Save The Last Dance and warm up sets for the New York Knicks.

artist.name: Eric B. & Rakim
artist.profile: American hip hop duo from Long Island, New York, composed of DJ [a69709] ([a=Eric Barrier]) and MC [a=Rakim] ([a671902]). Formed in 1986, the group stopped in 1993, and reunited in 2018.

artist.name: Eddie Bo
artist.profile:
Eddie Bo (Born: September 20, 1930 – Died: March 18, 2009) was an American singer and pianist from New Orleans. Schooled in jazz, he was known for his blues, soul and funk recordings, compositions, productions and arrangements.
Bo wrote many songs for [l=Ric Records] under his wife [a=Dolores Johnson]'s name. Founder of the [l120190] record label.

artist.name: Andreas Schmidt
artist.profile:
German trance and house producer, born in 1976. (Label : Venom Recordings / Publishing company Head of AceAge Musikverlag).
For the classical vocalist, please see [a=Andreas Schmidt (2)]. For the death metal drummer, please see [a=Andreas Schmidt (3)]. For the jazz pianist, please see [a=Andreas Schmidt (6)]. For the German actor, please see [a=Andreas Schmidt (7)]. For the classical double bassist, please see [a=Andreas Schmidt (8)]. For the classical violinist, please see [a=Andreas Schmidt (13)].

artist.name: Havana
artist.profile: Progressive house outfit from Scotland, perhaps best known for the hits "Schtoom" and "Ethnic Prayer".

artist.name: Sublime
artist.profile:
Progressive house / trance project from Scotland, United Kingdom.
[b]For the American Rock band, please use [a98600].[/b]

artist.name: Harri
artist.profile: DJ/producer based in Glasgow, Scotland. One half of [a=Subculture (11)], active for over 25 years. His son is [a=Jasper James (3)].

artist.name: Probe
artist.profile: Progressive house producer trio.

artist.name: Praga Khan
artist.profile:
Praga Khan is a pseudonym of Belgian producer Maurice Engelen, sometimes credited additionally as Somora on early releases. Other artists who record with Maurice under the guise of Praga Khan include Jade 4U, Marco Rosso, Oliver Adams and Ransom.
As well as being co-founder of many successful acts like 101, Lords of Acid and MNO, Maurice was also co-founder of Antler Subway Records along with Roland Beelen, better known as Bellucci in [a=Morton Sherman Bellucci].
Has a younger nephew in Maurits Engelen, better known as [a=Maurizzio].

artist.name: 2 Unlimited
artist.profile:
Popular Eurodance duo founded by Belgian producers [a=Jean-Paul de Coster] and [a=Phil Wilde].
First released as an instrumental in spring 1991, "Get Ready For This" received an immediate success (#2 in the UK charts). Conscious of their popularity, Wilde & De Coster wanted a more accessible, formatted formula for their project to grow. [a=Raymond Lothar Slijngaard] was then contacted to write lyrics and add a rap to the track. On his suggestion, [a=Anita Dels] (a singer from the Amsterdam club scene) joined in as the female vocalist. The new re-version of "Get Ready For This" was released in September 1991 and helped to perpetuate the success of 2 Unlimited around the world. The following single, "Twilight Zone", did even better in the charts and became a well known club anthem. A full album announced two more successful tracks: "Workaholic" and "The Magic Friend". Their second opus, "No Limits!", was released in 1993. It's popularity was such that no less than five singles were taken out of it. Among them, "No Limit" and "Tribal Dance". The single 'No Limit' became their biggest hit. It reached number one in more than 35 countries and was the best selling single of 1993 in Europe.
In 1996, a complicated relationship between Ray and Anita precipitate the breaking of 2 Unlimited. For a moment, a background vocalist was due to replace Anita beside Ray, but ultimately, she refused the contract offered to her. A relaunch attempt (2 Unlimited 2) featuring two female singers ([a=Romy (2)] and [a=Marion Van Iwaarden]) was tested, but only had a mitigated success. Both singers left once their contracts ended in 1998. Recent petitions asking for the return of 2 Unlimited were ignored as Ray and Anita do not wish to work together again.
In Feb 10. 2009 the original members Ray & Anita announced in a 10 minute interview on the Dutch TV Show "De Wereld Draait Door" there reunion concert on the 11th. of April 2009 in Hasselt - Belgium. In the interview Ray hinted at a possible reunion but no plans has surfaced yet.

artist.name: Lords Of Acid
artist.profile:
Sex-obsessed Belgian-American post-industrial/techno band founded in Antwerp in 1988 as Major Problem with frontperson [a=Nathalie Delaet]. One of [a=Maurice Engelen] (Praga Khan)'s many New Beat/Hard Beat projects, originally with [a=Jos Borremans] (Chris Inger) and [a=K. Hendrickx] (Harry Van Oekel). [a=Nikkie Van Lierop] (Jade 4U) became part of the group with the first proper Lords of Acid single "[m=12499]." Natalie Delaet walked out of the band, so Nikkie took over vocal duties for the early singles and the "[m=12543]," album.
In 1991, [a=Oliver Adams] replaced Inger and Van Oekel and Lords of Acid became the main [a=M.N.O.] project (along with [a=Channel X] and [a=Digital Orgasm]), along with [a=Ludo Camberlin] (Carl S. Johansen), and has outlived all others to become a semi-permanent on-going fixture.
[a=Ruth McArdle] (Lady Galore) became the lead singer for 1994's "[m=12738]", she left the band in 1995 during the "The Sextacy Ball," shows with [a=My Life With The Thrill Kill Kult], to join the [a=The Bomb Gang Girlz].
Nikkie took over as lead singer again and was the female vocalist on "[m=12586]," and the 1997 tour.
[a=Deborah Ostrega] then took over vocal duties for the "[m=1230464] Tour," in 2000 (released on CD as "[m=1355106],") and the subsequent "[m=12401]," album, the first without Nikkie. The band went into hiatus after the 2001/02 Tour, that followed the release of the album, with Maurice and Oliver concentrating on Praga Khan's solo output.
In 2010, Maurice Engelen revived Lords Of Acid for the US "Sextreme Ball," tour with a new lineup: [a=Lacey Conner], [a=Sin Quirin], [a=Murv Douglas] and [a=Kirk Salvador], taking over the production duties on his own. [a=Mea Fisher] became the new lead singer in 2011. They released "[m=430771]," in 2012, and the band was put back on ice until a tour in 2017. Mea left the band at the end of the year.
[a=Marieke Bresseleers] was announced as the new frontwoman in January 2018 and "[m=1364753]," was released in May the same year.
Gigi Ricci took over as lead vocalist in 2022 and Olivier Adams rejoined the band for the “Make Acid Great Again” tour in 2024.
Lords Of Acid has featured various additional writers and musicians throughout the years, including [a=Rembert de Smet], [a=Ferre Baelen], [a=Luc Van Acker] and [a=Wim Daans] among the contributors.

artist.name: Channel X
artist.profile:
One of the three longest living projects (alongside [a=Lords of Acid] and [a=Digital Orgasm]) by the Belgian production trio, [a=M.N.O.] - Maurice Engelen (Praga Khan, Somora), Nikkie Van Lierop (Jade 4 U, Miss Lie) and Oliver Adams.
The first two Channel X singles "Groove To Move," and "Rave The Rhythm," were hits on the emerging rave scene and are featured on numerous compilations of the era. In 1992, "Rave the Rhythm," was also featured in the original motion picture "Basic Instinct."
In 1994, they were joined by vocalist Julie Wells, bassist Hans Bruyninckx and fellow producer Marco Rosso to record an album "To the Top."

namevariations.name: 2 Little Boys
The Two Little Boys
Two Little Boys: Amadeus Mozart & Guy Garrett

artist.name: Code Red
artist.profile: Belgian house / techno outfit, active early '90s, one of the many projects involving [a118541] aka [a4056], [a425034] aka [a31109] and collaborators.

artist.name: Human Resource
artist.profile: Dutch hardcore/gabber group established in 1990 by Robert Mahu & Johan van Beek. Later joined by keyboardists Jasper Drexhage and Guido Pernet (also MC), as well as by rapper and former professional basketball player Larenzo Nash. All came from Ridderkerk, The Netherlands. In 1992 Van Beek left without being replaced. Soon after rapper [a=Marvin D (2)] (aka Marvin Tholen) became a band member but left in 1995. After Drexhage's departure over dissatisfaction with their new hardcore sound he was replaced by Sander Scheurwater. Human Resource disbanded at the end of 1990s. In 2004 Guido Pernet formed the band once again, this time with Zenon Zevenbergen (as MC) and Maurice Steenbergen. Zevenbergen left soon after the tour in 2005. The same year they introduced the new hardcore-gabber music festival [l=Dominator].

artist.name: Shut Up & Dance
artist.profile: English music duo and DJ's. Carlton Hyman ([a=Smiley]) & Philip Johnson ([a=PJ (3)]) were rappers on the sound system called Heatwave, trying to get a record deal. No record label would sign them so they decided to start their own label, Shut Up and Dance Music. Way back in 1986 the Heatwave were four guys named Smiley, PJ, [a=DJ Daddy] & [a=DJ Hype].

artist.name: Airheadz
artist.profile: Airheadz was a UK trance production duo based in London, comprising Leigh Guest and Andrew Peach. They were later joined by Caroline De Batselier who acted as vocalist and live performer. They are best known for the trance hit single "Stanley (Here I Am)" which charted in many countries and started off as a bootleg ("Stanley - Stanley's In A Trance Mix") due to it originally containing a sample from Dido's hit single "Thankyou" (also sampled by Eminem's "Stan" during the same period). Despite being an unofficial release, the track rapidly became popular on the UK and European club scene and radio circuit during late 2000 to early 2001. However, due to it being unable to acquire publishing clearance for the use of Dido's vocal segment, the track wasn't able to get an official release. In Spring 2001, the track was re-released by AM:PM with the Dido sample having been removed and replaced with an entirely new vocal segment (Caroline De Batselier), modifying the feel of the track quite profoundly. In April 2001, the official (modified) version reached a peak position of #36 in the UK Singles Chart and is currently listed in the Top 100 trance anthems of all time.

artist.name: Sunbeam
artist.profile: German Trance/Hard Trance project.

artist.name: Future Breeze
artist.profile:
The DJs and producers Markus Boehme and Martin Hensing from Essen in Germany established Future Breeze in 1995.
Markus and Martin managed to get their first record contract in the founding year and hit the mark by entering the national dance charts with the single "Read My Lips“. The big international success came one year later with the massive cult hit "Why Don’t You Dance With Me“, which also reached the formidable chart position 14 in the German sales charts. The follow-up singles „How Much Can You Take“ and „Temple Of Dreams“ were similarly strong and performed well on the worldwide dancefloors.
Parallel to their own singles, they earned great reputation due to numerous remixes for artists like Sash!’s „Encore Un Fois“, a single which ranked at position 2 of the British sales charts in 1997.
In 2021, Markus and Martin announced that producer Vincent Price had joined the group.

artist.name: CJ Stone
artist.profile:
German trance producer and DJ, part of the Bass Bumpers production team since 1994 whose productions and remixes have achieved some chart success. Co-founder of [l343233] along with partners Michel Beugel and Adrie Berk.
Fun fact: The name stems from his childhood nickname due to his love for cassettes: CJ = Cassette Jockey
See also [a1153764].

artist.name: Peewee Ferris
artist.profile: DJ, producer, composer from Sydney, Australia. House DJ from the Hordern Parties in Sydney that started the modern dance culture in Australia. Composed music for events such Sydney Olympics Opening and Closing Ceremony 2000, World Expo Shanghai and Dubai, and T20 World Cup Opening, Sydney Mardi Parade and Vivid Light Festival. Long-time resident of super club Home Night over 20 years. Co owner of Australian dance label Two Tribes Records, recording artist alias include Pipi Le Oui and Splice Collective.

artist.name: John Truelove
artist.profile: British producer and label founder. Established the house label [l=Truelove] in 1990, followed by the publishing company [l=Truelove Music] and the [l=Truelove Label Collective]. Known for managing rights and royalties for a wide range of electronic music releases throughout the 1990s and 2000s.

artist.name: Des Mitchell
artist.profile: UK DJ & producer, born in Birmingham to Jamaican parents. He scored a UK #5 hit with the [a=Airscape]-produced single "[url=https://www.discogs.com/master/87008-Des-Mitchell-Welcome-To-The-Dance]Welcome To The Dance[/url]" in 2000. Currently residing in Palma de Mallorca, Spain.

artist.name: Woob
artist.profile: English ambient musician who started in the 1990s. His music combine elements of ambient, downtempo and space music with samples from field recordings, movies and television. After a period working in the advertising industry, he has started releasing music as Woob again in 2010.

artist.name: Bomb The Bass
artist.profile: English musician and producer of electronic music Tim Simenon. With ‘Beat Dis’, Bomb The Bass helped mould late 1980s acid house. Tim Simenon, has led the group through a number of different incarnations. More recently Bomb The Bass have settled as a duo, vocalist Paul Conboy providing lyrical complements to Simenon’s productions.
