artist.name: Fink
artist.profile: English singer, songwriter, guitarist, producer and DJ, born in Cornwall and currently based in Berlin and London.

artist.name: Super-A-Loof
artist.profile: Degiorgio being the main player, some Super-A-Loof work was done with Jamie Odell while other tracks were done with Ian O'Brien.

artist.name: Amp
artist.profile: Amp is an English group based around the core members Richard Walker and Karine Charff. Walker was a member of a band in Bristol called The Secret Garden that incubated a number of groups including Flying Saucer Attack and Third Eye Foundation. In 1992 he left the band; singles from Amp began to come out on a number of small English labels and a debut album called Sirènes was released in 1996 on the Wurlitzer Jukebox label in the UK and a short-lived American label called Petrol. Walker made some guest appearances on Third Eye Foundation recordings, and Matt Elliot did a TEF remix of Amp music for the In Version album. A London-based, live Amp unit coalesced around Walker and Charff with Walker's bandmate from The Secret Garden, Gareth Mitchell, joining on guitar. Amp contacted Kranky when their American label dissolved and began work on a new studio album in summer 1996.

artist.name: De La Soul
artist.profile: De La Soul is an American hip-hop trio of Posdnuos, Trugoy, and Maseo, formed in 1987 in the Amityville area of Long Island, New York. They are best known for their eclectic sampling, quirky lyrics, and contributions to the evolution of jazz rap and alternative hip-hop subgenres. The three formed the group in high school, catching the attention of producer Prince Paul with a demo tape of the song "Plug Tunin'." With its playful wordplay, innovative sampling, and witty skits, the band's debut album, '3 Feet High and Rising', has been called "a hip-hop masterpiece." They are original members of the progressive collective known as the [a=Native Tongues], which includes [a=A Tribe Called Quest], [a=Queen Latifah], [a=Black Sheep], [a=Monie Love], and founding members the [a=Jungle Brothers]. Their output throughout the '90s, 1991's 'De La Soul Is Dead,' 1993's 'Buhoone Mindstate' and 1996's 'Stakes Is High,' while not as commercially successful as their debut, were all formative for hip-hop and are now widely considered cult classics. After 2004's critically acclaimed 'The Grind Date,' and winning a Grammy for their collaboration with [a=Gorillaz] on their single "Feel Good Inc." in 2006, De La Soul went on hiatus sans a few singles and an EP, returning in 2016 with the crowd-funded 'And the Anonymous Nobody.' In 2023, after years of legal battles over rights and ownership, De La Soul regained access to their back catalog from [a=Tommy Boy Records] and finally released their classic albums on streaming services and new reissues for the first time. Unfortunately, it was bittersweet after the death of original member Dave 'Trugoy' Jolicoeur, days before the release. Jolicoeur passed away at 54 after suffering from congestive heart failure diagnosed in 2017. Industry rumors suggest an untitled tenth album may be released with production from [a=Pete Rock] and [a=DJ Premier]. Individual aliases: [a=Dave Jolicoeur] AKA [a=Trugoy The Dove] / Plug Two / [a=Plug 2] [a=Kelvin Mercer] AKA [a=Posdnuos] / [a=Plug Won] / [a=Plug 1] [a=Vincent Mason] AKA [a=Pasemaster Mase] / [a=Maseo] / Plug Three

artist.name: Soma Sky
artist.profile: The is completely a studio project. I (Thomas) perform all the instrumental parts, and am the composer of all the songs. Todd Milne, an outstanding brilliant producer and guitar player performs improvisational lead guitar, and Christie brings in the vocal arrangements, and washes. I also am the brother in law of Lenny Kaye, Patti Smith's lead guitarist and producer. If your interested in Patti Smith and Lenny check out my site. Also, check out the music of Todd Milne on mp3.com.And please watch our website for future releases coming to mp3.com.

artist.name: UTFO
artist.profile:
UTFO = [b]U[/b]n[b]T[/b]ouchable [b]F[/b]orce [b]O[/b]rganization.
Also spelled as U.T.F.O.
Hip-hop act from Brooklyn, New York City. In 1984 they released “Roxanne, Roxanne” which spawned what is known as the “Roxanne Wars”, with over 30 answer songs from over a dozen artists & groups including [m34851].
[a=Shiller Shaun Fequiere] AKA [a=The Kangol Kid] [a=Fred Reeves] AKA [a=Doctor Ice] [a=Jeffrey Campbell] AKA [a=Educated Rapper] [a=Maurice Bailey] AKA [a=Mixmaster Ice].

artist.name: Roxanne Shanté
artist.profile: American MC. Born March 8, 1970 in the Queensbridge Projects of Queens, New York City, Shante first gained attention through the Roxanne Wars and was part of [a173222]. The 2017 film, Roxanne Roxanne, is a dramatization of Shante's life. She first rose to prominence in 1984 when she recorded the vocals for [m34851] in response to [m82829]. Following this, the "Roxanne Wars" started.

artist.name: Whodini
artist.profile: American electronic boogie & hip-hop band from Brooklyn, New York founded in 1981.

artist.name: Egyptian Lover
artist.profile:
Los Angeles-based producer Greg Broussard is the man behind pioneering Electronic Boogie artist the Egyptian Lover.
Egyptian Lover records didn't start appearing until several years after the New York and Miami electro scenes were already in full swing, by the mid-'80s Egyptian Lover cuts like "Egypt, Egypt" were standards among club DJs, particularly in the break-dance scene. Early cuts such as "Computer Love" and "Dance" were among Egyptian Lover's most experimental, with the material appearing through the latter half of the '80s increasingly flirting with full-blown rap by adding more vocals and melodic hooks. While more of his material dates from the latter phase, it's his earlier releases that remain his most accomplished, and they're routinely namechecked by a new generation of producers such as Autechre and I-F inspired by electro's first wave. Also a member of Uncle Jamm's Army and the force behind a half dozen labels including Freak Beat and Egyptian Empire. After nearly a decade, Egyptian Lover released the digitally edited compilation "Pyramix", then hit the studio again and produced new tracks in the same funky electro style for the album "Back From the Tomb".
Egyptian Lover Fan Club (defunct): P.O. Box 19301 L.A., Calif. 90019

artist.name: Bobby Broom
artist.profile: Bobby Broom is an American jazz guitarist, born in Harlem, New York, on January 18, 1961. in the world today, touring with Steely Dan. Broom has performed as a side man with Art Blakey and The Jazz Messengers , Sonny Rollins, Kenny Burrell, Stanley Turrentine, Charles Earland, Miles Davis, and Dr. John, among others, as well as with his own groups.

artist.name: André Tanneberger
artist.profile: Born on February 26, 1973 in Freiberg, Saxony, Germany

artist.name: Ratty
artist.profile: Trance side project of [a=Scooter].

artist.name: Transa
artist.profile: Born in Lancashire, but based in Aberdeen, Transa are brothers Brendan Webster & David Clifford John Webster. Influenced at an early age by the likes of 'Oxygen' and later Depeche Mode.

artist.name: Gouryella
artist.profile: Dutch trance project, originally by [a=Ferry Corsten] and [a=Tijs Verwest] a.k.a [a=DJ Tiësto]. After the release of the third Gouryella single "Tenshi", Tiësto left the project.

artist.name: Tribes Of Superia
artist.profile: contact: info@tribesofsuperia.de

artist.name: Smoke City
artist.profile: English band that blended acid jazz and trip hop, borrowing from Brazilian styles such as samba and bossa nova. They are best known for their single "[i][m221429][/i]", which was a major hit in 1997, after being used in a Levi's television advertisement.

artist.name: Celvin Rotane
artist.profile: German DJ and producer.

artist.name: Everything But The Girl
artist.profile: Tracey Thorn and Ben Watt first emerged as pioneers in the lite-jazz music scene in the early 1980's. They continued to refine their jazz versus electronica sound through eight studio albums until they gained larger exposure in 1994 through a collaboration with [a=Massive Attack] on "Protection" and their hit album "Amplified Heart." [a=Todd Terry]'s remix of "Missing" was a smash hit in 1995 and continues to be remixed and played to this day. After the success of "Missing", the duo took their music in a more beat-oriented and clubbier direction, with Tracey lending her vocals as guest singer on several club tracks by [a=Deep Dish] and others. In 2017, Watt's label [l=Buzzin' Fly Records] took over the Everything But The Girl back catalogue from [l=Warner Music Group], securing rights to most of the band’s recordings. In 2022, Thorn and Watt announced that they were reuniting and had recorded an album set for release in 2023, their first in 24 years. The duo got their name from a furniture store ad which proclaimed, "For your bedroom needs, we have everything but the girl."

artist.name: Todd Terry
artist.profile: American House Music Producer ( Keyboards & Programming) and DJ. Born 18th April 1967 in Brooklyn, New York. Recognised as being instrumental to the development of New York's house music during the 1980's. His productions can be described as a varied collection of samples blending the sounds of classic disco and the Chicago sound pioneered earlier in the decade. In 1991, he co-founded [l=Freeze Records] with [a=Will Socolov], and later established [l=In House Records] in the early 2000s.

artist.name: Olive
artist.profile:
Formed in 1995 by singer Ruth-Ann Boyle, keyboardist Tim Kellett and beat-master Robin Taylor-Firth. Kellett was a member of [a=Simply Red] for their first nine years, as well as an occasional collaborator with UK pop-soul band [a=Lighthouse Family]. Taylor-Firth was one of the original members of [a=Nightmares On Wax]. Boyle used to sing in local bands in Sunderland, where she is from. She had not been singing professionally before Olive was formed. Kellett was playing at a gig in Portugal with [a=The Durutti Column] where he came across Ruth's voice which he then sampled. He loved it so much he asked Durutti Column "who is the vocalist I'm making tape loops of?"
Their peak hit "You're Not Alone" charted at Number 1 in the UK official charts on May 17th 1997 for a total of 2 weeks, despite the original release on September 7th 1996 only reaching number 42 in the UK charts.

artist.name: Faithless
artist.profile:
British electronic band formed in London, England in 1994.
Faithless were [a=Maxwell Fraser] AKA [a=Maxi Jazz] (Conscious Rap) (passed away on 23 December 2022), [a=Ayalah Bentovim] also known as [a=Sister Bliss] (Music), Roland Armstrong AKA [a=Rollo] (Producer, Music). Many of the songs are sung by [a=Pauline Taylor]. [a=Jamie Catto] was originally part of the group but left after the release of the second album "Sunday 8PM". [a=Zoë Johnston] joined for the album "Outrospective".
Their first album, 1996's "Reverence", was an underground sensation but did not quite break the mainstream. 1998's "Sunday 8AM" changed all of that, the single "God Is a DJ" became a big club anthem while the album enjoyed bigger sales than anything they had previously released. Critics may have been harsh, but the group kept moving forward with all members also pursuing solo careers on the side.
When playing live they were joined by guitarist [a=Dave Randall (2)] and bassist, [a=Aubrey Nunn]. Some of the original members (Sister Bliss, Maxi Jazz and percussionist Sudha Kheterpal) continue to perform under the name Faithless Sound System.

artist.name: Deee-Lite
artist.profile:
Deee-Lite was an American house and club/dance group, formed in Williamsburg Brooklyn, New York, United States. The group's best-known single was "Groove Is in the Heart", from their 1990 debut album, "World Clique". However, Deee-Lite achieved longer lasting success on the U.S. Billboard Hot Dance Club Play chart, where they scored six number one hits.
Formed in 1986, the trio was led by vocalist Lady Miss Kier (born Kier Kirby in Youngstown, Ohio) and fleshed out by a pair of DJs, Super DJ Dmitry (a classically-trained guitarist and Ukraine emigre born Dmitry Brill) and Jungle DJ Towa Towa (born Chung Dong-hwa in Yokohama, Japan). Fusing house, techno, rap, ambient and funk music with an outrageous visual flair largely influenced by the Lower East Side's drag-queen community (Kier's fondness for Fluevog platform shoes helped the 1970s fashion revival gather steam), Deee-Lite became hugely popular among New York club denizens, and the trio's own unique cultural make-up earned them a following which ignored racial and sexual boundaries.
In 1990, they debuted with the album [r=1016561], a crossover smash thanks to hits like the looping classic [r=58995] (featuring the fluid bass guitar sampled from a [a=Herbie Hancock] song, vocals by [a=Bootsy Collins], and a rap by a young [a=Q-Tip] of [a=A Tribe Called Quest]) and [r=69234] (the latter of which hit #35 in the Top 40 Billboard). With Bootsy Collins as musical director on the road, the band toured (without Towa) for two sellout world tours in 1991 opening for the likes of [a=Grace Jones], [a=Prince], [a=Kid Creole and the Coconuts], and [a=Parliament] [a=Funkadelic].
With their 1992 follow-up, [r=1138879], Deee-Lite's music turned openly political, with songs about the environment, safe sex and democracy. Lady Miss Kier won the Environmental Media award by the WWF in 1992 and spoke at Princeton University and Cooper Union in 1993.
Kier and Dmitry enlisted DJ Ani (previously know as, On-E) for 1994's [r=122801], a sensual outing influenced by the growing rave culture. After recording the album, the band did one final world tour. With minimal record label support, Deee-Lite still managed to have a sellout world tour—consequently, "Dewdrops in the Garden" sold more records than "Infinity Within". 1996 saw a release of a remix compilation, [r=38618].

artist.name: Energy 52
artist.profile: Project by [a=Paul M.] (DJ [a=Kid Paul]) The biggest hit "Café Del Mar" is composed by Paul Schmitz-Moormann together with Harald Blüchel ([a=Cosmic Baby]), also for every other Energy 52 release beside "Weak", Paul worked together with Harald.

artist.name: DJ Friction
artist.profile: British drum & bass DJ and record producer from Brighton, England. Edward Keeley, alias DJ Friction or Friction, also heads up his own record labels, [l=Shogun Audio], [l=Elevate Audio] and [l=Maraki Records] He has started his career in 1998.

artist.name: Kosheen
artist.profile: British trip hop, breakbeat, and rock band, founded in 1998 in Bristol. The trio had their European breakthrough in 2000-2001 with the drum 'n' bass track Hide U, followed by the debut album [m=130257], which included another string of (minor) chart hits. On later albums, Kosheen experimented with various other styles of music. The 2013 album [m=639980] is their last to date.

artist.name: The Preacher (2)
artist.profile: Italian House project.

artist.name: Ramirez
artist.profile:
Italian dance project created in the early 90's by the Italian [l=DFC] label. Produced by various members of their production team alongside the inseparable Spanish voice of Alex Quiroz Buelvas.
Musicians: Davide Rizzatti, Elvio Moratto, Ricki Persi Singer/frontman for videos/performances: Alex Quiroz Buelvas

artist.name: V
artist.profile: Soul/R&B singer and songwriter.

artist.name: Seven
artist.profile: Techno producer from Australia. Active in the early 2000s. Released an EP on Australian label [l=Dirty House Records].

artist.name: Mario J
artist.profile: Techno/House artist from Toronto, Canada.

artist.name: Sun Children
artist.profile: https://controlteam.bandcamp.com/music

artist.name: L.A. Williams
artist.profile: American DJ and producer from Chicago. Owner of the [l=Chisel] label.

artist.name: Arthur Baker
artist.profile:
Highly influential American producer, remixer, DJ and musician (drummer, percussionist). Runs [l=Baked Recordings]. Born on April 22, 1955 in Boston, Massachusetts. He is best known for his work with artists such as [a=Afrika Bambaataa], [a=Planet Patrol] and the British group [a=New Order]. Baker began working as a DJ in Boston in the mid 1970s, where he played early disco and soul for the early Boston club scene alongside famed local DJs including John Luongo and Jimmy Stuard. He had little patience for DJing, saying in an interview: "[If] I didn't get a good reaction on a record, I'd just rip it off, break it up and throw it on the dancefloor." In 1977 he turned to production with the Hearts Of Stone project, released on the Canadian Disco 1 label. He also was part of the group [a=North End] in Boston, releasing two well-regarded 12"s, respectively on West End and Emergency Records.
In 1981, Baker moved to New York, where he continued as a producer, working with Michael Jonzun and Maurice Starr on several releases for the Posse Records label. He also worked with Joe Bataan, Larry Wu, Tom Moulton, Jo Bisso, and his then wife [a39293] by the start of the 1980s, which would be his most prolific and influential decade of work. He was especially well known for his association with [a=The Latin Rascals] who did edits on many of Baker's productions through the 1980s, including Hall and Oates and The Rolling Stones.
He went on to produce for Tommy Boy Records, where he produced [a=Afrika Bambaataa & Soulsonic Force]'s classic Planet Rock single, which was a hit in the summer of 1982. The record combined elements from two [a=Kraftwerk] recordings, viz. Trans Europe Express and Numbers, which were interpolated by studio musicians, rather than sampled. From the groundbreaking electro recorded at the same session, he produced [a=Planet Patrol]'s Play At Your Own Risk single in 1982, another Tommy Boy group that hit big in 1983, establishing a major precursor to freestyle and "urban contemporary" music that was to come. Also during 1982, he produced the single Walking On Sunshine by [a=Rockers Revenge] featuring [a=Donnie Calvin] which hit number one on the U.S. Dance chart on September 18 that year. Baker launched his first record label Streetwise in the same year. Later labels including Criminal Records and Pow Wow, as well as Baker's Shakedown Studios, would soon follow.
In 1983, Baker found work doing dance remixes of pop and rock hits, first with [a=Cyndi Lauper]'s Girls Just Wanna Have Fun and [a=Bruce Springsteen]'s Dancing In The Dark, Cover Me, and Born In The U.S.A. from his Born In The U.S.A. album. He also came to the attention of British alternative dance group [a=New Order], which asked him to produce its singles Confusion and Thieves Like Us. The 12-inch only Confusion (Baker can be seen prominently in its music video) was a crossover hit on the U.S. dance charts, and established a relationship between Baker and the band that has continued for more than 20 years.
In 1984, Baker contributed his Breakers' Revenge to the Beat Street score and movie soundtrack, which he also helped produce. The infamous and much sought after remixes for [a=Pet Shop Boys]' Suburbia followed in 1986 which happens to be the birth year of [a=Hurts] lead singer [a=Theo Hutchcraft] with a notable collaboration in 2011 (Wonderful Life). Narrowly missing out on signing the [a=Beastie Boys] to his Streetwise label, Baker did manage to sign the group [a=New Edition], which had success with its single Candy Girl.
Also in 1984, the New York-based Baker also worked with Philadelphians [a95886] as mix consultant on their album Big Bam Boom, and the result was a markedly urban and electronic sound for the duo. Baker co-wrote the opening instrumental Dance On Your Knees with Hall. He also remixed that song and the album's other three chart hits Out Of Touch, Method Of Modern Love and Possession Obsession. In 1985, Baker helped [a=Bob Dylan] complete his Empire Burlesque album as mixer and arranger, and with [a=Little Steven] organized and produced the anti-apartheid anthem Sun City by [a=Artists United Against Apartheid].
In the late 1980s and later into the 1990s, Baker worked with soul star [a=Al Green], writing and producing the international hit The Message Is Love and the anti-handgun song Leave The Guns At Home. In 1989 he released the album Merge on A&M Records as [a50161], and remixed [a=Neneh Cherry]'s debut single Buffalo Stance. He was also the music supervisor of the films Fried Green Tomatoes and Listen Up - The Lives Of Quincy Jones.
Following a break from production for some years, Baker moved to London, England, in the 1990s and established a chain of successful bars (The Elbow Rooms) across the city. He also owns the Tiny Robot restaurant and The Starland Social Club members bar in London, located in Notting Hill. He continues to work as a DJ and producer.

artist.profile: Romanian producer from Constanța, based & active in Bucharest

artist.name: Lil' Devious
artist.profile: Production duo from Carlisle. Ran [l=Natural Selection Recordings].

artist.name: Basco
artist.profile: Dutch breakbeat/house project.

artist.name: Tranquil Elephantizer
artist.profile: Since our first incarnation in South London during the early 1990’s, Tranquil Elephantizer have output a string of eclectic tracks and remixes and appeared on multiple DJ compilations. Over the years we’ve produced tracks for underground labels like Matrix, Language, Definitive and Primal whilst consistently playing out live and DJ’ ing at festivals and clubs around the world. Live highlights include: Sugarlump, Glastonbury, Megadog, DIY, Drum Club, Universe, Megatriplolis, Club UK, Raindance, Joshua Tree and Burning Man. Original Members: Alexi Worrel, DjShakra, Caspar Kedros, Darius Kedros.

artist.name: Stephanie Cooke
artist.profile:
USA Singer and songwriter.
Stephanie grew up in the inner city of Newark, New Jersey and like most of her contemporaries got her start by singing in church. At the tender age of 9, she belted out a stirring rendition of "I Made A Vow" as stunned parishioners listened to her sing. At 15 she wrote her first song, but it wasn't until 10 years later that she would pen her first non-gospel song. Naturally, her latter influences would never stray far from the former. Stephanie credits [a=Fred Hammond], [a=The Clark Sisters] and [a=Marvin Winans] as being her greatest influences as a vocalist. Her songwriting influences, however lean more towards [a=Stevie Wonder], [a=Diane Warren], and [a=Babyface].
Stephanie's professional music career began when she entered a production studio in hope of procuring some studio time as a prize for a talent show she was coordinating. After obtaining an impromptu a cappella session, she was signed to a production deal on the spot. Unfortunately, the following two and a half years of writing and recording went by without the production company releasing one song. As if bred by defiance, her motivation propelled her lifelong dedication to music and songwriting. Determined to make up for lost time, she gathered all her songs and started making cold calls to every publishing company she could find. Before long she was picked up and the following year would yield some dramatic results. Stephanie has since worked as a songwriter with [a=Chanté Moore], [a=Jermaine Dupri] (for [a=Aretha Franklin]'s "Every Little Bit Hurts"), All-Star, and with Soul Shock and Carlin wrote [a=Shiro]'s "I Like".
Additionally she's worked on remixes for [a=SWV], [a=Changing Faces], [a=The Braxtons] with [a=DJ Enuff], and background vocals for [a=Diana Ross] with [a=Malik Pendleton]. She's written "Do You Need" for Tyrese, "Be Mine" for Charlotte, "Are You Ready", "You'll Never Find a Love" from [a=Jody Watley]'s "Off the Hook" album, and "I'm Your Lady" for [a=Meeka] and has also written for Phaja. Furthermore, on an international level, she wrote the song "Love of Mine" for Japanese R&B diva, Double. In 1997 she wrote and sang 3 songs for "Closer]", an album composed by Japanese producer [a=Shinichi Osawa] also known as [a=Mondo Grosso].
Stephanie has propelled herself as an artist with her own identity, combining her prowess as an R&B/Gospel & Garage House songstress alongside the buzz that has already ignited the dance community. She operates the [l=Angeltown Recordings] label.

artist.name: Chord Symbols
artist.profile: Dutch producer, used Ian (or Ijan) Van Dalen as alias on his releases

artist.name: Patrick Coutin
artist.profile: French singer born 21/03/1952 in Tunisia.

artist.name: Oliver Ho
artist.profile:
Prolific London, UK based DJ/producer who debuted on [l=Blueprint] in 1996.
Founded his own label, [l=Meta] in 1997 and continued to develop his style towards a more dancefloor friendly brand of funky tribal techno, while retaining experimental elements and continuing to produce for other labels. Later set up a number of sublabels under Meta to cater for a widening repertoire of electronic styles, incorporating influences from jazz, funk, fusion, rock and avant garde styles of all kinds.

artist.name: Chris McCormack
artist.profile: Former techno producer, now mastering and mixing engineer with his company [l312477]. Born in 1973, from UK. Has also produced a number of library music albums including collaborations with Paul Gannaway for Standard Music Ltd.

artist.name: Giorgio Moroder
artist.profile: Italian musician, composer, arranger, producer, performer and DJ born on April 26, 1940 in Ortisei, Bolzano, Italy, and currently living in Los Angeles (USA). Winner of 3 Academy Awards, 3 Grammy Awards & 3 Golden Globes, he is considered one of the most innovative and influential musicians in electronic and disco music. He founded the [l=Oasis] label and [l265005]. In the 1970s he invented the [i]Munich Sound[/i] (often confused with [i]Italo-Disco[/i] which will arrive later) and created the first iconic releases with [a=Donna Summer] and [a=Roberta Kelly]. Today, he is back to DJing for passion. He was called Hansjörg by his mother.

artist.name: DJ Remy
artist.profile: Dutch Progressive-House DJ & Producer from The Hague. Ran labels [l=Taste Recordings] (1994-2004) & [l=Area Code 070] (1996-2001), and now [l=68 Recordings] since 2006.

artist.name: Savannah
artist.profile: French house duo.

artist.name: Max Graham
artist.profile: British-Canadian DJ, composer, and producer of trance and dance music. Born in April 17, 1971 in London, and raised in Vancouver in the late 1990s.

artist.name: James Holden
artist.profile: UK-based producer, head of [l=Border Community]. Started as a Progressive House and Trance producer, but later managed to travel into different worlds, such as Ambient, IDM, Electronica, Noise, Minimal, Techno, Leftfield, Krautrock and even Gnawa.

artist.name: Black Strobe
artist.profile: Artists project from Paris, France (UE), initially composed of Arnaud Rebotini and Ivan Smagghe. Ivan left in 2006 and the group has since gradually expanded with the arrival of other musicians. Their music is closer to electronic body music and electroclash.

artist.name: User (4)
artist.profile: Some releases on [l=User] / [l=00A] / [l=Extra Length Recordings] were produced under the pseudonym of [a=Dean Cole] and some releases were produced by the pseudonym of [a=Richard Harvey].

[b]For anonymous-type credits, please use [a967691] + ANV.[/b]

artist.name: Union Jack
artist.profile:
Long-running trance production outfit that has been affiliated with the [l=Platipus] label since its start-up. Most well-known for their early acid hits like "Red Herring" and "Two Full Moons And A Trout".
The group originally consisted of Berry & Giussani, but in 2008, Paul Brogden (AKA [a=Pob]) joined the group to create the new track: "Papillon". Giussani is no longer with the group.

artist.name: Ferry Corsten
artist.profile: Dutch DJ, producer and label owner. Born: 4 December 1973, Rotterdam, The Netherlands. Older brother of [a=Robbert Corsten].

artist.name: Art Of Trance
artist.profile: English trance DJ and producer, active from 1993 onwards. Founder and manager of [l925].

artist.name: Albion
artist.profile: Born: 4 December 1973, Rotterdam City, The Netherlands.

artist.name: Pob
artist.profile: House music artist from London, UK.

artist.name: Cygnus X
artist.profile: Early Cygnus X releases were a collaboration between [a=Ralf Hildenbeutel] & [a=Matthias Hoffmann]. Now it is only [a=Matthias Hoffmann].

artist.name: Clanger
artist.profile: Project of Simon Paul Berry and Paul Hirstwood Brogden.

artist.name: Quivver
artist.profile: DJ, producer and vocalist from UK. Progressive house and trance, melodic house, tech-house and house productions and remixes. Owner of [l15712] and [l1392323].

artist.name: Oliver Lieb
artist.profile: Frankfurt based DJ & producer Oliver Lieb is involved in the production of electronic music since 1988. With more than 250 singles and albums including remixes for Faithless, Moby, Snap, Yello, Human League, Mory Kante, Utah Saints to name a few. At the age of 14 he started playing bass in several Funk/Soul/Jazz bands. In late 1989 Oliver’s first record [a=Force Legato] “[m=76733]” got released by Dorian Gray resident DJ Torsten Fenslau on his [a=Abfahrt] label and it was the first significantly recognised release by him. In the years to follow Oliver released everything in the electronic genres from downtempo and chill-out to hard experimental techno and trance. His project [a=Spicelab] on [l=Harthouse] established a platform for him, including the release of three full length album releases. Oliver created several pseudonyms for each of his different styles. His [a=L.S.G.] and [a=Paragliders] releases on [l=Superstition] were his trance projects with 10 years and 5 Albums. With other aliases, he's been mainly into techno and minimal. One of his first own labels was [l=1dot12]. His maxis and albums got licensed on Hooj Choons, Yoshitoshi, Platipus, Duty Free, Magic Muzic and many others. He left Superstition in 2004. Oliver announced the start of his own label “[l=Maschine]” in summer 2005 which is distributed worldwide by “Neuton” and online exclusively through Beatport and released his first single “[a=Solieb] - [m=22521]” in July. The label was focusing on experimental and minimal techno. In 2007 Oliver started a new company doing mastering and cutting vinyl with his friend [a=Jörg Henze], called [l=LH Audio Services]. Around this time, he took a break from DJing and music productions. In 2010 Oliver started a new label, [l193186].

artist.name: Fascia
artist.profile:
Formed - 1997.
Disbanded - 2001.
Short-lived musical/performance project, which involved Todd Kitchens & Chandler Rentz (plus multimedia artists Honnie Goode and Elizabeth Nayadely).

artist.name: Chris Vrenna
artist.profile:
Chris Vrenna (b. February 23, 1967 in Erie, Pennsylvania) is an American musician, producer and remixer. Mostly known for being drummer for Nine Inch Nails from 1989 until 1997 and subsequently working solo on his project Tweaker.
Chris Vrenna played the drums on Marilyn Manson's "Against All Gods" tour in 2005. He replaced [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/Ginger+Fish]Ginger Fish[/url], who was injured. Since 2009, he has replaced [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/Madonna+Wayne+Gacy]Madonna Wayne Gacy[/url] as the permanent keyboardist for Marilyn Manson.

artist.name: Stefano Noferini
artist.profile: Italian techno/house DJ and producer born in Florence. He is the founder of [l=Sound4Group].

artist.name: Medway
artist.profile: Electronic music producer & mastering engineer from London, UK. Owner of [l268075].

artist.name: Tekitha
artist.profile: Wu-Tang Clan affiliated singer and vocalist. One half of mother-daughter duo "O.N.E. The Duo" with Prana Supreme (Tekitha’s daughter with Wu-Tang Clan’s de-facto leader RZA).

artist.name: Duane Harden
artist.profile: American vocalist on many big House music productions, such as 'You Don't Know Me' by [a=Armand Van Helden] and 'What You Need' by [a=Powerhouse].

artist.name: Mita
artist.profile: Singer in the electronic genre.

artist.name: Salz
artist.profile: German duo.

artist.name: Joachim Spieth
artist.profile: German-based artist, DJ and producer. Founder of [l=Affin].

artist.name: Mental Power
artist.profile: UK drum'n'bass duo from Sheffield, comprised of Patrick James & Richard Williams.

artist.name: Tekniq
artist.profile: Tekniq are JT and DJ Hostyle otherwise known as John Williams and Jonathan Wright.

artist.name: Grasscutter
artist.profile: UK Drum n Bass DJ duo based in Sheffield, England.

artist.name: Tango & Ratty
artist.profile: David Smith, Jamie Giltrap......Sadly Jamie Giltrap passed away (27 May 1971 - 31 Jan 2018) R.I.P

artist.name: Barcode
artist.profile: UK drum'n'bass duo from Sheffield, comprised of Patrick James & Richard Williams.

artist.name: Skanna
artist.profile: Early alias of [a=John Graham], used to produce dark and atmospheric drum-'n'-bass in the early- to mid-1990's.

artist.name: Bert
artist.profile: UK Jungle, Drum n Bass producer.

artist.name: Dean Coleman
artist.profile: House DJ and producer.

artist.name: Junior Jack
artist.profile: Italian DJ and producer, born in Rutigliano 31 August 1971 but grew up in Belgium. He started his music career in 1985. Owner of label [l=Adesso Music].

artist.name: Steve Smooth
artist.profile: Adored by crowds worldwide, Steve has taken his skills as a DJ to over 30 different countries in the last 5 years & to over 150 shows just last year alone. Steve’s talent as a DJ is only matched by his proficiency as a producer/remixer with releases on such labels as Ultra Records, Ministry of Sound, Spinnin Records, Republic & Atlantic Records. Steve has released 5 full length albums with multiple #1 records on Beatport.com and is also credited for having the 1st song ever sold on Beatport.com. Steve along with DJ Bam Bam under their alias Dirty Werk have recently done remixes for Martin Garrix, Janet Jackson, Daddy Yankee, Enrique Iglesias, Ariana Grande, Mark Ronson, Camila Cabello, Bebe Rexha & Kygo. Their latest 2 original releases titled “Stand Up” & “No Letting Go” both hit #1 on the Billboard Dance Club Chart along with having 15 #1 remixes achieve this honor. He has also just recently won Back to Back awards in 2017 for “Best Rise & Drop!” & 2018 for “Best Remix Collaboration” at the Remix Awards which take place at Miami Music Week.

artist.name: Josh The Funky 1
artist.profile: Josh "The Funky 1" hails from Chicago, the home of House Music. A DJ for over 12 years, Josh blends a unique style of funky house, tech-house and breaks, while incorporating ill-scratching and turntable tricks. Josh’s passion for the art of DJing first came about when he discovered hip-hop while still in grade school. A fan of early pioneering hip-hop groups like Public Enemy, EPMD, Eric B. and Rakim, he was enthralled by the art of scratching. One day a friend lent Josh a videotape of a DJ battle, which unknowingly turned out to be a D.M.C. battle, the most renowned DJ battle in the world. Mesmerized by what he saw could be done with 2 turntables and a mixer, Josh practiced and honed his skills relentlessly. His passion and persistence proved to be worthwhile, as he has since displayed his skills in some of the most prestigious DJ battles in the world. 1997 saw Josh become a D.M.C Midwest Finalist. In 1999 he competed in the Winter Music Conference scratching competition where he was a runner up to 3-time world champion DJ Craze. In 2000 Josh took 1st place in the beat-mixing competition at the Winter Music Conference DJ spin-off. Aside from competing, Josh’s passion for music has led him to compile several mix compilations including his own "House Funktion" series, which showcases his diverse music selection and wicked turntable skills. Josh has also helped co-produce legendary DJ BadBoyBill’s "Bangin the Box Vol. 4" and "Banging in London" mix compilations. As if his DJing accomplishments weren’t enough, Josh has also had his hand in the studio producing tracks for the infamous Chicago labels I.H.R, Kid Dynamite, and Strictly Hype Recordings. His tracks have been featured on mix compilations and DJ play lists throughout the world, further establishing his reputation as a DJ/Producer. Josh has also lent a hand in remixing Chicago’s number one house diva, Dajae. Her first single on Kid Dynamite records entitled "Time" is an uplifting vocal anthem that has received Billboard attention and has since been licensed to Credence Records in the U.K. Most Recently Josh has remixed Dajae’s newest single "Everyday," also on Kid Dynamite Records and already licensed to Defected Records U.K. However, “The Funky 1” is not one to be content with only producing records for other labels. In 2001, “The Funky 1” will present his own record label, Funktion Recordings. The first release “Everybody Everywhere” by BSJ and Fun-K is sure to find its way into the record boxes of DJ’s worldwide. When not in the studio or running his label, Josh is constantly touring the world bringing his sound and skills together for the masses. Be on the look out for Josh "The Funky 1" in your area soon

artist.name: Hancock
artist.profile: Progressive House act on Vortex Records from 2002

artist.name: Vogel
artist.profile: From Los Angeles, Calif., USA.

artist.name: Rhythmiker vs. Ratio
artist.profile: Berlin based Techno act, originally from Vienna. Runs [l=Central] label.

artist.name: Andreas Krämer
artist.profile:
German DJ & Producer from Frankfurt. Published by Universal Music.
Andreas Krämer started producing back in the early nineties and formed the Frankfurt based label, [l=Thai Records], together with Planet Core Productions (PCP) when he was 16. A short time later the well known Techno/Hardtechno Label construct rhythm was born and formed by him.
At the dawn of the 21st century together with [a=Thomas Pogadl] he formed CR Studios, which are based in Frankfurt/Main. In these studios they produced many tracks and remixes together under different aliases.
After a quiet period Andreas and Thomas started 2 new labels: Wildbite Music and [l=Kreisssaal Music].

artist.name: Ellen Allien
artist.profile: German electronic musician, music producer, and the founder of [l=BPitch Control] and [l1588155] music labels.

artist.name: Access 58
artist.profile: Paulo Nascimento & Philippe Quenum

artist.name: Clemens Neufeld
artist.profile: Austrian House and Techno Pioneer. Lives and works in Vienna. Producer, Remixer, Audio Engineer, Film Music Composer, Labelwoner, DJ. Founder of Vienna's first Techno Club "Space Jungle" in 1991. Head of [l=Fön] and [l=Giant Wheel] records.

artist.name: Takkyu Ishino
artist.profile: Japanese DJ, producer and member of Denki Groove

artist.name: Christian Morgenstern
artist.profile: Christian Morgenstern was born on the 10th of November 1975 in Düsseldorf, Germany. Being interested in technical issues since he was a child, he bought his first Commodore Amiga when he was 15 and started interesting in graphics. Together with a classmate he founded a graphic-duo and pixelled the artwork for early computer-animated movies. By 1994 Christian also started making music with the Amiga and produced Synthie-Pop and C-64 like tunes, only using Amiga internal samples and music capabilities. Three tracks from that period can be heard on "Night Of The Living Deaf", "Sexy World 1-4" and "Hawaii Blue" ("X-Mas In Freiburg"). In 1995 he had his first contact with synthesizers, when Elef Tsiroudis (now owner of Shokoy Records) called him and told him about a Yamaha SY85 he bought and about his plans to produce electronic music. So Christian also bought some equipment (after he worked at the local steel-melting factory) and travelled to Stuttgart. About six months later they released their first (and last) single on their own label "? Records" as Human Target. 1996 the team split, Christian bought a sampler and started producing on his own. He released his first single on Important Extracts entitled "... Minus 38 And Counting...", met Heiko Laux and gave him a demo tape containing the tracks of the "Miscellaneous EP", that was released a month later on Kanzleramt Records. In October 96 Christian started working at a computer-animation studio for several months, and created two experimental music clips for his tracks. 1997 he moves to Cologne and starts studying at the Academy of Media Arts. Further releases on Kanzleramt. Besides producing records for labels like Kurbel, Konsequent and Creation Rebel, Christian gave birth to his own outlet Forte Records in 1999. Where as Christian used to produce his first releases only using an Amiga, a sampler, a mixing desk and an effects unit, he turned into a true gearhead, mainly using vintage equipment and tube amplifiers from the 70's and early 80's. He developed his own, unique style and with the release of "Death Before Disko" in 1999, he proved once again, that he was one of the most versatile Techno musicians. Along with "Death Before Disko" Christian produced his first experimental short movie entitled "M.I.L.K." as part of his pre-exams. Followed by this is the super-moody album "The Lydia EP", released end of 1999, which is played by Djs throughout the world. After a little production break, Christian released his highly acclaimed Electro-Pop album "Hawaii Blue" in early 2001. This production is highly influenced by his undeniable affection for 80's Pop and Rock music and can be seen as a further step in his development from a Techno artist to a musician. As already suggested in previous works, with "Hawaii Blue", he nearly totally refused to accept the club-compatibility dogma, and mixes Pop tunes with listening tracks and retro-ballads. End of 2001 Christian cancels his studies to concentrate on Forte Records and making music. Christian was a person who felt the world very deeply and often struggled with how to cope with this keen understanding. On June 17th, 2003 he decided to take his own life. There have been many speculations and rumours about his death ranging from a skateboarding accident to a heart condition, but after time revealed details surrounding the incident it became increasingly evident that it was a decision that Christian himself made. Christian was working night and day on his new album at the time..

artist.name: Jerome
artist.profile:
At the age of 17 (after having been a bedroom/local dj for some years) he started to dj at the "Fresh to Impress" hiphop nights at Paradiso Amsterdam and played on many fashionshows around the Netherlands. With the appearance of house music his focus changed to this new energetic form of dance music. He then started his residency at Dwiezels in Haarlem where he dj-ed for more then three years. Mixing funky house grooves with melodic techno he quickly became one of the players in the dj scene.
Since then he's been playing at big events like Tortured (The End, London), Impulz (Netherlands), DanceValley (Netherlands), I Love Techno (Belgium) and Awakenings (Netherlands) and is resident dj on the Syntech technobeachpartys (Netherlands).
Nowadays Jerome is a respected producer, dj and remixer in his own style, his records often enter the charts. Alongside solo projects on Alola, Musicman, 100% Pure, Outland, PhysicalSoul, Placktownsounds, Fierce and MusicmanPocket, Jerome has recently remixed for the likes of Jamie Anderson, Laidback Luke, Steve Rachmad and Vince Watson. Known for his deep, groovy dynamic flow he`s gradually risen to become a regular at clubs and partys all over Europe.
Since August 2005 Jeroen Krom is the official owner of the Outland Records store in Amsterdam, The Netherlands. The store's label, [l=Outland Records] is rebooted too by Jeroen.
UPDATE, To our great regret, Jeroen Krom, known under the DJ-producer name Jerome, passed away on December 22, 2024.

artist.name: Cherry Bomb
artist.profile: See [b][a=Richard Brown][/b].

artist.name: Jochem Paap
artist.profile: Dutch DJ and producer, born 1969-02-14 in Rotterdam. Active since the early 1990's.

artist.name: John Thomas
artist.profile: French Electronic Music producer & Co-founder of [l1741668], born in 1975 in Paris. Founder of [l44581]. He is married to [a722803].

artist.name: T-Quest
artist.profile: T-Quest's earliest musical influences go back as far as Kraftwerk, Fad Gadget, Nitzer Ebb or Liaisons Dangereuses. The short-lived but fierce New Beat era and the acid house period further sharpened his appetite for electronic dance music. In the early 90's T-Quest started out as a DJ on a local radio station with 'Behind The Beat', a two hours non-stop program with the latest and upcoming sounds from the underground. At about the same time he started working in the Music Man record store and a couple of years later moved on to the A&R department of the Music Man label for which he created the instant-recognisable 'silver' outlook. Since 1994 Stefaan signed artists such as Secret Cinema, Cherry Bomb, Metro Dade, Sound Associates, Percy X, Steve Rachmad, Green Velvet, Steve Stoll an did compilations with Dave Clarke and Jeff Mills. He holds a residency at Kozzmozz, played in Belgian's most famous clubs (Food, Cherry Moon) as well as festivals (10 Days of Techno, I Love Techno). He travelled the world with his record bags and was invited to New York (Limelight), Paris (Rex), London (The End) and Moscow. For his first studio production, Stefaan Vandenberghe joined hands with Frank De Wulf. 'Play' was released on R&S records as Frank De Wulf vs. T-Quest. His second release, a solo effort, first surfaced somewhere at the end of 1996 as a limited twelve-inch (500 copies). Due to popular demand 'Upiter' was re-released on Music Man Records in March '97 including Steve Rachmad and Bandulu remixes. Later on Stefan produced an exclusive track ('Cakewalk') for the Kozzmozz compilation under the moniker of SubSurf. T-Quest is resident on the fuse level at Fuse since January 2001.

artist.name: Andreas Kauffelt
artist.profile: German (born and raised in Frankfurt, Germany) electronic musician, producer and mastering engineer. Founder of [l=Schnittstelle (2)] and [l=Aural Satisfaction].

artist.name: Co-Habits
artist.profile: Swedish project.

artist.name: Frederik
artist.profile: Dutch producer and Gyrate label boss.

artist.name: Keith Fielder
artist.profile: Used to work at Troublesome Records. Ex-promoter of Chemistry and Sex, Love & Motion at the Soundshaft.

artist.name: Jack De Marseille
artist.profile: Techno / House DJ & producer from Marseille, France.

artist.name: Kiko
artist.profile: Techno/electro house/melodic techno DJ & producer based in Grenoble, France. From [l=Ozone Records] to [l=Black Jack], to [l=GoodLife] via [l=Sekence], Kiko's original and eclectic productions make him stand out from the crowd. Released in 1998, Kiko's [r=71301] has gone on to achieve anthem status and can still be heard today in sets played by some of the world's most notorious DJs. At the same period, he was part of [a=Phunky Data] with friend [a=Olivier Raymond]. Two albums were released. Late 2000, Kiko distanced himself from turntables and parties in order to focus on producing a series of EP's ([r=9224], [r=39216], [r=33564] and [r=98959] with [a=Fafa Monteco]). After which he composed and produced his debut solo Album "Midnight Magic", followed in 2002 by the release of "Sinema", an electro-pop album which success was massive worldwide, including the hit "In My Eyes". In 2003, Kiko founded his own label: [l=Hot Banana].

artist.name: Beroshima
artist.profile: Techno/electro project from Germany. Formed in 1994 by [a=Frank Müller], later joined by [a=Ulrich Schnauss]. Owned [l=Acid Orange]. From 2004, Beroshima was a Frank Müller solo project. In 2011, the two began working together again, but now Beroshima is Müller solo once more.

artist.name: Savas Pascalidis
artist.profile: German DJ and producer. He is the founder of [l=Lasergun] and [l=Sweatshop].

artist.name: Fuselage
artist.profile:
Fuselage formed in 1996 & hail from Peterborough, UK.
The “Put Your Foot Down” E.P was released in February 1998. Later that year, The E.P was followed by an exclusive track for the “Big Kahuna Kicks” compilation. “Monkey Guts” was recorded at DrumAttic studios, produced & engineered by the Drum Attic Twins.
During the “Monkey Guts” sessions Fuselage recorded new tracks which would go on to be their second Kahuna release “Ponk Ruck” featuring “Ponk Ruck”, “Don’t Stop (You’re Killing Me)” & the Rasmus remix of the title track.
Fuselage also weighed in with two tracks on the second Big Kahuna Kicks compilation, “1974” & “Almost Monday Morning”. Fuselage’s debut album “All In The Mind” was conceived at DrumAttic studios during 1999 & to this day remains unreleased due to the sudden & unexpected death of Kahuna Cuts.

artist.name: Cirrus
artist.profile: Cirrus is an American big beat group formed in 1995 in Los Angeles, California.

artist.name: Soul Hooligan
artist.profile:
In 1997, the "band" was simply Austin Reynolds. After the first single "Sweat Pea" was successful, there were pressures to play live. To do this, he enlisted the help of friends from his hometown of Brentwood, Essex: Dave Jay (who had worked with him in the early 1990s) and James Sumner.
Maverick heard an early demo of Music Like Dirt and signed the band for distribution in USA (Soul Hooligan was originally distributed through Freskanova Records in the UK). Various tracks from Music Like Dirt have been used in TV and movies, with a few new songs appearing, but no word on the "forthcoming new album" yet.

artist.name: Dream Traveler
artist.profile: Trance producer from Arizona. Dream Traveler shot to fame when Paul Oakenfold found heavy favor with his track "Time", and opened his popular [i]Tranceport[/i] mix-CD with it.

artist.name: Three Drives On A Vinyl
artist.profile: [b]DO NOT USE.[/b] Please use [a4488] + anv

artist.name: Agnelli & Nelson
artist.profile:
Trance duo from Northern Ireland. Most notable for hit singles "El Niño," "Everyday," & "Holding On To Nothing."
[b]Don't use this group for full name credits. Split them into their separate names instead.[/b]

artist.name: Binary Finary
artist.profile: UK act who shot to fame after their track "[url=https://www.discogs.com/master/11221-Binary-Finary-1998]1998[/url]" charted on the UK Singles Chart at number 24. When it was re-issued in 1999 (as "[url=https://www.discogs.com/release/112250-Binary-Finary-1999]1999[/url]"), it charted at number 11. Currently based in Sydney, Australia.

artist.name: Gifted
artist.profile: Producer from The Netherlands.

artist.name: Boy
artist.profile: House music artist of the 1990s.

artist.name: Poker Face
artist.profile: Not to be confused with [a=Pokerface].

artist.name: The Horrorist
artist.profile: American artist and producer based in New York City. He is the founder of [l=Things To Come Records]. Prior to releasing music as The Horrorist, he had his "fifteen minutes of fame" when he won a contest and appeared on the Depeche Mode documentary [url=http://www.discogs.com/master/21221]101[/url].

artist.name: The Noodle Project
artist.profile: Dutch DJ and producer.

artist.name: Orlando Voorn
artist.profile:
As one of the first Dutch musicians to establish a vital connection between Detroit and Amsterdam, Orlando Voorn has long been recognized as one of the Netherland’s most original and inventive producers in the world of electronic dance music.
Voorn (born 25 April 1968, in Amsterdam) began djing at the tender age of 12 and went on to win the Dutch DMC DJ Championships in 1986 with his own characteristic blend of hiphop and electro. Towards the end of the eighties he acquired his first sequencer, packed away his turntables and turned his hand to producing.
Following a series of dancefloor tracks recorded for the now defunct Lower East Side Records under the monniker of Frequency in the early nineties (Where Is Your Evidence, Kiss The Sky…), he was introduced to Juan Atkins (Industrial Metal, Game One) and went on to work with both Derrick May and Blake Baxter (as one half of the Ghetto Brothers). Voorn was also recording as Nighttripper (Tone Exploitation), Format (Solid Session - one of the most sought after classic techno records until its re-release in 1998), Basic Bastard, The Living Room, Baruka, Fix (Flash on Kevin Saunderson’s legendary KMS label), Dope Dog, Boy (Paco Di Bango’s World) and Stalker (The Stalker/The Riderman)… Many of these tracks can be heard on the double cd Best of Nightvision.
Having made his mark producing his own vision of techno, ambient and hiphop and adamantly sidestepping genre limitations thus far, Voorn’s announcement that his "Redeye" album would see a foray into drum’n’bass territory aroused both anticipation and curiosity. While Voorn undoubtedly has an ear for melody, this has always been combined with an innate understanding of the importance of atmosphere and tension in music. Like Squarepusher, Amon Tobin, and Photek, he uses drum’n’bass rhythms to create something beyond pure dance music.

artist.name: M1
artist.profile: Matteo DiMaria is a Canadian producer and Jinxx label manager. He collaborated with labels such as Brique Rouge (as Neon Lights), Stickman or Aquarius and signed the big mainstream hit Electronic Funk under his M1 moniker.

artist.name: Birdman
artist.profile:
Electronic TechHouse artist.
Not to be confused with the owner and rap artist on Cash Money Records [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/Birdman+%282%29]Birdman (2)[/url].

artist.name: Dima
artist.profile: French artist and producer.

artist.name: E.K.O.
artist.profile: Their name is written as E.K.O. (Electrical Knock Out).

artist.name: Kellee
artist.profile: Juan Lopez (aka [a=Johnny Fiasco]) produced and co-wrote the original "My Love" with Kellee for Moonshine Music in 1995. The song was remixed (at least) 17 times. Kellee Warren lives in Chicago but no longer records house music.

artist.name: Steve Levy
artist.profile: Apr 2020- Chief Marketing Officer, Virgin Fests, Los Angeles Feb 2016- Founder/CEO, Clanger Digital, Greater Los Angeles Area Nov 2017-Apr 2020 Head of Marketing and Digital, Insomniac Events, Greater Los Angeles Area Jan 2000-Mar 2016 Founder/CEO, BPM Media, Greater Los Angeles Area 2004-2006 Director/Partner, R-Nation UK, London Jan 1995-Nov 2005 Founder/President, Moonshine Music

artist.name: DJ Urban
artist.profile:
Techno & Ghetto House DJ & Producer from Chicago.
Half of duo [a=Pair Of Jacks] with [a=Angel Alanis].
Runs Ghetto House label [l=Public Housing] & Techno label [l=Urban Audio].

artist.name: Dean Cole
artist.profile: Dean Cole gave his name to be used for the [a=Richard Harvey] and [a=User (4)] projects, but actually did not produce these tracks. The original producers remain in the background.

artist.name: Ominus
artist.profile: Goa trance trio project from UK, active from 1996 to 1998

artist.name: Da Mongoloids
artist.profile:
Da Monogoloids is a common source of confusion amongst fans of the mid to late nighties house scenes of New York, Paris and Chicago.
The name represents two separate creative outlets. The first of these was “Spark Da Meth” a House single produced solely by Armand Van Helden in 1996 and the second was a loose DJ collective featuring Van Helden, Daft Punk, DJ Sneak, Junior Sanchez, Roger Sanchez, Todd Terry, Ian Pooley, Laidback Luke, Basement Jaxx and The Rhythm Masters.
As a DJ collective Da Mongoloids would often perform a mixed bill of DJ sets at clubs around the world with individual sets by the artists mentioned above.
Although Da Mongoloids never officially disbanded as each artist's musical output drifted away from the original House sound on which Da Mongoloids was founded the collective naturally ceased to exist.

artist.name: Krimp
artist.profile: Prolific House Music producer hailing from Boston, MA. He died January 28th, 2013.

artist.name: Wassermann
artist.profile: Born in Cologne, Germany. Brother of [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/Reinhard+Voigt]Reinhard Voigt[/url]

artist.name: David Carretta
artist.profile: French DJ and producer, born in Marseille and one of the founders of [l=Space Factory]. His style is a mixture between electro, techno, new beat, EBM and italo disco.

artist.name: Atomic Babies
artist.profile: Atomic Babies started out when Joey Jupiter started cranking out hard acid tracks in the early 90s in his parent's basement. Since those days Atomic Babies has toured the world headlining the live PA sounds he developed in that same basement at everything from dirty warehouse parties with secrete map points to mega one off events with 5,000 plus, to the pretentious New York, LA and Chicago night clubs. In 2006 Joey Jupiter & The Germ joined forced to bring this legendary Live Act back on it's feet with their passion for hardware and devotion to the scene, this duo will stop at nothing to bring the vacant dance floors back from the dead.

artist.name: Vector Two
artist.profile: Italian project.

artist.name: Paris The Black Fu
artist.profile: Stage name of [a725297], mainly used for his performance as vocalist and feature artist.

artist.name: DJ Jes
artist.profile:
Chicago house and techno DJ/producer. Born in Mexico and grew up on the West Side of Chicago. Runs [l=Fresca Recordings] and records for numerous other labels.
Also organises events through the Conjugate Project including: Motioness: the Chicago El Train Event, Resonance Part One: An Afternoon of Audial Stimulation in Grant Park, and the original Conjugate Project. Booking enquiries contact: infofordjjes@gmail.com

artist.name: Tony Rohr
artist.profile: DJ producer from Brooklyn, New York, USA.

artist.name: Dropsonic
artist.profile: Rock band from Atlanta, Georgia, USA.

artist.name: Underwater Research And Design
artist.profile: Associated with [a=Underwater].

artist.name: Mick Harris
artist.profile: English musician, born in October 1967 in Birmingham. Best known as ex-[a=Napalm Death] drummer, now producing dark atmospherics. Early bands missing include Martian Brain Squeeze (two demos), Motherfuckers from Mars (with [a=Pete Nash]), and Drop Dead (with [a=Shane Embury], [a=Jim Whitely], [a=Andy Whale]). Founder of [l=Possible] and [l=416 Records].

artist.name: OCOSI
artist.profile:
OCOSI (official capitalization; since 2015 - O C O S I) is an illbient project from the UK. Formed in 1998 by Simon Smerdon and Paul Molyneux. Simon Smerdon left in 2001, and since that time OCOSI was a solo project of Paul Molyneux. In 2020 again became a duo of Simon Smerdon and Paul Molyneux.
Since 2015 official name is O C O S I (all caps with spaces).

artist.name: The Dustmite
artist.profile:
Emitting his melodic beatscapes, hidden amongst distant traces of amber light, the Dustmite lurks. These uncanny instrumentals provide the energy that drives his Emptylight Soundsystem into uncharted horizons.
DJ at WSMU 91.1 FM

artist.name: Silk Saw
artist.profile: From Belgium.

artist.name: NOS
artist.profile: NOS is a pseudonym used by [a606169] a drum and bass artist, who along with his brother [a129394] formed the production/mixing team [a290].

artist.name: Montagnn
artist.profile: Project from Canada. The two members are aliases for some of Szkieve's compilation work.

artist.name: Turn
artist.profile:
Turn were formed in 1998 by long-time friends Ollie Cole (vocals, guitars) and Ian Melady (drums). Since then they've released two albums, 2000's Antisocial (Infectious) and 2003's Forward, on their own Nurture label, which crashed straight into the Irish Top Twenty. They've built up a reputation as one of the finest live bands to grace a stage, with ever-growing audiences enraptured by their captivating stage presence and quality tunesmithery.
Turn finished 2004 on a high, scoring highly in the prestigious Hot Press Readers Poll, where music fans voted them No. 5 Best Band and No. 6 Best Live band. Forward was named No. 7 Best Album, while frontman Ollie Cole was voted No. 4 Best Irish Musician, No. 6 Best Irish Male and No. 8 Best Irish Songwriter.
They signed to celebrated London-based independent label Setanta (The Divine Comedy, A House) in early 2005 and released their third and final album, 'TURN' before calling it a day in July 2006.

artist.name: Not Breathing
artist.profile: Not Breathing is the creative vehicle of one Dave Wright, maker of experimental electronic music and instruments. Beginning in the late 80s with an impressive and prolific series of self-distributed cassettes, Not Breathing’s career now encompasses six full-length CD releases, several vinyl releases, over forty compilation appearances, one 8-track release, six US tours and totalling over 300 live performances. Worked for six years with Chicago’s industrial label Invisible Records, spawning three CD albums as well as a collaboration with [a=Dead Voices On Air]. As an electronics engineer, Wright is able to create instruments that deliver an absolutely unique spectrum of sound. Wright also has an extensive body of work as a collaborator, remixer and session musician.

artist.name: Louise Rhodes
artist.profile: Singer and songwriter from Manchester, England, now living in Wiltshire, England. Daughter of [a=Annie Burton], founder of [l=Infinite Bloom].

artist.name: Wendy Page
artist.profile: Singer-songwriter and producer

artist.name: David Simpson
artist.profile:
Brother of Gerald Simpson, aka [a=A Guy Called Gerald].
For the classical cellist, see [a=David Simpson (2)].

artist.name: Public Domain
artist.profile:
[b]WARNING: Please do not use this artist to credit the publishing information "Public Domain" or any of its variations. This information must go to the notes. Do not confuse this artist with the Dutch Hardcore act [a=Public Domain (2)].[/b]
From Ayr, Scotland, UK. "Operation Blade" entered the UK national charts at Number Five and sold upwards of 250,000 copies with Platinum for sales in Australia.
Public Domain changed their line up in 2002 when Mallorca Lee and David Forbes continued with their solo projects. Public Domain originally were James Allan, Mark Sherry and Alistair MacIsaac, that penned the hit "Operation Blade". After signing the track to Slinky it was decided they needed a frontman - enter Mallorca Lee with David Forbes; and both Mallorca and David contributed to the album "Hardhop Superstars". After touring the album worldwide in 2001/2003, Mallorca and David continued with their solo projects, and in 2004 Alistair MacIsaac also left the band to concentrate on other projects. Enter Neil Skinner (MC Cyclone), a lifelong friend of all the guys and well-known for his skills with Dyewitness.
In 2009 Mark Sherry moved on to concentrate on his Solo projects.
The present lineup consists of James Allan and Neil Skinner.

artist.name: Lady Kier
artist.profile:
Born August 15, 1963 in Youngstown, Ohio, USA and moved around much as a kid from Pittsburgh, PA, Va. Beach, Va, Washington DC, before moving to NYC at the age of 17.
After leaving [a=Deee-Lite] in 1994, she moved to London for 10 years and embarked on a successful artist/DJ career. She battled for 8 years to get out of her contract with Elektra records which had prohibited her from releasing music under her name with other labels or independently. As a DJ she was a resident at horsemeat disco in London before moving back to NYC in 2004.

artist.name: MRI
artist.profile: German duo based in Berlin. They are the founders of [l=Resopal Schallware].

artist.name: Dano
artist.profile: Resident of California, US. Associated with the Red Melon label. This is NOT the Dutch Gabber [a=DJ Dano] !

artist.name: Terry Farley
artist.profile: London-based DJ and producer. Founded [l=Boy's Own Productions] and its off-shoot [l=Junior Boy's Own]. Now part of the [l=Faith (12)] collective with [a=Stuart Patterson] and [a=Dave Jarvis]. Worked extensively with production partner [a=Pete Heller] as [a=Fire Island], [a=Roach Motel], [a=Heller & Farley] and other aliases.

artist.name: Pal Joey
artist.profile: Disco, House & Hip Hop DJ from New York, NY, USA. Owner of the [url=http://www.discogs.com/label/Loop+D%27+Loop]Loop D'Loop[/url], [url=http://www.discogs.com/label/Cabaret]Cabaret[/url] and [url=http://www.discogs.com/label/Footstompin%27+Records]Footstompin' Records[/url] labels. Also see: [l187846] and [l187846].

artist.name: Steve Hillage
artist.profile:
UK-based guitarist born on August 2, 1951, in London, England. Formerly a member of prog-rock act Gong but best known solo, as half of ambient techno duo System 7 and as a frequent collaborator with The Orb. Hillage started with the band Uriel then left for college and they continued on as Egg. They reunited to record under the pseudonym Arzachel, then Hillage ended up with Kevin Ayers, from which he went on to Gong where he picked up on Daevid Allen's glissando guitar techniques, which Allen had evolved from watching Syd Barrett play with a butter knife bowing strings. He left Gong at the time of [m=25501] and started a solo career.
Hillage evolved from prodigy to virtuoso and the guitar sound evolved with echoes and reverb blending with synthesizers. he was a pioneer in new age ambient music and retired from recording for a while as he went on to be a prominent producer in the early 1980's, most known for his work on the Simple Minds albums of that era. Some years later he was in a club and heard The Orb sampling his music and hooked up with them. As a result he resurfaced with his longtime partner [a39787] as System 7 (aka 777 due to concerns from Apple over their operating system at the time). Hillage reunited with Gong circa 2008 and toured with them in 2009 and 2010. That version of Gong also released [m=183833] in 2009.

artist.name: Subsurfing
artist.profile: Collective fronted by English producer Greg Hunter and Japanese instrumentalist / producer OBI. Their music has been described as ambient / atmospherics, with enchanting Indian textures and deranged hi-tech dub, through the combined use of electronics and live instrumentation.Obi and Greg first met in Tokyo, where The Orb were recording Assassin, and the pair immediately found a lot of common ground between them. Already during the first Subsurfing sessions at the Butterfly studio in London they attracted the attention of musicians like Kenji Jammer (Bomb The Bass, also collaborating with a.o. Tim Simenon and Keith LeBlanc in the live project Night Of Interference), Kris Weston, Charlie Hall (The Drum Club), Steve Hillage (System 7) and Youth. Some of them contributed to the Frozen Ants album and together with the band and other guest musicians Baluji (sitar, auto harp) and Jean (Chinese cello, kokyu) made it a electro-ethnic landmark (1996).

artist.name: State Of Bengal
artist.profile:
Britain's Asian underground music scene project headed by [a518468].
In 1987, Zaman set up the project in London after a visit to Noakhali, Bangladesh, where he interacted with traditional folk musicians and dancers. Original members of the group included his youngest brother [a721123] (later became vocalist of [a3129]) who was 7, and MC [a183937] (later became the lead rapper for [a3234]) who was 15.
Then the project (even before the releasing of the first album "Visual Audio") became into some kind of alias to Sam Zaman. He DJ'd, recorded and toured under this name inviting various collaborators and also by himself.

artist.name: Lelonek
artist.profile: For two decades Friedel Lelonek has been involved in perpetual records, remixes and sound sculpturing - His adventurous synthesis has led him to new heights in Drum & Bass. The Lelonek sound was conceived and created during his first encounter with Talvin Singh's seminal 'Anokha' club night, at the legendary Blue Note in 1996.

artist.name: A.R. Rahman
artist.realname: Allah Rakha Rahman (née A. S. Dileep Kumar)
artist.profile: Indian music composer, record producer, singer, songwriter, multi-instrumentalist and philanthropist, born: 6 January 1966 in Madras. In 1989, he converted from Hinduism to Islam and changed his name to Allah Rakha Rahman.

artist.name: Amar
artist.profile: Amar (born 1982) is a British Indian singer.

artist.name: Osmani Soundz
artist.profile: DJ and producer based in UK.

artist.name: Fluke
artist.profile:
Formed in the late 1980s by three friends living together in London, Fluke started out making dreamy pop before the explosion of acid house influenced them. It was the growing electronica sound that influenced their early singles ("Thumper" "Joni" & "Slid") and their techno-rock debut album [i]"The Techno Rose Of Blighty."[/i] They signed with Virgin for their next albums [i]"Six Wheels On My Wagon"[/i] (1993) and [i]"OTO"[/i] (1995), which proved Fluke to be a driving force in the progressive house and trip-hop scenes. Their fourth album [i]"Risotto"[/i] was a runaway success in 1997 and 1998, powered by the cartoony big-beat of "Atom Bomb" and the gentle breakbeat track "Reeferendrum." After enjoying worldwide touring for several years, Fluke took time off to expand their already-massive remix-ography, releasing the retrospective [i]"Progressive History X"[/i] in 2001. Their final and most mature album, [i]"Puppy"[/i] followed in mid-2003, this time incorporating Jon Fugler and Mike Bryant only, while Mike Tournier concentrated on his own band [a=Syntax (4)] together with vocalist Jan Burton.
After Puppy came out, Fugler and Bryant reinvented themselves as [a=2 Bit Pie].

artist.name: Dax Riders
artist.profile: Dax Riders are a French Electro funk band founded in 1994 by Cédric "Bad Ced" Azencoth and Olivier "Daxman" Ruel. They're also running the [l=Subscience] label. In 1999 they did a live tour with Nicolas Berger-Vachon (Alias Erman) and then expanded to a trio formation with him.

artist.name: Linet
artist.profile: Israeli-Turkish singer (5 March 1975 in Tel Aviv).

artist.name: Tony Senghore
artist.profile: Swedish House producer who runs/ran the labels [L=Anonym], [L=Gungeligung], [L=Horehaus] & [L=Wack!].

artist.name: Bad Ced
artist.profile: French DJ / Producer. Founder of the trio [a=Dax Riders] and owner of the label [l=Subscience]. The name 'Bad Ced' was originally given to Azencoth by [a=Kay One (2)] from the [a=Suprême NTM] crew.

artist.name: Nicole Graham
artist.profile: French vocalist.

artist.name: Lady Bird
artist.profile: Ladybird (aka [a=Julie Monnin]) is a French/Australian songwriter, singer and DJ based in Paris and the UK.

artist.name: DJ Shadow
artist.profile:
Joshua Davis (born June 29, 1972 in San José, California), better known by his stage name DJ Shadow, is an American record producer and DJ. He first gained notice with the release of his acclaimed debut studio album, "Endtroducing…". He has a personal record collection of over 60,000 records.
DJ Shadow is widely credited as a key figure in developing the experimental instrumental hip-hop style associated with the London-based [l111] label. Inspired by hip-hop's early years, he then grew to absorb the heyday of crews like Eric B. & Rakim, Ultramagnetic MCs, and Public Enemy; groups which prominently featured DJs in their ranks.
Josh "Shadow" Davis had already been experimenting with making beats and breaks on a four-track recorder while he was in high school in the Nor-Cal college town of Davis, but it was during university that he co-founded his own Solesides label as an outlet for his original tracks. Hooking up with Davis' few b-boys (including eventual Solesides artists Blackalicious and Lyrics Born) through the college radio station, Shadow began releasing the Hip-Hop Reconstruction mix tapes in 1991, eventually catching the attention of The Source magazine and Dave Funkenklein. Shadow was featured in the magazine's "Unsigned Hype" column in 1991, and Klein signed him to a production deal with Hollywood BASIC records. Concurrently, Shadow provided beats and scratches for Bay Area rapper Paris and was featured on his second album. In 1993, Shadow pressed his 17-minute beat-head symphony "Entropy". His tracks spread widely through the DJ-strong hip-hop underground, eventually reaching James Lavelle of Mo Wax.
Shadow's first full-length, "Endtroducing…" was released on the label in late 1996 to immense critical acclaim in United Kingdom and the United States. "Preemptive Strike", a compilation of early singles, followed in early 1998. Later that year, Shadow produced tracks for the debut album by U.N.K.L.E., a longtime Mo' Wax production team that gained superstar guests including Thom Yorke (of Radiohead), Richard Ashcroft (of The Verve), Mike D (of Beastie Boys), and others.
His next project came in 1999, with the transformation of Solesides into a new label, Quannum Projects. Nearly six years after his debut production album, the proper follow-up, "The Private Press", was released in June 2002. The following year Shadow released a mix album, "Diminishing Returns", and in 2004 he released a live album and DVD, "Live! In Tune and on Time". In 2006 he released another long awaited full-length album "The Outsider", which featured rising Bay Area Hyphy rappers including Keak Da Sneak and E-40. "The Outsider" also featured a single with Q-Tip (of A Tribe Called Quest), which led to Shadow's first appearance on The Late Show with David Letterman.
Between 2007 and 2009 Shadow released three volumes of "The 4-Track Era Collection", a series of his earliest recordings. The 4-Track Era project was exclusively available through his web store, ShopDjShadow.com. In the midst of all of these solo projects, DJ Shadow collaborated with fellow hip-hop DJ Cut Chemist. Together they created a series of mixes that fused soul, funk, and rock, in the framework of a cohesive concept involving using only 45 rpm records (7 inches). These mixes include Brainfreeze, Product Placement, and The Hard Sell, which would be debuted at the Hollywood Bowl.
Shadow's website relaunched in August 2009, enabling him to sell digital downloads direct to his fans through his own autonomous storefront. 2009 also saw the announcement of Shadow's involvement with DJ Hero, an Activision video game which features Shadow as a playable character within the game. Shadow also contributed several mixes to the game.
2010 and 2011 saw a tour throughout Europe and North America entitled "Live From The Shadowsphere". Hailed for its visual innovations, the tour was cited by Beatport as one of the top 10 DJ shows of all time, and was capped off by two memorable performances at the Coachella Festival.
In 2011, DJ Shadow released "The Less You Know, The Better" purportedly his last full-length album to prominently feature samples. 2012 included "Total Breakdown, Hidden Transmissions From The MPC Era (1992–1996)", an archival project; and "Reconstructed: The Best of DJ Shadow", a greatest hits album.
2012 saw a shift in Shadow's live person...at the request of trendsetting Los Angeles club night Low End Theory, he returned to playing traditional, contemporary DJ sets, often eschewing his own music for that of peers.
The hard-hitting sets Shadow played coexisted with the rise of trap, juke, and the fledgling beat scene, catching the ear of large-scale EDM icons such as Diplo and Bassnectar, with whom Shadow performed. An incident at a Miami superclub, in which Shadow refused to temper his set to suit management, was captured on video and went viral, prompting a debate about the autonomy of DJs and the music they play. This only seemed to heighten demand for Shadow as 2013 reportedly saw him play over 100 shows.
Also in 2013, Shadow lent a hand to video game franchise Grand Theft Auto V, compiling and arranging elements of the soundtrack. Notable was his return to production, crafting a remix for Machinedrum, (whom Shadow performed with at Fabric in London and Amsterdam) and 1970s Dutch progressive band Supersister, who Shadow had previously sampled. In 2014, Shadow toured Australia and performed at SXSW for Vice.
According to his Twitter account, Shadow plans to spend time in the studio and continue to perform his contemporary DJ sets. He has indicated that a new, "very different"; type of tour is planned for the Fall of 2014.
Launched his own record label [l=Liquid Amber] in 2014.
Publishing companies include: [l400205], [l400279], [l431815], [l440747].
