Motorcycle John
DJ Shadow And The Groove Robbers
Shimovis

artist.name: Rob D
artist.profile: [b]For Australian dance composer and producer Rob D, mostly known for his work with Rollo, please use [a22969] with the correct ANVs.[/b]

artist.name: Massive Attack
artist.profile:
Collaborative British music production group from Bristol, England.
The band currently consists of Robert Del Naja, Tricky, and Daddy G. The original lineup also included Andrew Vowles. The group name is taken from a slogan sprayed by New York graffiti artist '[url=https://www.discogs.com/artist/1894642-Brim-2]Brim[/url]', who sprayed 'Massive Attack' underneath a 'piece' in Bristol, UK in 1985. '[url=https://www.discogs.com/artist/1894642-Brim-2]Brim[/url]', along with 'Balogun' and '[url=https://www.discogs.com/artist/5007984-Bio-14]Bio[/url]' were part of the TATS CRU from NYC who sprayed a 'piece' each together at the Malcolm X Centre, St. Paul's in Bristol. [a151718], who was an upcoming graffiti artist at the time, with the tag 3-D, has stated that he/they had adopted the slogan initially as a name for a record label, and then later took it on as a band name. During the 1991 Gulf War the group name was shown on some releases as 'Massive' in order to maintain airplay after pressure from the British Government on radio programmers.

artist.name: Brother Brown
artist.profile: Danish house duo.

artist.name: Steve Lawler
artist.profile:
Steve Lawler is a British DJ and artist known for styles of house, techno and electronica.
Born in 1973 in Birmingham, England, Steve began organizing infamous motor way raves in the early 90ʻs underneath the M42 motor way, in the UK.
As an artist his releases & remixes have been signed by many recording labels and his own label VIVa MUSiC which he founded in 2006 and is also the label manager, he has also worked with many of the worlds dance imprints as well as recording a track for the blockbuster horror movie by Ian Softly "The Skeleton Key," and released mix series, including "Dark Drums," "Lights Out" and most recently, "VIVa."
As a DJ he has held residencies at the super club Cream in the mid 90ʼs, nine years at the Balearic club Space in Ibiza where he again returned to in 2008, VIVa Nights from doors open to door close at The End in London for a span of 7 years and also a residency at Londonʼs Home Club.

artist.name: Three Drives
artist.profile: Dutch trance group founded by Erik de Koning and Ton van Empel (DJ Ton T.B.) in 1997. Best known for their track "Greece 2000". In June 2014, Van Empel decided to leave the group.

artist.name: Ciudad Feliz
artist.profile:
Argentinian electronic group.
Ciudad Feliz (en: Happy City) consists of a group of producers born at the South American beaches of Mar del Plata, Argentina.
A young Julian Sanza initially funded the project in 1996. Later, musicians Ariel Lanfrit, Lucciano Siclari, Juan Martinez, and Fernando Pulichino joined and team together.
After two years of intense work as DJs and offering a stunning live act, they started to be one of the referrals of the electronic music scene in Argentina.
Being discovered at the 2000 Winter Music Conference in Miami, they released a series of recordings through different independent labels in France, Canada, and USA, giving them a wider audience.
As a result, their firsts singles were featured in different compilations and they gained more international recognition.
They have worked with Lucas Konk West for KW Music Group, and produced for many labels including 2020 Vision, 3AM, Soundplant, Silver Network, Shaboom, Aquarius, Stickman, Solid, et al.

artist.name: Dixon
artist.profile:
German DJ and producer based in Berlin. He is one of the founders of [l=Innervisions].
Before becoming a DJ was a professional footballer for FC Magnet Mitte and once played for the East Germany national team.

artist.name: Freestyle Man
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Finland, now based in Berlin.

artist.name: Richard Bone
artist.profile: Richard Bone (1952) is considered one of the founding members of the New York electronic music scene. He began composing songs and improvisational pieces using the very first commercially available synthesizers as early as 1979. The exploration of electronics in underground pop and the new wave sounds of the early 1980s continued until the middle of that decade. At that point Richard's music began to reflect his admiration for the pioneering works of Harold Budd and Brian Eno. By the 1990s Bone had stopped composing and recording vocal works entirely and the intervening years have seen Richard Bone experiment with ambient permutations of electronica, lounge, jazz and even bossa nova.

artist.name: B.L.I.M.
artist.profile: DJ from London, United Kingdom.

artist.name: Adam Freeland
artist.profile:
English DJ and producer associated with breakbeat based electronic music. He was born 7 August 1973 in Welwyn Garden City, UK.
He used to promote the Friction club night with [a=Rennie Pilgrem] and had a show in Kiss FM.

artist.name: Ils
artist.profile:
Drum and bass producer from the UK who has more recently been producing breaks for Distinct'ive.
Built his studio with the aid of a Princes Trust grant, and began engineering and producing DnB. Unhappy with the escalating BPMs, he opted to switch to the breaks scene and joined [l=Fuel Records (UK)], tempted by the album deal they offered. After some disappointment with how the album was released, he switched to the [l=Marine Parade] label.

artist.name: The Infinity Project
artist.profile:
[a=Ron Rothfield], a trained jazz flutist and member of the 1960’s band Quintessence, formed The Infinity Project in 1989 along with Graham Wood. In December of that year they organised their first party and played their first primitive tracks. Over the next few years The Infinity Project started releasing their first material on labels such as Fabulous and Dragonfly Records. Then in 1994, Raja Ram and Graham Wood, along with Ian St. Paul, formed the legendary Goa Trance label TIP Records.
[a=Simon Posford] ([a=Hallucinogen]), although not a full member of The Infinity Project has co-produced/engineered many of their tracks, and has a long-standing friendship and musical partnership with Raja Ram. Other notable collaborators include [a=Martin Freeland] ([a=Man With No Name]) and [a=Nick Barber] ([a=Doof]). By 1998 The Infinity Project had split-up, with Raja Ram and Graham Wood concentrating on their other projects.

artist.name: Etnica
artist.profile: Italian band producing goa and psy-trance, started in 1993-1994.

artist.name: Psychaos
artist.profile: Psy-trance project from London, United Kingdom.

artist.name: Sasse
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Finland, now based in Berlin. He is the founder of the label [l=Moodmusic] and the studios [l=West20 Turku] in Finland and [l=Blackhead Studios] in Berlin.

artist.name: Morris Brown
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Finland, now based in Berlin.

artist.name: Spylab
artist.profile: Collab between [a7377] & [a1235615]. They released their debut album 'This Utopia' in 2001

artist.name: House Of Venus
artist.profile: Collaboration of [a=Eddy De Clercq] and [a=Quazar] ([a=Gert Van Veen] and [a=Erik van Putten] AKA [a=Eric Cycle]).

artist.name: Paradise 3001
artist.profile: Dutch techno producer, since 1991. Specialises in techno, ambient techno, dub and spacey tracks. Alias of Aad de Mooy (his real name is written as Aad de Mooij), a key figure in the Dutch dance scene since 1989. Also known as D-Shake, Hallucination Generation, Timewarp and Alex Cortiz.

artist.name: D-Shake
artist.profile: Dutch techno producer, since 1991. Specialises in techno, ambient techno, dub and spacey tracks. Alias of Aad de Mooy (his real name is written as Aad de Mooij), a key figure in the Dutch dance scene since 1989. Also known as D-Shake, Hallucination Generation, Timewarp and Alex Cortiz.

artist.name: Elevation
artist.profile: Early 90's UK based Hardcore producer

artist.name: Thee Maddkatt Courtship
artist.profile: DJ and producer from US.

artist.name: Spanky
artist.profile: Spanky (died September 21, 2016) was one of the professional names used by [a630106], a DJ, vocalist, drum programmer, producer and a pioneer of the acid house genre. In 1985 he co-founded the group [a1705]. In addition to his work with Phuture, he also released solo works.

artist.name: Roland Casper
artist.profile:
Born: 02 January 1966
In 1988 Roland Casper begun his career as a DJ together with his longtime partner Claus Bachor at the innovative and now legendary Rave Club in Cologne. 2 years later, in 1990, the Space Club openened its doors in Cologne, with Casper as resident DJ. In 1992 Casper moved to the Warehouse in Cologne, where he played as a resident DJ alongside the most prominent Djs of the scene and also started to develop an international reputation. During that year, he also took off initial work on his own projects, releasing his first 12" single "Feel The Speed" under the alias Exeter and building his own studio where he recorded "The Warehouse" EP, first to be released under his real name.
In addition to all of this, he co-hosted the [l=Psycho Thrill] venue night together with its founder [a=Claus Bachor] at the Ballhaus in Bonn. In 1994, The Warehouse got closed, but Psycho Thrill developed a huge cult following and an unparalleled reputation either. In 1996, Casper started his own label, [l=Essence (2)], in cooperation with Roadrunner. Besides his own releases, the label has been primarily set up as a forum for newcomers from the german Techno and Acid underground. In the course of the first year, thirteen 12" singles and the highly recommended Psycho Thrill compilation were released to great critical acclaim.
Today he is still continuing his DJ work worldwide.

artist.name: Spinback Society
artist.profile: Erik van den Broek's release on the Brooklyn based Thrust imprint of BML N.Y.

artist.name: Whirlpool Productions
artist.profile: Experimental house music project from Cologne (Köln), founded in 1991.

artist.name: Natural Born Grooves
artist.profile: Duo producer from Belgium. Aka N.B.G.

artist.name: Arling & Cameron
artist.profile: Dutch electronic music duo of Gerry Arling and Richard Cameron. They evolved from lounge / kitsch to electro / dance pop. The duo also recorded an album with [a816833] as the trio [a42161].

artist.name: Tekonivel
artist.profile:
Tekonivel was a pseudonym used by [a4725] (born 1963, Helsinki, Finland - died 13 April, 2017, France, aged 53) a Finnish electronic musician, composer and producer.
He began his musical career playing electronics and drums as part of the early Finnish industrial and noise scene. In 1993, together with [a11329] and [a549915], he founded the minimal electronic group [a3822]. Sami Salo left Pan Sonic in 1996 and the project continued as a duo until disbanding in 2009. Vainio then established a successful solo career, often collaborating with experimental French composer [a669623].

artist.name: Circuit Breaker
artist.profile:
DJ and producer.
For German Techno DJ/producer see [a=Circuit Breaker (2)].

artist.name: Dan Morgan
artist.profile: Dan Morgan is an early 1990's American techno artist from the New York City area.

artist.name: X-102
artist.profile: [a=Underground Resistance]-related techno project of Mike Banks, Jeff Mills and Robert Hood. Hood did not take part when the project was revived in the 2000s.

artist.name: Martin Rev
artist.profile: American musician and the instrumentalist, born 18 December 1947 in Brooklyn, New York, USA. His recording studio in New York is named [l280949].

artist.name: Roberto Rodriguez
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Helsinki, Finland. He is the founder of [l=Serenades (2)].

artist.name: Nicole Willis
artist.profile:
US singer, visual artist, and artist.
Born: 1963 in Brooklyn, New York City, New York, USA.
Willis began singing and writing songs in New York with bands "Blue Period" and the "Hello Strangers" in 1984, then sang with a group called "Washington Week In Review" alongside members of [a=The Brand New Heavies] in London in 1985.
In 1989, she sang back-ups and lead vocals on [a=The The]'s 'Mind Bomb' tour. The following year, she joined band [a=Repercussions], releasing "[m=1567486]" (1992) on [l=Mo Wax], collaborating with [a=Curtis Mayfield] on "[url=https://www.discogs.com/master/view/222954]Let's Do It Again[/url]" (1984) on [l=Reprise Records], and recording two albums "[m=207069]" (1995) and "[r=4273733]" (1997), as their vocalist and songwriter. She also recorded track "[m=100029]" (2000) with [a=Leftfield] from their album "[m=100010]" (1999).
She relocated (and now lives/works) to Helsinki, Finland, producing and recording with [a=Maurice Fulton] and ex-husband [a=Jimi Tenor], releasing the albums "[r=3576]" (2000) and "[m=216922]" (2004) on [l=Sähkö Recordings]/[l=Puu].
Between 2003-2015, she recorded three soul/funk studio albums with [a=Nicole Willis] & [a=The Soul Investigators], "[m=52897]" (2005), "[m=519682]" (2013), and finally "[m=893876]" (2015). She also returned to working with Tenor during this time, releasing two house music albums under the name [a=Cola & Jimmu], "[m=574436]" (2013) and "[m=709195]" (2014), with which she began her label [l=Herakles Records], along with the eponymous album "[m=696719]" (2014) which she produced with Tenor.
Further collaborations followed on [url=https://www.discogs.com/label/1349657-Persephone-Records-2]Persephone Records[/url] (a sublabel of Herakles Records) with artists [a=Jonathan Maron]; Jimi Tenor; with [a=UMO Jazz Orchestra] on album "[m=1250099]" (2017); and with [a=Banda Palomita] on album "[r=13272669]" (2019).
Education wise, Willis graduated from the High School of Art and Design in 1980. She later graduated with a Bachelor of Fine Arts from her studies at Lahti Institute of Fine Arts, Lahti University of Applied Sciences, with professor Markus Heikkerö in Finland, and Erasmus studies at Sir John Cass Department of Art, London Metropolitan University in the UK, in 2008 & completed in 2009. Willis' concentration in her studies were oil painting, time-based media, and performance.
Ex-wife of producer [a=Lassi Lehto] (aka [a=Jimi Tenor]), between 1999-2016.

artist.name: Candy Apple
artist.profile: Remixer duo from UK.

artist.name: Maurice Fulton
artist.profile: DJ, producer and musician from Baltimore, Maryland. Relocated to Sheffield in the UK in the 2000s from where he runs [l=BubbleTease Communications]. Married to [a=Mutsumi Kanamori] aka [a=MU].

artist.name: Bridge & Tunnel
artist.profile: Electronica/rock band based in Berlin, Germany from 2000-2004, comprising New York multi-instrumentalist Nathan Bennett and Berlin DJ Mark Bihler. Assisted on some recordings and live work by [a116851] and Kevin Williams.

artist.name: Sabrina Johnston
artist.profile:
American singer, songwriter and producer. Sabrina grew up in the United States attending summer music camps, dance and voice classes before enrolling at Chaney State University as a music major.
Johnson's vocal range is stylistically diverse and distinctive, transitioning smoothly from R&B to Soul and Pop. On entering the music scene, she quickly found herself in great demand as a session singer with such artists as [a=West Street Mob] and [a=Lauryn Hill]. Johnston expanded her potential and pushed the possibilities by writing, arranging and producing the songs she sang, and also for other artists.
The self-penned, gospel-tinged Pop (dance/urban) hit, [i]Peace[/i] caught the attention of the [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/Rolling+Stones%2C+The]The Rolling Stones[/url], who asked her to audition for their Steel Wheels Tour. However, they lost out to [l=JBR Records] and [l=EastWest] when she was signed for a self-titled album. This was followed by the album [i]Yum Yum[/i] several years later.
Married to [a=Ken Johnston].

artist.name: Suspects
artist.profile: US electronic duo

artist.name: Noreaga
artist.profile: American rapper from Queens, New York. Born: September 6, 1977, Queens, New York

artist.profile: Hip hop crew from Marseille, France.

artist.profile: Musician, producer and DJ from Frankfurt, Germany, co-founder of the label "Dial Records".

artist.name: DJ Slip
artist.profile: Started his career in the early 1990s in Minneapolis, DJ Slip played in countries around the world as a rave DJ. He later moved to New York and started working with Thema, a label from Brooklyn. Eventually DJ Slip landed in Portland where he continued to work the beats and operate his [l=Do Something] label.

artist.name: London Elektricity
artist.profile: Drum & Bass DJ / Producer from London, UK. Co-founder and owner of [l1386] Owner of defunct sublabel [l68555] Founder of label [l3851374].

artist.name: Signum
artist.profile:
Dutch trance project founded in 1997 by Ron Hagen and Pascal Minnaard. The name was inspired by the Opel/Vauxhall car model of the same name.
In 2007 Pascal Minnaard left the project, which has since been continued as solo project by Ron Hagen.

artist.profile: Deep House project.

artist.name: Junko '73
artist.profile: Finnish duo.

artist.name: The Step
artist.profile:
Richard Barratt, Sarah Jay, Winston Hazel
Collaboration between two DJs from Northern England and vocalist Sarah Jay, their sole release became the eighth single on Warp. Hazel and Barratt also appeared on Warp as members of [a=Forgemasters] (Hazel) and [a=Sweet Exorcist] (Barratt).

artist.name: Tomas
artist.profile: House music artist with 1 release [m80252].

artist.name: Michael Wells
artist.profile:
One half of [a=GTO] together with late [a=Lee Newman].
Not to be confused with [a=Mike Wells] from [a=Force Mass Motion].

artist.name: Gordon Matthewman
artist.profile: House DJ from London, United Kingdom. Co-founded the label [l=Rabbit City Records] with [a=Colin Faver] in 1991.

artist.name: Snakebite
artist.profile: Italian euro house / trance project.

artist.name: Mark Pilkington
artist.profile: Dr. Mark Pilkington is a lecturer, composer, and record producer based in Manchester UK. He completed a Ph.D. in electroacoustic composition at NOVARS, The University of Manchester, UK

artist.name: Gaetek
artist.profile: Italian DJ and producer.

artist.name: GHOS
artist.profile: Greek DJ and producer.

artist.name: Leandro Gamez
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Madrid, Spain.

artist.name: Richie Rich
artist.profile: Founder of [l=Gee Street] label which also released some of his early hip-house tracks. DJ on London's Kiss FM - originally as a pirate station from 1986 on his 'Good Groove' show, through the mid-1992 as a legal station on the 'Rap Academy'.

artist.name: Kraftwerk
artist.profile:
German group formed in Düsseldorf in 1970. Formerly known as [a=Organisation], Kraftwerk debuted live as the trio of Ralf Hütter, Florian Schneider-Esleben and Charly Weiss at the Tivoli Popfestival, Aachen on 11 July 1970. Klaus Dinger then took over as drummer. Gigs between 26 December 1970 and 31 July 1971 omitted Hütter, with various line-ups including Michael Rother and others. After a gig in the Weserberglands of Germany on 31 July 1971, Dinger & Rother then split-off forming [a=Neu!]. Shortly after, Hütter returned. First known Hütter/Schneider gig is 22 November 1971 at the Sachsenwald Gymnasium, Hamburg.
Current members: Ralf Hütter (July 1970 to December 1970, September 1971-) Lead Vocals, Vocoder, Synthesizers, Keyboards, Organ, Drums and Percussion, Guitar, Bass Guitar Henning Schmitz (1987-) Electronic Percussion (1987–) Sound Engineering Falk Grieffenhagen (2013-) Electronic Percussion Georg Bongartz (2023-) Video Technician
Former Members: Karl Bartos (1974-1991) Electronic Percussion Klaus Dinger (1970-1971) Drum Wolfgang Flür (1973-1987) Electronic Percussion Andreas Hohmann (1970) Drum Eberhard Kranemann (1970-1971) Bass Guitar Thomas Lohmann (1970) Drum Houschäng Néjadepour (1970-1971) Electric Guitar Michael Rother (1971) Electric Guitar Florian Schneider-Esleben (1970-2008) Charly Weiss (1970) Drums Plato Kostic (a.k.a. Plato Riviera) (1970) Bass Guitar Peter Schmidt (1970) Drums Emil Schult (1973) Electric guitar, Electronic violin Klaus Röder (1974) Electric guitar, Electronic violin Fritz Hilpert (1987-2022) Electronic Percussion, Sound Engineering Fernando Abrantes (1991) Electronic Percussion, Synthesizer Stefan Pfaffe (2008-2012) Video Technician

artist.name: Front 242
artist.profile:
Front 242 were formed in Aarschot, Belgium, by Daniel Bressanutti and Dirk Bergen in 1981. Together they produced the first single "[r=105830]" (released October 1981). Jean-Luc De Meyer (vocals) and Patrick Codenys (synth) were looking for support from Daniel to complete the first single in their [a217080] project. Daniel asked them to work on the second single "[r=90098]" (released April 1982) and on Front 242. Richard Jonckheere (Richard 23) was not yet an official member of the group but already had a small singing part in the song "U.Men". The first album "Geography" followed in December 1982. Richard 23 was increasingly integrated into the group, especially because of his presence on stage. In contrast, Richard criticized the lack of this imposing presence in Dirk. Dirk then took over management but then left the band in April 1983. With this line-up, the band released many albums of EBM, gaining more popularity with every release.
EBM stands for Electronic Body Music, a term first used on their album "[m=16375]" appearing on the inner sleeve, but not used as or considered as a genre name yet. The term was actually established as a genre through a marketing concept by promoter [a=Jens-Markus Wegener]. It was strongly pushed to German media to promote the SPV compilation [m11378] in 1987/1988. Sometimes the term itself is also credited to Kraftwerk from an interview in 1978, but according to Wegener the Kraftwerk reference wasn’t known at that time neither to him nor Front 242.
They also established a solid live reputation with an energetic show in para-military outfits. The year 1993 brought a radical change in style, with guest musicians, the disappearance of their military look and the exploration of both harder (guitar samples) and softer (female vocals, ambient) sounds. After many years of silence, except for a few live/remix albums, Front 242 re-appeared in the spotlight in the late nineties with their "RE:BOOT" tour bringing modern, techno-like cover versions of their own songs. In 2003, they took a step back towards their EBM roots with the release of "[m=27372]" and "[m=15495]".
Before Front 242, the members were already involved in other projects, like [a148769] ([a43841]), [a217080] ([a92674] & [a43842]) and Tranik Ind. ([a232851]).

artist.name: JT Company
artist.profile: Italian House / Euro-House project founded by [a1504] in 1990. JT Company (also called JT Company Team) regrouped many different artists over the years.

artist.name: Daphne
artist.profile:
Daphne Rubin-Vega is a two-time Tony nominee who is best known for originating the role of Mimi on and off-Broadway in Johnathan Larson's rock musical "RENT". However, Daphne has been on the scene starting from her days in freestyle latin girl group Pajama Party.
After "RENT", Daphne released a string of hit dance singles including "When You Love Someone" and "I Found It". She recorded her first album "Souvenirs" which is only available on her official website due to the demise of the Mercury record label.
Daphne has appeared on and off-broadway numerous times over the years and later this year will star as Fantine in the new revival of "Les Miserables". In October 2006, she and her rock band DRV released "Redemption Songs", a collection of mostly-self written songs.

artist.name: 7th Movement
artist.profile: Originally credited as Morena, Frescati, Barrajo and Aquaviva - but it was actually Rocky, Diesel and Ashley Beedle of [a2937] fame.

artist.name: The Boss
artist.profile: Born in Brooklyn, New York, in 1961.

artist.name: Thunderdome
artist.profile: Norwegian 80's hard rock band from Sandnes/Stavanger.

artist.name: Lee Genesis
artist.profile:
[b]Disco - soul - house singer[/b]
Born in Brooklyn, New York. Played his part in the late 70's - early 80's disco - boogie underground. Well known for his collaboration with [a=Bob Sinclar] on his single "My Only Love". He released his first album "The Soul Of Lee Genesis" in 2007.

artist.name: Kathy Sledge
artist.profile:
Kathy Sledge (born January 6, 1959, Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, USA) is an American singer and songwriter. Together with her sisters [url=https://www.discogs.com/artist/751549-Debbie-Sledge]Debbie[/url], [url=https://www.discogs.com/artist/690440-Joni-Sledge]Joni[/url] and [url=https://www.discogs.com/artist/751550-Kim-Sledge]Kim[/url] formed the group [a37224]. She sang lead on Sister Sledge's hits "He's the Greatest Dancer," "We Are Family," "Lost in Music," "Got to Love Somebody," "Reach Your Peak," "All American Girls," "My Guy," and "B.Y.O.B. (Bring Your Own Baby)."
She left the group in 1989 and in 1992, Kathy Sledge signed with [l1005] as a solo artist. Her first LP "Heart", featured the singles "Take Me Back to Love Again" and the ballad "All of My Love." The remix of "Take Me Back to Love Again" was a huge dance hit. The album's title track was released as a single and became a dance hit. After she left Epic, Sledge recorded several dance hits on independent and foreign labels (a cover of another song from [a18956]'s "Songs In the Key Of Life", "Another Star," and the maxi CD single "Another Day").

artist.name: Millionaire Hippies
artist.profile: DJ and producer from UK.

artist.name: Viola Sykes
artist.profile: violasykes@gmail.com

artist.name: Barbara Tucker
artist.profile: Barbara Tucker is one of the most prolific and successful soul/dance/house music vocalists. Tucker has had six #1 hits on the US Hot Dance Club Songs chart. With the production team [a=Masters At Work] she has created dance classics like "Deep Inside", "Beautiful People", "I Get Lifted", "Stay Together" and "Stop Playing With My Mind". She has also worked with [a=David Guetta] and [a=Blaze]. Tucker's backing vocal credits range from [a=George Clinton] to [a=Deee-Lite]. Raised in Brooklyn, New York, Tucker found her love for singing in the church, as well as from her father Jayotis Washington who was a member of [a=The Persuasions]. As an actress, Tucker has performed in various off-Broadway plays, earning her the American Theater T.O.R. award. As a dancer, Tucker has choreographed and danced for various dance artists such as [a=C + C Music Factory], [a=Soul System], [a=Jay Williams] and [a=Deee-Lite]. Runs [l=B Star Music Group].

British singer.

Recorded for a great many artists and projects, and continues to do so. Probably best known for her chart and club hit "[m=33738]" (1990).

artist.name: T.P.M.
artist.profile: Female band that does simple punk and cried, with a wistful vocals "Love it or hate it". Started in 1997, changed bassist often, recorded two demos, toured around and then released a CD in 2000. The band ended in 2001. Members: Marina - Vocals Quel - Guitar Carol - Bass Nessa - Drums

artist.name: Roger Sanchez
artist.profile: Roger Sánchez (born: June 1, 1967, in New York City, USA) is an American DJ, remixer, and music producer from New York City. He began DJing in the late 1980s, emerging from the city's underground house scene. He gained early recognition through his remix work and club performances, which led to international bookings by the early 1990s. In 1991, he founded the management company [l=Indeep Productions]. Together with his manager Marts Andrups, he launched the label [l=Narcotic] in 1994. From 1999 to 2002, he ran the imprint [l=R-Senal], followed by the founding of [l=Stealth Records (2)] in 2002, which focused on house music releases and his own productions. Throughout his career, Sánchez has released music under various aliases, including [a=The S-Man], and has remixed tracks for artists across genres.

artist.name: Brodsky Quartet
artist.profile: British string quartet formed in Middlesbrough, North Yorkshire, in 1972 as the "Cleveland Quartet", and subsequently named for Russian violinist Adolph Brodsky. [a959103] and [a403626] are original members with [a789912] (until 1999) and Alexander Robertson (until 1982). Other members: [a184286] (from 1982), [a527933] (1999-2007), [a5058652] (2007-2019), [a2199347] (2019-2021) and [a916362] (from 2021).

artist.name: Evelyn Glennie
artist.profile: Born: July 19, 1965, Aberdeen, Scotland. Scottish percussionist. Deaf since the age of 12, in live performances she feels the music through ground vibrations.

artist.name: Dobie
artist.profile: Producer from London. Dobie got his first pair of turntables way back in 1982 and began cutting up anything and everything on vinyl. Hip-hop was his first love but punk, rock and electronica have always been a part of his eclectic musical equation. The Dobie nickname comes from his quietness in the studio, which friends said reminded them of the Dolby on their hi-fis, and his resemblance to Captain Dobey (Bernie Hamilton) from the TV series Starsky & Hutch.

artist.name: Graham Massey
artist.profile: British musician, producer & remixer. Born 1960-08-04 in Manchester, UK.

artist.name: Mika Vainio
artist.profile:
Mika Vainio (born 15 May, 1963, Helsinki, Finland - died 12 April, 2017, France, aged 53) was a Finnish electronic musician, composer and producer.
He began his musical career playing electronics and drums as part of the early Finnish industrial and noise scene. In 1993, together with [a11329] and [a549915], he founded the minimal electronic group [a3822]. Sami Salo left Pan Sonic in 1996 and the project continued as a duo until disbanding in 2009. Vainio then established a successful solo career, often collaborating with experimental French composer [a669623].

artist.name: Tortoise
artist.profile: American post-rock band formed in Chicago, Illinois in 1990.

artist.name: John Callaghan
artist.profile:
Not to be confused with trance artist John O'Callaghan.
John Callaghan is an unusual songwriter/performer of thoughtful and spiky electronica from Birmingham (UK). His self-directed video for "I'm Not Comfortable Inside My Mind" aired on MTV. His live performances are energetic and imaginative and range from one-man Auto-Karaoke shows to specially written dancefloor sets. Recent well-received shows have included London's Electrowerks, Cambridge's Portland Arms, and Crystal Palace Bowl.

artist.name: Kid Unknown
artist.profile: Fitzpatrick was a regular DJ at the legendary Hacienda nightclub in Manchester. He had two singles released on Warp in 1992 before going on to record under the name Nipper and to co-found the [l=LCD Records] label.

artist.name: DJ Marky
artist.profile:
Marco Antonio da Silva, (born in 14 June 1973), known professionally as DJ Marky, is a Brazilian drum and bass DJ.
Together with DJ Patife, XRS Land and Drumagick, Brazilians were forging a new sound in drum and bass that became popular around the world with releases such as LK and Só Tinha Que Ser Com Você.

artist.name: Waveform
artist.profile: Earlier Breaks / Breakbeat alias of UK producer Mike James.

artist.name: Calibre
artist.profile:
Northern Irish multi genre electronic music artist from Belfast, now lives in Cologne (Köln) Germany. Runs his own [url=http://www.discogs.com/label/Signature+Records]Signature Records[/url] label, focusing mainly on his own music.
He helped to operate the [url=http://www.discogs.com/label/Soul:r]Soul:R[/url] imprint with [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/Marcus+Intalex]Marcus Intalex[/url] and [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/ST+Files]ST Files[/url], the label ceased operations in 2018 after Marcus passed away in 2017.

artist.name: Slinky Wizard
artist.profile:
Adam Boyd and Dominic Lamb composed Slinky Wizard's first track 'The Rising' in 1992. After this George Barker and Ronnie Biggs joined the group, and with the assistance of Simon Posford they created Flying Rhino Records' first release, the Slinky Wizard EP.
In the summer of 1994, Adam Boyd died in an accident. In late 1995/early 1996 Gus Till joined the group. Starting around this same time, Ronnie Biggs was no longer credited on Slinky Wizard tracks, but it is not clear if he officially left the group.
Since 2001, Slinky Wizard has had no output.
Through the years, Slinky Wizard was variously joined by Simon Posford, James Monro, Dick Trevor, Matt Evans, and Jules Evans.

artist.name: Slinky Nuns
artist.profile: Psychedelic / Goa Trance project from U.K. Essentially, collaboration between George Barker of [a4745] and Richard Trevor of [a60766]. Other "Slinky Wizard" members also participated: Ronnie Biggs on the tracks 'Shitty Stick' and 'Caught In The Zipper', and Gus Till on the track 'Stun Gun'.

artist.name: Hallucinogen
artist.profile:
Hallucinogen is the stage name of [a=Simon Posford] (born 28 October 1971) an English electronic musician, specializing in Psychedelic trance music. His first studio album, Twisted, released in 1995, is considered one of the most influential albums in the genre. In particular, the first track, "LSD", which remains the defining sound of Goa trance to this day.
His second album, The Lone Deranger, was released in 1997. Hallucinogen's third album, In Dub (Hallucinogen tracks remixed by [a=Ott]), was released in 2002.
Although an album called "Asnesnothine" is frequently encountered on the Internet, it is only a bootleg.
Simon Posford is also the founder of the successful label [l3336].

artist.name: Sheyba
artist.profile: Jean Borelli & Kristian Thinning (under the pseudonym Chris Anderson)

artist.name: Technossomy
artist.profile: Former Goa Trance act that was formed in 1992. Matt Evans came from a background of percussion and drumming in rock bands, and James Monro from a background in electronic music and DJ’ing. They became famous with tracks like Elektron Bender, Pyramid, and V.T.O.L. There has been no new material since 1997.

artist.name: X-Dream
artist.profile: Goa trance / psy-trance / techno group, hailing from Hamburg, Germany and active since 1991.

namevariations.name: X - Dream
X Dream
X Dream And The Trancesylvania Project
X-Dream & The Trancesylvania Project
X-Dream And The Trancesylvania Project
X. Dream
X.Dream
Xdream

artist.name: Blue Planet Corporation
artist.profile: French trance - goa trance and downtempo/ambient project. Originally a duo, consisting of Gabriel Masurel and Christophe Lebras, the latter left after releasing [url=https://www.discogs.com/Blue-Planet-Corporation-Cyclothymic-Crystal/master/20416]Cyclothymic 12"[/url] on [l164] in 1997, so BPC is Masurel's solo project from then on.

artist.name: Syb Unity Nettwerk
artist.profile: Psychedelic / Goa Trance Pioneer Act since 1994 project from Kris Kylven Live : Kris Kylven: Leader, composer and producer Past live member : RX Heaven: Tweaker & live electronics (1995 - 2019 )

artist.name: Darshan
artist.profile: Goa & psychedelic trance project, based in the South U.K. Started in 1995 as a duo. Mark Robinson left the project in 1998.

artist.name: Eat Static
artist.profile:
Eat Static is an Electronic music project from Frome, Somerset, England formed in 1989 by Merv Pepler and Joie Hinton, former [a=Ozric Tentacles] members.
Their band name comes from a sample they lifted from Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan.

artist.name: The Overlords
artist.profile: Synth-pop / EBM / electro / techno / goa trance project from Denmark.

artist.name: Dance 2 Trance
artist.profile:
German trance duo.
They met in 1990 and decided to work together; their first release was in the same year with a promo named "Dance 2 Trance." Their commercial break came in 1992 with the release of "P.ower Of A.merican N.atives."
In 1995, they decided to go their separate ways, with DJ Dag going solo, and Ellmer concentrating more on his other project, [a=Jam & Spoon].

artist.name: Unconscious Collective
artist.profile:
Formed in 1996, Oxford, UK. Now located in Bristol, UK.
Originally, UC was a separate psy-trance project to OOOD but between 1999-2005 this project was the home for the non-dancefloor aspect of [a41548]'s output.

artist.name: Planet B.E.N.
artist.profile:
Trance and techno DJ and producer since 1983.
In 2003 DJ Planet B.E.N. started his own label, [L=Planet B.E.N. Records] (sold in 2010) and Planet B.E.N. Publishing (under [L=ROBA Music Verlag GmbH]). Circa 2009 he started label by [L=Planet B.E.N. Techno].

artist.name: Ololiuqui
artist.profile: Psychedelic / Goa Trance and Ambient / Downtempo electronic music project from Hamburg, Germany. The group was formed in the summer of 1996 and derives its name from the Mexican, LSD-like, substance "Ololiuqui", which is derived from the seeds of a variety of morning glories. While it originally started as a duo, Oliver Elschenbrioch effectively retired from the band at the late 90ies and the project went on by Volker König alone, who broadened the project's musical directions.

artist.name: Bumbling Loons
artist.profile: Psychedelic / Progressive Trance project from the UK.

artist.name: Alienated
artist.profile: Danish electronic duo

artist.name: Planet Slink
artist.profile: A one track psy-trance collaboration between members of Blue Planet Corporation & Slinky Wizard.

artist.name: Space Tribe
artist.profile:
Space Tribe, aka [a167571] (born 8 March 1958, London, England – died 23 August 2021, London, England) was an English Goa and psytrance musician, and clothing producer. Olli was a pioneer of Psychedelic trance for many years and Space Tribe is one of the best known names in the psychedelic scene.
In the early 1980s, Olli was the singer in the theatrical Goth/Glam band [a=Specimen], and founded the famous London goth club "The Batcave". After the band split up in 1986, Olli went traveling in Asia and in 1988 and arrived in Koh Phangan, Thailand where he first encountered the electro-driven psychedelic parties on the beach. He stayed there for 2 years, DJ-ing, and set up a small studio and made tracks for parties, before moving to Goa, India. Over the next 4 years, Olli spent his time mostly in Goa, but also travelled around the world making parties in other places, including: San Francisco, Switzerland, UK, Japan.
In 1993, Olli and his brother [a=Miki Wisdom], and fellow space cadet, Richmond, started Space Tribe clothing in Bali. The fractal fluoro party gear was an instant success and can be see at parties all over the planet.
In 1995, the first Space Tribe tracks were hatched in the Butterfly studios with [a=Simon Posford] ([a=Hallucinogen]/[a=Shpongle]). Olli moved to Byron Bay, Australia, and set up the Rainforest Space Base studio. Space Tribe released the first album, 'Sonic Mandala' on Spirit Zone in 1996, and since then Space Tribe has released at least an album a year. In 2004 [l=Space Tribe Music] was set up as a record label providing an outlet for Space Tribe releases and compilation albums. Space Tribe have also made many tracks in collaboration with other artists: [a=GMS] ([a=Alien Jesus]), [a=X-Dream], [a=Menog],[a=Samy Guediche] (CPU), [a=Luke Psywalker], [a=Electric Universe] ([a=ESP (8)], [a=Tristan], [a=Dickster] ([a=Green Nuns Of The Revolution]) and [a=Mad Maxx (3)].
Since 2008 [a=Jon Klein] ([a=Specimen], Siouxsie & the Banshees, Sinead O’Connor) has been brought his guitar skills to Space Tribe live shows and in the studio.

artist.name: Mary J. Blige
artist.profile: American R&B/Soul singer/songwriter, actress and philanthropist. Born: 11 January 1971 in The Bronx, New York City, New York, USA. In November 2010, Billboard Magazine ranked Blige as the most successful female R&B artist of the past 25 years, she ranked number two (overall) ahead of [a=Janet Jackson], [a=Mariah Carey], and [a=Whitney Houston].

artist.name: Jodeci
artist.profile: American R&B quartet with members DeVanté Swing, Mr. Dalvin, K-Ci, and JoJo. Formed in 1989 in Charlotte, North Carolina.

artist.name: Carlito & DJ Addiction
artist.profile: UK electronic dance music duo Styles include: Drum 'n' Bass

artist.name: Fellowship
artist.profile: UK Drum 'n Bass artist.

artist.name: Brian Lavelle
artist.profile:
Scottish musician and writer, born in Glasgow in 1972 and based in Edinburgh since 1995. He has been recording improvised and experimental music since 1990. His work involves acoustic and electronic sound sources, as well as field recordings. Much of his work has focused on free improvisation.
With Richard Youngs, he created the Radios series of albums centred around the notion of chiasmus, and he has released music with others including Caroline McKenzie, Andrew Paine and Matthew Shaw.

artist.name: Radboud Mens
artist.profile:
Radboud Mens is a sound-artist, composer and sound designer. He started creating noise-machines in 1988. In the early 90s he attached contact mics to dog’s brushes that he used to destroy vinyl records by scratch-playing them, boosting the signal through broken cassette-decks and recording the results. He also made rhythmic-noise using a broken turntable. In 1994 these experiments were released as a two CD set by the British label [l18368] under the project names [a119981] and [a224473]. To achieve the full effect, listeners had to shuffle-mix the two discs together themselves. In 1995 he released as [a74821], together with [a74823], the drone CD [r578811] that came with a 5 inch vinyl locked groove record to play along with the long drone pieces on the CD. From 1997 on he also released music under his own name, music that is more minimal and conceptual. For his CD [r68660], which was made in 1997/1998, but released two years later, he used digital clicks and errors he collected while cleaning up digital recordings during his stint as a sound engineer at the Centre Of Electronic Music in Amsterdam. [r371393], his next CD release, is based on a DSP function called ‘convolution’. He worked on the vocabulary for this CD for two years.
Radboud Mens has worked with a broad variety of sound artists and composers, including [a53728], [a12543] and [a6933], voice-artist [a68069], guitarist [a431777], [a186445], [a74819] (as [a26849]), [a264874] (ex-[a74984]),[a376082], [a58243], [a508445], [a955971], [a237503], [a7991995] and [a253819]. His music has been released by [l1371], [l13613], [l4747], [l5853], [l1687], [l1586], [l30935], [l55756] and [l5368]; both [l4747] and [l9874] released some of his minimal-techno as a series of 12” LPs. In live situations he collaborates with video artists such as [a2816329], [a141819], [a60845] and [l79558]. He co-founded the [l=ERS] label with [a431777] in 1999, which is dedicated to experimental ambient-noise and drone music; after a break of 10 years, ERS is now active again. He presented a radio show called Earbitten for the Amsterdam free radio station Radio 100 for sixteen years between 1986 and 2003. He still has a radio show called 230 Volt on the Concertzender in the Netherlands and alternates as the producer of the Staalplaat radio shows on dfm.nu

artist.name: Immedia
artist.profile: "A duo from London, UK. Paniale studied fine art at Central Saint Martins College of Art, creating immersive light and sound environments. Reynolds studied graphic design, creating a number of art books and magazines, and now works primarily as a sound artist. As immedia they have participated in a number of group events and independent shows, exploring the subtle and fragile nature of sound. they currently organise the quarterly Extrasensory sound art event at the 291 Gallery in London, UK."

artist.name: Monotonos
artist.profile:
[b]Nano-Bio:[/b] 1995 (first experiments). 1997 - now (continuous).
[b]Genre:[/b] atmospheric drones experimental doom
[b]Record Labels:[/b]
[l1687] / [l1017] / [l1371] [l11897] / [l25544] [l29944] [l118324] [l113088] [l520920] [l35958] [l27731] [l22415] [l130144] [a716481]

artist.name: Freiband
artist.profile: Born : Nijmegen, Netherlands Freiband is the latest project by Frans de Waard, also founding member of Kapotte Muziek, Beequeen, Goem aswell as solo projects such as Shifts and Quest. Frans de Waard has been producing music since the mid 80s, firstly on cassettes in the 80s and then on LPs and CD's afterthat. Freiband was inspired by the tape-scratching of Asmus Tietchens on his CD 'Daseinsverfehlung', but here applied to an entirely digital context. Using recordings that were made for the new Beequeen, a record that has a lot of guitars, drums, organs, Frans de Waard started to rework them by scratching the harddisc. The result was warm, glitchy popmusic, in which the original instruments are barely recognizable, but sounding entirely electronical. Tongue in cheek, De Waard calls this popmusic, music made out of popping sounds, rather then popular music, but entirely inspired by De Waard's love for popmusic. Since the inception of this new monikker, Freiband has played concerts in The Netherlands, Japan and a surprise gig at Mutek, Canada, 2001. The first CD 'Microbes' was released late 2001 by Ritornell, a side label of Mille Plateaux. In september 2002 the second CD 'Homeward' was released on Bottrop Boy and this is all with reworked popmusic from the late 70s and early 80s.

artist.name: Kim Cascone
artist.profile: Italian-American musician, composer and producer of electronic music who is known for his releases in the ambient, drone, industrial and electro-acoustic genre. Born on December 21, 1955. Cascone studied electronic music at Berklee College of Music, Boston and worked since the late 70's in electronics. In the late 80s, Cascone was assistant music editor for David Lynch's films 'Twin Peaks' and 'Wild At Heart'. In 1986 Cascone founded the record label [l=Silent], where he released first results of his project [a=PGR]. Cascone now releases music on Sub Rosa, Mille Plateaux and runs a small vanity label called [l=Anechoic].

artist.name: Surge
artist.profile:
Surge is a Bristol-based brother duo of Andy and Dave Bell
Note: Not the Erasure Andy Bell, and not the Hurricane #1 / Ride / Oasis one either.
For the Drum & Bass producer use [a=Surge (5)].

artist.name: Nuclear Ramjet
artist.profile:
Nuclear Ramjet started in 1997 with three components; Felix aka "Miracle Flash" pre-Nuclear Ramjet and "Flash" currently a disco and house music producer, trance producer Max, also known as "Aeon" and Nivoc, a DJ giving direction and advice on the music and taking part in composition and production. Nivoc also managed their releases, promotion and distribution. Although they had different musical interests, they had a common desire for sonic exploration and musical innovation.
The group's name was found accidentally by curiosity: Some scientists back in the 60's dreamed up the "Nuclear Ramjet", a concept engine that harnesses properties of nuclear physics. It was conceived as a theoretical form of interstellar propulsion that will use the same power that drives the sun. The group found inspiration in this advanced concept for futuristic trance music. The tracks are composed in their home studio in Montreal.
After the release of their second album, "Music For Spaceports" in 2001, Nivoc split from the group to concentrate on Ascend Music Group.
Phone: Max 1(514)845-3612 info@nuclearramjet.com

artist.name: 45 Roller
artist.profile: Jungle and Drum n Bass producer from the UK.

artist.name: Black Masses
artist.profile: House music collective led by [a=Cleveland Anderson]. The photo shows vocalists [a=Julia Vaneikirk], [a=Angie Brown] and [a=Alaena Adams] who featured on [m=172696].

artist.name: James Ingram
artist.profile:
American soul singer - songwriter - producer - instrumentalist. Born February 16, 1952 in Akron, Ohio, USA, died January 29, 2019 in Los Angeles, California, USA.
[b]Do not confuse this artist with [a=Jimmy Ingram], member of family soul group [a=Ingram].[/b]
Older brother of [a=Phillip Ingram]. He has sung duets with [a=Patti Austin], [a=Linda Ronstadt], [a=Kim Carnes], [a=Dolly Parton], and [a=Anita Baker], among others. Musically, outside of soul music, he has also performed vocal work for house music producers such as [a=Masters At Work].

artist.name: Scott Findley
artist.profile: Multi-genre artist & DJ based in Los Angeles, releasing house/tech-house projects under the names Scott Findley (2001-2006), Charles Scott (2010 - Present) and Interdimensional Warlock (2021-present).

artist.name: Red Moon
artist.profile:
Red Moon are Roger Watson (aka DJ Hal) and Tony Thomas (Producers) . . . since licensing tracks to Eukahouse (forthcoming in may) they have signed an Album deal with London label "Surround Sounds".
contact info: redmoon@spacestation.co.uk

artist.name: Brett Dancer
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Kalamazoo, Michigan. Met producers [a=Jay Denham] and [a=Fanon Flowers] at Western Michigan University. Released his first production on Denham's [l=Black Nation Records], with Flowers, as [a=Incense and Basslines]. Relocated to New York in late 1993 where he started his own label [l=Track Mode] in 1994. Established a second label, [l=Ethnic Blend Music], in 2023. Based in Baltimore, Maryland.

artist.name: Erotek
artist.profile: (passed away in 2019)

artist.name: DJ Falcon
artist.profile:
Before producing on [l=Roulé] he worked in A&R department at Virgin. In 1999 he released his first EP (on [l=Roulé]) and produced a remix of [a=Cassius]'s "La Mouche" which is an all-time classic now. One year later, he started working with [a=Thomas Bangalter] on a track called "[r=8094]". Two years later, the second release "[m=85933]" of the duo appeared, which also remained the last release of DJ Falcon for a very long time. After 10 years of releasing nothing, DJ Falcon finally made a comeback in 2012 with several remixes such as [a=Justice (3)]' "New Lands", [a=Alex Gopher]'s "Hello Inc." and [a=Alan Braxe]'s "Time Machine". In 2013 he reunited with [a=Thomas Bangalter] to release the track "Contact" on the [a=Daft Punk] album "Random Access Memories" together with [a=Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo].
Brother of Delphine Quême ([a=Quartet]) and Cousin of Alain Quême ([a=Alan Braxe]).

artist.name: Dakar & Grinser
artist.profile: German duo.

artist.name: Jay Williams
artist.profile: Recording artist and songwriter from Queens, New York City. He began as a young background vocalist and R&B studio session singer for major R&B artists before he was discovered by music mogul Craig Kallman and producer Nelson Roman. He later launched JW2 Entertainment Group in which he is currently songwriting, scoring soundtracks, co-writing a musical stage play, consulting, mentoring and developing new artists.

artist.name: Michael Procter
artist.profile: Procter (died 23rd January 2020) was originally from Peoria, but his rise to fame began when the aspiring singer/songwriter was recruited as a player in the long-running pop vocal group 5th Dimension. The group had been founded by Billy Davis Jr with a line-up that included Marilyn McCoo, and was frequently backed by legendary Los Angeles session musicians The Wrecking Crew. Procter joined the group in 1979 according to the Encyclopedia of Pop, Rock & Soul, just after the departure of McCoo and Davis He departed 5th Dimension (which released their first single all the way back in 1966 and has been frequently rekindled with surviving members) in 1988 for a solo career. Unlike many soul vocalists who achieved a level of professional renown in the 1980s, Procter slid easily into house music and his voice became both a familiar and a welcoming sound inside the clubs. Among his most popular singles from the first phase of his career were two singles with Urban Blues Project (aka Brian Tappert and Marc Pomeroy, later founders of Traxsource): “Deliver Me” on Victor Simonelli‘s Bassline Records and “Love Don’t Live” on Tappert and Pomeroy’s Soulfuric. His latest single was released in October 2019 – Change with Audio Heritage on Grounded Records.

artist.name: T.C. Izlam
artist.profile: Founder of Hip Step. Died on April 11th, 2017.

artist.profile: Lemon Jelly is a British downtempo music duo from London, formed in 1998 and founded by [a=Fred Deakin] and [a=Nick Franglen]. The majority of bands releases and videos are designed by [a=Fred Deakin]'s [a=Airside] studios.

artist.profile: Born 1968 in Vienna. Austrian DJ and producer.

artist.name: Jon Carter

British electronic DJ and producer.
Born March 21, 1970 in Essex, England, UK.

artist.name: Terranova
artist.profile: German band from Berlin, established in the early 1990s.

artist.name: Howie B.
artist.profile: British musician, producer, songwriter, remixer and engineer. Founder of [l=Pussyfoot Records] and [l=HB Recordings]. Born in 1963 in Glasgow, Scotland. Studied psychology at Manchester University. Started in studios as tea boy, then tape operator, then assistant engineer. Worked three years as assistant to soundtrack composer [a=Stanley Myers]. He provided music for [a=Wim Wenders]' movie [i]End of Violence[/i] and a short film by [a=Run Wrake] accompanied [i]Music For Babies[/i] and has won numerous awards.

artist.name: Priest
artist.profile: Backing vocalist who appeared on 2 early 90s US house records.

artist.name: Stereo MC's
artist.profile: The Stereo MCs were formed in London, UK in 1985 by singer [a639016] (Birch) and [a639015] (The Head). The current line-up includes the two founders, drummer [a283736] and singer [a24483].

artist.name: BFC
artist.profile: Born: May 22, 1969 in Detroit, United States BFC stands for Betty Ford Clinic.

artist.name: The Junkyard Band
artist.profile: The Junk Yard Band is a Washington DC go-go band, founded in the early 1980s by children playing on improvised instruments. They are best known for their song, Sardines.

artist.name: Spoonie Gee
artist.profile: Spoonie Gee was the nephew of veteran R&B producer [a=Bobby Robinson] and one of the earliest rap artists. He was known as the "love rapper," an image that was established by his first record, "Love Rap", released on his uncle's Enjoy label as the flip side of the Treacherous Three's "The New Rap Language". The bulk of early rap records reproduced an MC's party routine with a loose sequence of narrative, boasting, and call and response. Spoonie's initial outing, however, organized a hip-hop styled record around a romantic theme, coming closer to the lyrical norms of pop music. The intimate "Love Rap" was accompanied only by drum set and congas, and Spoonie's next record continued in a similarly minimalist vein. The voice-over on 1979's "Spoonin' Rap" stuck to more conventional old-school boasting but looks forward to the gangsta attitude in its jailhouse references. "Spoonin' Rap" was also prophetic in its use of flexatone and heavily echoed voice, suggesting the Jamaican connection that was denied in early interviews by some of the rap originators. In 1980, Spoonie collaborated with Sequence on a classic single, "Monster Jam", probably the last word on the series of "Good Times" / "Another One Bites the Dust" variations, and a classic in the Sugar Hill vein, complete with a bone-crushing bass line and ecstatic crowd noises.

artist.name: Leandro Fresco
artist.profile: Born in Buenos Aires, Argentina.

artist.name: Angel Molina
artist.profile: Spanish DJ and producer born in Barcelona. He started DJ career in 1991.

artist.name: Agent Cooper
artist.profile: German Techno DJ and producer based in Berlin.

artist.name: Saul Williams
artist.profile: American singer, musician, poet, writer and actor, born February 29, 1972. Has released a variety of solo works and also collaborated with the likes of drum & bass producer DJ Krust and Trent Reznor. He has also starred in the movie "Slam" (1998).

artist.name: Kool G Rap
artist.profile: Nathaniel Thomas Wilson (born July 20, 1968), better known as [a=Kool G Rap] (or simply G Rap), is an American rapper. He started his career in the mid-1980s as part of the duo [a=Kool G Rap & D.J. Polo] and as a member of the [a=Juice Crew All Stars]. Recognized as one of the most skilled and influential MCs in hip-hop, he helped pioneer mafioso rap, hardcore street narratives, and intricate multisyllabic rhyming. On his album The Giancana Story, he mentioned that the "G" in his name stands for "Giancana" (after mobster Sam Giancana), though he has also stated it stands for "Genius."

artist.name: Richard Ashcroft
artist.profile: Singer, songwriter and guitarist, born 11 September 1971 in Wigan, Lancashire, England. Married to [a=Kate Radley] since 1995. Together they have two sons

artist.name: Badly Drawn Boy
artist.profile:
British indie-rock singer, songwriter, and artist.
Born: 2 October 1969 in Dunstable, Bedfordshire, England, UK.

artist.name: Mike D
artist.profile: [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/4043933-Michael-Diamond-3]Michael Diamond[/url], a.k.a. Mike D, (born November 20, 1965) is a founding member of the Beastie Boys.

artist.profile: English musician, singer and songwriter, born 7 October 1968 in Wellingborough, Northamptonshire, England, UK.

artist.name: Exile
artist.profile: Thrash metal group from Jordan who formed in 2008.

artist.name: Nu:Tone
artist.profile: Drum & Bass DJ / Producer from Cambridge, UK. Brother Of [a130180] Signed exclusively to [l1386] since 2003.

artist.name: Charles Webster
artist.profile: British electronic DJ, producer, and artist. Starting out playing in local electronic bands in Sheffield, UK, Webster also wrote tracks for hip hop outfit The Rock City Crew and another early techno-house band [a=T-CUT-F]. He became in-house engineer for [l=Square Dance Studios] in Nottingham. During his time there, he began to record his own music. In 1995 he started [l=Remote], two years after relocating to San Francisco, USA.

artist.name: Daze Maxim
artist.profile: Producer from Düsseldorf, Germany.

artist.name: Glove
artist.profile: German producer and remixer duo based in Berlin and Hamburg. It was established around 2002 by [a39078] and [a13797].

artist.name: Sikora
artist.profile:
Sikøra (= Stephan Straka, born 1968 in a small town close to Frankfurt am Main) had been caught by any kind of music very early on.
He chose a hammond organ as his first instrument to play on and experiment with.
Then detouring electronic music for some time he tried post-punk (playing bass), classical (orchestral drums) as well as pop-combo (keys) before finally arriving in the arising techno world of mid-1990s Frankfurt, where he eventually met Jörn E. Wuttke again (alter ego and much more) in 1996 - whom he already knew from earlier musical adventures - and committed himself to electronic music ever since.
Releases soon followed on Klang Elektronik, Playhouse, Ongaku, Cocoon and others, where many of his hereos suddenly became labelmates. some time later he started Nummer-Schallplatten with Frank Lorber and released a set of fine EPs with Frank.
A special incident forced him to pause with production and gigs for some years, but he never lost track of electronic music and began his way back into the still (although slower) moving electronic- and club-carousell in 2015. He's released several EPs on Alex Dijksterhuis' (aka Jamez) RoughCut (sub of Touché) and did some remixing since.
SikØra's sound may be best discribed as 'melancholicly distorted', and melancholy is truthfully the one thing he always loves in all kind of music throughout genres. the constant search for the perfect balance between allowed kitsch, wanted noise and accepted listening habits drives him through all his productions. He certainly stepped down a bit from the speed and raw power of the early days.

artist.name: Little Computer People
artist.profile: German artist and producer, born 29 April 1972 in Frankfurt am Main. Under this alias he follows Electro/Chiptune style. Little Computer People alias is inspired from a videogame released in 1985 by Activision.

artist.name: Oliver Bondzio
artist.profile: DJ since 1983, working in the German clubs Warehouse (Cologne), Omen (Frankfurt). Also co-owned the [l=Jakpot] label.

artist.name: Pascal F.E.O.S.
artist.profile: German Techno DJ, producer, and labelhead (of Greek origin) from Frankfurt am Main. Born March 11, 1968 in Bad Nauheim, Germany. Passed away on 9th May 2020 due to stomach cancer. He was the owner of [l=Level Non Zero] and [l=Need For Groove Recordings]. Together with [a=Alex Azary] he was the founder of [l=Elektrolux], [l=Hey Babe!] and [l=Planet Vision].

artist.name: Toni Rios
artist.profile: Born: 13.10.1971 DJ since: 1989

artist.name: Electronic
artist.profile: "Electronic" began as a [a33928] ([a3898], [a3909]) solo project, but became a duo in 1989 with [a37120] ([a83080]) aiming to follow a more "electronic" style of music, blending synthesizers with guitars and rock with contemporary dance music. First signed to [l857], later to [l2294]. Despite international commercial success and collaborations with [a62963], the band is on indefinite hiatus since 1999.

artist.name: Chaz Vincent
artist.profile: "We live in a world that has become more eager to be shaped by a mirror than by a hammer."

artist.name: Keynotes
artist.profile: American DJ and producer, born 5 September 1964.

artist.name: Galactica
artist.profile: Ken Harada & Christian Smith

artist.name: A Split - Second
artist.profile:
A Split-Second is: Marc Heyndrickx, and Peter Bonne (aka Chrismar Chayell). Formed in 1985 as a solo project of Marc Heyndrickx (under the name Marc Ickx), who self-released the demo-tape "Stained Impressions" with Peter Bonne (later re-named Chrismar Chayell) producing. Bonne at that time was releasing music as [a=Twilight Ritual] together with vocalist Geert Coppens.
After signing to Antler Records, A Split-Second made their debut in 1986 with an EP which included the hit "Flesh". It was followed the next year by the album Ballistic Statues. At that time the band was composed by Ickx and electronic musician Philip Vargod (better known for his new-beat/acid-house project Lips Kiss. Bonne of course was present as producer and co-author of 4 songs (including hit single "Flesh").
After a brief tour Philip V. left the band, replaced by Bonne who became an official member and a driving force behind A Split-Second. With this new line-up the band released another single, "Rigor Mortis", in 1987.
In late 1988, they were signed to the American label, Wax Trax! Records, and released the albums A Split-Second and From The Inside in the US. The singles "Rigor Mortis", "Mambo Witch" and "Colosseum Crash". The single "Mambo Witch" peaked at #29 in 1989 on the Billboard US dance club charts in 1989. New members Fedjean Venvelt (guitars) and Peter Boone (keyboards) joined the band for their first US tour in 1989.
Their 1990 album, Kiss of Fury, featured the track "The Parallax View", which was also a successful single and saw the band moving towards more of a guitar-based sound. Nico Mansy (keyboards) joined the band for their second US tour. "Flesh" peaked in the UK Singles Chart at #68 in December 1991.
In 1991, Bonne became involved in a side project, Wasteland, which soon became more important for him than A Split-Second. Notwistanding this, he released, under the name A Split-Second, the remix album "Flesh & Fire - 1991 Remixes" without the presence of Ickx (the real founding member of the band).
In late 1993 the Ickx & Chayell released one more album Vengeance C.O.D. and, after a Swedish Tour in early 1994 the duo split. Ickx decided to continue alone under the name A Split-Second and joined Gitane Demone in her electronic project Demonix.
In March 1995 A Split-Second/Marc Ickx released the album "Megabite" and the tour saw Ickx performing live with guitarist Fedzjean Venvelt and old keyboard-player Philip Vargod. After this tour and new Demonix remixes (released only in 2000), Ickx announced his retirement from the music scene in 1997.
The band returned to the live scene in 2001. Since 2009, they have been playing live fairly regularly.
A Split-Second's debut single "Flesh" is credited with starting the New Beat genre, at 33 rpm, "Fat Ronnie's inspiration snowballed when Marc Grouls and a handful of other DJs were listening to "Flesh", the latest 12" from Belgian electronic band A Split-Second in Antwerp's USA Import record store. By slowing the pitch control down to a lurching 33 (33+8), Marc transformed the track from pleasant Euro-Industrialism to the melodramatic, pomp-laden epic that's been firing London warehouses all summer."
In 2002, Perfecto Records released a progressive trance remix of "Flesh", produced by Paul Oakenfold.
2023 and 2024 saw Marc Ickx and additional musicians perform as A Split-Second at Cold Waves in Chicago along with additional North American tour dates both years.

artist.name: Hysterix
artist.profile: UK house project, active through the 1990s. The group evolved out of [a=Coloursound (2)]. Toured with [a=Sunscreem], [a=D:Ream] & [a=Technotronic]. Backing vocalists included [a=Sally Anne Marsh] and [a=Marie Harper].

artist.name: Kimball Collins
artist.profile: American trance DJ. Born and raised in Orlando, FL, Collins began DJing in 1987. By the end of the 1980s, his club nights at the Beacham Theater helped jump-start the dance scene in Orlando.

artist.name: Current Value
artist.profile: Hard and dark experimental Drum & Bass, Neurofunk and Dubstep producer/DJ from Germany. One half of [a=Machinecode]. Born 1976. He's active since 1997.

artist.name: Fantastic Plastic Machine
artist.profile: Fantastic Plastic Machine (now abbreviated as FPM) is a Shibuya-kei ("Shibuya-style") dance music project by DJ Tomoyuki Tanaka. After the self-titled debut was released in 1997, Tanaka worked closely with [a169825] and started to incorporate elements of Philly soul and house. In 2009, Tanaka, now abbreviating the name to FPM, started working with [a193783] and [a97729] to pivot FPM to a much more streamlined electro house sound.

artist.name: Titan
artist.profile: In 2012 their debut album [r=11594672] was included as one of "The 25 records that changed Mexican rock", an exhibition curated by Ricardo Bravo at [i]Centro Cultural de España (CCE)[/i] in Mexico City.

artist.name: The Apples In Stereo
artist.profile: American indie rock band, largely the project of lead vocalist/guitarist/producer Robert Schneider, who writes the majority of the band's music and lyrics.

artist.name: DJ Swamp
artist.profile: DJ Swamp is an acclaimed American turntablist, producer, and DJ, renowned for winning the 1996 USA DMC Championship in his rookie year. Best known for touring with Beck for four years, he gained notoriety for his chaotic live performances, which often involved scratching on his tongue, breaking records, and using fire. A versatile artist, he has contributed to albums for artists like The Crystal Method and Kid Rock, blending hip-hop, industrial, and punk influence. DJ Swamp formed Decadent Records in 1996, and is still active in releasing vinyl as of 2026.

artist.name: Infinite
artist.profile: Drum n Bass Duo, better known as [a285] and [a218].

artist.name: Loxy
artist.profile: Drum & bass DJ and producer based in London, UK.

artist.name: Hijackers
artist.profile:
Hijackers…
…are Steven Lee, Paul Batley and Joe Frost, all based outside Norwich.
Joe has been into music since about the age of 14 when he got his first synth. "Playing tunes was okay but I used to enjoy programming sounds more than anything…". He got into DnB when some friends played him tracks by Photek and Bukem but it wasn't until hearing the likes of Alien Girl that he was inspired to work on harder sounding tunes.
Steven is the instrumentalist in Hijackers, being a very good guitar player he also got interested in samplers and computers after initially looking into using them to prototype guitar tracks that he was working on. With several friends (including Paul) DJing locally he soon started experimenting with techno and DnB.
Paul has DJ'd for quite a while, starting mainly in techno and then getting more into Drum n Bass, he used to make mix tapes for his friends when he started out and it was through Steven playing one of these tapes to Joe that they first all got talking.
They first all started working together in mid 1999 and after sending out a few demos their first ever track - "Polymorphic" was signed by Moving Shadow.

artist.name: Ryme Tyme
artist.profile: Part of the No U Turn crew - MC for DJ Trace on Don FM and Kool FM in the early 1990s.

artist.name: Signal To Noise
artist.profile: Group from Australia

artist.name: nCode
artist.profile: Ed Holmes, Jon Skinner & Simon Foster

artist.name: Hex
artist.realname: Matt Black, Robert Pepperell, Miles Visman & Karel Dander
artist.profile: A multimedia VJ group from the early to late 90s that specialized in creating music, art, games, video & interactive CD-ROMS. Formed through DJ [a=Coldcut] with [a=Rob Pepperell], [a=Miles Visman] & [a=Karl Dander].

artist.name: TJ Rizing
artist.profile:
Originally from Armenia, Yan Manou emigrated to the UK in 1992. As a professional guitarist for over ten years, he cites jazz as a major musical influence. Having been seduced by the intricacies of drum & bass programming, Yan decided to try his hand at producing some tracks of his own. Just a year later he had releases on [url=https://www.discogs.com/label/32427-Forty-Five-Revolutions]45 Revolutions[/url], and '[url=https://www.discogs.com/release/189077-Various-Ambient-Northern-Lights-Volume-II]Just Create RCDS[/url]'‎ – and was receiving strong reviews from the music press. Jan uses only basic equipment in his studio, believing strongly in a minimalist approach to equipment as well as to music. He loves his EMU 6400 Sampler, works like a fanatic and is naturally dedicated to his world of music!
After living in London for 12 years, TJ moved to Los Angeles where he currently lives and works as a professional photographer.

artist.name: Oliver Kapp
artist.profile:
DJ and producer from Germany, born in 1969 and devoted to Techno. He is the founder of [l=Indulge Records] & [l=Raygun Records].
Having grown up in the early 80’s eclectic combination of funk/house music (Lil' Louis, Derrick May), European electronica (Kraftwerk, Liaisons Dangereuses), synth-infused 80s music (Newcleus, Cerrone) … all which became infused with his own love for both house and detroit music, Kapp ended up with quite a huge mixed spectrum of musical influences. Today, these circumstances translate all into sets where he picks apart and infuses together all the pieces of the music that he loves at its very best.
As a producer, label owner and promoter, he has also found plenty of ways to express himself musically. He still runs the labels Indulge & Raygun and has releases on International DJ Gigolo, Theory, Logistic, Stockholm Ltd. ect so far...
He is married to [a=Larissa Kapp].

artist.name: Adrian Sherwood
artist.profile:
English record producer & musician, born on 1958-01-20 in London. Sherwood is best known for his [l2409] label and his dub music, as well as for remixing a number of popular acts such as [a=Coldcut], [a=Depeche Mode], [a=Sinéad O'Connor], [a=Primal Scream] or [a=Skinny Puppy]. Sherwood is also the founder of the labels [l=Hitrun Records], [l=Green Tea] and [l=Sound Boy], and cofounder of [l=Carib Gems] & [l=Pressure Sounds].
His most well-known label is [l2409], (= label page), a name Sherwood sometimes also operates under cf. [a59141] (= artist credits page). He is also the 4th member of industrial hip-hop outfit [a=Tackhead], credited as "mixologist". During the early 1980s he brought together many Jamaican artists under the collective name of [a=Singers & Players] including [a=Prince Far I], [a=Mikey Dread], [a=Bim Sherman] and many others, this helped promote the individual artists at the same time as promoting the On-U label.

artist.name: Soul II Soul
artist.profile:
Led by producer/vocalist/songwriter DJ Jazzie B, Soul II Soul was one of the most innovative dance/R&B outfits of the late '80s, creating a seductive, deep R&B that borrowed from Philly soul, disco, reggae, and '80s hip-hop. Originally featuring [a=Jazzie B], producer/arranger [a=Nellee Hooper] and instrumentalist Philip [a=Daddae Harvey], the musical collective came together in the late '80s. Two singles, "Fairplay" and "Feel Free", began to attract attention both in clubs and in the press.
Featuring the vocals of [a=Caron Wheeler], Soul II Soul's third single "Keep on Movin'", reached the U.K. Top Ten in March 1989. Released in the summer of 1989, "Back to Life" also featured Wheeler and became their first number 1 hit. Soul II Soul released their debut album, Club Classics, Vol. One, shortly afterward. Wheeler left the group before the recording of the group's second album, Vol. 2: 1990 — A New Decade. The album featured lead vocalists Victoria Wilson-James, Lamya, Marcia Lewis, and Kym Mazelle. It peaked at number one in the U.K. Throughout 1991-1996, the group featured new female vocalists including Melissa Bell, Charlotte Kelly, and Penny Ford.
Since 2007, Soul II Soul has reunited with the lineup of Jazzie B and Caron Wheeler. Wheeler took a departure from the group after their reunion tour in 2010, with Charlotte taking her place as lead vocalist. In 2013, Charlotte left the group and Wheeler rejoined the group. In December 2016, Soul II Soul released their second live album "Origins", which features Wheeler and Jazzie B as the lineup. In 2017, Kelly rejoined the group and alternated lead vocals alongside Wheeler. In 2019, Nadine Caesar began touring with the group as a featured lead vocalist.

artist.name: Lisa M
artist.profile: Born January 16, 1974, better known by her stage name Lisa M or her nicknames "The Queen of Spanish Rap" or "La Suprema" (The Supreme), is a Puerto Rican singer-songwriter, record producer and dancer who fuses genres such as pop, merengue, rap, and reggaetón. She is known as one of first female rap artists to debut in Puerto Rico. She disclosed her lesbianism on Facebook on April 19, 2010.

artist.name: Big Fun
artist.profile: Big Fun were a British boy-band (1989-94), founded by Phil Creswick (Philip Creswick), Mark Gillespie and Jason John (Jason Herbert), and were first produced by [a=Marshall Jefferson] and then by [a=Stock, Aitken & Waterman].

artist.name: Neil Rushton
artist.profile: DJ and record label executive. Ran [l=Inferno (4)] while a northern soul DJ in the late 1970s/early 1980s. Released the influential [r=57919] compilation on Virgin and managed [a=Derrick May], [a=Juan Atkins] and [a=Kevin Saunderson]. A&R manager at [l=Kool Kat], [l=Network Records] and its subsidiary [l=6 x 6 Records]. Ran [l=SuSU] and [l=Endulge Records] with [a=Everton Webb] in the 2000s. Incorporated [l=NRD Music Ltd.] in 2020. Father of [a=M.C. Crazy Claire].

artist.name: Justin Robertson
artist.profile:
DJ and producer from UK. Used to work in Eastern Bloc Records in Manchester as well as co-promoting the Most Excellent club night.
Also owned the [l=Master Detective] label.

artist.name: Alec Empire
artist.profile:
Born: May 2, 1972, West Berlin, Germany
Founded [l=Digital Hardcore Recordings (DHR)] together with Peter Lawton, as well as [l=Geist Records] and [l=Eat Your Heart Out].
Alec Empire's productions have been extremely varied, but he remains most noted for being key in the development of the digital hardcore sound, which was an initiator to breakcore.

artist.name: Gas
artist.profile: Electronic music artist from Cologne, Germany. Known for co-founding the German techno label Kompakt alongside Michael Mayer and Jürgen Paape.

artist.name: Dr. Israel
artist.profile: Brooklyn musician who also ran the [l339684] studio in Williamsburg, Brooklyn, New York.

artist.name: Patric Catani
artist.profile: Electronic / Hip-Hop / Experimental and Chiptune musician, producer, remixer based in Berlin. Known for his work in/with [a=Ec8or], [a=Candie Hank] and [a=Puppetmastaz].

artist.name: Spectre
artist.profile: Skiz Fernando Jr. started his career as a journalist. Writing articles in different fanzines or magazines like Rolling Stone, interviews and records' back cover, he published in 1994: "The New Beats : exploring the music, culture and attitudes of hip-hop" (Anchor Books). Thanks to 1000$ that he borrowed from the producer Bill Laswell, he founded Wordsound Recordings in December of the same year.

artist.name: DJ Soul Slinger
artist.profile: DJ Soul Slinger is a pioneer of electronic music in the US. As the founder and creative head of Liquid Sky Music and Liquid Sky D-Sign, he has been responsible for some of the most forward-thinking music and clothing to emerge from the New York City underground over the last several years. In 1994, he released the Jungle Liquid Sky/Ethiopia 12", the first release on Jungle Sky Records, and the first drum and bass record by a US producer. The second 12", Abducted/Jungle Bossanova, is now a classic, and inspired an entire album of remixes (Missing Time - The Abducted Remixes). Since then, he has released several singles, such as the seminal Diana/Theresa white label with DJ Wally, as well as appearing on every one of the This Is Jungle Sky compilations. In 1997, Soul Slinger released his first full-length album, Don't Believe, a record of surprising diversity and genre-hopping. While Don't Believe is a reflection of Soul Slinger's production skills, Upload, his continuous mix CD, is a display of what he can do to move the dancefloor. Soul Slinger's unique blend of hip-hop, drum and bass, illbient, latin, and other influences. In the past few years, he has emerged as one of the most popular and sought-after drum and bass DJ's. While Soul Slinger generally appears by himself, SUPA MC TC Izlam of the Zulu Nation also frequently accompanies him. Soul Slinger has also collaborated at jazz festivals with artists such as Bill Laswell, Elliott Sharp, and Derek Bailey.

artist.name: Zulutronic
artist.profile: Zulutronic is only one of a dizzying abundant cachet of names used by Cologne-based producer/Pharma label boss Cem Oral, who has also released records as G104, Jammin' Unit, and, together with longtime partner Walker, Air Liquide. Zulutronic is a collaborative project between Oral and fellow Cologne-based producer Roger Cobernuss, aka Kerosene; both share credentials as part of the influential Structure label group, an important component in the experimental wing of Germany's early-'90s dance music underground. Although apart the pair's material has ranged from hardcore acid, techno, and trance to downtempo breaks and even beatless ambient, Oral and Cobernuss with Zulutronic focus on mid- to up-tempo experimental hip-hop and electro, bringing an artful, scientific economy and a new-school weirdness to minimal, funk-fueled breakbeat. While most of the group's material (including the full-length LP/CD Mission Zulu One) has appeared on Oral's own Pharma label, the group have also contributed tracks to compilations on Mille Plateaux (Electric Ladyland, Vol. 3) and Virgin (Macro Dub Infection, Vol. 2). The pair have also played a number of live gigs under the name, including club and festival appearances.

artist.name: Ice
artist.profile: ICE (official capitalization) is an industrial music band formed by guitarist Justin Broadrick and saxophonist/vocalist Kevin Martin.

artist.name: Mark B
artist.profile: Mark B was from Kingston, South West London. Or as he called it, 'K Boro' (the Royal Borough of Kingston) which he also named his self owned independent record label. He passed away 1.01.2016.

artist.profile: Members: Mathis Mootz & Philip von Eschwege

artist.profile: Hanayo (born 1970) is a Japanese musician and artist, she currently lives and works in Berlin.
