artist.name: Brooks
artist.profile: Producer from Derby, UK.

artist.name: Tonuri
artist.profile: Nurhee aka Tonuri started his musical career when he was quite young, giving his first steps into DJying by the age of 16. He grew up in between many sound influen- ces. From 1990 on he has been listening to a lot of different kinds of Black Music. so by that time he used to mix Rap, Hip Hop and Funk, merging and balancing right these styles of music. Nurhee represents the mystic side of Blue Dye, he doesn't opens his mind to other people easily, keeping his soul and emotions preserved. or at least, it takes a while to get into his deepest feelings. And surely that if you ask him a reason for such, his answer will be: "listen to my music - then you will see me and understand me." Nowadays Nurhee has become much more then a DJ only. He is a global artist and musician that combines the best of a few worlds: DJying, music composition, multimedia programmer and label management. He produced tracks like HOPE under his pseudonym "Tonuri" in 1999...

artist.name: Amber
artist.profile:
Born (17 September 1969) in Holland and grew up in Germany, Amber's parents first inspired her to music. She is well known for her #1 hits "This Is Your Night" and "Sexual", and also appeared with Jocelyn Enriquez & Ultra Naté as "Stars on 54" to cover the Gordon Lightfoot hit "If You Could Read My Mind".
In late 2004, after a long string of hits with Tommy Boy, Amber stepped out and created her own label, JMCA Enterprises, whose first relases were (to no surprise) Amber's album "My Kind Of World," and the LP's lead single "You Move Me."

artist.name: Gabrielle
artist.profile:
British RnB and soul singer/songwriter born on May 16, 1970 in Hackney, London.
She released her debut single "Dreams" in 1993 which topped the UK Singles Chart the same year. After a few quiescent years Gabrielle made a comeback with "Rise", which became Gabrielle's second UK number one in 2000. The album of the same name also reached the top spot in the UK Albums Chart, where it stayed for three weeks. "Out of Reach" from the soundtrack to the film Bridget Jones's Diary reached number four in the UK Singles Chart.

artist.name: Markus Schulz
artist.profile: German-American DJ and producer, born 3 February 1975 and based in Miami, Florida. Founder of the label [l=Coldharbour Recordings] and Schulz Music Group (SMG), an artist management company.

artist.name: Paul Brtschitsch
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Germany. He is the founder of [l=Rootknox].

artist.name: Pablo Gargano
artist.profile:
In the early nineties Italian born and bred DJ Pablo Gargano accepted an invitation from his friend to join him in Belfast, Northern Ireland. There, he started work for the 'Underground' record shop in Queen Street and became their resident DJ at their series of events known as 'Hellraiser'. Taking place at the Ulster hall, they regularly got crowds of 1300 people and within a few months his success rate was soaring with regular exposures in national press and undergrounds radio show passion fm. He found he was getting the frequent opportunity to hadline alongside the highest echelon of world D.J.s he soon obtained a strong reputation within the techno acid and trance genre with his individual style.
After moving to London and virtually having to start from scratch it only took 6 months to get back in the business, releasing singles for Rabbit City Records & Metropolitan Music getting DJing sets in clubs such as: Eurobeat2000, Innersenses, Club 414, and Heaven in London. After the return of his old partner Steven from Italy, they decided to open Eve Records in 1995 thanks to a P&D Deal with Flying distribution.
As the aforementioned doors and the rest of the world started to open for him as a DJ playing in Holland, US, Canada, Israel, Turkey, Australia and all over the UK. More of his releases were seeing success getting played in sets by the likes of Paul Oakenfold, Pete Tong, Sasha, Digweed and John '00' Flemming.

artist.name: Lost Witness
artist.profile: British Trance project founded by Simon Paul in 1997. Over the years Paul worked with producers Miguel Sassot, Paul Janes & Simon Kemper and vocalists Tiff Lacey, Kate Smith, Nina James, Tracey Carmen & Vicky Fee to write and produce many successful hit-singles such as "Happiness Happening" and "Red Sun Rising", which both entered the UK charts at no. 18 and 22 respectively.

artist.name: DJ Mishka
artist.profile: London-based techno/trance/acid producer. For many years Andreas worked alongside [a=Peter Ward] behind the counter at Choci's Chewns in Soho, London. He took his DJ name from one of his cats - Mishka.

artist.name: Ariel (3)
artist.profile: Ariel Belloso (born 14 September 1967) is an Argentinian DJ. He began his career from the age of 15, he has to date held a total of 31 successive international club residencies since his beginning in the city of Rosario in 1983. He relocated in Europe in 1991 to further continue his career holding key residencies at some of London’s major clubs. Ariel was first signed by A&M Records, more releases and remixes followed on London Records, EMI, Ministry Of Sound.

artist.name: Mark Tyler
artist.profile: UK Techno & Hard House Producer / DJ.

artist.name: Corellian
artist.profile: Progressive psychedelic trance collaboration from UK

artist.name: Ayla
artist.profile:
Ayla is the recording and performing name used by German trance music producer, songwriter and DJ, Ingo Kunzi.
The project was created in 1995 by Ingo Kunzi, along with the single of the same name,"Ayla" went onto be remixed by [a=Taucher] as the single's most known remix. It entered the UK Singles Chart at #22. After "Ayla's" success Kunzi co-wrote/produced the collaborative track "Ayla Part 2" in 1998, which entered the German sales chart top 40. In the same year, Kunzi remade Cosmic Baby's "Liebe" and entered at #1 in the German dance chart.
Ayla's fifth single, "Angelfalls" was released in 1999, which went to the top 3 of the German dance chart. In late 1999, Ayla released his first album, Nirwana, which went gold in Norway and Singapore. Kunzi went on to produce two more Ayla tracks ("Singularity" / "Brainchild II, Sun Is Coming Out"), before taking a break on the project in 2003.
After putting his Ayla project on hiatus for many years, Kunzi released the digital single, "Ayla 2010", in 2010. On 23 February 2011, the second Ayla studio album, 'Unreleased Secrets', was released.

artist.name: IG Culture
artist.profile: Early innovator in Broken Beat. Ran [l=Main Squeeze]. IG Culture is perhaps best known as one of the most notable producers responsible for the wealth of material that has emanated from West London under the tag ‘Broken Beat’ and spawned the CoOp night at Velvet Rooms then Plastic People. IG Culture is one half of NameBrandSound with Alex Phountzi (Green Money, Bugz In The Attic) with releases on Ninja Tune and Arena Yard that blend the styles of footwork, dancehall, rare groove, and broken beat into their 'brukwurks' style. In 2017 launched "CoOp Presents" and founded "Selectors Assemble", a growing loose collective of DJ's producers, artists, movers and shakers that assist projects connected with CoOp presents and beyond.

artist.name: GK Deep
artist.profile: West London in 2001. The first all worlds are released from label PEOPLE to which BROKEN BEATS founder IG CULTURE serves as PRODUCER as a Japanese and Big Name such as Ayers Roy and Marvin Gaye and the name are placed in a line in the compilation of IRAMA.

artist.name: Julie Dexter
artist.profile: UK artist, currently based & living in Atlanta, Georgia, USA.

artist.name: T.Kolai
artist.profile: T.Kolai is the collaborative effort of DJ Taha and flutist Kolai ([a=Nicolas Laget]). This core duo never misses an opportunity to include in their music the many talented musicians which reside around the DC area. Percussionist Tony Tavaras often joins t.kolai live at club Andalu (see also the link below) where they have a residency. Other local and international talents swing by the studio for impromptu sessions: Will Smith (tenor and soprano saxophone - no relation with the actor), Max (santur - traditional Iranian instrument), David Cosby (Jazz guitar), Mustafa (vocals), Ishara (vocals), etc. Contact Info: taha@vinylicious.com OR kolai@mac.com

artist.name: Hellpass
artist.profile: Progressive house producer and DJ. Founder of [l=Hellpass Records]. Co-founder of [l8480].

artist.name: Electric Envoy
artist.profile: German techno project founded by [a=Sven Dedek] in 1998. Worked with [a=Alex Bau] until 2004 and with [a=Marco Polo (6)] for a brief period in 2012. The project ended in 2014.

artist.name: Casino Versus Japan
artist.profile: Milwaukee-based musician/producer, born on May 5, 1973. Son of [a2591999].

artist.name: Humrush
artist.profile:
Humrush was formed in Kristiansand, Norway by brothers Torkjel Austad (keyboards/samples) and Halvard Helle (guitars) in the beginning of 1998.
In July 1999, Humrush debuted live with three intimate and successful concerts at the Quart Festival with audiences including Massive Attack and Blur. The concerts set the standard for the sound of Humrush being a mix of live instruments and samples. This could not have been done without the additions of talents like Klaus Blomvik (drums), Roger Williamsen (double bass) and Tine Wulff (vocals).
In May 2000, Torkjel and Halvard starts working on their "Turbotape" release. The songs chosen were "Sugarpop" and "Only Way" and the ground work is produced together with Daniel Steinsland in Oslo.
Later, bass (Roger Williamsen) and drums (Klaus Blomvik) for "Turbotape" were recorded in Kristiansand. Vocals by Tine Wulff are recorded in Oslo. The final production is finished a month later by Torkjel, Halvard and Peter Edwards.
Humrush - Turbotape (12") was released on Groovecentral Recordings in April 2001 with remixes by Christian Watkins (Slowpho) and Dan Drehn (Selsius).
On April 25 2001 "Sugarpop" played at NRK P3 (Norwegian radio) for the first time. But this was literately just the start. In the following months the various songs of the Turbotape release was played regularly through the year.
Humrush last performance was at By:Larm 2002 festival.

artist.name: Slowpho
artist.profile:
Slowpho is Hilde Drange from Bergen and Christian Watkins from Kristiansand, a duo making electronic music that can lead the way to new and interesting soundscapes combining Hilde's vocals and Christian's programming and production.
The band was originally formed in the spring of 1997 by Kjetil Nordhus and Christian Watkins as a band majorly inspired by the Bristol sound (Massive Attack, Portishead, Tricky). The summer 1997 they were joined by Hilde Drange on vocals. in the end of 1998 Kjetil Nordhus left, but Drange and Watkins choose to continue. their first big live concert were held during the Quart festival in 1999 where they played for a celebrity audience such as Blur, Massive Attack and the crownprince of Norway. By then Slowpho had moved towards a more drum'n'bass inspired material.
After a period of testing of various styles, constellations and band members, they finally released their first EP in 2001. Musically they are often placed in the nujazz genre, but the duo themselves are not quite sure if they fit in there. They fiddles around with everything from strange and eerie sounds, to lavish string arrangements. If you add a dash of drum’n’bass, electronica and jazzy vocals, you get what Slowpho’s all about. The result can be enjoyed on "Hi-Fi Sounds For Young Norwegians", released on Beatservice early 2002, and licensed to Rambling Records in Japan (with bonus tracks) and Water Music Records in USA (as "Hotel Sleep" with new mixes of some of the tracks). The album have been very well received around the world, it has even been hailed as "Album of the year" by buyers on CDnow.com.

artist.name: David Axelrod
artist.profile: American composer, arranger and producer (born April 17, 1931, Los Angeles, California, USA - died February 5, 2017, Los Angeles, California, USA). His music has been sampled by [a=A Tribe Called Quest], [a=The Beatnuts], [a=Organized Konfusion], [a=Diamond D], and many, many others.

artist.name: Noel Gallagher
artist.profile:
Guitarist and singer, b. May 29, 1967 Longsight, Manchester, England. Best-known as lead guitarist and songwriter with Manchester group Oasis, he has also collaborated with numerous artists in the dance field, notably Chemical Brothers with whom he recorded the UK number one hit "Setting Sun".
On August 28, 2009, Noel announced that he was leaving Oasis. In July 2011 he held a press conference announcing the new group [a2384642].
Brother of [a53887].
Used to be an [a=Inspiral Carpets] roadie before he was famous.

artist.name: Liquid Liquid
artist.profile:
Early-80s band on the legendary [url=http://www.discogs.com/label/99+Records+%282%29]99 Records[/url] label. Often namechecked because of the track "Cavern" from which parts were replayed by [a=Grandmaster Flash & The Furious Five] for their hit single "White Lines".
Liquid Liquid were originally a much more experimental sounding band called [a4559502] who released one 7" before reforming as Liquid Liquid and basing their sound around a much more percussive framework. They would invite members of the audience to bring their own percussion to their gigs and at one of these, they were so impressed when [a=Dennis Young] brought a marimba that they invited him to join. They developed their own unique sound which they called 'big beat' (a term some English producers would use more than a decade later for something entirely different).
Liquid Liquid initially broke up in 1984, but got back together and have worked with [l=DFA] on new material.

artist.name: Cut Chemist
artist.profile:
As well as being one of the ablest solo turntablists on the globe, Cut Chemist is also a member of two highly rated crews: underground rap kings Jurassic 5 and the Latin-funk band Ozomatli. He came up with the L.A. rap group Unity Committee, and debuted on wax with the B-side of UC's 1993 single "Unified Rebelution." The track, "Lesson 4: The Radio," was a tribute to and continuation of Double D and Steinski's seminal hip-hop collage masterpieces "Lessons 1-3,"and included nods to Indeep, Bob James, Spoonie Gee, and Dan Ackroyd.
Soon after the record's release, Unity Committee came together with another group, Rebels of Rhythm, to form Jurassic 5. Cut Chemist kept quite busy with the group, contributing "Lesson 6" to the group's eponymous EP and producing the entire record. He also delved into remixing (DJ Shadow, Liquid Liquid) and outside work (scratching for Less Than Jake; appearing with another Los Angeles group, Ozomatli). In mid-1997, Cut Chemist recorded his album debut, Live at the Future Primitive Sound Session, with Shortkut of Invisibl Skratch Piklz. His tracks have also appeared on two seminal turntablist compilations, Return of the DJ, Vol. 1 and Deep Concentration. - John Bush
He left Jurassic 5 in 2006.

artist.name: Blue Foundation
artist.profile:
Danish cinematic dream pop, shoegaze and electronics band centered around singer, multi-instrumentalist, and producer [a=Tobias Wilner]
Wilner founded the group in 2000, inspired by Mark E. Smith's approach to forming a band (the Fall), recruiting a rotating cast of traditional musicians throughout the band's history to inspire creativity. Today the band is based in Prospect Heights, Brooklyn and Copenhagen, Denmark. Since 2008 their line-up has consisted of Tobias Wilner (producer, multi-instrumentalist and vocalist) and Bo Rande (producer, multi-instrumentalist). Blue Foundation has gained major recognition with the remix of their song "Eyes on Fire" by Zeds Dead with more than 80 million plays on YouTube. Blue Foundation is sampled by Lil Durk featuring French Montana on his track "Fly High", Young Thug's song "She Noticed".
Blue Foundation released their fifth album "Blood Moon" worldwide September 2. 2016. The album features appearances from Mark Kozelek (Sun Kill Moon/ Red House Painters), Erika Spring (Au Revoir Simone/ Nice As Fuck), Jonas Bjerre (Mew), Sonya Kitchell, Sara Savery (aka Drop the Gun) and Findlay Brown and is out on Dead People's Choice Records.
Blue Foundation has been featured in major films like Twilight, Miami Vice and on TV shows like CSI: Miami and The Vampire Diaries. The band has been touring all over the world for the last 5 years.

artist.name: Cai Bojsen-Møller
artist.profile: Danish drummer, composer and producer.

artist.name: Big Bud
artist.profile: DJ, producer and label owner of [l=Sound Trax].

artist.name: Intense
artist.profile: Drum and bass group who began life in the early-1990s as a hardcore dance act. They have recorded for labels such as [url=http://www.discogs.com/label/1559-Rugged-Vinyl-Records]Rugged Vinyl[/url], [url=http://www.discogs.com/label/5035-Underground-Level-Recordings]Underground Level[/url], and [url=http://www.discogs.com/label/124-Good-Looking-Records]Good Looking[/url].

artist.name: Fabio
artist.profile: British Drum 'n' Bass DJ and occasional producer, born in September 1964. Former resident DJ at influential club nights Speed, Tempo, Promised Land and resident DJ/promoter at Swerve. Hosted radio shows on Phase One and, with [a=Grooverider], Radio 1, Kiss FM and Rinse FM. Founder and owner of the [l=Creative Source] label. Fabio's nickname came from his time on Brixton-based pirate station Phase One around 1984 when he was asked to come up with a DJ name before his first show. He remembered a conversation with a girl he knew about baby names and thought "Fabio" was a great one. He introduced himself as "DJ Fabio" and the name stuck. Fabio is known for pioneering a style of Drum 'n' Bass he named 'liquid'. The name came from hip hop group [a=The Likwit Junkies]' description of their music as 'liquid funk'.

artist.name: Lovesky
artist.profile: Progressive house/progressive trance DJ from New York, United States.

artist.name: Rob Rives
artist.profile: US engineer, producer, performer and writer in the modern dance scene, based in Brooklyn, NY. He was one of the in-house engineers at [a=Francois Kevorkian]'s [l=Axis Studios]. Uses various guises as well as the nickname "floppus".

artist.name: DJ Tool
artist.profile: Psy-trance project from India.

artist.name: DJ Disciple
artist.profile: American DJ/producer based in New York City. Runs [l=Catch 22 Recordings] and [l=Nympho Soundz].

artist.name: Erick Morillo
artist.profile:
Born: March 26, 1971 in New York, NY. Died: September 1, 2020 in South Beach, FL.
Colombian-American DJ and house music producer. Erick was raised in his pre-teen years in Cartagena de Indias, Colombia and returned at the age of 11 to the USA in Union City, New Jersey where he began DJing at weddings for friends and family. Founded [l=Subliminal] Records in 1997 with [a=Harry "Choo Choo" Romero] and [a=Jose Nuñez].

artist.name: Steve Gurley
artist.profile: UK-based producer.

artist.name: Slide
artist.profile: Psychedelic goa trance project based in UK

artist.name: Corrina Joseph
artist.profile: Corrina Joseph is a British singer who gained recognition through her collaborations with Basement Jaxx. She later signed to and released solo material through their label, Atlantic Jaxx, later affiliated with One Little Indian. She has also sung on productions by artists like Underground Solution, Kamasutra, Masterbuilders, Nightmares on Wax, Classen Collective, Russ Gabriel and Harlem Zip Code. The most successful production she has appeared on is Basement Jaxx's single "Flylife", which reached number 18 on the British singles chart in 1999.

artist.name: People Under The Stairs
artist.profile: People Under the Stairs were an American hip hop group from Los Angeles, California, formed in 1997 and disbanded in 2019. Since their inception, the group has consisted solely of Christopher Portugal (Thes One) and Michael Turner (Double K). The group employs a DIY work ethic of sampling, MCing, DJing, and producing all of their output. Despite difficulties achieving mainstream or chart successes, the group has shunned critiques that they are an "underground" group. They have achieved notoriety and praise from fellow musicians.

namevariations.name: P.U.T.S
P.U.T.S.
P.u.t.s.
PUTS
People Down The Stairs
People Under The Stairs Of Los Angeles
Puts
The People Under The Stairs

artist.name: Shirley Bassey
artist.profile:
Born: 8 January 1937 in Tiger Bay, Cardiff, Wales, UK.
Formally known as Dame Shirley Bassey DBE (Dame Commander of the Order of the British Empire), after HM Queen Elizabeth II awarded her recognition with the 1999/2000 New Years Honours List announced on 31 December 1999.
Shirley Bassey is a singer, often regarded in the 'diva' sense, due to her strong and masterful prowess in her vocal talents, continuing into her latter years. She is perhaps best known as having done the vocals on the theme tunes to three films in the popular British espionage film franchise James Bond.

artist.name: Shuggie Otis
artist.profile: American singer-songwriter and multi-instrumentalist. Born November 30 1953 in Los Angeles, California, USA. Son of [a=Johnny Otis] and brother of drummer [a=Nicky Otis].

artist.name: Sleep Walker
artist.profile: Japanese jazz group. Members: [a=Masato Nakamura] (saxophone), [a=Hajime Yoshizawa] (piano), [a=Tomokazu Sugimoto] (bass), [a=Nobuaki Fujii] (drums).

artist.name: Timothy Pedersen
artist.profile: A seasoned digital creative Art Director and Designer, focusing on entertainment marketing, from movies and TV shows to video games and automotive commercials.

artist.name: Stanton Warriors
artist.profile: British DJ and production duo formed of Dominic Butler and Mark Yardley. Both met while working for garage label Tuff Jam and decided to release their own music together in 2001.

artist.name: Desert
artist.profile:
Liverpool based producers/remixers
Desert also features regular guest appearances by vocalist Fitzy and percussionist Mav.

artist.name: DJ Gogo
artist.profile: House/Tech-House/Progressive-House DJ & producer from Zürich, Switzerland.

artist.name: Capricon
artist.profile: This is a duplicate/misspelled entry. Please see artist Capricorn

artist.name: Stone Factory
artist.realname: Ramon Van Den Heuvel & Alex Van Der Voort

artist.name: Junk Project
artist.profile: Electronic music project created in 1994 by [a=Andreas Krämer], [a=Faris Al-Hassoni] and [a=Markus Schneider]. Markus Schneider left the project in the ‘90s. In 2019 [a=SLT] joined the group.

artist.name: Slacker
artist.profile:
Well-known UK production team which was responsible for hits like "Your Face," and "Scared," which became anthems of the mid-90's progressive house/trance scene. Later Shem McCauley went solo and continued work under the 'Slacker' guise with multiple tracks being released on his [l=Jukebox In The Sky] label.
Sadly, Shem put an end to his own life in Bangkok, Thailand over the weekend of 21st/22nd January 2012.

artist.name: Hybrid
artist.profile:
UK based electronica collective that were formed in 1995 by producers Mike Truman, Chris Healings, and Lee Mullin. Hybrid are known for their ever-evolving sound, blending the likes of Breakbeat, Progressive House, Trip Hop, Trance and Classical refrains. Throughout the 90's the trio enjoyed success as master remix artists, reworking the likes of BT, Filter, Moby and many more, before finding their own success with the 1999 single 'Finished Symphony'. This was soon followed by the debut album 'Wide Angle', which featured Julee Cruise providing vocals. The album was reissued as 'Wider Angle' a year later and included their cover of 'Kid' by The Pretenders (titled 'Kid 2000') with Chrissie Hynde recording new vocals for the track and tracks from a live session.
The outfit's second album 'Morning Sci-Fi' saw the team add semi-permanent vocalists and guitarists Adam Taylor and Tim Hutton. The 2003 outing also includes guest musicians Peter Hook & Kirsty Hawkshaw and came with a bonus DVD that featured live footage as well as a documentary of their time supporting Moby on his 2000 tour. The following year Hybrid were approached by composer Harry Gregson-Williams to provide music for the movie 'Man On Fire'. This would become a long standing relationship with not only the songwriter but also in the movie industry, providing scores and music production for 'X-Men Origins: Wolverine', 'Total Recall' (2012), 'Fast Five' and more.
Returning to a more symphonic sound, Hybrid released their 3rd album 'I Choose Noise' in 2006 and was Co-Produced by Gregson-Williams. It was in the Spring of 2007 while on tour promoting the album that vocalist Charlotte James became their new lead vocalist. In the same year record label Distinct'ive released 'Re_Mixed', a compilation showcasing remixes of Hybrid's back catalogue.
2008 saw the release of 'Soundsystem 01', a double-disc mix collection with the first CD being based around actual film scores fused with ambient electronica and the second containing more upfront, club-oriented music, represented by remixes of music by the likes of Sasha, Quivver and Elite Force. Later that same year Hybrid released a new single 'The Formula of Fear', which featured Charlotte James on the vocals. Their fourth album was finished the same year, but the team wasn't happy with its sound so decided to start from scratch.
Finished in 2010, 'Disappear Here' was Hybrid's boldest album at that point, focusing on more vocal led songs - all of which by singer Charlotte - and garnered 5 singles as well as a special edition reissue featuring a collection of remixes & reworkings of songs from the original record. In 2012 the compilation 'Classics' was released, which was a greatest hits featuring the biggest hits from the group's career.
In 2015 it was announced that founding member Chris Healings had departed the group, leaving the now husband and wife duo Mike & Charlotte Truman helming the project.
In 2018 the band released their 5th record 'Light Of The Fearless', leading with the single 'Light Up'. It featured the City of Prague Philharmonic Orchestra and a cover of Tom Petty's 'I Won't Back Down'.
In 2021 Hybrid released their 6th album 'Black Halo'. They also have a patreon where many rare songs are made available, as well as Hybrid's extensive remix back catalogue.

artist.name: Tekara
artist.profile: The name Tekara is derived from that of far-east toy company Takara, who make everything from Transformers to rock'n flowers.

artist.name: Pink Bomb
artist.profile:
UK collaboration formed in July 1996 by Darren Anthony Bayton (initial financial backer & DJ) and Robert A Preston (lead producer/programmer and engineer), Matt Booker (executive producer) joined them in 1997.
The name "Pink Bomb" came from the Roland MC303 Groove Box's sound bank list that Darren purchased for the studio as new extra equipment that he had already built up. The self-titled "Pink Bomb" was the second track that Darren and Robert wrote together. The group "disbanded" in 2002.
From 2017 Rob Preston has revived the Pink Bomb moniker as a solo project, and is releasing new material retaining its distinctive melodic house and chill-out combination - with the added inclusion of a retrowave 80's edge.

artist.name: Dave Randall
artist.profile:
One of the original DJ club circuit pioneers, Dave Randall has played his own music and his sound to critical acclaim that have landed him many friends in club scene's from Bogota to Bombay. His particular fondness of the Dutch club scene started at small underground parties in the center of Amsterdam and the renowned Dance Valley events, twice to over 50,000 party people on the huge main stage, and regularly at the Impulz festivals around Holland. His UK residencies have included some very popular and ground breaking nights including HQ in Holland, Trade, Fish, DTPM, Ripsnorter, FF and seven years at the Zap Club in Brighton alongside Eric Powell and his Bush label. Other prolific dates have included Gods Kitchen in Sao Paulo, Bogota, and Melbourne, Enchanted Forest in Adelaide, Ice and Gas in Johannesburg, Advenjah in Adelaide and Brisbane, Ambassador Gavioli in Slovenia and a full season at both Eden and Amnesia in Ibiza.
His record labels include [l=Chug N Bump Records], [l=South Coast Records] and [l=BooM].
His promotion company 'Clubnet' worked with many artists during the nineties. 'Clubnet' were responsible for the club, radio and TV promotions behind many established club acts including S-Express, Bomb The Bass, The Beatmasters, The Beloved, 808 State, Seal, Depeche Mode, Erasure and Neneh Cherry.

artist.name: Chris C
artist.profile: UK hard house / trance producer, remixer & DJ. He's been the force behind many labels such as [l=Mind Over Matter], [l=Mohawk Records], [l=Nile Records], [l=Aztec], [l=Pirate Wax Records] & [l=Apache Records].

artist.name: DEX
artist.profile: Hard House project from UK.

artist.name: Montreal Sound
artist.profile: UK House act related to the label [l=Shift Recordings]. For the Disco duo of the same name look for [a=Montreal Sound (2)].

artist.name: Shakira
artist.profile: Colombian singer and songwriter, born on February 2, 1977 in Barranquilla, Atlántico, Colombia, to a Colombian mother of Spanish-Italian descent and an American father of Lebanese-Italian descent. Writing songs and belly-dancing since childhood, her first album was released when she was a mere 14 years old. Other Spanish-language albums followed, each faring better than its predecessor, but it was not until 2001 that she achieved a world-wide breakthrough with the English-language "Laundry Service" album. Shakira is fluent in Spanish, English, Italian and Portuguese.

artist.name: Tony De Vit
artist.profile: Born 12/9/57: Died 2/7/1998 - DJ from Kidderminster, UK active from the 1970s until the 1990s. Owned [l=Jump Wax Records].

artist.name: Point 2
artist.profile: Adrian aka Dj SDR and Justin TROOPER Johnson wrote all these tracks together in the mid to late 90's messing with different sounds. SDR already running Shoebox records with Subsonic was the engineer working with Q bass and Akai hardware. Justin his dj mate wanted to push for sounds outside the Drum and bass SDR was famous for. Together they made banging beats with crazy twists. Tony de vit picked up on these records and signed 1 to his Live in Tokyo mix album in 1997!

artist.name: Jon The Dentist
artist.profile:
UK hard house/trance producer.
Jon The Dentist has released tunes on such labels as Tidy Trax, Sony, EMI Positiva, Nukleuz, ID&T, and his own labels Phoenix Uprising, Platinum and Boscaland.

artist.name: Polaroid
artist.profile:
Pop / Alt. Rock band from Athens, Greece. Initially founded under the name EMPTY by Grigoris Daralas, it was renamed to POLAROID in the beginning of 2007. Contact: polaroid@mail.gr
[b]Members:[/b] Kelly: vocals Grigoris: guitar Costas: bass Thomas: drums Konstantinos: keys Achilles: electronic sounds

artist.name: Adamski
artist.profile:
Born December 4th, 1967 in New Forest, England. Nowadays he's known as [a=Adam Sky]. He has an older brother [a=Mark Tinley].
He took his artist name from the American UFO enthusiast George Adamski. In 1988 he moved into a bedsit above the Paradise Kebab shop in Camden, with [a=Jimi Polo] living in the adjoining bedsit. Jimi introduced him to house music, and taught him how to use a sequencer and a 909 drum machine. Adamski also played the piano line from Jimi's track 'Better Days'. Adamski began touring the UK with his set-up, starting off playing at Le Petit Prince in Kentish town, a club owned by his then manager Phil Smith. He ended up as the resident live show for Amnesia in Ibiza.

artist.name: Polarity
artist.profile: Irma Van Dijk, Mirko van Dommelen

artist.name: Force Mass Motion
artist.profile: Not the [url=http://www.discogs.com/artist/Michael+Wells]Michael Wells[/url] from GTO. Techno/trance producer from Crowborough, East Sussex, born 1973. Heavily influenced by Humanoid's "Stakker Humanoid" and the Sterns club in Worthing. He started making dance tracks in 1989. He was discovered by Colin Faver after passing a cassette of his tunes to him during a DJ set. He combined his music production in the early 90s with studying Aerospace Engineering at Kingston University.

artist.name: Liquid Language
artist.profile: House producers Shuren and Squeed active in London between 1996-2002. Liquid Language tracks are credited to Shuren-Squeed on the slevee notes

artist.name: Kobayashi
artist.profile: Born inner-metro, Brooklyn1979 and transported to the stix outside NYC half a decade later (Spring Valley,NY), KoBAyASHi has creatively crafted the melodic/chaotic sound of his generation since the mid-90s. His influences span from Wonder to Monk to Ayers to Sly to CoFlow to Bjork to Tjader to Puente to Radiohead to Portishead to Primo to Pete Rock. Such an indiscriminate love of music allows KoB to expand the boundaries of HipHop by introducing the lost sounds of the past to his culture..!> Today, having relinquished himself to the music, KoB serves as a relentless faucet that pipes the ancient audio magic revived by his musical forefathers from 12deep black vinyl wells of sound, in hopes that it will Saturate the Space Between Souls and Soil Washed Brains.!. Just listen for the changes...

artist.name: Mortal
artist.profile: Instrumental-only side project from electronic experimentalists, CODE

artist.name: Pako & Frederik
artist.profile: Dutch progressive duo from The Hague, Netherlands

artist.name: Deepsky
artist.profile: Hailing from Los Angeles, Jason Blum -the electronic producer behind Deepsky- is a New Mexico native who has produced music since the mid-90's. Jason formed Deepsky with former member J. Scott G. after the two met at the University of New Mexico through mutual college friends. They initially came together as rave promoters and organized some music events in New Mexico. Some of their remixes includes progressive treatments of tracks for Madonna, Seal, David Bowie, Paul Oakenfold, America, and the Crystal Method. J. Scott G. left the group in November 2006. [a=Jason Blum] continues to produce and perform under the Deepsky name.

artist.name: Mystica
artist.profile: Mystica are Avi Peer, Yossi Master and Charlie Ben-Moha, and they are working together making trance music since 1996. They come from the town Ashdod in the south of Israel. In Israel's independence day '97, Paul Oakenfold arrived in Israel with the Perfecto Fluoro tour, Paul played Mystica's Ever Rest twice that night in the Cinerama Club, and a few days later Perfecto contacted BNE. In February 1998 Ever Rest was released on a single in Perfecto with two remixes (by Brain Bug and Tour De-Force). The single was a big success and entered the charts in Britain. Following that success they started to work on a second single and in May that year Perfecto released a second single- African Horizon. The single had two mixes by X-Cabs and another track Bliss, which became a big hit. Again the single entered the charts. The guys also released an album on Purple Trance/Planet Music in 1997 under the name One Man Game- Total Trance. Mystica's tracks where included in a number of compilations, among them: Paul Oakenfold- 2 Years of Cream, Pete Tong- Essential Collection, Global Clubbing- mixed by DJ Tiesto, Paul Oakenfold- 007: New-York, Israel's Psychedelic Trance vol. 1, Israel's Psychedelic Trance vol. 2, Future Trax, Destination Israel, Trance Mix 7, Spiritual Moves. For more than a year Mystica worked on their album in BNE and finally Age of Innocence was released in July 1999 on BNE/Dragonfly/Cosmophilia also as a double LP. They also released a single out of this album- China.

artist.name: Fathers Of Sound
artist.profile: Italian duo devoted to House. They started in 1991. Studio: [l=F.O.S. Recording Studios] Productions: [l=Fathers Of Sound Productions]

artist.name: Miro
artist.profile: (Hardcore) Techno artist from Frankfurt am Main, Germany. His mother is German, his father Serbian.

artist.name: Ambrosia
artist.profile: Vocalist from Newark, New Jersey.

artist.name: Krystal
artist.profile:
Kenneth Bager, Michael Pfundheller & Nosie Katzmann were the main forces behind Krystal. Some recordings included Dino Breit & Steen Thøttrup.
Nosie went to Copenhagen for a recording session with Kenneth & Michael. He stayed less than 24 hours and 9 songs were recorded, but only a few has been released so far.

artist.name: Cyclone Tracy
artist.profile: Paul Hutsch, Steve Baltes

artist.name: Planet Heaven
artist.profile:
Carl Daniel aka Planet Heaven has been producing and performing music for over 15 years. His first major success was writing, producing and performing with the Bassheads culminating in three top 20 UK hits and an album including the prolific 'Is There Anybody Out There?' In 2000 he set up Helix Music and Hard Discs Recordings with Jonny Williams ( Polydor, Island, Sony BMG) and Rob Murphy off the back of Planet Heaven's 'Nautical Bodies', a huge progressive trance track championed by Paul Oakenfold. Helix/Hard Discs released Planet Heaven's dark and dirty Fire Fly/ Revoltions EP whilst working with new artists including Nelski who has since had his own success's with Rythm Code, his solo work and calloborations with Chris Lake.
In 2001 after remixing for 3 Beat, they released the next three Planet Heaven singles, Predator, Prey and 'Where Are We Going To?. The last Planet Heaven work was in 2004 producing Clannad's Caislean Oir for their remastered album Macala.

artist.name: Private Productions
artist.profile: Dutch house producer.

artist.name: B.B.E.
artist.profile: Italian - French project. Producers [a76923], [a16317] and [a1240], most known for "7 Days And One Week," a Top 10 hit around the world in 1996. Retired for a couple of years, reformed in 2001 to build a new studio in Lille, France.

artist.name: Stereo Dancer
artist.profile: Their track "Absolute Reason" from [m926467]. appeared under the Stereo Dancer alias.

artist.name: The Scumfrog
artist.profile: Producer/songwriter/recording artist/DJ/remixer Jesse Houk was born in 1971 and raised in Amsterdam, the Netherlands, where he lived until 1997, the year he moved to New York. Before he created his Scumfrog alter ego, Jesse DJ-ed under his own name in various clubs in Amsterdam, Greece, and the UK. In 1997 he had his first international Scumfrog club hit on the dutch label "Mighty High Musique" called "The Watersong".

artist.name: Miss Kittin
artist.profile: French DJ and producer, born July 15, 1973 in Grenoble, France. She is member of [a8767] and founder of [l=Nobody's Bizzness] and well known for collaborations with a wide range of electronic artists.

artist.name: Robbie Rivera
artist.profile:
Roberto Luis "Robbie" Rivera (born August 7, 1973) is a Puerto Rican house music producer and DJ. He has an extensive catalog of original productions and remixes to his credit, ranging from tribal to progressive house, as well as incorporating garage and Latin elements. He and his wife Mónica Olabarrieta have homes in Miami and Ibiza.
Runs [l=Juicy Music] and Cruciformed Music.

artist.name: The Return
artist.profile: House alias used by Hip Hop engineer, DJ, and producer Jose "Choco" Reynoso.

artist.name: Nathan Hawks
artist.profile: Claude Monnet, Frédéric Devanlay

artist.name: DJ Linus
artist.profile: German house producer and DJ, started his career in 1991. Owner of the Exun record label and record shop. Also founder of Exania Publishing.

artist.name: Schiller
artist.profile:
Schiller is the main project of Christopher von Deylen, a German electronic music composer who produces music which can be considered trance and ambient. Since he himself does not sing on his tracks, he often collaborates with other famous singers in the electronic genre, such as Peter Heppner, Kim Sanders, Maya Saban, miLù, Sarah Brightman, Moya Brennan, Despina Vandi, Xavier Naidoo, Helen Boulding, Alexander Veljanov and musicians like Mike Oldfield.
The name of the band is taken from one of von Deylen's heroes, Friedrich Schiller.

artist.name: Devilfish
artist.profile:
Frederik Soderstrom and Sina Morshed Solouk are the 'Evil Minded Producers' behind the awesome live techno assault known as Devilfish. The Devilfish collaboration began after the two DJs met in Greece and decided to return to their native Sweden to conquer the world of techno.
From their first-ever studio production "Strawberry Fields" and debut album "Freakshow", right through to the instant classic "Manalive" and the new album "Techwise and Otherwise", Devilfish have been prolific producers of solid, slammin' techno and innovators of their very own 'Evil Minded' sound. Devilfish tracks and remixes have occupied the crates of major techno taste-makers across the world including Luke Slater and Joey Beltram, and their track "Manalive" was recently licensed to a mix CD by the legendary Carl Cox.
The Devilfish duo has also earned an extensive range of remix credits for such high-profile artists as Trevor Rockcliffe, Thomas Schumacher and La Familia, and their remix of Andrew McLauchlan's "Love Story" has dominated dancefloors worldwide for its fusion of hard-edged techno beats with funky percussion.
The Devilfish sound is best described as funky and hard techno designed for the dancefloor, and represents sophisticated studio production techniques and an uncanny ear for quality drum loops and effects. The Devilfish collaboration combines the resources of two extremely talented producers in their own right, as both Frederik and Sina record for numerous solo projects and other collaborations under different monikers.
Together Sina and Frederik are also responsible for the techno madness created by Intergalactic Phonk (on Frequent), the funky house grooves of SF Express and Pimp Squad (on Boo Records) and for the dark, evil electro sounds of CFA (on Meltdown Communications). Through their innovative recordings within these and other projects, Devilfish are constantly pushing the barriers of traditional techno. The complex loops and effects created by the duo are continuously evolving and consistently funky.
The two 'Evil Minded' Swedish producers continue to release a range of diverse and innovative, but always slammin' and funky tracks through Bush Records and its offshoots Boo and Meltdown Communications. After a successful Australasian tour in early 2001, Devilfish returned to Europe to continue their solid studio production and to take their awesome live show on tour across the world.

artist.name: Otto Von Schirach
artist.profile:
Otto von Schirach is an IDM and breakcore artist from Miami, Florida (USA), active since 1998. He is of Cuban/German descent.
He is infused with life energy healing force from the Bermuda Triangle and is capable of teleportation. His Bass is unrivaled among contemporaries. His style is more sporadic and noisy than other artists in the genre, and his visual aesthetic leans on the heavy metal side rather than electronic.
He has released most of his work on the Schematic and Beta Bodega labels, and was featured in the 2002 documentary Electro-Dziska. Otto's live performances include outfits, masks and props, and he has toured with the band Skinny Puppy. He is also credited on the Skinny Puppy albums The Greater Wrong Of The Right and Mythmaker. In 2011 he has toured U.S.A. with Atari Teenage Riot.
He also does a large amount of work in the areas of music production and remixing and has collaborated with several artists such as Modeselektor on their records Happy Birthday! and Monkeytown.
Recently von Schirach has become increasingly involved with the Miami Bass Warriors, a Miami Bass focused project with Jose el Rey, El Tigre and Debbie D from Avenue D. Their debut video, "Going Back to Calle 8," was featured on Pitbull's show La Esquina.

The artist name Ongaku (音楽) means "music" in Japanese.
The track title Mihon (見本) means "sample" or "specimen".

artist.name: 2000 & One
artist.profile: The name for his project 2000 And One got Dylan from his former pincode: Two, Zero, Zero, One

artist.name: Wladimir M
artist.profile:
Wladimir M. founded Eevo Lute Muzique together with his friend Stefan Robbers. He was sometimes described as the conscience of the label and its artists, something he expressed in poems, lyrics and art. Together with music producer Stefan Robbers he created this and many other electronic compositions, where the influence of great minds is taken into a new, unexplainable direction.
Nowadays Wladimir is an active visual artist and creates urban street art on locations you wouldn't expect. In 2001 he stayed on the streets of his beloved hometown Eindhoven for the better part of the summer and shot a 30 minute documentary with lots of highways, train tracks and industrial concrete, accompanied by a driving techno sound. Wladimir is now fantasizing about a sequel tho this project: Eindhoven 2010. But before that he might make some new Tricky style music; crazy beats and strange noises with soft-spoken dutch words about responsibility and fatherhood.

artist.name: Karl The Voice
artist.profile: French West Indies soul singer and composer based in Paris. Played [a=Nelson Mandela] in the 2008 musical Soweto. Died in February 2024.

artist.name: Lo-Fi Sensibilities
artist.profile: Lo-Fi Sensibilities is the pseudonym assumed by Steve Paton (4th Wave) whilst recording for Mo Wax.

artist.name: Plavka Lonich
artist.profile: Plavka Coleridge (née Lonich) is an American born singer- songwriter of Peruvian and Croatian descent. Currently lives in Los Angeles, California, USA. She is best known for her work as lead singer with trance duo [a=Jam & Spoon]. Previous to that she was the singer for [a=The Shamen]. She writes and guests for other dj's and producers as well.

artist.name: Nik Kershaw
artist.profile: British singer, composer and multi-instrumentalist (born on 1 March 1958). He was born in Bristol as the younger son of a flautist and an opera singer. Kershaw grew up in Ipswich where he started to sing and play guitar in several bands until he became the today known solo artist.

artist.name: Mos Def
artist.profile:
Brooklyn, NYC-based rapper of Trinidadian descent, Dante "Beze" Terrell Smith (better known simply as Mos Def) is also making a career as a Hollywood actor.
Born: 11 December 1973 in Brooklyn, New York.
He first gained interest as part of the group [a=Urban Thermo Dynamics] with his younger brother [a=DCQ] and sister [a=Ces]. Has since worked with many respected artists from many different genres, notably with [a=Talib Kweli] together as group [a=Black Star].
Also known as Yasiin Bey since he handed his old name Mos Def to TV pundit [a=Stephen Colbert] after appearing on his TV show "The Colbert Report".
Father of [a=Laila!]

artist.name: Soulphiction
artist.profile: Soulphiction (born 1972, died of cancer on May 22, 2021, aged 49) was a Stuttgart based, German DJ and producer. He was a co-founder of [l=Philpot].

artist.name: Lorez Alexandria
artist.profile: Lorez Alexandria (b. August 14, 1929 in Chicago, Illinois – d. May 22, 2001 in Los Angeles, California) was an American jazz and gospel singer. She began as a singer in churches and spent numerous years as part of an a cappella choir. She worked the local Chicago club scene before moving to Los Angeles in 1962 to further her career.

artist.name: Leroy Hutson
artist.profile: American vocalist, keyboardist, songwriter and producer. Born 4th June 1945, Newark, New Jersey, U.S.A. He was a roommate of [a=Donny Hathaway] and co-wrote the 1970 hit “[m=119694]”. In 1971 he replaced Curtis Mayfield as The Impressions’ lead singer. He left The Impressions in 1973 for a solo career but still stayed with the Curtom label until December 1979. Owns [l=Triumph Records (17)].

artist.name: Basement Khemists
artist.profile: Kansas City’s legendary hip hop crew, The Basement Khemists, comprised of J Lee, Taha Khaliq aka Bro Neves, and Joc Max, formed in the mid-1990’s.

artist.name: Andy Harlow
artist.profile: Andy Harlow (born 1945) is an American Latin music flute player. He currently hosts a Latin jazz Radio program in Miami. Brother of [a=Larry Harlow].

artist.name: Record Player
artist.profile: Soul/funk group from Dayton, Ohio during the disco/show band era. Organized team with multi-faceted musicians that switched instruments between songs, bringing drummers to the front line, and guitar players to the horn section. Moved to LA and disbanded in the early '80s. Group members: Charles Carter • Sam Carter • George Gee • Charles Jackson • Delbert Jones • Keith Nash • Ronald Nooks • Alan Fostor • Kevin Hurt • Larry Kidd • Mike Jennings • Dallas Webster • Lamar Webster • Tom Black • Cheryl Rutledge • Lorenzo Pryor • Alan Bridges • Geoffrey Trammel • Keith Trammel • Dale Ogletree.

artist.name: Family Of Eve
artist.profile:
Dance band from Pittsburgh, PA, USA.
In 2005, Family of Eve was featured in the February edition of the Pittsburgh Courier as the “longest running band in the Pittsburgh tri-state area.” They were recognized for some of their original works, such as “I Want To Be Loved by You” and “Having It So Bad For You.”

artist.name: Masta Ace
artist.profile: Masta Ace (real name Duval Clear, born December 4, 1966) is a Brooklyn-based hip-hop MC known for his storytelling, longevity, and influence in the genre. He first gained recognition in 1988 as part of [a=Marley Marl]'s "In Control, Vol. 1", where he made his debut with the track "The Symphony". Shortly after, he released his first solo album, Take a Look Around (1990) on [l=Cold Chillin'] Records. In 1993, Masta Ace transitioned to [l=Delicious Vinyl], releasing SlaughtaHouse (1993) and Sittin’ on Chrome (1995) with his group [a=Masta Ace Incorporated]. These albums blended East Coast lyricism with West Coast-influenced production, showcasing his versatility. After a brief hiatus, he returned in 2001 with Disposable Arts, a critically acclaimed concept album. He followed up with A Long Hot Summer (2004), both released under his own label, M3 Macmil Music. Ace is also a member of [a=E.M.C. (4)], [a=Crooklyn Dodgers], and [a=Action (2)]. His influence spans generations, having inspired numerous artists with his intricate rhymes and conceptual storytelling. He has been married to singer and radio host [a=Leschea] since 2001, and they have one daughter together.

artist.name: Carlos Garnett
artist.profile:
Panamanian American jazz tenor saxophonist, flutist, composer, arranger and singer. Born on December 1, 1938 in Red Tank, Panama Canal Zone. Died on March 3, 2023.
While in Panama, he was very influenced by Panamanian latin music and jazz scene as well as his West Indian heritage and calypso music. He was then heavily influenced by [a=James Moody], [a=Paul Desmond], [a=Zoot Sims] among other saxophonists.
Garnett moved to New York from Panama in 1962, where he played at jam sessions with upcoming icons such as [a=Rashied Ali] and [a=Roland Alexander] as well as in jazz organ trios and rock groups. He would go on to play and record with [a=Freddie Hubbard] (1968-1969), [a=Art Blakey & The Jazz Messengers] (1969-1970), [a=Andrew Hill] (1969-1970), [a=Charles Mingus] (1970), [a=Miles Davis] (1972), [a=Pharaoh Sanders] (1972) and others. From 1970 he led a number of large ensembles combining jazz with samba, Afro-Cuban, calypso and rock. His group The Universal Black Force was a pioneering presence in the Afrocentric and Afrofuturist movements of the early 1970s.
In the mid 1970s Garnett released 5 albums on Muse, starting with possibly his best known album Black Love (featuring Mother Of The Future and Banks Of The Nile), which opened the path for musicians like [a=Dee Dee Bridgewater]. He also collaborated on several of [a=Norman Connors] albums of the 1970's providing arranging and some of his compositions. Other collaborations include: [a=Milt Ward], [a=James Mtume], [a=Robin Kenyatta] and [a=Charles Earland]. In the 1980s he only did a few scarce recordings, but in the 1990s he returned with new music for his albums Resurgence, Fuego En Mi Alma, Under Nubian Skies and Moon Shadow on Highnote among other recordings.
His autobiography Mango Tree Musician (written with [a=Jaime J. Ortiz]) was published by McFarland Press Publishing in 2024-09 (ISBN 9781476690247).

artist.name: Joe Washington
artist.profile: Guitarist and arranger best known as the writer of the book "Beatles for Classical Guitar."

artist.name: Pendro
artist.profile: Pendro is the name of an ongoing solo experimental music project by Tim Jones from North Wales, since the 1990s. During his teenage years in the 1970s, he started experimenting with various musical instruments, sound sources and tape recorders. He sometimes uses his tape archives as additional sound sources, together with more modern technology and devices, as part of his various project recordings and collaborations. He was also a member and co-founder of the groups~ Berkowitz Lake & Dahmer, White Cube and Triclops.

artist.name: Space
artist.profile: Space was a French group, created around Didier Marouani (aka Ecama) and Roland Romanelli, acclaimed for their 1977 UK disco hit "Magic Fly". They then began to release 'obscure' underground disco material like the blue vinyl LP "Just Blue", containing tracks like "My Love Is Music", "Final Signal" and "Symphony". Also work such as "Tango In Space" and "Carry On, Turn Me On" on a 12" version.

artist.name: Complex
artist.profile: Aka Orlando Voorn, Dutch [b]techno[/b] artist.

artist.name: Bango
artist.profile: DJ and producer from Detroit, US.

artist.name: Aphrodisiac
artist.profile: US-american House artist.

artist.name: Looney Tunes
artist.profile: Electronic/ house, US, since late 1980s, known for the hit [i]Just As Long As I Got You[/i]

artist.name: Emjay
artist.profile: Dance artist of French-Canadian descent from Rockland, Ontario

artist.name: Auch
artist.profile: Side project house, dance floor

artist.name: Tomas Jirku
artist.profile: Canadian techno/experimental electronic musician, producer and photographer from the Czech Republic, based in Vancouver (b. 1979). [b]Tomas Jirku[/b] released his first EPs in the early 2000s on a Canadian net-label [l=No Type], coming to prominence after performing at the inaugural [l=Mutek Festival] in 2000. He was signed by [l=Force Inc. Music Works], which released Jirku's debut full-length album, [i][m=139654][/i] 2xLP/CD, in November of the same year. Tomas Jirku graduated from the [url=https://discogs.com/label/1682535]Ontario College of Art and Design[/url] in 2003. The artist further produced six full-length albums and numerous EPs on many prestigious independent labels, including [l=Traum Schallplatten], [l=Klang Elektronik], [l=Revolver Recordings], [url=https://discogs.com/label/4747]Audio.NL[/url], [l=Alien8 Recordings] and others. He had a hip-hop/parody "gangsta rap" side project, [a=GI Joe Killaz], and released a few records in a collaborative duo with a fellow Canadian musician, [a=Robin Judge].

artist.name: Jeremy
artist.profile: Gabber/hardcore producer.

artist.name: Soulstance
artist.profile: Italian brothers influenced by the easy-swinging dance music of Brazil and '70s groove, based in Milano

artist.name: Missing Channel
artist.profile: Rob Noise ([a=Robert Hood]) and DJ 2120 ([a=Claude Young]) used this alias for their releases on Hardwax.

artist.name: Lawrence Burden
artist.profile: Brother of [a=Lynell Burden], [a=Lenny Burden], [a=Lorne Burden] and [a=Lance Burden]. Lawrence Burden is a co-founder/owner of [l=430 West] and [l=Direct Beat].

artist.name: Ballet Mechanique
artist.profile: Dutch producer and co-founder of graphic design studio Stoere Binken Design. Made his debut on Eevo Lute Muzique in 1996 and was Eevo’s in-house graphic designer from 1995 till 2003.

artist.name: Source
artist.profile: The Source banner was dropped when an English pop/dance combo carrying the same name threatened action. It was later changed to "The Source Experience".

artist.name: Junior Vasquez
artist.profile: DJ, producer and designer from Lancaster, Pennsylvania, born August 24, 1949. Moved in New York City in the early 1970s with the ambition to become a fashion designer. Resident DJ at [l=Sound Factory, New York] (1989-1995), Tunnel (1995-1996), Palladium (1996-1997) and Twilo (1997-2001). Ran [l=Junior Vasquez Music] in the 2000s.

artist.name: Judge Jules
artist.profile:
Born on 26th October 1965.
Julius studied at the London School of Economics (LSE) from 1986 - 1988 and earned a 2:1 degree in law. During his first year he was involved in the student union and started organising parties where he DJ'd and due to the nature of his studies he earned the moniker "Judge Jules".
